conclusion.marcel: Who would have thought an Islay whisky could be so cheeky and playful? The Caol Ila 11 Years Ambrosial Feast is a true mood-lifter with its exotic, fruity charm and surprising umami notes – an experience you simply have to enjoy.
conclusion.sascha: This 11-years Caol Ila impresses with its unusual aromatic profile and the harmonious balance between a powerful 57.3% ABV and delicate yeast notes. Despite deviating from typical Islay profiles, it displays a confident structure that marks it as an exciting representative of the Special Releases 2024.

faq.question: How does the Caol Ila 11 Years Ambrosial Feast differ from classic Islay whiskies?
faq.answer: Unlike typical Islay whiskies, which are often heavily smoky and peaty, the Caol Ila 11 Years Ambrosial Feast presents exotic fruit notes like lychee and mango alongside unusual yeast aromas. It combines the island’s characteristic mineral base with surprising sweet and hop-like nuances.

faq.question: Why does this whisky have such an intense flavor variety at 57.3% ABV?
faq.answer: The high alcohol content of 57.3% amplifies the aromas and delivers a lively spiciness ranging from white pepper to vanilla ice cream and yeasty notes. At the same time, it allows for a complex balance between fruitiness, spice, and mineral influences without the alcohol overpowering the subtleties.

faq.question: What role does maturation of only 11 years play in the character of this whisky?
faq.answer: Although the Caol Ila 11 Years is relatively young, it has achieved remarkable maturity thanks to the special cask selection and Islay’s climate. The medium-length aging permits intense fruit and yeast aromas while retaining enough fresh, vibrant notes that make the whisky particularly dynamic.

item.name: Cigar Malt 14 Years (2024) The Tasteful 8 - Brühler Whiskyhaus
item.description: Some whiskies are for drinking, others for savouring - and then there are the whiskies that simply go perfectly with a good cigar because they hardly differ from it in taste.

image.alt: Cigar Malt 2024 14 Years The Tasteful 8 - Brühler Whiskyhaus

tasting_notes.nose: Rich caramel meets ripe banana and a deep leather note. These are joined by honeyed dates, which add a pleasant sweetness. The distinct red fruit component is particularly exciting - a hint of berries that gives the whisky a complex, elegant flavour. A flavour that is best imagined with a freshly cut cigar in your hand.
tasting_notes.palate: The dried fruits, especially plums and dates, provide a full, rich base. But then comes the real star: tobacco. The flavour is reminiscent of a well-matured cigar leaf - spicy, slightly earthy and with a pleasant dryness. Old leather adds an extra dimension and provides a touch of nostalgia, like sinking into an old leather armchair in front of a fireplace.
tasting_notes.finish: his shows why this whisky has earned the name Cigar Malt. The finish is slightly earthy, with a pleasant leatheriness and a subtle burnt note reminiscent of caramelised cask edges. There is also an intense spiciness that lingers for a long time and harmonises wonderfully both on its own and in combination with a cigar.

conclusion.marcel: If you're looking for a whisky that scores with character and drinkability, you've found it here – the Cigar Malt 2024 seduces with sweet leather spice and a wonderful tobacco note. Perfect for cozy evenings with a cigar in hand, it simply brings joy to the glass without being overly complicated.
conclusion.sascha: The Cigar Malt 2024 displays an impressive balance between sweet dried fruits, spicy tobacco, and an elegant leather texture that come together harmoniously on the finish. Technically well matured, it convinces with its complex structure, making it an excellent representative of its category, especially for lovers of robust, well-composed whiskies.

faq.question: Why does the Cigar Malt 2024 pair particularly well with cigars?
faq.answer: The 14-years Cigar Malt 2024 is characterized by aromas of tobacco, aged leather, and subtle spiciness that strongly evoke the flavors of cigars. These shared notes create a perfect harmony when enjoying whisky and cigar together.

faq.question: How does the 14-year age influence the taste of this whisky?
faq.answer: With 14 years of maturation, the whisky develops a complex depth expressed in notes of dried fruits, caramel, and leather. The age ensures a balanced interplay of sweetness, spice, and a pleasant dryness that shapes the whisky’s character.

faq.question: What makes the independent bottler Brühler Whiskyhaus special with this whisky?
faq.answer: Brühler Whiskyhaus carefully selects casks and whiskies to create unique bottlings. For this Cigar Malt 2024, particular attention was paid to the harmonious alignment of aromas, enabling an intense and characterful tasting experience.

item.name: Teaninich (2016/2023) - Sansibar
item.description: Teaninich is one of those Scottish distilleries that rarely appear as an original bottling - making the releases from independent bottlers all the more exciting. Zanzibar has taken on Teaninich, which was distilled in 2016 and bottled in 2023, and bottled it at a whopping 60.2 % ABV.

tasting_notes.nose: From the very first sniff, you realise that this is not a typical Highland whisky. Instead of heavy sherry or smoky notes, you get a surprisingly fresh and light fruity nose. Vanilla and citrus are in the foreground, accompanied by a fine herbal note reminiscent of herbal tea. The alcohol is noticeable, but not disturbing - you realise that the whisky has strength. All in all, a very exciting, lively flavour!
tasting_notes.palate: The first sip brings with it an oily, almost syrupy texture. In terms of flavour, it starts with a clear vanilla note before fresh lemon and apricot come into play. Then it becomes more exotic: a hint of rose water provides a slightly floral note. The wood is also noticeable, but without overwhelming the whisky. The honey sweetness provides balance, but the strength of the high alcohol content is always present. A small drop of water can help to develop the flavours even better.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is medium to long, but not quite as harmonious as hoped. The fresh wood remains in the foreground, accompanied by a slight bitterness that makes the whisky appear somewhat unrounded.

conclusion.marcel: This Teaninich is a real character with a fantastic freshness and a solid punch – perfect for anyone who isn’t afraid of young, intense Highland whiskies. Sure, the woodiness and bitterness in the finish aren’t to everyone’s taste, but that’s exactly what makes it exciting and lively. A few drops of water are almost mandatory here to round off the experience.
conclusion.sascha: The Teaninich 2016/2023 presents itself with a clear structure of bright fruit notes, vanilla, and subtle herbs, all shaped by the high alcohol strength. Despite its robust 60.2% ABV, it feels quite balanced, although the slightly bitter woody tone in the finish somewhat disrupts the harmony. Adding water is recommended to more finely bring out the balance between sweetness and spice.

faq.question: Why is the Teaninich 2016/2023 from Sansibar not a typical Highland whisky?
faq.answer: The Teaninich 2016/2023 stands out due to an unusually fresh and bright fruit aroma that combines vanilla, citrus, and herbal notes. Typical heavy sherry or smoky notes, which characterize many Highland whiskies, are largely absent here, making it particularly lively and different.

faq.question: How does the high alcohol content of 60.2% affect the taste and drinking experience?
faq.answer: The high alcohol content provides a powerful presence that is noticeable both on the nose and the palate. It intensifies the aromas but can also come across as sharp. A few drops of water help to better unfold the aromas and make the whisky more approachable.

faq.question: Why is the finish of the Teaninich 2016/2023 somewhat uneven and what causes the bitterness?
faq.answer: The finish is medium to long but dominated by fresh wood and a slight bitterness. This bitterness can be influenced by the young maturation of 7 years and the choice of cask, which makes the whisky’s finish less harmonious than expected.

item.name: Allt-a-Bhainne (2006) A dreamy nighttime drink Cask 108.34 - The Scotch Malt Whisky Society
item.description: The Allt-a-Bhainne 2006 A Dreamy Nighttime Drink (Cask 108.34) from the renowned Scotch Malt Whisky Society encapsulates the quintessence of the Speyside region in an impressive single malt matured for 14 years. With a powerful alcohol content of 64.4% ABV, this whisky is not for the faint-hearted, but an experience for connoisseurs who want to engage with complex worlds of flavours.

image.alt: Allt-a-Bhainne 2006 A dreamy nighttime drink Cask 108.34 - The Scotch Malt Whisky Society

tasting_notes.nose: From the very first sniff, the whisky reveals an unusual but fascinating palette of flavours. A hint of WD40 - industrial but not overpowering - is paired with floral notes and sweet honey. A hint of roasted sugar is reminiscent of a crème brûlée crust, while vanilla ice cream and coconut add a tropical accent. A subliminal scent of Marsala rounds off the olfactory experience.
tasting_notes.palate: This whisky envelops the palate with its oily, smooth mouthfeel. Notes of honey and malt come to the fore, while subtle floral nuances and a light spiciness unfold. The sweetness on the lips gives the whisky a pleasant balance, while a hint of hay adds a refreshing, earthy component. Despite the high alcohol content, the flavour remains soft and inviting.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is long, flowing and warming - a real highlight that emphasises the character of the whisky.

conclusion.marcel: The Allt-a-Bhainne 2006 impresses with its intense aroma and smooth mouthfeel, turning every sip into a moment of enjoyment. Despite its powerful 64.4% ABV, it remains surprisingly approachable and invites relaxed lingering in the evening. A true dream for anyone who loves their whisky with character and warmth.
conclusion.sascha: With a remarkable balance between intense aromatic density and soft texture, the Allt-a-Bhainne 2006 showcases its technical finesse. The harmonious blend of sweet, floral, and spicy notes, along with a long-lasting, warming finish, makes it a structured single malt offering complex moments of enjoyment. Ideal for connoisseurs who appreciate precise composition and depth.

faq.question: How does the high alcohol content of 64.4% ABV affect the taste and drinking experience of the Allt-a-Bhainne 2006?
faq.answer: The high alcohol content delivers intense aromas and a powerful mouthfeel but can also increase the sharpness. Many enthusiasts recommend diluting the whisky with a little water to better perceive the complex flavor notes such as honey, malt, and floral nuances.

faq.question: What makes cask number 108.34 special for this whisky?
faq.answer: Cask 108.34 is a single sherry cask that imparts the whisky’s characteristic sweet and spicy notes, including hints of Marsala and crème brûlée. The single cask bottling guarantees a unique flavor profile that sets it apart from other Allt-a-Bhainne releases.

faq.question: Why is the Allt-a-Bhainne 2006 referred to as 'A Dreamy Nighttime Drink'?
faq.answer: The name reflects the complex, soothing aromas and the long-lasting, warming finish that make this whisky especially suited for relaxed evenings. Its layered flavor structure invites slow enjoyment, making it ideal as a day’s end dram.

item.name: Glen Spey (2015/2023) - Sansibar
item.description: Young whiskies with over 60 % ABV can either be real insider tips or completely impetuous alcohol clubs. The Glen Spey 2015/2023 from Sansibar with 60.1% ABV lies somewhere in between. Sometimes it is round and pleasant, sometimes you wonder whether it could have done with a few more years in the cask.

tasting_notes.nose: The nose is light and spring-like. Vanilla, hay and floral notes dominate the picture, accompanied by lemony freshness and unripe fruit. Sounds nice at first, but somehow that certain something is missing - the flavours seem as if they haven't quite found each other yet.
tasting_notes.palate: Here comes the surprise: despite the 60.1%, Glen Spey is round and surprisingly accessible. Caramel provides a pleasant sweetness, while red fruits blend in discreetly. The high alcohol strength is well integrated without burning the palate - an unexpected plus point.
tasting_notes.finish: Here the whisky shows another interesting side: chocolate, oak, a hint of red fruit and a pleasant spiciness. The bitterness is pleasant and well integrated this time - not a disruptive tannin bomb, but a worthy finish.

conclusion.marcel: A whisky that, despite its youth, remains surprisingly charming and approachable – the Glen Spey 2015/2023 shows that 60% ABV doesn’t necessarily mean fireworks. The floral notes and pleasant sweetness are enjoyable, even if it still lacks that certain extra maturity. For anyone brave enough to explore new dram territories, it’s definitely worth a try.
conclusion.sascha: The Glen Spey 2015/2023 impresses with a remarkably balanced integration of its high alcohol strength and a clean structure that feels pleasantly rounded despite its youthful freshness. The balance between floral, fruity, and spicy elements is solid, yet the lack of complexity and slight immaturity limit the potential of this 7-years whisky. Recommended for fans of powerful Speyside who are looking for a well-tuned, albeit still developing, dram.

faq.question: How does the high alcohol strength of 60.1% affect the tasting experience of the Glen Spey 2015/2023?
faq.answer: Despite the high 60.1% ABV, the Glen Spey 2015/2023 presents itself as surprisingly smooth and approachable. The alcohol strength is well integrated without burning the palate, allowing the whisky’s pleasant sweetness and subtle fruit notes to shine through.

faq.question: Why does the aroma of the Glen Spey 2015/2023 seem somewhat underdeveloped on the nose?
faq.answer: The spring-like nose with vanilla, hay, floral notes, and unripe fruit suggests that the whisky is still young and that the aromas haven’t fully melded together yet. A longer maturation in cask could bring more harmony.

faq.question: What distinctive characteristics does the Glen Spey 2015/2023 bring to the finish?
faq.answer: On the finish, the Glen Spey offers a pleasant balance of chocolate, oak, red fruits, and a subtle spiciness. The bitterness is well integrated, providing a dignified and not overly dominant conclusion.

item.name: Distilled in Australia 7 Years (2024) The Tasteful 8 - Brühler Whiskyhaus
item.description: Australian whisky has received more and more attention in recent years - and for good reason. The climatic conditions ensure accelerated maturation, which often leads to intense and unique flavour profiles.

image.alt: Distilled in Australia 2024 7 Years The Tasteful 8 - Brühler Whiskyhaus

tasting_notes.nose: The first impression is unusual and complex: umami notes, old leather shoes and mushrooms - that may sound daring, but it adds depth. There are also clear spicy and herbal notes reminiscent of Mediterranean cuisine. Caramel and a hint of solvent then emerge in the background.
tasting_notes.palate: On the tongue, the whisky shows its intense side. Oily, very dry and fruity at the same time - a mixture that creates tension. The fruitiness is present, but it does not go in the typical sherry or tropical direction, but rather remains on the dense, concentrated side. The dryness gives it a sophisticated, almost tannic structure.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is earthy with notes of chilli and pepper. A pleasant warmth remains, but oak is barely noticeable - which is not surprising for a 7-years whisky from Australia.

conclusion.marcel: This Australian whisky is a true adventure in a glass – with its umami notes and spicy dryness, it challenges the drinker and rewards with a distinctive character. Those looking for straightforward sweetness should move along, but explorers will find plenty of drinking pleasure and an exciting flavor expedition here.
conclusion.sascha: The Tasteful 8 demonstrates a remarkable balance between intense dryness and dense fruitiness, with spicy and earthy nuances providing a complex structure. Despite its youth, it impresses with a precise aromatic profile that, far from classic vanilla or sherry tones, occupies a unique position within Australian whiskies.

faq.question: How does the Australian climate influence the maturation of this 7-years whisky?
faq.answer: The warm Australian climate accelerates maturation, causing flavors to develop more quickly and distinctly. This results in a complex flavor profile despite the relatively short aging period.

faq.question: What role does the independent bottler Brühler Whiskyhaus play with this whisky?
faq.answer: Brühler Whiskyhaus purchases the whisky from the distillery and independently handles selection, maturation, and bottling. This process creates a unique character that can differ from the distillery’s original product.

faq.question: Why is the finish of this whisky more earthy and spicy rather than woody?
faq.answer: The relatively short maturation of 7 years combined with the Australian climate leads to less dominant oak flavors. Instead, earthy notes and spicy elements like chili and pepper come to the forefront.

item.name: Glendronach Cask Strength Batch 12
item.description: Do you remember how we used to talk about "sherry bombs"? If you've been around the whisky world for any length of time, you'll remember when a really sherry-heavy whisky was automatically considered a flavour grenade. The Glendronach Cask Strength Batch 12 with its 58.2% vol. is such a whisky that makes me think - not because it's bad, but because it perfectly shows how our perception has changed over the years.

tasting_notes.nose: Today, from today's perspective, this Glendronach seems almost "mild" - although objectively speaking it is still intense enough to take a beginner's breath away. From the very first nosing, it is clear that the sherry cask speaks for itself. Sherry clearly dominates the nose, accompanied by candied oranges that smell like Christmas market memories. Cinnamon joins in - not intrusive, but like a warm coat on a cold Highland day. Walnut and black pepper provide the necessary complexity, while liquorice adds an interesting dark note. This is classic Glendronach as you know and love it. But if you smell it today and think of the extreme first-fill maturations that are now standard, you'll understand what I mean: This is a nice sherry bomb, but one from a time when subtlety was still part of the game
tasting_notes.palate: This Glendronach is very sherry forward, no question. But do you know what surprised me the most? The toffee moment. Yes, you read that right: Nut, chocolate and caramel merge here to create a flavour experience that immediately reminds me of those golden sweets. Black pepper returns and shows that despite all the sweetness, there is still bite. Light strawberry flits by - almost shyly, as if it didn't dare to join in with all the dark fruitiness. Dried plums dominate the "dried fruit fuss", as I affectionately call it - those concentrated, sweet flavours that are typical of good sherry maturation. The whole thing is slightly dry and pleasantly spicy. It's like an old friend telling you a story: familiar, warm, but still interesting enough to listen to.
tasting_notes.finish: The medium-long finish shows why Glendronach has had such a good reputation for decades. It finishes dry, with dark chocolate that is elegant rather than bitter. Slightly bitter oak mingles with it. Sultanas round off the finish.

conclusion.marcel: This Glendronach Cask Strength Batch 12 feels like a warm embrace from the good old days – not an overpowering sherry bomb, but a dram full of character and charm. If you appreciate classic, more subtle sherry notes, here you’ll find a reliable companion for a cozy dram. Simply a whisky that’s enjoyable and evocative of fond memories.
conclusion.sascha: The Glendronach Batch 12 impresses with a balanced integration of sherry that, despite its high cask strength, never feels overloaded. The harmony between sweet dried fruits, spicy oak, and a subtle sharpness speaks to a mature cask selection and a well-rounded maturation profile. A structured Highland malt offering classic sherry elegance without exaggeration.

faq.question: What makes the Glendronach Cask Strength Batch 12 stand out compared to other sherry-matured whiskies?
faq.answer: The Glendronach Cask Strength Batch 12 is distinguished by its balanced sherry intensity, which doesn’t overwhelm despite its 58.2% ABV. It combines classic sherry aromas like candied oranges and dried fruits with a pleasant spice of black pepper and licorice, giving it a special complexity and subtlety.

faq.question: How should one best enjoy the Glendronach Cask Strength Batch 12 to discover all its nuances?
faq.answer: The best way to enjoy this whisky is neat or with just a few drops of water to fully unlock its aromatic diversity. Water opens up the complex notes of Toffifee, dried fruits, and spices without disturbing the balance. Room temperature around 18-20 degrees Celsius is ideal to experience the full range of flavors.

faq.question: Why does the Glendronach Cask Strength Batch 12 have no age statement, and does that affect its quality?
faq.answer: Batch 12 is a so-called No-Age-Statement (NAS) whisky, meaning no exact minimum age is specified. Glendronach uses various cask strengths and maturation levels here to achieve a consistent flavor profile. The absence of an age statement says nothing about quality; rather, the focus is on sherry cask maturation and harmonious taste.

item.name: Royal Brackla 12 Years
item.description: Who says that royal whiskies always have to be meek and polite? The Royal Brackla 12 years from the Scottish Highlands region thoroughly dispels such prejudices. This bottling from the traditional distillery in Cawdor proves that even with an alcohol content of just 40%, there can be plenty of steam in the kettle.

tasting_notes.nose: There is more going on here than you would expect at 40 per cent. A marvellous aroma of dark plums rises to the nose, accompanied by a pleasant hint of cocoa. This is accompanied by slightly leathery nuances - not necessarily what you would expect after a wellness weekend, but certainly full of character. There is a hint of something musty, which gives the whole thing an earthy depth. Most interesting, however, are the pickled red fruits, which smell like a well-stocked jam jar from grandma's pantry.
tasting_notes.palate: Black pepper dominates the first encounter and makes it clear that this Highland malt makes no compromises. Nutmeg joins the mix and creates a thoroughly spicy mood. If you like it mild, you should perhaps try a Glenfiddich 12 first - this Royal Brackla means business. Sultanas and prunes bring a pleasant sweetness into play, but it never dominates. It's as if someone has added a generous helping of spices to a clay pot full of pickled fruit. This combination works surprisingly well and gives the whisky a nice complexity.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is quite dry and warming - exactly what you want after a long day. The Royal Brackla finishes with a medium length and shows its peppery side once again. At the end, light fruit orient spices come to the fore again, giving the whole thing a nice finish.

conclusion.marcel: Not a whisky for the faint-hearted, but that’s exactly what makes the Royal Brackla 12 Years so exciting – full of spice and character, and genuinely enjoyable. A Highland malt that leaves a definite impression with its intense aroma and a hint of grandma’s jam jar. For anyone who likes their whisky with a good kick of pepper and fruit, this one is a bullseye.
conclusion.sascha: The Royal Brackla 12 Years impresses with a remarkable balance between spicy pepper notes and fruity complexity, despite its moderate 40% ABV. Its clear structure and dry finish show that this is no watered-down Highland malt, but a spirit with strong character that especially appeals to connoisseurs. A solid representative of the distillery, scoring points with its harmonious interplay of spices and fruity accents.

faq.question: How does the Royal Brackla 12 Years differ from other Highland whiskies?
faq.answer: The Royal Brackla 12 Years stands out due to its spicy and complex aroma dominated by black pepper and nutmeg. Unlike many mild Highland whiskies, it delivers a pronounced spiciness and an earthy depth, making it more characterful and less gentle.

faq.question: Why does the Royal Brackla 12 Years have such an intense flavor despite its 40% alcohol content?
faq.answer: Although the alcohol level is moderate, the combination of ripe fruit notes like plums and red preserved fruits alongside spicy elements such as pepper and nutmeg creates a surprisingly full and complex tasting experience.

faq.question: What does it mean that the Royal Brackla 12 Years has an 'earthy depth' and 'slightly musty' notes?
faq.answer: These terms describe aromas reminiscent of damp earth or old warehouses, giving the whisky a natural, grounded character. Such notes are typical for some Highland whiskies and contribute to the complexity and uniqueness of the Royal Brackla.

item.name: M&H APEX White Wine
item.description: The whisky landscape of the 21st century is increasingly characterised by producers from outside the traditional whisky nations. Israel, a country that could hardly be more different from Scotland or Kentucky in terms of climate and culture, is establishing itself as a force to be reckoned with on the global whisky scene. At 61.2% vol., the M&H APEX White Wine from the Milk & Honey Distillery presents itself as a fascinating object of study for analysing how Mediterranean conditions and innovative cask maturation influence the sensory characteristics of whisky. The M&H Distillery in Tel Aviv operates under completely different climatic conditions than traditional whisky producers. The average temperatures of 20-30°C and the low humidity significantly accelerate the maturation process. This leads to a more intensive exchange between the distillate and the cask, which is reflected in the high viscosity and flavour complexity observed.

tasting_notes.nose: The nosing profile of APEX White Wine shows clear characteristics of accelerated maturation. Caramel initially dominates - an indicator of intensive cask-whisky interaction due to high ambient temperatures. The acidic component is particularly noteworthy and can be directly attributed to the white wine cask maturation. Apricot as a fruit component harmonises perfectly with the white wine influence and demonstrates the successful fusion of distillate and cask character. The slightly fruity base note completes an olfactory profile that clearly reflects Mediterranean influences.
tasting_notes.palate: The mouthfeel analysis reveals the outstanding characteristics of this Israeli whisky. "Viscosity turned up to 100" is a precise description of the exceptionally oily, almost syrupy texture. This characteristic is the result of intensive cask extraction under hot climatic conditions. Accompanied by the sweet, dry and white-peppery flavour of grapes, the result is an exciting taste experience.
tasting_notes.finish: Vanilla and honey with buttered toast. Delicious.

conclusion.marcel: The M&H APEX White Wine is a true character whisky, offering a lot of fun with its intense viscosity and fruity, tart notes. Sure, the 61.2% ABV demands respect – but those who dare will be rewarded with an exciting, Mediterranean-inspired whisky that definitely leaves you wanting more.
conclusion.sascha: With its complex cask maturation and pronounced balance between sweet and sour aromas, the M&H APEX White Wine impressively showcases the influence of the Mediterranean climate on whisky structure. The high alcohol strength and intense texture challenge the connoisseur but provide a fascinating study in cask interaction and maturation dynamics.

faq.question: How does the climate in Israel affect the maturation of the M&H APEX White Wine?
faq.answer: The warm and dry climate, with temperatures ranging from 20-30°C, significantly accelerates cask maturation. This leads to a more intense exchange between the spirit and the wood, resulting in higher viscosity and more complex aromas.

faq.question: What role does the White Wine Cask play in the whisky's aroma?
faq.answer: Maturation in White Wine Casks imparts a distinct tart component and fruity notes such as apricot to the whisky. This cask choice contributes significantly to the Mediterranean fruitiness and the harmonious balance in the flavor profile.

faq.question: How is the unusually high alcohol strength of 61.2% ABV in the M&H APEX White Wine explained?
faq.answer: The high alcohol strength is an intentional stylistic choice to preserve the intense cask maturation and complex aromas. It also allows for dilution to taste without losing flavor intensity.

item.name: Talisker 8 Years Tidal Churn - Diageo Special Releases (2024)
item.description: Sometimes you come across a whisky that literally takes you back to the Stone Age - in the very best sense. The Talisker 8 Years Tidal Churn from the Diageo Special Releases 2024 is just such a candidate. It invites you on a geological expedition where every sip tastes like an open history book. The name "Tidal Churn" says it all. Tidal churn describes the wild churning of the sea by the tides - exactly what this whisky does in the glass and later on the palate.

image.alt: Talisker 8 Years Tidal Churn - Diageo Special Releases 2024

tasting_notes.nose: From the very first nosing, it becomes clear that someone has captured the sea and put it in a bottle. The nose is extremely maritime - and that is an understatement. Seaweed that remains on the beach at low tide mixes with flavours that are actually reminiscent of seafood. Marcel even spoke of "fossils" - and this description hits the nail on the head. Cold smoke wafts over, as if you were standing on a Scottish beach while a peat fire smoulders in the distance. Chalky notes give the whole thing a mineral base, which is rounded off by subtle vanilla tones and fresh lemon zest.
tasting_notes.palate: In the mouth, the Tidal Churn unfolds its full geological complexity. Plenty of pepper provides the initial wake-up call, followed by a salty wave that is typical of every good Talisker. But then things get interesting: clear mineral notes dominate the flavour, supported by aromas of fresh bread. The flavours reminiscent of pebbles, limestone and fossils are particularly fascinating. This may sound abstract at first, but anyone who gets involved will understand: This whisky really does taste like the history of the earth. Apple and pear fruit provide a pleasant counterpoint to the basic mineral structure and prevent the whole thing from becoming too stony.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is dry and smoky - classic Talisker characteristics that are particularly striking here. The famous chilli catch, for which the distillery is known, appears in the form of white pepper, which lingers on the tongue for a long time.

conclusion.marcel: This Talisker 8 Year Tidal Churn is a rugged treasure brimming with maritime power and mineral depth – an intense experience that leaves you wanting more. Its honest, unvarnished character strength brings the spirit of the islands straight to the glass, delivering a drinking pleasure with bite. For those who crave smoky coastal adventures, this is a reliable companion.
conclusion.sascha: The Tidal Churn impresses with its clear structure and the successful balance between mineral, maritime, and smoky notes. Despite its expressive aroma profile, the whisky remains well harmonized, highlighting its technical precision. A fascinating example of skillfully integrating experimental elements without losing the core Talisker DNA.

faq.question: What does the name “Tidal Churn” mean for this Talisker 8 Year?
faq.answer: “Tidal Churn” describes the wild stirring of the sea caused by the tides. This effect is reflected in the whisky through its intense maritime and mineral aroma profile, evoking the rugged coastal landscape and the crashing of the waves.

faq.question: How does the Talisker 8 Year Tidal Churn differ from other Talisker releases?
faq.answer: The Tidal Churn is particularly defined by a strong maritime and geological complexity with notes like seaweed, fossils, and mineral aromas. Additionally, at 58.7% ABV, it is relatively high-proof and features a pronounced chilli catch on the finish, making it more intense and complex than many standard releases.

faq.question: How should one best enjoy the Talisker 8 Year Tidal Churn to fully experience its aromas?
faq.answer: It is best enjoyed neat at room temperature to fully perceive the layered maritime and mineral aromatics. A small splash of water can help soften the intense pepper and smoke notes and highlight additional flavor nuances such as fresh bread and fruit.

item.name: GlenAllachie 14 Years (2010/2024) - Koval Bourbon Barrel
item.description: Imagine it's the year 2010, Barack Obama is president, the iPad has just been invented and somewhere in the Scottish Speyside, a young GlenAllachie New Make is slumbering away in an American bourbon cask. 14 years later, in 2024, this whisky comes back to life - at 62.4% vol. and with a story to tell that takes us on a journey between continents and worlds of flavour.

tasting_notes.nose: Our journey through time begins with the first nosing. It's like walking along a green apple beach - an imaginary place where sweet Gala apples lie on the shore and the sand is made of the finest vanilla sugar. The nose of the GlenAllachie 14 Years Koval Bourbon Barrel first takes us to this paradisiacal scene. Grain mingles with white peach, while grape notes drift by like a gentle summer breeze. A hint of wax lends the whole an almost meditative calm.
tasting_notes.palate: The first sip catapults us straight into an American fairground of the 2010s. Candyfloss sticks sweetly to the taste buds, while caramel rolls over the tongue like warm sweets. This is where the true magic of Koval bourbon barrel maturation comes into play: this GlenAllachie becomes almost (actually) bourbon-esque without losing its Scottish identity. Honey drips golden through the flavour experience, accompanied by cereals - again the grain comes to the fore, as if the whisky wants to remind us where it originally comes from. A pinch of cinnamon spices up the finale of this flavour chapter and gives the whole thing an autumnal warmth.
tasting_notes.finish: The end of our journey through time takes us to a warm, long sunset over the Scottish Speyside. Dry but not brittle, fruity sweet but not overloaded - this GlenAllachie says goodbye like a good friend who will be remembered for a long time to come.

conclusion.marcel: This GlenAllachie 14 Year Koval Bourbon Barrel is a charming blend of Scottish soul and American flair – a genuine taste experience that grabs you immediately and never lets go. With its sweet vanilla notes and warm cinnamon spice, it almost feels like being at a fairground, only with style. For enthusiasts who aren’t afraid of a robust 62.4% ABV, this is pure drinking pleasure with plenty of character.
conclusion.sascha: The GlenAllachie 2010/2024 impressively demonstrates how precise cask management can strike a balance between intense bourbon barrel influence and classic Speyside structure. Despite a proud 62.4% ABV, the whisky remains harmonious, with clearly defined fruit and grain aromas accompanied by a subtle spiciness. A technically convincing, complex single malt that skillfully explores the boundaries between tradition and innovation.

faq.question: How does the Koval Bourbon Barrel influence the flavor profile of this GlenAllachie 14 Year?
faq.answer: The Koval Bourbon Barrel imparts distinctive bourbon-typical notes such as cotton candy, caramel, and honey to the GlenAllachie 14 Year, without overshadowing the whisky’s Scottish identity. Maturation in this American cask creates a harmonious fusion of sweet, fruity, and spicy aromas.

faq.question: Why is the alcohol content of 62.4% ABV so high in this whisky?
faq.answer: The high alcohol content of 62.4% ABV results from a cask strength bottling, where the whisky has not been diluted. This allows for a more intense tasting experience and preserves the complex aromas developed during the 14 years of maturation in the Koval Bourbon Barrel.

faq.question: What distinguishes the GlenAllachie 2010/2024 14 Year from other Speyside whiskies?
faq.answer: This GlenAllachie combines the typical fruitiness and grain notes of Speyside with a unique American bourbon barrel maturation from Koval. The long 14-year maturation at cask strength and the blend of Scottish tradition with American influence make it particularly complex and multi-layered.

item.name: Glenlivet 13 Years 1st Fill American Oak
item.description: Regional exclusives often hold a special charm. The Glenlivet 13 Years 1st Fill American Oak, crafted specifically for the French market, showcases how even within the classic Speyside style, intriguing variations can arise. Matured in first-fill American oak casks and bottled at 40% ABV, this whisky promises an easy-drinking experience. But what makes it stand out?

tasting_notes.nose: On the first nosing, the classic Speyside character immediately shines through: light sawdust and sweet strawberries with cream and sugar evoke bright, fruity aromas. A subtle floral soap note adds complexity, complemented by hints of toast, pomelo, and lemon sherbet. The bouquet feels inviting and harmonious, with a sweet undertone that’s pleasant but not overly complex.
tasting_notes.palate: The first sip confirms its approachable nature. Creamy and smooth, it glides over the palate, delivering notes of vanilla, almond, and white chocolate reminiscent of cocoa butter. The juicy sweetness of honeydew melon adds a fresh, light dimension. This whisky clearly benefits from the influence of American oak, known for imparting mild and sweet flavors.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is buttery smooth but relatively short. Subtle hints of orange and a touch of oak create a pleasant conclusion. It’s a whisky that quietly exits the stage, leaving a gentle impression.

conclusion.marcel: A whisky that convinces without much fuss and delivers exactly what you expect from a cozy evening: sweet, soft, and pleasantly unobtrusive. The Glenlivet 13 Years 1st Fill American Oak is like a relaxed chat with an old friend – straightforward, likeable, and simply comforting. If you’re not after big dramatic effects, here you have a charming companion for light moments of enjoyment.
conclusion.sascha: The Glenlivet 13 Years 1st Fill American Oak impresses with a balanced harmony of fruity sweetness and a mild wood note typical of first-fill American oak. Its moderate complexity and gentle, though short, finish make it a solid Speyside whisky, especially suitable for beginners or as a relaxed everyday dram. Technically clean, but without profound depth or exceptional structure.

faq.question: What distinguishes the Glenlivet 13 Years 1st Fill American Oak from other Glenlivet releases?
faq.answer: The Glenlivet 13 Years 1st Fill American Oak matured exclusively in first-fill American oak casks, giving it a particularly mild and sweet aromatic profile. Additionally, it was bottled specifically for the French market, which sets it apart from classic Glenlivet whiskies through its accessibility and fruity notes.

faq.question: How does the first-fill American oak cask influence the flavor of this whisky?
faq.answer: First-fill American oak casks impart intense vanilla and almond notes as well as a creamy texture to the whisky. In this Glenlivet, they provide the characteristic sweet lightness with aromas of white chocolate, honeydew melon, and a buttery-soft finish typical of American oak.

faq.question: Is the Glenlivet 13 Years 1st Fill American Oak suitable for beginners?
faq.answer: Yes, with 40% alcohol by volume and its soft, creamy flavor, this whisky is very approachable. The fruity and sweet aromas make it an uncomplicated introduction to the world of Speyside whiskies, without being too complex or heavy.

item.name: St. Kilian Double Trouble – Peated
item.description: With the special release Double Trouble – Peated, St. Kilian celebrates the 40th anniversary of a cult film, yet keeps the true protagonist in focus: the whisky. Known for its experimental spirit and bold cask choices, this German distillery delivers another striking example. Aged in rare Ramandolo sweet wine casks from northern Italy, this peated single malt masterfully balances power and refinement.

tasting_notes.nose: Intense, sweet, and ashy smoke meets subtle fruity undertones. Notes of ripe figs and apricots blend with a hint of citrus zest. A pleasant maltiness adds depth.
tasting_notes.palate: Bold and voluminous: Earthy peat smoke expands across the palate, accompanied by a generous dose of spice and prominent oak character. The malty foundation brings a lovely honeyed sweetness, while a delicate fruit sweetness almost feels sugary.
tasting_notes.finish: The smoke lingers long, underlined by spicy notes with hints of cinnamon and anise. A light tingling sensation and comforting warmth lead to an intense yet balanced conclusion, rounded off by a subtle fruity touch.

conclusion.marcel: A whisky that really packs a punch and immediately invites you to dive in with its smoky sweetness. Double Trouble – Peated delivers fruity surprises and a great dose of character, ensuring uncompromising drinking pleasure. Not for the faint-hearted, but that’s exactly what makes it so exciting!
conclusion.sascha: St. Kilian presents a technically impressive blend of robust peat smoke and nuanced fruity sweetness, supported by an unusual cask maturation. The balance of spice, malt, and oak is well achieved, even though the intensity occasionally slightly overshadows the harmony. A complex, structured peated single malt with real potential.

faq.question: How do the Ramandolo sweet wine casks influence the flavor of the St. Kilian Double Trouble – Peated?
faq.answer: Maturation in Ramandolo sweet wine casks imparts a subtle fruity sweetness with notes of ripe figs and apricots, which harmoniously complement the powerful peat smoke and create a complex flavor balance.

faq.question: Why does the St. Kilian Double Trouble – Peated have no age statement on the label?
faq.answer: The whisky is a no-age-statement (NAS) release, meaning the age is not disclosed. St. Kilian focuses on cask maturation and flavor to guarantee quality rather than relying on a specific age.

faq.question: How intense is the peat smoke in the St. Kilian Double Trouble – Peated compared to other peated whiskies?
faq.answer: The peat smoke is strong and earthy but well integrated thanks to the sweet wine cask maturation. It is more intense than many standard whiskies, yet remains balanced and is complemented by fruity and spicy notes.

item.name: Braeval (1994/2019) - Sansibar
item.description: Braeval - a distillery that is often overshadowed by its more famous Speyside neighbours, even among whisky connoisseurs. This is mainly due to the fact that official bottlings are virtually non-existent. Instead, most of the production ends up in blends. Fortunately, there are independent bottlers such as Sansibar that make it possible to enjoy such hidden treasures as single malts.

tasting_notes.nose: A gentle, inviting sweetness from the glass. Honey and vanilla dominate the first impression, accompanied by creamy caramel that suggests an almost buttery creaminess. But this whisky has more than just sweetness: a malty depth provides structure, while oak notes are subtle. After a few minutes in the glass, the flavour opens up further - and suddenly a light coconut note is added. Very exciting! An underlying floral freshness gives the whisky a fine elegance. No alcoholic burn, no intrusive notes - simply a wonderfully balanced nose.
tasting_notes.palate: The first impression on the palate confirms what the nose promised: creamy, round, sweet and at the same time full of character. The vanilla and honey are immediately present and envelop the palate with a silky texture. The malt then becomes more prominent, and with it a pleasant spiciness that testifies to the long maturation in oak barrels. However, the highlight is the subtle coconut note that slowly blends into the flavour - almost as if the whisky has been given a hint of tropical exoticism. The oak remains present, but never dominant. There is no excessive bitterness or dryness, but a perfect balance between sweetness, spice and wood.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is exactly what you would expect from a 25-years Speyside malt: pleasantly long, warm and deep. A slight peppery note provides a spicy kick without being overpowering. The oak notes come to the fore, but they are mild and pleasant. At the very end, a fine blend of sweet and spicy flavours lingers on the tongue.

conclusion.marcel: This Braeval 1994/2019 is a true charmer from Speyside, delighting with its creamy texture and sweet coconut notes. It’s not about an exaggerated cask influence, but pure drinking pleasure that even brings a smile – perfect for connoisseurs seeking a classic malt with character.
conclusion.sascha: The Braeval 1994/2019 impresses with its balanced interplay of sweet vanilla and malty wood tones, subtly accented by a fine hint of coconut. Its complex structure and long, spicy finish make it a solid example of elegant Speyside whiskies without overwhelming cask dominance.

faq.question: Why are there so few official releases of Braeval?
faq.answer: Braeval primarily produces whisky for blended expressions, which is why official single malt releases are rare. Most single cask bottlings come from independent bottlers like Sansibar.

faq.question: What role does cask maturation play in this Braeval 1994/2019 from Sansibar?
faq.answer: The extended maturation in oak casks imparts the whisky’s characteristic spiciness and delicate oak notes, which harmonize beautifully with sweet aromas like vanilla and honey.

faq.question: How does the Braeval 1994/2019 differ sensorially from other Speyside whiskies?
faq.answer: Alongside the typical Speyside sweetness and maltiness, this whisky reveals an unusual coconut note and a floral freshness, giving it a distinctive elegance and tropical nuances.

item.name: Stauning Heather Distillery Edition (2017/2021)
item.description: While other countries are still debating whether they can distil whisky at all, the Danes are simply marching through and demanding premium prices for their Danish whiskies. And you know what? They have the courage to do it. At 47.80% vol. and without an age statement, this Danish whisky presents itself like a rebellious teenager: young, untamed and damn self-confident. Incidentally, the Heather Edition does not mean that heather has been thrown into the barrel here (although that would come as no surprise with the Danes), but that this whisky is intended to capture the rough, windswept landscape of Denmark in the glass.

tasting_notes.nose: Smoke breaks through like a Viking kicking in the front door. But wait! This is no ordinary peat smoke, but an interesting herbal smoke reminiscent of mate tea. Yes, you read that right - this whisky smells like the quintessential hipster drink. Caramel and malt join in before a note of furniture polish grounds everything down again. Danish design - even the whisky smells of designer furniture.
tasting_notes.palate: Ice-cold, almost refreshing smoke lingers on the tongue - as if you were standing by the sea on a winter's morning and sipping from a campfire at the same time. It envelops the palate as voluminously as a Viking beard. Mate tea is back (the Danes must have an obsession), followed by cherries and burnt bread. Then comes the memory of a burnt campfire days ago - melancholic and somehow poetic. Baked apple provides cosiness, while the creamy texture holds everything together.
tasting_notes.finish: The long, smoky finish shows that serious work has been done here despite the lack of an age statement. Slightly dry with hints of spicy oak - exactly how you would imagine the end of a Danish whisky saga.

conclusion.marcel: This Stauning Heather is like a wild ride through the Danish coastal landscape – rough, untamed, and packed with plenty of character. For the price, you can afford to be a bit rebellious, even if it won’t win over everyone immediately. Still, if you’re craving an unusual peat smoke with a hint of hipster flair, this one will definitely make you happy.
conclusion.sascha: The Heather Edition from Stauning impresses with a well-balanced blend of smoky, herbal notes and a creamy texture that supports its complex structure. Despite lacking an age statement, it reveals a layered depth that at least justifies the premium price from a flavor perspective – although the value for money remains debatable.

faq.question: Why does the Stauning Heather Distillery Edition have no age statement, and how does that affect the taste?
faq.answer: The Stauning Heather Distillery Edition deliberately omits an age statement to allow greater flexibility in cask selection. This enables the distillery to blend different vintages to achieve the signature smoky and herbal flavor profile without being tied to a specific age.

faq.question: What does the name 'Heather' mean for this whisky if no heather is used?
faq.answer: 'Heather' in the name doesn’t refer to an ingredient but rather to the inspiration drawn from Denmark’s rugged, wind-swept landscape, which the whisky aims to capture sensorially. It’s about evoking the feeling and atmosphere of Danish nature, not about adding heather.

faq.question: What role does the mate tea aroma play in the Stauning Heather Distillery Edition and how does it develop?
faq.answer: The mate tea aroma is an unusual but defining note in this whisky. It arises from the combination of special peat and herbal aromas during malting and maturation. This nuance gives the whisky a fresh, slightly bitter complexity that sets it apart from classic peat-smoked whiskies.

item.name: Eiskeller-Whisky 12 Years Single Cask Fass 4
item.description: Award-winning and rightly celebrated! The Eiskeller whisky 12 years single cask cask 4 from the Lambertus Bräu und Brand distillery has made history: As the winner of the German Whisky Awards 2025 in the prestigious "Single Malt Cask Strength (unpeated)" category, it came out on top against over 100 German whiskies. Organised by the Verband Deutscher Whiskybrenner e.V. and blind-tasted by a jury of experts, this award is the accolade for German whisky. Anyone looking for an exceptional German single malt that not only meets international standards, but exceeds them, should definitely read on. This whisky is a shining example of what German distilleries are capable of - and why they increasingly deserve the attention of the whisky world.

tasting_notes.nose: From the very first nosing, it becomes clear why this whisky won over the jury. White pepper dominates the nose - not aggressive or disruptive, but aromatic and inviting. Behind this, layers of orange and honey unfold, giving the whole a warm, sweet base. Malt and cinnamon join in, accompanied by exotic notes such as turmeric and liquorice with an earthy undertone. This combination of flavours is fascinating and already shows that this is a whisky with personality in a glass. The red wine flavour of the cask is already noticeable on the nose and promises great things for the palate.
tasting_notes.palate: The ice cellar whisky unfolds its full splendour in the mouth. Oily and extremely full-bodied, it envelops the palate like a silky cloak. The white pepper from the nose turns into black pepper and brings a pleasant spiciness that is perfectly rounded off by cinnamon. The flavour experience is nothing less than spectacular: a spice aroma like an Indian spice ship - this poetic description hits the nail on the head. Cardamom, coriander, fennel and a multitude of other spices dance on the tongue. The French oak and the red wine pre-fermentation of the barrel have created a great flavour profile here.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish delivers what the nose and palate promised. Clove and wood dominate the finish, while a furry texture fills the mouth and ensures persistence. Liquorice and slightly nutty notes round off the taste experience and leave a lasting impression.

conclusion.marcel: This Eiskeller whisky is a genuine experience – full-bodied, spicy, and packed with character. It’s not mainstream, but rather a bold dram that’s fun and challenges your taste buds. Anyone familiar with GlenAllachie fans will find a German powerhouse counterpart here, showing serious muscle with its 56.6% ABV.
conclusion.sascha: With its complex spice profile and expertly executed cask maturation, this 12-years single cask is a prime example of high-level German whisky. The balance between intense peppery heat and sweet-spicy notes is remarkable, even if the robust strength doesn’t gently caress every palate. Overall, the cask profile impresses with depth and a lasting finish, making it ideal for fans of structured cask strength whiskies.

faq.question: What does 'Single Cask' mean for this 12-years Eiskeller whisky?
faq.answer: 'Single Cask' means the whisky was bottled from just one individual barrel, without blending with others. This ensures every drop reflects the unique aromas and characteristics of that specific cask, making it distinctive and limited.

faq.question: How does the red wine pre-treatment of the cask influence the whisky’s flavor?
faq.answer: The red wine pre-treatment imparts complex fruity notes and a certain depth to the whisky. In the 12-years Eiskeller whisky, this is especially evident through aromatic hints of orange and honey on the nose, as well as the full-bodied, spicy flavors on the palate that enrich the tasting experience.

faq.question: Why did the 12-years Eiskeller whisky win the category 'Single Malt Cask Strength (unpeated)'?
faq.answer: The whisky impressed with its intense aroma profile at a high drinking strength (56.6% ABV) without smoky peat notes (unpeated). The combination of complex spice notes, harmonious cask maturation, and exceptional balance enabled it to stand out among over 100 German whiskies.

item.name: Steinkeller-Whisky 12 Years Single Cask Fass 8
item.description: Who would have thought that the Rhineland - actually known as a white wine region - is home to a German whisky that can hold its own against its Scottish relatives? The Steinkeller 12-years single cask whisky from the Lambertus Bräu und Brand distillery impressively proves that German distillers have long been capable of more than just schnapps and grain.

tasting_notes.nose: The idiosyncratic character of this German whisky is revealed from the very first sniff. Vanilla and popcorn dominate the nose - as if a bag of cinema popcorn had been mixed with vanilla ice cream. But then things get interesting: slightly grassy notes mingle with spicy curry herb and provide a pleasingly funky twist that makes you curious. This combination of flavours is polarising. While some will love the sweet vanilla popcorn character, purist whisky drinkers may find the funky notes too experimental.
tasting_notes.palate: In the mouth, the Steinkeller whisky shows its gentlest side. It glides over the tongue in a very soft and mild manner, without leaving even a hint of sharpness. The sweetness literally jumps out - sugary and creamy, as if you had liquid corn cream in front of you even though there was no maize in the original mash.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish surprises with a pleasant twist: White pepper gently tickles the tongue, while the oak finally gets its chance to show itself. The Virgin Oak makes its presence felt - not intrusive, but clearly noticeable. The cool funk from the beginning returns and provides a nice dry finish that makes you want to take the next sip.

conclusion.marcel: With its playful vanilla popcorn character and a pleasantly funky twist, the Steinkeller 12 Years whisky is a charming companion for relaxed summer evenings. It enchants with a gentle sweetness and invites you to simply enjoy it without any fuss – a true mood booster from the German whisky scene.
conclusion.sascha: The Steinkeller 12 Years Single Cask Cask 8 impresses with a balanced harmony of sweet vanilla notes and subtle spice aromas, while the virgin oak imparts a pleasantly dry structure to the finish. Despite its mild softness, it offers an intriguing range of flavors that particularly appeals to lovers of complex yet approachable German whiskies.

faq.question: What makes the Steinkeller 12 Years Single Cask Cask 8 taste special compared to Scottish whiskies?
faq.answer: The Steinkeller whisky combines sweet vanilla and popcorn notes with unusually spicy and lightly grassy aromas like curry herb. This combination is rather uncommon for classic Scottish whiskies and gives it a distinctive, sometimes polarizing character.

faq.question: Why does the Steinkeller 12 Years taste sweet and creamy despite containing no corn in the mash?
faq.answer: The sweet-creamy perception resembles liquid corn cream but is not derived from corn; rather, it results from the specific malt and cask maturation. Aging in virgin oak casks contributes to the intense sweetness and creaminess without the use of corn.

faq.question: How does the virgin oak cask influence the finish of the Steinkeller 12 Years Single Cask Cask 8?
faq.answer: The virgin oak imparts a subtle woody note to the finish that harmonizes with a lightly peppery conclusion. It provides a dry and spicy finish that underscores the whisky’s characteristic funk without being intrusive.

item.name: Wolfburn Langskip
item.description: When it comes to honest, unadulterated whiskies, the Wolfburn Langskip is a real insider tip at a very good price. The still young Wolfburn distillery, based in the far north of Scotland near Thurso, brings a statement to the glass with this strong representative that experienced connoisseurs in particular will appreciate: blunt, direct, sweet - and full of character.

tasting_notes.nose: The first impression: surprisingly smooth for the cask strength. An aroma unfolds that combines maritime freshness with dessert notes. The salty breeze of a coastal whisky meets crème brûlée, salted caramel and apple crumble. Vanilla custard dances in the background with a light malt note and a hint of honey - a real dessert for the nose. No alcoholic sting despite the strength - just multi-layered, balanced flavours.
tasting_notes.palate: The flavour of the Wolfburn Langskip is like an unadorned summer whisky at cask strength: creamy, dry, honest and very sweet. The first impression is of hay, almonds and a clear cereal note. Then banana, papaya and pineapple come to the fore - tropical fruits in the cask strength version. The texture is thick, almost oily, with a real tang. White pepper adds the necessary spice to the sweet profile.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is pleasantly warming, sweet and medium-long - without becoming bitter. Light oak notes provide structure without dominating. The finish is reminiscent of melted sugar with a hint of malt - pleasantly round and accessible.

conclusion.marcel: A real crowd-pleaser, the Wolfburn Langskip impresses with its creamy sweetness and the fresh breeze from the sea – not a complex cask monster, but pure drinking pleasure with character. For anyone looking for an uncomplicated, cask-strength Highland malt, it’s a bullseye that even manages to impress without smoke or sherry.
conclusion.sascha: The Wolfburn Langskip presents itself as a balanced Highland single malt with a clear structure and a harmonious interplay of maritime notes, tropical fruits, and sweet dessert aromas. Despite its powerful 58% ABV, the whisky remains well-balanced and offers a dense, oily mouthfeel that particularly appeals to connoisseurs who appreciate precise cask strength.

faq.question: What makes the Wolfburn Langskip so approachable in taste despite its cask strength?
faq.answer: Although the Wolfburn Langskip is bottled at a robust 58% ABV cask strength, it reveals itself to be surprisingly gentle and balanced. The combination of a creamy texture, sweet dessert notes, and a well-measured spiciness creates a rounded flavor experience without any harsh alcoholic bite.

faq.question: How does the location of the Wolfburn distillery influence the character of the Langskip whisky?
faq.answer: Situated close to the coast in the far north of Scotland, the Wolfburn distillery imparts a maritime freshness to the Langskip. This salty breeze blends with the sweet and fruity aromas, giving the whisky a distinctive coastal character.

faq.question: Why is there no age statement on the Wolfburn Langskip bottle?
faq.answer: The Wolfburn Langskip is a so-called No-Age-Statement (NAS) whisky, meaning the age is not disclosed. This allows the distillery to combine various casks and maturation stages to achieve a consistent and characterful flavor profile.

item.name: Wolfburn Cognac Cask 8 Years (2017/2025)
item.description: The young Wolfburn distillery in the northern Highlands has ventured into an elegant cask experiment: eight years of maturation in cognac casks, an unusual choice that combines character and charm. The result is a fruity, malty single malt that arouses curiosity - especially on the nose.

tasting_notes.nose: A warm, soft start. Grapes and sultanas envelop the nose like the first breath in an old wine cellar. Lots of honey, buttered toast, a hint of milk chocolate and a surprising funky twist - almost like mango chutney on warm bread. Malty and inviting, with a clear cognac echo. The nose is the big highlight: round, expressive, slightly playful.
tasting_notes.palate: The Wolfburn is softer on the palate. Almond and honey lead the way, carried by cognac sweetness and light oak. That butter toast note again, a trademark that runs throughout. It is round, pleasantly fruity and surprisingly smooth despite being 46%. However, the body remains rather delicate - a little more depth would have been good here. Nevertheless, the balance between fruit, sweetness and malt is finely struck
tasting_notes.finish: The medium-long finish brings vanilla and green tea, with a hint of bitter oak. A slightly dry finish, malty and delicately bitter. Not a dramatic finish, rather a quiet ending.

conclusion.marcel: This Wolfburn Cognac Cask is like a quiet friend who sweetens your evening with gentle fruit notes and honeyed sweetness – perfect for those who appreciate elegance and simplicity. It may lack a bit of depth, but that’s precisely what makes it charming and approachable, almost like a cozy chat by the fireplace.
conclusion.sascha: The Wolfburn Cognac Cask strikes a successful balance between fruity Cognac notes and malty sweetness, yet remains somewhat restrained in its complexity. Its delicate structure and medium-length, slightly dry finish make it a solid, if not outstanding, example of cask-finish experimentation.

faq.question: How does maturation in Cognac casks influence the flavor of the Wolfburn Cognac Cask 2017/2025?
faq.answer: Maturation in Cognac casks imparts a fruity sweetness and complex aromas such as grapes, raisins, and a subtle Cognac note. This results in an elegant, malty character with a warm, soft entry and a subtle vanilla hint on the finish.

faq.question: Why does the Wolfburn Cognac Cask 2017/2025 feel so smooth on the palate despite its 46% alcohol content?
faq.answer: The combination of Cognac cask maturation and the whisky’s delicate structure creates a surprisingly gentle sensation despite the relatively high alcohol level. Flavors of honey, almond, and buttered toast contribute to its smooth texture.

faq.question: What makes the Wolfburn distillery in the northern Highlands special compared to other distilleries?
faq.answer: Wolfburn is a young distillery known for its experimental cask finishes, such as the use of Cognac casks. Additionally, its location in the northern Highlands influences the raw materials and climate, which in turn shape the character of its whiskies.

item.name: Wolfburn Vibrant Stills Sauternes Cask 8 Years (2016/2024)
item.description: Wolfburn, the northernmost distillery on the Scottish mainland, is known for its handcrafted and elegant whiskies. With the Vibrant Stills Sauternes Cask 2016/2024, they've once again delivered something exciting. Aged for 8 years – first in ex-bourbon, then in first-fill Sauternes wine casks from France – this release offers bright fruitiness with structure. Bottled exclusively for Germany, it’s definitely worth a closer look.

image.alt: Wolfburn Vibrant Stills Sauternes Cask  2016/2024 8 Years

tasting_notes.nose: The nose is fresh, grassy, and green – like a spring meadow somewhere between a lemon grove and a haystack. There’s lime, green grapes, and a hint of nectarine. Dive deeper and you might even discover something like pineapple sponge cake. The wine influence is there, but it leans more bitter than sweet – intriguing!
tasting_notes.palate: On the palate, this whisky turns creamy. Nectarine, hints of passionfruit yogurt, and honey meet a surprisingly dry oaky spice. A touch of rye bread, maybe even beer-like notes come through. The fruit fades a little, replaced by structure – although the balance could be better.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is medium, with a gentle tingle and warming feel. There’s a light spice and a hint of oak, but sadly, not much fruit left. It feels like the cask could have given just a bit more.

conclusion.marcel: A lively and approachable whisky that simply delights with fresh fruit and a creamy texture. A real mood-lifter for beginners, even if the finish could use a bit more punch. Wolfburn shows character here that makes you eager for the next sip – straightforward and charming.
conclusion.sascha: The Wolfburn Vibrant Stills Sauternes Cask offers an interesting blend of fruitiness and spice, but overall falls a bit short of expectations in balance. The structure is solid, yet the finish feels somewhat flat and lacks the complexity one would hope for from a first-fill Sauternes cask. Technically clean, but with room for improvement in finesse.

faq.question: How does the finishing in Sauternes wine casks influence the flavor of the Wolfburn Vibrant Stills 2016/2024?
faq.answer: The finishing in first-fill Sauternes wine casks imparts a fruity opulence, expressed through notes of nectarine, lime, and green grapes. The wine influence adds a slight bitterness that complements the whisky’s creamy and spicy aromas.

faq.question: Why is the Wolfburn Vibrant Stills Sauternes Cask 2016/2024 only available in Germany?
faq.answer: This bottling was released exclusively for the German market, making it a special offering for collectors and enthusiasts in Germany. Such exclusive releases are often limited to specific countries to highlight regional uniqueness.

faq.question: What is special about the 50% alcohol content of the Wolfburn Vibrant Stills?
faq.answer: At 50% ABV, this whisky is stronger than the standard 40-46%. This higher strength delivers a more intense aromatic profile and a bolder flavor, particularly emphasizing the spicy and fruity notes, while the cask maturation keeps it pleasantly rounded.

item.name: Wolfburn 10 Years (2023 Release)
item.description: Ten years - that's quite a milestone for a young distillery like Wolfburn. Since its foundation in 2013, Scotland's northernmost mainland distillery has made a name for itself with consistent quality and characterful small-batch releases. The 2023 10th anniversary whisky is more than just an anniversary statement - it's a kind of assessment: what can Wolfburn do if given time - and rest?

tasting_notes.nose: The aroma is multi-layered, but never overloaded: burnt toast, honey, daisies - a mixture of light-coloured fruits such as apple and pear, interspersed with cinnamon, leather and a hint of dark chocolate. It is not a wild ride, but rather a slow, calm unfolding. The flavours stand side by side without drowning each other out.
tasting_notes.palate: What the nose hints at becomes reality on the tongue: milk chocolate, sultanas, a little date and burnt caramel. The dram has a dark character without being heavy. It is creamy, round and scores with hints of nutmeg and - very subtle - strawberry in the background.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is rather short, but harmonious: sweet coffee, coffee grounds, a little bitter, but pleasantly woody. A fine sherry note shimmers through, never dominant, more like a greeting from afar. No drama, no fireworks.

conclusion.marcel: A whisky that quietly convinces and invites you to linger with its gentle character. Wolfburn 10 Years isn’t a firework, but that’s exactly what makes it the perfect everyday dram – honest, rounded, and pleasantly approachable. For anyone looking for an uncomplicated Highland whisky with heart, it’s a real winner.
conclusion.sascha: The Wolfburn 10 Years displays a remarkable balance between fruitiness and spicy sherry notes without ever feeling overloaded. Its clear structure and subtle complexity make it a solid representative of the young Highland distilleries, standing out especially through its harmonious bottling. For lovers of well-composed, mature whiskies, it offers a convincing benchmark.

faq.question: How does the Wolfburn 10 Years differ from younger releases from the distillery?
faq.answer: The 10-years Wolfburn shows a distinctly matured complexity and balance compared to younger versions. While the younger releases often feature fresh, lively notes, the 10-years unfolds calm, layered aromas with hints of sherry, milk chocolate, and spices that develop through the extended maturation.

faq.question: What role does maturation play in the flavor profile of Wolfburn 10 Years?
faq.answer: The ten-year maturation allows for a harmonious development of flavors, where robust wood notes, sweet coffee, and subtle sherry tones emerge. This aging period provides a creamy texture and a balanced interplay of fruitiness, spices, and gentle woodiness without any single component dominating.

faq.question: Why is the Wolfburn 10 Years an important milestone for the distillery?
faq.answer: As a young distillery founded in 2013, the 10-years whisky marks the first significant age milestone, demonstrating how Wolfburn has developed in quality over time with patience. It serves as a benchmark for the distillery’s potential to combine character and depth in a small-batch release.

item.name: Wolfburn Vibrant Stills Bourbon Barrel 8 Years (2015/2023)
item.description: The Vibrant Stills Bourbon Barrel 2015/2023 edition by Wolfburn is a small batch release made exclusively for the German market. Aged for 8 years in fresh bourbon barrels and bottled at a robust 50% ABV, this Single Malt Scotch Whisky presents both the gentleness of the Highlands and a surprisingly pronounced smoky tone. Non-chill filtered and bottled in its natural colour, it feels pure – and delightfully complex.

image.alt: Wolfburn Vibrant Stills Bourbon Barrel 2015/2023 8 Years

tasting_notes.nose: Right from the start, the aroma is bold: warm BBQ smoke, hints of smoked ham, and scorched wood dominate the nose. Beneath, a creamy vanilla softness adds depth. A subtle maritime breeze occasionally surfaces – a whisper of seaweed in coastal air. This whisky doesn’t fully reveal itself at once; it rewards a patient nose.
tasting_notes.palate: On the palate, Wolfburn Vibrant Stills is rich and full-bodied. Notes of dark plum compote meet clove spice in a creamy, well-rounded mouthfeel. The bourbon cask influence returns with warming vanilla. Hints of citrus and a light salinity give it structure and clarity. The smoke is present yet never overwhelming – well-integrated and character-defining. Everything here feels genuine.
tasting_notes.finish: In the finish, the smoky notes intensify: charred wood and burnt toast mingle with light honey. A slightly gritty texture appears, reminiscent of oak tannins. Bright fruit is almost absent; instead, a rustic, subtly bitter warmth lingers – satisfyingly long.

conclusion.marcel: A Highland whisky that really delights with its smoky depth and creamy vanilla notes – here, character meets drinkability without being loud. Anyone who enjoys a robust, maritime dram will love this Wolfburn. A genuine everyday companion with edges and quirks that charmingly surprise.
conclusion.sascha: Technically precise and structurally balanced, this Wolfburn shows a successful integration of smoke and bourbon cask notes. The balance between spicy complexity and fresh saline hints makes it a convincing example of modern Highland whisky. Although the finish carries a certain rustic roughness, the overall impression remains coherent and thoughtfully crafted.

faq.question: How does the bourbon cask maturation influence the taste of the Wolfburn Vibrant Stills 2015/2023?
faq.answer: The 8-year maturation in fresh bourbon barrels imparts a warm vanilla note that is present both on the nose and the palate. It provides a creamy sweetness and brings out spicy as well as fresh citrus accents, which harmoniously complement the smoky character.

faq.question: Why is the Wolfburn Vibrant Stills 2015/2023 non-chill filtered and what does that mean for the drinking experience?
faq.answer: Forgoing chill filtration preserves the whisky’s natural aromas and full texture. This means oils and delicate flavor compounds remain intact, resulting in a more layered and authentic tasting experience, especially at a drinking strength of 50% ABV.

faq.question: What role does the smoky character play in this Highland whisky?
faq.answer: The smoky note is present but never overpowering. It evokes BBQ wood fires and smoked ham, adding a distinctive depth typical of this edition. The smoke is well integrated, giving the whisky a rustic, characterful finish without feeling artificial.

item.name: Glen Garioch 21 Years (2003/2024) - Wu DRAM Clan
item.description: For the first time ever in the history of the Glen Garioch distillery, located in the Scottish Highlands, a single malt has been launched that was created in partnership by the independent bottler Wu DRAM Clan from Germany and the distillery. The Wu DRAM Clan is known for exquisite bottlings and so it is not surprising that the bottling was sold out within a few minutes.

image.alt: Glen Garioch 21 Years 2003/2024 - Wu DRAM Clan

tasting_notes.nose: The very first nosing reveals a dramatically dense flavour that doesn't do things by halves. Dried plum and sultanas greet the nose - dark, rich, almost mystical. Behind this lies bittersweet dark chocolate, paired with a hint of old leather and woody honey. A subtle woody note with a nutty undertone provides depth without being overpowering. The cinnamon plays a subtle role - more suggestive than demanding. This whisky doesn't just whiff - it speaks. It speaks of dark cask ageing, of patience, of craftsmanship and character.
tasting_notes.palate: On the palate, Glen Garioch explodes in an inimitable way. Spices dominate: Clove, cinnamon and a hint of black pepper, accompanied by tobacco notes and again the leather that the nose already hinted at. These are joined by blackberries, sultanas and dates, but with a restrained sweetness - never sticky, always balanced. The texture is extremely full-bodied, almost creamy. The alcohol is present, but excellently integrated. Everything seems harmonious, intentional, composed - not by chance. A dram that invites you to chew, it is so multi-layered.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is eternally long, dry, warming and pleasantly peppery. It leaves an impression of old oak, coffee grounds and spice - but not an aftertaste that tires. Rather a dignified finish, like the last sentence in a novel that you want to read again straight away.

conclusion.marcel: With the Wu DRAM Clan, Glen Garioch has crafted a true character whisky – complex, profound, and charmingly spicy in a way that leaves you wanting more. A whisky to savor, it invites you back for another sip and turns every taste into an experience.
conclusion.sascha: A well-balanced single malt that impresses with its harmonious blend of intense spice notes and fruity depth. The remarkable cask maturation and precisely integrated alcohol level make this Glen Garioch a technically accomplished Highland whisky with a long-lasting, dry-spicy finish.

faq.question: What makes the collaboration between Glen Garioch and Wu DRAM Clan special for this whisky?
faq.answer: For the first time, Glen Garioch created a single malt in partnership with the independent bottler Wu DRAM Clan from Germany. This collaboration combines the traditional craftsmanship of the Scottish distillery with the bottler’s expertise and palate, resulting in a unique edition.

faq.question: How does the 21-year maturation influence the flavor of this Glen Garioch whisky?
faq.answer: The extended 21-year maturation develops a complex flavor profile featuring deep notes of dried plums, raisins, dark chocolate, and leather. The cask aging imparts a balanced, full-bodied texture and a long, spicy finish with hints of old oak and spices.

faq.question: Why is the 53.9% ABV significant for the taste of this whisky?
faq.answer: At 53.9% ABV, the Glen Garioch 21 Years is robust and intense without overwhelming. The higher alcohol content helps to carry the complex spice and fruit notes and provides a full-bodied mouthfeel that highlights the whisky’s character.

item.name: St. Kilian Single Cask – Ultra Heavily Peated
item.description: When a whisky comes with 135 ppm phenol content and the malt comes exclusively from Glen Esk - a Scottish malting estate that doesn't deliver this kind of extremism to anyone else - then you can expect great things. And this 6-years single malt from an Oloroso sherry hogshead delivers.

image.alt: St. Kilian Single Cask – Ultra Heavily Peated

tasting_notes.nose: What rises from the glass here is not for the faint-hearted. Pure campfire, no moss, no dirt - just glowing hot, dry smoke. You need a little patience behind it - but it's worth it: sultana sweetness, a fresh sugariness and light sherry fruit carefully peek out from behind the fog.
tasting_notes.palate: On the palate, the whisky is initially dry, oily and powerful, with a warm wave of peat that envelops everything. Then red fruits, dried fruits and malty notes set in - an almost classic combination, but served under high pressure. The Oloroso influence is present, but remains in the background: the smoke sets the tone, but the orchestra plays along.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is - as expected - long, dry and clearly smoky. But there is more: spice, dark chocolate, a hint of leather and - surprisingly - a maritime note reminiscent of seaweed and coastal fog.

conclusion.marcel: This Ultra Heavily Peated is a true powerhouse with heart and soul – smoky to the max, yet still playful and lively. If you’re into fire, spice, and character, here’s a dramatic companion that leaves you wanting more, even if it sometimes feels a bit overpowering.
