faq.answer: The high alcohol content delivers an intense aroma and strong spiciness that highlights the flavors of bourbon and rye. For a more enjoyable drinking experience, it’s recommended to dilute the whisky with a little water to better reveal the aromas and soften the sharpness.

faq.question: What makes the whisky from St. George’s Distillery stand out compared to classic Scottish single malts?
faq.answer: St. George’s Distillery in England produces whiskies with an unusual profile that is more reminiscent of American bourbon or rye whiskies. This 9-years single malt combines fruity notes like peach and pear with robust oak spice and caramel, clearly setting it apart from typical Speyside whiskies.

faq.question: Why is this whisky called “Slicing and dicing” and how is that reflected in the taste?
faq.answer: The name “Slicing and dicing” refers to the complex and layered aroma profile that unfolds during tasting. Just like cutting and chopping reveal different pieces, various flavor notes emerge – from fruity stone fruits to creamy vanilla and spicy, sweet elements – creating a dynamic and varied tasting experience.

item.name: Puni Sole 4 Years (2017)
item.description: Whisky from Italy? What sounded exotic a few years ago has become a reality thanks to the Puni distillery. The Puni Sole 4 years (release 2017) is a young but exciting whisky that develops a complex interplay of flavours thanks to its maturation in bourbon and sherry casks. At 46% vol., it has a pleasant strength without being overpowering.

tasting_notes.nose: At the first nosing, classic bourbon barrel notes fill the nose: vanilla, honey and a malty sweetness characterise the first impression. This is joined by a fresh, floral note that is almost reminiscent of lemonade. But that's not all - the flavours of the sherry cask resonate subtly in the background: a hint of marzipan, pecan and a hint of milk chocolate provide an exciting depth.
tasting_notes.palate: An impressively high flavour density meets an oily texture. Notes of green apple and fresh apple juice provide a lively fruitiness, while gooseberry and honey create a sweet yet slightly acidic balance. White pepper adds a gentle spiciness, while a hint of sugar syrup appears underneath.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is somewhat lighter compared to the rest of the profile, but still pleasantly long. The sweetness remains, accompanied by a fine oak flavour. Not a powerful finish, but an elegant, gentle finish.

conclusion.marcel: The Puni Sole 4 Years surprises with lively fruitiness and a pleasant sweetness that simply makes you want to take the next sip. For a young whisky, it is remarkably rounded and shows that Italian whisky can be more than just exotic – pure drinking pleasure!
conclusion.sascha: A well-structured whisky with a balanced interplay between bourbon-typical vanilla and subtle sherry notes, which, despite its young age of 4 years, offers a remarkable flavor density. The elegant finish and the harmonious integration of 46% ABV make it a solid representative of modern Italian whisky craftsmanship.

faq.question: How does the combination of bourbon and sherry casks influence the taste of the Puni Sole 4 Years?
faq.answer: Maturation in bourbon casks imparts vanilla, honey, and malty notes to the whisky, while the sherry casks add delicate aromas of marzipan, pecan nuts, and milk chocolate. This combination creates a complex and well-balanced aromatic profile.

faq.question: What makes the Puni Sole 4 Years special as an Italian whisky compared to traditional whiskies from Scotland or Ireland?
faq.answer: The Puni Sole 4 Years stands out due to its Italian origin, where the climate and distillation techniques foster a unique character. Its fruity freshness and the blend of bourbon and sherry cask maturation give it a distinctive profile that sets it apart from classic Scottish or Irish whiskies.

faq.question: How does the age of 4 years affect the complexity and taste of the Puni Sole?
faq.answer: At 4 years old, the Puni Sole is a young whisky that nevertheless offers surprising flavor density and complexity. The maturation in two types of casks adds depth, while the relatively short time in the barrel preserves lively fruitiness and a certain freshness.

item.name: Ben Nevis 10 Years (2013) Mingle into darkness Cask 78.84 - The Scotch Malt Whisky Society
item.description: Some whiskies are like a relaxing evening stroll - gentle, cosy, predictable. And then there are bottlings like the Ben Nevis 2013 from cask 78.84 of the Scotch Malt Whisky Society: a wild ride through dense flavours, strong sherry influences and a concentrated load of oak spice.

image.alt: Ben Nevis 2013 10 Years Mingle into darkness Cask 78.84 - The Scotch Malt Whisky Society

tasting_notes.nose: From the very first sniff, it is clear that a lot is happening here! A wave of dark berries meets a tart freshness reminiscent of blackberries and a hint of lemon. At the same time, a deep sweetness resonates - brown sugar mixes with fresh hay and a hint of crusty bread. Everything seems extremely concentrated, almost compressed, as if the barrel has condensed all the flavours into a very small space over the years.
tasting_notes.palate: The first sip? A powerful start. Malty sweetness covers the tongue like a velvety film, followed by a mixture of red fruits, creamy coconut notes and voluminous oak. The power of the old sherry cask is immediately noticeable here: spice, strength and depth dominate without tipping the whisky into extremes. The balance between sweet malt, berry freshness and heavy oak notes makes it an exciting powerhouse.
tasting_notes.finish: Long, warming and dry. The fruitiness gives way to a spicy mixture of cinnamon, tobacco and a hint of cranberry. Over time, a pleasant bitterness develops that is reminiscent of dark chocolate and brings the whole thing to an elegant close.

conclusion.marcel: This Ben Nevis is a true powerhouse that you can’t just knock back – it demands nerves of steel and a craving for intense flavors. The concentrated sherry spice and robust oak aren’t for the faint-hearted, but that’s exactly what makes it so enjoyable. A whisky that commands your attention and tells stories with every sip.
conclusion.sascha: With its high alcohol content and intense sherry cask maturation, the Ben Nevis 2013 presents itself as a complex and well-structured Highland whisky. The balance between sweet malt notes, berry freshness, and dominant oak tannins is remarkable, even if the oak spice might feel a bit overpowering for some. Overall, an exciting bottling with clear depth and a long-lasting finish.

faq.question: How does the sherry cask influence the flavors of the Ben Nevis 2013 10 Years?
faq.answer: The sherry cask imparts intense notes of dark berries, brown sugar, and a robust oak spiciness. It provides a deep sweetness and spicy depth that shape the whisky’s character without overwhelming it.

faq.question: Why does the Ben Nevis 2013 have such a high alcohol content of 67.3%?
faq.answer: The high alcohol content results from the cask strength bottling, where the whisky is not diluted. This preserves the full intensity and complexity of the flavors but requires careful enjoyment, ideally with a little water to open up the aromas.

faq.question: Which flavor notes dominate the finish of this whisky?
faq.answer: The finish is long and warming, characterized by spicy notes like cinnamon and tobacco, complemented by a subtle bitterness reminiscent of dark chocolate. The fruity elements give way to an elegant, dry spiciness.

item.name: Mac-Talla (2009/2024) Feis Ile (2024) - Morrison Scotch Whisky Distillers
item.description: The Feis Ile bottlings are a highlight for Islay fans every year, and 2024 doesn't disappoint either: the Mac-Talla 2009/2024 Feis Ile from Morrison Scotch Whisky Distillers has everything you would expect from a classic, smoky dram - and a little bit more.

image.alt: Mac-Talla 2009/2024 Feis Ile 2024 - Morrison Scotch Whisky Distillers

tasting_notes.nose: From the very first nosing, it is clear that a lot is happening in the glass. A strong plume of smoke meets burning elderberry, followed by a slightly leathery note reminiscent of well-worn leather shoes. This is joined by more unusual accents such as sweaty feet - yes, really - and a chalky freshness. But before that puts you off: the smoke skilfully softens the whole thing and is accompanied by sweet notes of caramel, blood orange and candy floss. A wild but fascinating combination.
tasting_notes.palate: The first sip brings with it an earthy spiciness, followed by an intense citrus note. Then the peat smoke hits with full force - almost like a whack-a-mole game with different smoke flavours that keep coming from different directions. However, the Mac-Talla not only remains smoky and strong, but also has a lovely sweetness reminiscent of dried fruit. A hint of turmeric gives the whole thing a spicy, slightly exotic flavour.
tasting_notes.finish: This shows that we are dealing with a well thought-out Islay whisky. The finish is long, full-bodied and remains earthy. A slightly nutty note resonates while the smoke slowly dissipates and leaves a pleasant warmth behind.

conclusion.marcel: This Mac-Talla 2009/2024 Feis Ile is a real fireworks display for the senses: smoky, sweet, and with a touch of madness – exactly what you expect from an Islay dram. A whisky that’s great fun and simply captivates with its complex aroma profile, even if the sweaty sock note is a bit daring. For fans who like to enjoy something off the beaten path, this is a bullseye!
conclusion.sascha: The Mac-Talla 2009/2024 impresses with an outstanding balance between intense peat smoke and complex sherry notes that give it depth and structure. The unusual but well-integrated aromas like chalky freshness and spicy turmeric speak to a thoughtful composition that harmoniously rounds off the long, earthy finish. A technically solid example of modern Islay whisky with character.

faq.question: What makes the Mac-Talla 2009/2024 Feis Ile from Morrison Scotch Whisky Distillers stand out compared to other Islay whiskies?
faq.answer: The Mac-Talla 2009/2024 Feis Ile is distinguished by its complex aroma profile, which alongside classic peat smoke also offers unusual notes like burning elderberry, chalky freshness, and even a slight leathery touch. This combination of intense smokiness and sweet nuances such as caramel and blood orange makes it particularly multifaceted.

faq.question: How should you best enjoy the Mac-Talla 2009/2024 Feis Ile to fully experience its aromas?
faq.answer: It’s best enjoyed neat at room temperature to optimally perceive the complex aromas like peat smoke, citrus notes, and dried fruits. A large nosing glass helps the scent unfold, and a few drops of water can help soften the intensity of the smoke.

faq.question: What does the designation 'Independent bottler' mean for Morrison Scotch Whisky Distillers regarding this whisky?
faq.answer: As an independent bottler, Morrison Scotch Whisky Distillers purchases the whisky from the distillery and bottles it themselves. This often allows for limited editions with unique flavor profiles that can differ from the distillery’s standard releases. The Mac-Talla 2009/2024 Feis Ile is therefore a special selection not directly from the distillery.

item.name: Ballindalloch 8 Years (2016/2024)
item.description: Ballindalloch Distillery, one of the younger distilleries in Scotland's famous Speyside region, has created a whisky that combines both strength and elegance with its Ballindalloch 2016/2024 8 Years.

tasting_notes.nose: The first sniff reveals a floral splendour reminiscent of rose water and gardens in bloom. Underneath, notes of green apple and a fine, perfumed elegance resonate. Despite the high alcohol content, the ethanol scent is only subtly perceptible and only lends the whisky a subtle spiciness. Over time, vanilla and oaky nuances are added to round off the flavour.
tasting_notes.palate: The first sip surprises with a waxy texture and a tangy freshness that invigorates the palate. The flavour is dominated by malty and cereal notes that shape the character of the whisky. A pinch of parsley gives it an unexpected but pleasant spiciness, while vanilla and a certain clarity of flavour provide balance. This whisky puts the grain centre stage.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is short, but by no means insignificant. A warming sensation spreads, accompanied by the subtle bitterness of the oak. This slight astringency contrasts nicely with the initial sweetness and leaves a clean, inviting aftertaste.

conclusion.marcel: The Ballindalloch 2016/2024 8 Year brings a breath of fresh air to the Speyside scene: powerful, sweet, and with a charming spiciness that leaves you wanting more. Its floral and malty notes deliver pure drinking pleasure, even if the short finish could use a bit more depth. A fantastic companion for anyone who appreciates young whisky with character and a touch of humor.
conclusion.sascha: A well-structured Speyside whisky that impresses with its balanced interplay of malt, grain, and wood notes. The high alcohol strength supports the intensity without overshadowing the delicate floral and spicy aromas. Only the comparatively short finish limits the full enjoyment potential of this technically intriguing 8-years dram.

faq.question: How does the high alcohol content of 60.8% affect the taste and drinkability of the Ballindalloch 2016/2024 8 Year?
faq.answer: The 60.8% alcohol content delivers an intense aromatic profile and a certain sharpness, which is well balanced by the floral and malty notes. Many enthusiasts prefer to add a little water to this whisky to bring out the flavors more clearly and enhance its drinkability.

faq.question: What role does the grain note play in the Ballindalloch 2016/2024 8 Year compared to other Speyside whiskies?
faq.answer: In the Ballindalloch 2016/2024 8 Year, the grain is clearly at the forefront, setting it apart from many other Speyside whiskies that tend to be fruitier or sweeter. The malty and grain-forward aromas, complemented by a subtle hint of parsley spice, give it a distinctive and characterful flavor.

faq.question: How does the aroma of the Ballindalloch 2016/2024 8 Year develop in the glass over time?
faq.answer: Upon opening, the whisky presents a floral nose with rosewater and green apples, which after a few minutes is enhanced by vanilla and oak notes. The initial subtle sharpness from the alcohol softens, allowing the complex aromas to unfold further and create a well-balanced overall impression.

item.name: Glen Scotia 14 Years (2024) Icons of Campbeltown - Release No. 2: The Dragon
item.description: Campbeltown is a whisky region full of character - salty, rugged, maritime and often blessed with a sparse elegance. The Glen Scotia Distillery carries on this heritage and regularly brings exceptional bottlings to the market with its Icons of Campbeltown series. The Dragon, the second edition in this series, is a 14-years single malt with a powerful 56.8% ABV that challenges both experienced whisky lovers and adventurers alike.

image.alt: Glen Scotia 2024 14 Years Icons of Campbeltown - Release No. 2: The Dragon

tasting_notes.nose: A mixture of strong oak and an unexpected note of boiled egg - not unpleasant, but unusual. Behind this, a nutty depth unfolds with pistachios and pecan, accompanied by an underlying dark fruitiness. Plums and a hint of lime provide an exciting balance, while white pepper adds a spicy sharpness. A fragrance that unfolds and reveals new facets with every sniff.
tasting_notes.palate: Bone-dry and astringent - as if you had taken a sip from an old oak barrel. The maritime flavour is unmistakable, with salty notes mingling with the short, pithy oak. Despite the strength, the alcohol is very well integrated, giving the whisky structure. The mouthfeel is intense and sophisticated - a whisky that is uncompromising and certainly not immediately appealing to everyone.
tasting_notes.finish: Chilli heat meets the subtle sweetness of brown sugar. But that's not all - a pebbly, stony note remains, almost as if you were breathing over a salty sea cliff.

conclusion.marcel: Anyone searching for a whisky with character can look forward to a rugged, maritime adventure. The Dragon challenges the palate with its dry, spicy nature and rewards patience with a complex depth – this is not a whisky to be casually sipped, but one to be savored with reverence.
conclusion.sascha: A complex and powerfully structured malt that impresses with its intense wood notes and a balanced salt-and-pepper spiciness. The high alcohol strength is harmoniously integrated, yet the pronounced dryness and astringent mouthfeel make it a demanding whisky, appealing especially to experienced connoisseurs.

faq.question: What makes the Campbeltown region special for whisky?
faq.answer: Campbeltown is known for its maritime, salty, and often rugged whisky characters. The region offers a unique combination of austere elegance and bold flavors, which are clearly reflected in whiskies like the Glen Scotia 2024 14 Years Icons of Campbeltown.

faq.question: Why does the Glen Scotia The Dragon smell like boiled egg?
faq.answer: The boiled egg note arises from certain sulfur compounds that can be released during the maturation process in the cask. These are not unusual and can add an interesting depth and complexity to the whisky without detracting from the enjoyment.

faq.question: How should one best enjoy the Glen Scotia 14 Years The Dragon to fully unfold its aromas?
faq.answer: Due to its high alcohol content of 56.8%, it is advisable to dilute the whisky with a few drops of water. This helps open up the aromas of oak, maritime notes, and spices, making the intense mouthfeel more approachable.

item.name: Kingsbarns Balcomie
item.description: Scotland and golf – a classic combination. The Kingsbarns Balcomie is named after a golf course in Fife and comes from the Lowland distillery of the same name. The Wemyss family, already known as an independent bottler, has been operating Kingsbarns since 2014. For this whisky, Isabella Wemyss relies on maturation in Oloroso sherry casks made of American white oak. The result? A smooth, sweet dram that carries the signature of this young distillery.

tasting_notes.nose: Right from the first sniff, Balcomie reveals its fresh character. Vanilla rises to the nose, followed by crisp apples and pears. A hint of Federweißer adds a certain liveliness. Subtle notes of milk chocolate and a grainy base aroma reminiscent of freshly malted barley round out the profile.
tasting_notes.palate: On the palate, the whisky is malty and fruity-fresh. Notes of fruit bread and coconut lend it a sweet, slightly exotic component. The spice remains pleasantly restrained: white pepper, gentle oak spice, and a hint of cinnamon provide balance. Despite its youth, the Balcomie remains smooth and well-rounded.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is spicy and warming. Clove and hazelnut leave a pleasant impression. A slight youthful character shines through, but the overall picture remains harmonious.

conclusion.marcel: The Kingsbarns Balcomie is a pleasant companion for cozy evenings, offering plenty of drinking pleasure with its sweet vanilla and fruity notes. While it remains unpretentious and doesn’t revolutionize the whisky world, it convinces with its soft, approachable character – perfect for anyone seeking uncomplicated Lowland enjoyment.
conclusion.sascha: The balance and structure of the Kingsbarns Balcomie are well executed, with a harmonious blend of sweet sherry aromas and delicate spice. Despite its youthful nature, the whisky feels rounded and balanced without making any bold statements – a solid Lowlander that stands out especially for its elegant fruitiness.

faq.question: Why is the Kingsbarns Balcomie matured in Oloroso sherry casks made from American white oak?
faq.answer: Maturation in Oloroso sherry casks made from American white oak gives the Balcomie its characteristic sweet note and depth. The sherry casks contribute fruity and nutty aromas, while the American white oak adds vanilla and smoothness.

faq.question: How does the location of the Kingsbarns distillery in the Lowland region influence the whisky’s flavor?
faq.answer: The Lowland region is known for lighter, fresh, and often floral whiskies. Kingsbarns Balcomie displays these typical traits with its fruity freshness, maltiness, and gentle spice that are characteristic of Lowland whiskies.

faq.question: What does it mean that Kingsbarns Balcomie has no age statement?
faq.answer: A whisky without an age statement means that no minimum age is indicated on the bottle. Kingsbarns Balcomie is usually composed of younger whiskies, which is reflected in its fresh and lively character, without compromising on quality or complexity.

item.name: Kilkerran 8 Years (2024) Batch 10
item.description: Kilkerran is known for its raw, honest whiskies - and this 8-years Batch 10 is no exception. At a whopping 57.4% ABV, it packs quite a punch, but behind the strength lies a sophisticated blend of smoke, spice and sweetness.

tasting_notes.nose: The first impression? Herbal spice meets a pinch of curcuma - exotic, but not overpowering. There is also a slight hint of peat smoke, which combines elegantly with blossom honey. But then it comes: a bacony undertone with an almost medicinal note, reminiscent of bath concentrate with spruce needles. Sounds wild? It is - but in a really good way!
tasting_notes.palate: Sweet red fruits open the game, but a decent volume of smoke is quickly added. The whisky is oily, full-bodied and has an astringent dryness that creates tension. Brown sugar rounds off the whole and lends a pleasant sweetness that blends wonderfully into the overall picture.
tasting_notes.finish: Clearly earthy, with a dry, almost dusty smokiness reminiscent of a BBQ that has gone out. A finish with character that doesn't disappear too quickly.

conclusion.marcel: This Kilkerran 2024 Batch 10 is a spirited dram that really delights with its smoky spice and sweet fruit notes. The robust strength delivers plenty of drive without overshadowing the subtle nuances – a true Campbeltown classic to savor and admire.
conclusion.sascha: At 57.4% ABV, the Kilkerran 2024 Batch 10 presents itself as a complex, well-structured whisky that balances smoke, spice, and sweetness harmoniously. The earthy finish and astringent dryness provide a long-lasting finish, giving this Campbeltown whisky a particularly distinctive character.

faq.question: How does the high alcohol content of 57.4% affect the taste of the Kilkerran 2024 8 Year Batch 10?
faq.answer: The high alcohol content delivers an intense aromatic profile and a powerful mouthfeel. It accentuates the smoky and spicy notes but can also produce a noticeable warmth. Many recommend adding a little water to the whisky to better reveal the delicate flavors.

faq.question: What role does the Campbeltown region play in the character of this Kilkerran whisky?
faq.answer: Campbeltown is known for its maritime, often lightly smoky and complex whiskies. Kilkerran, as a representative of this region, exhibits typical traits such as a balanced blend of smoke, spice, and sweetness, shaped by its unique maturation process and local microclimates.

faq.question: What makes the tasting notes of Kilkerran Batch 10 stand out compared to other 8-years whiskies?
faq.answer: This batch impresses with an unusual combination of exotic herbal spice featuring turmeric, a bacon-like and almost medicinal undertone, and an oily texture. The earthy, dry smoke on the finish clearly sets it apart from the more fruity or sweet 8-years expressions.

item.name: Deanston 12 Years
item.description: The building was originally a cotton mill before it was converted into a distillery in 1965. Today, whisky flows here instead of lengths of cloth - and around 15% of the production ends up in our glasses as single malt.

tasting_notes.nose: The Deanston 12 is a fresh, lively Highlander. Vanilla and honey are immediately present and bring a lovely sweetness. These are joined by cereal notes and the scent of green apple, which makes the whole thing fruity and light. The hint of freshly cut wood, reminiscent of a carpenter's workshop, is particularly exciting. And if you sniff closely, you will discover a subtle nuttiness that pleasantly rounds off the whisky.
tasting_notes.palate: Soft, but present. Walnuts and distinctive oak provide a pleasant spiciness, while honey and vanilla literally spread out in the mouth - and do so for a long time. There is also a floral, slightly grassy note reminiscent of a summer meadow. The citrus-like acidity, which is somewhere between grapefruit and lemon zest, is particularly exciting. This provides a lovely freshness without the whisky becoming too sour.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is medium to long and has a spicy, fruity sweetness. Particularly striking: the fine black tea flavour, which provides a pleasantly dry finish. Not a big hit, but a nice, well-rounded thing.

conclusion.marcel: A whisky that’s fun without ever being overbearing – the Deanston 12 is like a good friend who’s always up for a relaxed evening with subtle vanilla and honey notes. It has character and a pleasant freshness that makes drinking it a real pleasure. Perfect for anyone looking for an uncomplicated yet far from boring Highlander!
conclusion.sascha: The Deanston 12 presents a well-balanced combination of sweet vanilla and honey notes, supported by a subtle oak spiciness and a fresh citrus touch that keeps the overall impression structured and lively. The medium-length finish with delicate black tea tones provides a dry, harmonious conclusion. Overall, a technically well-crafted Highland malt with enjoyable drinkability and a clearly defined profile.

faq.question: What makes the Deanston 12 years special compared to other Highland whiskies?
faq.answer: Deanston 12 years stands out for its fresh and lively aroma profile, combining vanilla, honey, and green apple notes. The hint of freshly cut wood and subtle nuttiness give it a distinctive depth that sets it apart from the classic Highland whisky.

faq.question: How does the former use of the distillery building as a cotton mill influence the whisky?
faq.answer: The conversion of the former cotton mill into a distillery in 1965 brings a unique history that indirectly reflects in the production. The artisanal atmosphere and use of traditional methods contribute to Deanston 12 years having an authentic, handcrafted character.

faq.question: How should one best enjoy the Deanston 12 years to fully experience its aromas?
faq.answer: To fully unfold the complex aromas of Deanston 12 years, it is recommended to enjoy it neat or with a few drops of water. This way, the vanilla, honey, and citrus notes come through especially well, while the spicy oak and black tea hints in the finish remain clearly perceptible.

item.name: Teeling Rose Wine Cask (2022)
item.description: In 2015, the Teeling Distillery revived whiskey production in Dublin. With their craft distillery in the Liberties, they honor Irish tradition while bringing a modern twist. This Teeling Rosé Wine Cask is part of their innovative lineup – but what does it have to offer?

tasting_notes.nose: The first impression is typically Irish: mild and smooth with a pleasant citrus note. Then it gets more interesting – fruity, almost wine-like aromas meet a solid dose of oak. Fresh sawdust comes through, accompanied by a subtle, nutty bitterness in the background.
tasting_notes.palate: The whiskey starts off smooth but carries a distinct woodiness. Earthy tones and fresh wood dominate, with oak once again playing a strong role. A particularly intriguing hint of red fruit bitterness adds depth and complexity.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is medium-long and surprisingly spicy for an Irish whiskey. A pleasant warmth spreads, slightly sharp with a tingling sensation. Hints of wild berry jam shine through, adding a sweet and fruity touch.

conclusion.marcel: The Teeling Rose Wine Cask brings a bold fruity note to the glass, adding plenty of character and delivering drinking pleasure with surprising accents. It’s a good choice for a relaxed evening, though it lacks a bit of the wow factor for long-term enjoyment. An Irish whiskey that’s fun without being overwhelming.
conclusion.sascha: The Teeling Rose Wine Cask displays an interesting balance between fruity freshness and a distinctive wood note that gives it structure. Despite the intriguing aromas, the complexity remains rather moderate, making it a solid but not outstanding representative of Irish whiskeys. Definitely recommended for fans of well-crafted, artisanal bottlings.

faq.question: What makes the Teeling Rose Wine Cask (2022) special compared to other Irish whiskeys?
faq.answer: The Teeling Rose Wine Cask (2022) matures in rosé wine casks, which imparts an unusual fruity and wine-like note. This cask maturation creates a combination of gentle citrus freshness, tart red fruits, and a pronounced wood character that sets it apart from classic Irish whiskeys.

faq.question: How does maturation in rosé wine casks influence the flavor of this whiskey?
faq.answer: Maturation in rosé wine casks brings out fruity, almost wine-like aromas that, combined with oak and a subtle nuttiness, create a complex flavor profile. This results in an exciting interplay of fresh, tart, and sweet notes, complemented in the finish by a tingling spiciness.

faq.question: Is the Teeling Rose Wine Cask (2022) suitable for beginners or more for experienced whiskey drinkers?
faq.answer: With its mild entry and fruity notes, the Teeling Rose Wine Cask (2022) is quite accessible for beginners. At the same time, the complex cask influences and spicy depth offer enough intrigue for experienced connoisseurs looking to explore new flavor profiles.

item.name: Roseisle 12 Years Origami Kite 2 - Diageo Special Releases (2024)
item.description: The Diageo Special Releases bring some of the most exciting bottlings to the market every year. In 2024, the Roseisle 12 Years Origami Kite 2 is a whisky from a distillery that was only founded in 2010 and has barely made an appearance as a single malt to date. Scepticism is appropriate - especially with a proud price that competes with established Speyside classics.

image.alt: Roseisle 12 Years Origami Kite 2 - Diageo Special Releases 2024

tasting_notes.nose: The first impression is characterised by a warm maltiness that combines with honey-like aromas. Apple crumble brings a beautiful, buttery-fruity component into play, while fresh wood and mint provide a certain lightness. A subtle candyfloss note lends the whisky a gentle sweetness. Overall, a pleasant, typical Speyside profile, but without any major surprises.
tasting_notes.palate: On the palate, the Roseisle 12 Years is unexpectedly herbal, which gives it an interesting, slightly bitter flavour. This is followed by a creamy vanilla ice cream sweetness, which is quickly overlaid by strong oak and spicy ginger. Forest honey provides a pleasant depth and a buttery croissant note gives the whisky a certain elegance. The 55.6% ABV is well integrated, but still gives the whisky a noticeable strength.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is long, spicy and dry, with dominant oak and an interesting mix of apricot, sandalwood and cinnamon. A slight white pepper note provides additional spiciness. Not spectacular, but a solid and aromatically well-balanced finish.

conclusion.marcel: If you're looking for a solid Speyside whisky with a good punch, the Roseisle 12 Year Origami Kite 2 is a pleasant companion that’s fun to enjoy at cask strength. However, it lacks that certain something to truly stand out from the crowd – and the price is simply too ambitious for a newcomer.
conclusion.sascha: The Roseisle 12 Year presents a balanced structure with clearly defined malt, herbal, and wood notes, yet it remains conventional and somewhat uninspired. The 55.6% ABV is well integrated, but the value for money suffers due to its limited complexity compared to established Speyside whiskies.

faq.question: How does the Roseisle 12 Year Origami Kite 2 differ from other Speyside whiskies?
faq.answer: Despite its typical Speyside profile, the Roseisle 12 Year Origami Kite 2 reveals an unusually herbal and slightly bitter note on the palate, complemented by spicy ginger and a noticeable oak presence, setting it apart from the classic, often fruitier Speyside whiskies.

faq.question: How does the high alcohol content of 55.6% affect the enjoyment of this whisky?
faq.answer: At 55.6% ABV, the Roseisle 12 Year is powerful but maintains a pleasant balance thanks to the good integration of the alcohol strength. The high alcohol level amplifies the spicy and woody aromas, so adding a little water is recommended to better unlock its complexity.

faq.question: Why is the Roseisle 12 Year Origami Kite 2 interesting as a single malt despite its young distillery and limited recognition?
faq.answer: Founded only in 2010, Roseisle has so far made little impact as a single malt. This release from the Diageo Special Releases 2024 offers a rare chance to taste a young Speyside malt with an independent character that combines traditional notes with distinctive spice and depth.

item.name: Compass Box Artist Blend
item.description: The Compass Box Artist Blend is a blended Scotch whisky that pays homage to the creative soul of Edinburgh. Since its introduction in 2011, it has established itself as a staple in the Compass Box core range, promising the high quality the independent bottler is known for. Bottled at 43% ABV, without an age statement, it boasts natural color and is non-chill filtered. This blend combines selected malt and grain whiskies, including distillates from Linkwood, Clynelish, and Cameronbridge.

tasting_notes.nose: On the nose, the Artist Blend reveals an enticing mix of fruity and sweet notes. Fresh apples and pears dominate, accompanied by hints of ripe peaches. Subtle nuances of coconut and butter biscuit lend a creamy sweetness to the bouquet. Touches of salted caramel, cereal, honey, cotton candy, and vanilla round out the aroma profile.
tasting_notes.palate: On the palate, the whisky is exceptionally creamy and mild. The vanilla notes harmonize with light fruit nuances, while a subtle spiciness and cereal elements provide balance. A hint of toffee and exotic passion fruit enrich the flavor profile.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is pleasantly long, emphasizing the spicy components. Lovely oak notes, paired with cardamom, leave a slightly warming sensation.

conclusion.marcel: A wonderfully approachable whisky that delights with its creamy texture and fruity aromas. The Artist Blend is like a friendly companion that pampers the palate without being overly complicated – perfect for anyone seeking uncomplicated enjoyment.
conclusion.sascha: The Compass Box Artist Blend strikes a successful balance of fruity freshness and subtle spice, supported by a solid structure with an elegant oak note. Despite its simplicity, it impresses with harmonious complexity and a precise blending of its components.

faq.question: Why does the Compass Box Artist Blend forgo chill filtration and added coloring?
faq.answer: Avoiding chill filtration and added coloring preserves the whisky’s natural aromas and original color. This ensures that the complex flavor notes and texture of the Compass Box Artist Blend remain authentic and unaltered.

faq.question: What role do the individual distillates like Linkwood, Clynelish, and Cameronbridge play in the blend?
faq.answer: Linkwood contributes fruity and floral notes, Clynelish adds a creamy texture and spicy nuances, while Cameronbridge, as a grain whisky, provides a mild and balancing base. Together, they create the distinctive balance of the Artist Blend.

faq.question: How does the absence of an age statement affect the quality and taste of the Compass Box Artist Blend?
faq.answer: Without an age statement, Compass Box can carefully combine various casks and distillates to achieve a consistent flavor profile. The quality therefore depends on the meticulous selection of components rather than age alone.

item.name: Talisker x Parley - Wilder Seas - XO Cognac Cask Finish (2023)
item.description: With Wilder Seas, Talisker has released a special limited bottling that not only impresses with its maritime character, but also with its sustainable mission. In collaboration with Parley for the Oceans, the sale of this edition is intended to help protect over 100 million square metres of marine space.

image.alt: Talisker x Parley - Wilder Seas - XO Cognac Cask Finish (2023)

tasting_notes.nose: From the very first sniff, Wilder Seas greets us with an unexpectedly charming note: subtle smoke meets a warm, sweet wave of cognac. This is joined by a maritime, salty freshness reminiscent of a gentle sea breeze. If you take a closer look, you will find dark berries and a pleasant spiciness. Very pleasant and inviting overall - the wild sea seems to be in the distance here.
tasting_notes.palate: You might expect a powerful, harsh storm on the tongue - but instead there is a surprisingly harmonious interplay of sweet smoke, peppery spice and fruity notes. Sweet smoke, not too intrusive, rather warming and soft, plays with salted caramel and plum - a perfect combination of salty sweetness and fruity freshness. Citrus zest and wet wood add a touch of spice and depth, while herbs make a playful appearance here and there.
tasting_notes.finish: In keeping with the overall experience, the finish is not a wild storm, but a gentle glide. Very salty, slightly astringent, calm with a hint of seaweed. A beautiful maritime finish that maintains the Talisker DNA.

conclusion.marcel: Wilder Seas surprises with its gentle nature – not a storm, but a smooth breeze of sweet smoke and maritime notes that’s simply enjoyable. For under 40 euros, you get a charismatic Talisker that leaves you wanting more without overwhelming you. Those hoping for a wild sea experience might be disappointed, but in return, you get an all-around pleasant companion.
conclusion.sascha: This bottling impresses with its balanced interplay between a sweet Cognac finish and maritime saltiness, while the 48.6% ABV drinking strength adds precise depth. Despite the evocative name, it lacks some of the expected intensity, positioning this Talisker as a harmoniously structured, elegant single malt with a clear focus on complexity.

faq.question: What makes the Cognac Cask Finish special in the Talisker x Parley Wilder Seas XO?
faq.answer: The Cognac Cask Finish imparts a warm, sweet note with fruity accents like plum and dark berries. It creates a harmonious balance between Talisker’s smoky character and an elegant fruity sweetness.

faq.question: How is Talisker’s maritime origin reflected in the taste of this whisky?
faq.answer: The maritime character comes through with salty and slightly astringent notes reminiscent of a gentle sea breeze and seaweed. These elements give the whisky a fresh, salty depth typical of Talisker.

faq.question: What is the sustainable significance of the collaboration between Talisker and Parley for the Oceans?
faq.answer: The sale of this limited edition supports the protection of over 100 million square meters of ocean space. The partnership raises awareness for environmental protection and actively contributes to ocean conservation.

item.name: Lagavulin 12 Years (2024) Fireside Tales - Diageo Special Releases (2024)
item.description: A new Lagavulin special release? The whisky nerd grabs his nosing glass faster than an Islay fisherman grabs his mackintosh. The ‘Fireside Tales’ from the Diageo Special Releases 2024 promises a warm, smoky story by the fireside.

image.alt: Lagavulin 2024 12 Years Fireside Tales - Diageo Special Releases 2024

tasting_notes.nose: Nose in and... Boom! A cloud of oil with a vanilla explosion meets cold smoke. Not that aggressive, medicinal Ardbeg smoke, but rather the subtle campfire flavour with a warming undertone. There is also a mineral flavour - almost as if someone had walked through a pine forest with a tropical fruit salad. Pineapple? Check. Peach? Check. A hint of banana? That's right. And somewhere in the background, a sea breeze blows past, reminiscent of salty spray. Sounds exciting? Yes! Are expectations rising? Oh yes!
tasting_notes.palate: Then the first sip - and oops, the fairy tale takes a sudden turn. Instead of sweet fruit, there is a good dose of ginger and bitter spiciness. This is joined by white pepper and, of course, the typical Lagavulin smoke - but this time cooler than usual. Fruit? Hardly at all. Instead, a curious mix of grilled vegetables, especially peppers, with a pinch of seaweed.
tasting_notes.finish: This is where the whisky comes into its own. Suddenly the pineapple reappears, as if it had got lost for a moment. Fruity, long-lasting and warming. There is also a light tar note and a hint of black pepper. Voluminous, intense - exactly what you want from a good Islay whisky.

conclusion.marcel: This Lagavulin 2024 12 Years Fireside Tales is like an evening chat by the fireplace – full of character, with smoky surprises and a touch of humor. It’s not quite the masterpiece you might expect, but that’s exactly what makes it charming and enjoyable for anyone who likes to embark on an unusual, fruity-vegetal journey. If you’re looking for a smoky Islay with some rough edges, this one definitely delivers the fun.
conclusion.sascha: The Fireside Tales presents an interesting balance between smoky intensity and complex fruit notes, but it loses some precision due to a somewhat inconsistent aroma profile. The structure feels robust, yet it lacks the elegance and harmony that define a Lagavulin 16. For fans of technically demanding Islay whiskies, it still offers an intriguing variation with unusual vegetable and ginger notes.

faq.question: How does the Lagavulin 2024 12 Years Fireside Tales differ from other Lagavulin releases?
faq.answer: The Fireside Tales from the Diageo Special Releases 2024 features a cooler, less medicinal smoke than typical Lagavulin whiskies. It combines a subtle campfire note with fruity aromas like pineapple, peach, and banana, alongside a mineral undertone and a salty sea breeze.

faq.question: Which flavors stand out most in the Lagavulin 2024 12 Years?
faq.answer: On the nose, vanilla, cold smoke, and tropical fruits such as pineapple, peach, and banana dominate. On the palate, ginger, white pepper, grilled vegetables, and seaweed come to the forefront. The finish is long-lasting with fruity pineapple, tar, and black pepper.

faq.question: How should one best enjoy the Lagavulin 2024 12 Years Fireside Tales to experience its complexity?
faq.answer: Best enjoyed neat and at room temperature to fully unfold the delicate smoke and fruit notes. Using a nosing glass helps capture the layered aroma profile. A few drops of water can soften the alcohol and open up new flavor nuances.

item.name: Highland Park 18 Years (2006/2024) - Signatory Vintage
item.description: An 18-years Highland Park bottled by Signatory Vintage – sounds intriguing, doesn’t it? What makes this release even more exciting is that it’s not another “Secret Orkney,” but a genuine Highland Park, showcasing the distillery’s signature style. On paper, it promises a lot – but does it deliver? Let’s find out.

tasting_notes.nose: The first sniff unveils a wonderfully complex bouquet: freshly cut grass, sun-dried hay, and a subtle maltiness set the stage. Fruity undertones follow – think a basket of ripe fruits, led by baked apples. There’s a delicate, pleasant smokiness intertwined with a maritime breeze that completes this aromatic symphony.
tasting_notes.palate: The palate keeps the intrigue alive with its distinctive maritime foundation. Ripe apples and a touch of caramel add sweetness, while hay and a slight chalky note provide a grounding earthiness. Despite its 52 % ABV, the whisky feels incredibly smooth and well-rounded – a testament to its exceptional balance.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is as rewarding as the palate. Notes of candied apples and a hint of honey meet a gentle peatiness. This peat doesn’t overwhelm but rather adds depth and volume, lingering gracefully on the tongue and inviting another sip.

conclusion.marcel: A Highland Park that simply delights with its maritime freshness and perfectly integrated peat smoke – the balance between character and drinking pleasure is spot on here. A whisky that captivates both connoisseurs and curious enthusiasts alike with its lively fruit basket and pleasant sweetness. Absolutely recommended for anyone seeking a multi-layered yet approachable single malt.
conclusion.sascha: This 18-years Highland Park from Signatory Vintage impresses with its balanced structure and the harmonious integration of its 52% ABV. The complex combination of maritime notes, ripe apples, and subtle peat smoke presents a precise flavor profile with a long-lasting finish. A technically sound whisky that clearly reflects the distillery’s signature while skillfully complementing the independent bottler’s style.

faq.question: How does this Highland Park 2006/2024 differ from other releases by the distillery?
faq.answer: The Highland Park 2006/2024 from Signatory Vintage is an independent bottling with 18 years of maturation and a higher alcohol content of 52%. Unlike many standard releases, it exhibits a more intense maritime character and a balanced smokiness that is typical for Highland Park but here feels particularly present and finely integrated.

faq.question: Why is the maritime character so pronounced in this whisky?
faq.answer: The maritime character arises from the distillery’s location on Orkney, a Scottish island group in the North Atlantic. The salty sea air influences both the maturation in oak casks and the whisky’s aroma development, resulting in a subtle salty and fresh note that defines this Highland Park.

faq.question: How does the high alcohol content of 52% affect the taste and drinking experience?
faq.answer: At 52% ABV, this whisky is powerful yet well balanced. The higher alcohol content intensifies the aromas and delivers a full-flavored experience without being harsh. For a harmonious drinking experience, it is recommended to add a few drops of water to better perceive the complex flavor nuances.

item.name: Nc'Nean Organic (2019/2024) Cask Strength Batch GD06
item.description: Nc'Nean is known for its high-quality organic whiskies. With the Nc'Nean Organic 2019/2024 Cask Strength Batch GD06, the distillery is now presenting a particularly strong and intense variant.

image.alt: Nc'Nean Organic 2019/2024 Cask Strength Batch GD06

tasting_notes.nose: The first sniff reveals complex aromas. Ripe fruits such as peach and apple dominate, accompanied by a hint of citrus fruit and forest honey. A slight alcoholic note is present, but not overpowering. Caramel and butter biscuit provide additional sweetness, while a hint of orange adds a fresh component.
tasting_notes.palate: The attack in the mouth is oily and syrupy, with an intense peach note reminiscent of a bomb. Sugary sweetness and vanilla combine with forest honey to create a harmonious overall picture. Interestingly, a slightly spicy cereal note emerges, reminiscent of rye, although there is no rye in the whisky.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is characterised by light oak, accompanied by a spicy aftertaste. The bready notes and the return of stone fruit ensure a lasting impression.

conclusion.marcel: This Nc'Nean Organic Cask Strength is a genuine taste experience that’s simply fun with its fruity power and oily, syrupy texture. Despite the robust 60%, it feels surprisingly smooth and proves that organic whisky can be not only sustainable but also truly delicious.
conclusion.sascha: With a balanced structure between intense fruitiness, subtle spice, and a harmonious oak finish, this Nc'Nean Organic 2019/2024 Batch GD06 demonstrates that cask strength and balance can go hand in hand. Its complex aroma profile and meticulous craftsmanship make it a technically impressive yet approachable Highland whisky.

faq.question: What does 'Cask Strength' mean for the Nc'Nean Organic 2019/2024 Batch GD06?
faq.answer: 'Cask Strength' means the whisky is bottled directly from the cask without dilution. For the Nc'Nean Organic 2019/2024 Batch GD06, this results in a potent 59.6% alcohol content, delivering more intense aromas and a fuller flavor.

faq.question: How can a whisky have a rye note even though no rye was used?
faq.answer: Certain flavor notes like spicy or bready aromas can appear in barley whiskies depending on the malt, yeast, and cask maturation. In the Nc'Nean Organic 2019/2024 Batch GD06, the spicy grain character evokes rye, even though only barley is used.

faq.question: What role does organic certification play in this whisky?
faq.answer: Organic certification ensures that ingredients and the production process are free from chemical pesticides or artificial additives. Nc'Nean uses organically grown barley malt and sustainable production methods for this whisky, which is reflected in its purity and quality.

item.name: Jameson Caskmates IPA Edition (2017)
item.description: Jameson is one of the world’s most renowned Irish whiskey brands, and the Caskmates series has become an exciting playground for experimentation. The concept: classic Jameson undergoes additional maturation in barrels that previously held craft beer – in this case, an India Pale Ale (IPA). The goal is to add fresh, hoppy notes to the smooth Irish whiskey. But how does that translate into flavor? Let’s find out.

tasting_notes.nose: Right from the first sniff, the Caskmates IPA Edition feels refreshing and vibrant. A noticeable citrus aroma emerges, accompanied by green apples and a subtle, underlying hint of hops. Despite these additional accents, the familiar Jameson DNA remains intact – mild, light, and inviting.
tasting_notes.palate: On the palate, the whiskey delivers an unexpected spiciness. Alongside Jameson’s characteristic smoothness, delicate garden herbs appear, blending with a subtle hop presence to create an intriguing nuance. A touch of bitter oak adds depth without compromising the whiskey’s inherent Irish lightness. It’s an interesting twist on the classic, though not a radical reinvention.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is rather short but pleasantly warming. Hints of ginger bring a gentle heat before ripe apple and citrus notes take over. A light touch of oak lingers in the background.

conclusion.marcel: Jameson Caskmates IPA Edition surprises with its lively freshness and hoppy accents, giving the classic a fun twist. It’s not a dramatic transformation, but just right for relaxed evenings when you simply want to enjoy without any fuss.
conclusion.sascha: The IPA Edition from Jameson presents itself as a well-balanced variation with clear structure and subtle spice, respecting the mild base character. The integration of hop and citrus notes is successful yet restrained, providing a delicate complexity without overwhelming the palate.

faq.question: How does maturation in IPA casks influence the flavor of the Jameson Caskmates IPA Edition Release 2017?
faq.answer: Maturation in casks previously filled with India Pale Ale (IPA) imparts a fresh, hoppy note to the Jameson Caskmates IPA Edition, with distinct citrus and herbal aromas that complement the mild and light character of Jameson without overpowering it.

faq.question: Is the Jameson Caskmates IPA Edition Release 2017 a single malt or blended whiskey?
faq.answer: The Jameson Caskmates IPA Edition Release 2017 is a blended Irish whiskey, like all Jameson whiskeys, combining various malted and unmalted barley malts that have been finished in special IPA casks.

faq.question: How long is the finish of this whiskey and which flavors dominate it?
faq.answer: The finish of the Jameson Caskmates IPA Edition is rather short but pleasantly warming, featuring notes of ginger, ripe apple, citrus fruits, and a fine oak touch that provides a subtle bitterness.

item.name: Ayrer's Red Organic Single Malt (2019/2024)
item.description: The AYRER's RED Organic Single Malt 2019/2024 is a German whisky from the Hausbrauerei Altstadthof in Nuremberg. This organic whisky is made from specialty malt from Franconia, which also gives traditional Nuremberg Red Beer its reddish color and velvety smooth taste. The mash is fermented in wooden vats and then gently distilled in a small copper still. After maturing for at least three years in new American oak casks, the whisky is bottled unfiltered by hand, with each bottle individually numbered.

tasting_notes.nose: On the nose, the malt unfolds a bouquet of toffee and roasted chestnuts. Fruity notes of mirabelle plums, with hints of orange, and ripe pear complement the aroma. A distinct malty presence rounds off the scent experience.
tasting_notes.palate: On the palate, this single malt surprises with a smooth and well-balanced texture. The fruity pear notes continue, accompanied by a slight berry-like acidity. While the whisky is not overly complex, it impresses with a pleasant and enjoyable flavor profile.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is very short but offers subtle spicy notes and a tingling sensation that rounds off the tasting experience.

conclusion.marcel: Fruity, malty, and surprisingly rounded – this young Ayrer's Red Organic Single Malt brings fun to the glass and proves that skilled craftsmanship can produce top-notch whisky even in Germany. A delicious companion for relaxed evenings that leaves you wanting more, although a bit more depth wouldn’t have hurt.
conclusion.sascha: The Ayrer's Red Organic Single Malt presents itself as structured and well-balanced, with fruity and malty notes blending harmoniously. Despite its youth, it still lacks some complexity and length in the finish, but remains a solid representative of German craft whiskies with clean execution.

faq.question: What makes the Ayrer’s Red Organic Single Malt 2019/2024 special compared to other German whiskies?
faq.answer: The Ayrer’s Red Organic Single Malt 2019/2024 uses Franconian specialty malt, which is also used in Nuremberg red beer, giving it its characteristic reddish color and a velvety smooth taste. Additionally, it is fermented in wooden vats and gently distilled in a small copper still, which imparts a distinctive artisanal quality.

faq.question: How does maturation in new American white oak barrels influence the flavor of this whisky?
faq.answer: The minimum three-year maturation in new American white oak barrels imparts a mild vanilla and wood note that supports the fruity and malty aromas without overpowering them. This results in a balanced texture and a pleasant, lightly spicy finish.

faq.question: Why is the finish of the Ayrer’s Red Organic Single Malt relatively short, and how does that affect the drinking experience?
faq.answer: The short finish, with subtle spicy notes and a tingling sensation, is characteristic of this whisky and reflects its fresh, not overly complex nature. This makes it an accessible single malt that stands out primarily for its pleasant fruitiness and gentle spice.

item.name: Glenturret 12 Years (2024)
item.description: The Glenturret 12 Years 2024 can be seen as the "big brother" of the Triple Wood 2024. With a clear age statement and a noticeably richer color, this whisky promises more depth and structure. Bottled at 46.4% ABV, it brings a combination of spice, fruitiness, and a creamy texture.

tasting_notes.nose: Right from the start, it's clear that this whisky is a more refined and complex version of the Triple Wood. Cinnamon and oak set striking accents, accompanied by dark berries and a hint of dark chocolate. An inviting graininess adds warmth, while vanilla provides a subtle creamy sweetness.
tasting_notes.palate: The initial impression is wonderfully creamy, followed by a pronounced spiciness. The oak imparts a pleasant depth, while cinnamon and sugar resemble an intensely dark, caramelized pastry. Dark currants bring a fruity note, well-balanced by a light acidity. Overall, a powerful and well-structured taste that skillfully blends spice and sweetness.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish remains pleasantly soft and subtly oak-driven. A warming note runs throughout, accompanied by fruity sweetness and a distinct cinnamon spice. Light spice notes add a fine complexity, while the balance between wood and fruit remains intact.

conclusion.marcel: A whisky that simply delights with its creamy texture and spicy fruit notes – perfect for cozy evenings when you’re looking for some depth and character. Sure, the price isn’t a bargain, but anyone who appreciates mature flavors and an intense tasting experience will be very well served here.
conclusion.sascha: The Glenturret 12 Year 2024 impresses with its balanced interplay between spicy oak and fruity acidity, supported by a creamy mouthfeel. Despite its obvious maturity, it lacks a bit of final precision in places, which makes the somewhat higher price feel a bit questionable.

faq.question: How does the Glenturret 12 Year Release 2024 differ from the Triple Wood 2024?
faq.answer: The Glenturret 12 Year 2024 is a more mature and robust expression with a clear age statement. It has a deeper color, more depth and structure, as well as a higher alcohol content of 46.4% ABV compared to the Triple Wood. In terms of flavor, it shows more spice, fruitiness, and a creamy texture.

faq.question: Which aromas dominate the Glenturret 12 Year Release 2024?
faq.answer: On the nose, cinnamon, oak, dark berries, and bittersweet chocolate dominate, complemented by grainy notes and vanilla. On the palate, creamy textures, spice with hints of cinnamon and sugar, as well as fruity blackcurrants are prominent. The finish is soft with a balance of oak, fruit, and spices.

faq.question: Why is the 46.4% alcohol content important for the Glenturret 12 Year Release 2024?
faq.answer: At 46.4% ABV, the Glenturret 12 Year Release 2024 is above the usual minimum standard of 40%. This higher alcohol content helps to carry the aromas more intensely and provides a stronger, more structured flavor without dilution.

item.name: Glenfiddich 23 Years (2023) Rude Olph - VAME Malts
item.description: The Scottish Glenfiddich distillery is one of the best-known names in the whisky world, but this bottling is something special: a 23-years drop, bottled by VAME Malts. The ‘Rude Olph’ promises a combination of tradition and a breath of fresh air. Will it live up to this promise? Let's find out!

image.alt: Glenfiddich 23 Years 2023 Rude Olph - VAME Malts

tasting_notes.nose: The first impression? Sherry all the way! A pleasant aroma of dates and plums immediately hits the nose - ripe, dark fruit that makes you want more. This is accompanied by honey and a malty sweetness that is wonderfully balanced. After a few moments in the glass, vanilla, raspberries and even a hint of pumpkin reveal themselves. A slightly vinous touch rounds off the bouquet and adds complexity.
tasting_notes.palate: The first sip makes it clear that the ‘Rude Olph’ remains true to its flavour. Pumpkin and a pleasantly sweet fruitiness dominate the opening, accompanied by a hint of sultanas and biscuits. This combination feels almost cosy, like an autumn day by the fireplace. Malt also plays a key role and adds a lovely, sweet and warm note. The whisky is very mild and accessible - perfect for beginners, but experienced connoisseurs will also appreciate the harmonious balance.
tasting_notes.finish: Here, the whisky shows yet another facet: the finish is warming, slightly oaky and ends with a surprising note of burnt toast. This nuance gives the whole thing a little character without being too overpowering.

conclusion.marcel: A whisky that scores primarily with its cozy, autumnal fruitiness and leaves you wanting more – perfect for relaxed evenings by the fireplace. Its gentle character and sweet sherry notes make it an accessible pleasure, even if it lacks that certain something for a true wow moment. Overall, a pleasant companion that charmingly surprises with its warm finish and subtle toasted bread note.
conclusion.sascha: This 23-years bottling strikes a successful balance between sweet sherry aromas and a well-integrated malty structure that supports its complex flavor profile. The warming, lightly oaky finish with an unusual toasted bread note gives the whisky an interesting yet understated depth of character. Despite its mild approachability, the Glenfiddich Rude Olph manages to present a harmonious and thoughtfully crafted composition that appeals to seasoned connoisseurs.

faq.question: What makes the Glenfiddich 23 Years 2023 Rude Olph bottling by VAME Malts special compared to standard releases?
faq.answer: This bottling was independently released by VAME Malts and matured for 23 years, giving it a particularly complex sherry and fruit character. Unlike standard Glenfiddich whiskies, it displays a more intense fruitiness and a distinctive, lightly oaky finish with notes of burnt toasted bread.

faq.question: How does the cask maturation influence the flavor of the Glenfiddich 23 Years Rude Olph?
faq.answer: The extended 23-year maturation, presumably in sherry casks, brings pronounced aromas of dates, plums, and honey. The cask aging also imparts malty sweetness, vanilla, and a warming, lightly oaky finish with a delicate note of burnt toasted bread.

faq.question: Who is the Glenfiddich 23 Years 2023 Rude Olph particularly suited for?
faq.answer: Thanks to its mild and approachable nature, this whisky is ideal for beginners looking to explore more complex flavors. At the same time, experienced enthusiasts will appreciate the balanced harmony between fruitiness, malt, and the distinctive finish.

item.name: Lichtburg 21 Years (2001/2023) Historic Series No. 8 - Rolf Kaspar GmbH
item.description: The Lichtburg 2001/2023 from the Historic Series No. 8 is a whisky that raises questions. The distillery is not officially named, but a glance at the label gives rise to suspicions: Jack Teeling and Dublin are written there - a possible clue to its origin? What we do know for sure: This 21-years bottling comes from Ireland, was bottled at a hefty 53.6% vol. and comes from Rolf Kaspar GmbH, an independent bottler that is always bringing exciting releases to the market.

image.alt: Lichtburg 2001/2023 21 Years Historic Series No. 8 - Rolf Kaspar GmbH

tasting_notes.nose: The first sniff reveals the depth of this whisky. A sumptuous bouquet unfolds: sweet wine flavours dominate at first, followed by ripe flat peach and juicy raspberries. These are joined by dark stone fruits - plums, perhaps a little blackberry. A malty note provides grounding, while a hint of caramel rounds off the composition. Subtle oak notes and an impression of damp autumn leaves provide a pleasant counterbalance to the fruitiness.
tasting_notes.palate: The first taste is soft and creamy, almost luscious. A warm mixture of vanilla and oak immediately unfolds, accompanied by a slightly honey-like sweetness. This is followed by an interplay of roasted pears and baked apple - a perfect interplay of flavours for cool autumn days. The texture is rich, almost buttery, with a nice balance between sweetness and spice. A hint of vanilla custard nestles on the palate, supported by gently toasted oak, which elegantly integrates the high alcohol content. Despite its strength, the whisky is harmonious and balanced.
tasting_notes.finish: It lingers on the palate for a long time, with a combination of almond notes and creamy vanilla custard. A hint of crème brûlée adds a sweet finish, while a warming spiciness lingers.

conclusion.marcel: This Lichtburg 2001/2023 is a true autumn delight – powerful, warming, and full of seductive fruit and spice. Its creamy character turns every tasting into an experience that can be wonderfully sweetened with a piece of baked Camembert and caramelized pears. Those seeking character will find here a charming companion with edges and angles.
conclusion.sascha: The Lichtburg 2001/2023 displays an impressive balance between sweet fruit aromas and an elegant, vanilla-infused oak note. Despite its robust 53.6% ABV, the texture remains buttery and the finish is long-lasting, featuring a fine interplay of almonds and spicy nuances. A technically well-structured Irish single malt that convinces through its harmony and depth.

faq.question: Why is the distillery of the Lichtburg 2001/2023 not officially named?
faq.answer: The distillery is not officially named because this is an independent bottling. However, clues on the label such as "Jack Teeling" and "Dublin" suggest that the whisky may originate from a distillery in Dublin.

faq.question: How does the high alcohol content of 53.6% ABV affect the taste of this whisky?
faq.answer: The high alcohol content provides an intense aroma development and a rich texture. Nevertheless, the whisky is well balanced, as the vanilla, oak, and fruit notes harmoniously integrate the alcohol without it becoming overpowering.

faq.question: What special qualities does the 21-year maturation of the Lichtburg 2001/2023 impart?
faq.answer: The long 21-year maturation gives the whisky a complex range of aromas with ripe fruit notes like peach and plum, complemented by malty, caramel, and spicy tones. This results in a full-bodied, balanced flavor with a long, pleasant finish.

item.name: HeiligenBergFeld (2018/2022) Oloroso Sherry Cask
item.description: The HeiligenBergFeld Distillery, nestled in the Bavarian Alpine foothills, presents the HeiligenBergFeld 2018/2022 Oloroso Sherry Cask, a remarkable single malt whisky. This expression stands out with its cask strength of 57.8% ABV and maturation in first-fill Oloroso sherry casks.

tasting_notes.nose: Upon the first nosing, an enticing bouquet of caramel and ripe stone fruits unfolds. A subtle, slightly musty undertone, accompanied by notes of cherry pits and an earthy hint, adds depth to the whisky.
tasting_notes.palate: On the palate, a red-fruity sweetness emerges, highlighting the stone fruit aromas once again. Nuances of leather and a prominent tobacco note lend character to the whisky. Molasses provides a pleasant sweetness, while a dry, slightly astringent texture and well-integrated oak notes round off the flavor profile. The oily consistency adds to the complexity.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is long-lasting, characterized by spicy oak presence. A distinct sherry influence remains noticeable, ensuring a harmonious finale.

conclusion.marcel: This HeiligenBergFeld 2018/2022 Oloroso Sherry Cask seduces with its intense fruity sweetness and spicy tobacco notes that leave you wanting more – though at times it still lacks a touch of velvety smoothness to fully crown the drinking pleasure.
conclusion.sascha: With a clearly defined cask strength and a precise balance between fruity sherry aromas and earthy leather notes, this single malt shows a solid structure, although a rounder texture could make the flavor profile even more harmonious.

faq.question: How does the Oloroso Sherry Cask influence the taste of the HeiligenBergFeld 2018/2022?
faq.answer: Maturation in first-fill Oloroso sherry casks imparts pronounced notes of caramel, ripe stone fruits, and a spicy oak presence to the whisky. Additionally, the sherry influence provides a harmonious sweetness and depth in the finish.

faq.question: What does the cask strength of 57.8% ABV mean for the enjoyment of this whisky?
faq.answer: At 57.8% ABV, the HeiligenBergFeld 2018/2022 is a cask strength whisky offering more intense aromas and a fuller texture. It can be enjoyed neat or diluted with a little water to further open up the flavors.

faq.question: Why does this whisky have no age statement even though it matured from 2018 to 2022?
faq.answer: The whisky is classified as a 'No Age Statement' (NAS) because, although it matured between 2018 and 2022, no specific minimum aging period is indicated on the label. This is common with many modern releases to allow flexibility in cask selection.

item.name: Dun Naomhaig Bay Batch 2 (2023) - Feinkost Reifferscheid
item.description: The 2023 Islay Single Malt Whisky, bottled by Feinkost Reifferscheid, is a characterful release without an age statement (No Age Statement), boasting a robust alcohol content of 56.0% ABV. As an independent bottler, Feinkost Reifferscheid has once again delivered a whisky that embodies the quintessential traits of the Isle of Islay.

image.alt: Dun Naomhaig Bay Batch 2 2023 - Feinkost Reifferscheid

tasting_notes.nose: Upon the first nosing, a complex aroma unfolds. Grassy notes harmoniously blend with sweet honey and a hint of hemp. An intense campfire smoke, accompanied by bacon-like nuances and a touch of BBQ sauce, adds depth to the whisky. Subtle hints of red bell pepper and toasted breadcrumbs round off the bouquet.
tasting_notes.palate: On the palate, the whisky is full-bodied and creamy. Dominant cold smoke and ashy notes define the initial impression. This is followed by fruity hints of passion fruit, imparting a certain freshness to the taste. Notes of fresh hay and heather complement the flavor profile, ensuring a balanced interplay between smoke and freshness.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is long-lasting, spicy, and marked by pronounced oak notes. A sensation reminiscent of smoldering coal lingers, while the whisky maintains an overall puristic character.

conclusion.marcel: A real crowd-pleaser for Islay fans: this whisky delivers bold smoke alongside a lovely fruitiness that leaves you wanting more. Despite the high alcohol content, it feels lively and not overbearing – a fantastic companion for cozy evenings by the fire.
conclusion.sascha: This bottling impresses with a clear structure where smoky and fruity elements harmonize in perfect balance. The long-lasting oak notes and its minimalist character make it a fine example of a complex Islay whisky with excellent balance.

faq.question: What sets the Dun Naomhaig Bay Batch 2 2023 apart from other Islay whiskies?
faq.answer: The Dun Naomhaig Bay Batch 2 2023 stands out through a combination of intense campfire smoke, bacon and BBQ notes, as well as fruity hints like passion fruit. The high bottling strength of 56% ABV enhances its full-bodied, creamy character and creates a well-balanced interplay between smoke and freshness.

faq.question: How does the No Age Statement (NAS) character influence the taste of this whisky?
faq.answer: Since the Dun Naomhaig Bay Batch 2 2023 carries no age statement, the focus lies on maturation and the blending of different vintages. This allows for a complex aroma profile featuring fresh, grassy notes, intense smoky aromas, and a spicy oak influence on the finish, without the typical constraints of age declarations.

faq.question: What is the best way to enjoy the Dun Naomhaig Bay Batch 2 2023 to fully experience its aromas?
faq.answer: To fully unlock its diverse aromas, it is recommended to enjoy the whisky neat or with a small splash of water. The water opens up the aromas, slightly softens the high strength, and highlights the fruity and herbal notes without diminishing the smoky character.

item.name: Dun Naomhaig Bay Batch 1 (2022) - Feinkost Reifferscheid
item.description: The 2022 single malt whisky, bottled by Feinkost Reifferscheid, hails from Scotland's Isle of Islay, a region renowned for its robust and smoky whiskies. As an independent bottler, Feinkost Reifferscheid has once again demonstrated its expertise with this release.

image.alt: Dun Naomhaig Bay Batch 1 2022 - Feinkost Reifferscheid

tasting_notes.nose: Upon the first nosing, intense aromas of ashy, cold campfire smoke emerge, accompanied by earthy notes. A subtle sweetness of honey and vanilla harmonizes with a pronounced sea breeze reminiscent of salty seawater. This combination imparts deep complexity and authenticity to the whisky.
tasting_notes.palate: On the palate, the whisky is oily and full-bodied. Fresh citrus notes, especially lemon zest, meet chalky hints and a touch of medicinal iodine, reminiscent of bandages. The voluminous smoke is complemented by nuances of heather and a slight nuttiness, adding depth and complexity to the whisky.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is medium-length, characterized by a salty, seawater-like note. A light fruit sweetness emerges, while the lingering campfire smoke provides a warming conclusion. The complexity of the finish both surprises and delights.

conclusion.marcel: A whisky that immediately captivates with its smoky campfire aroma and salty sea breeze – here you can taste the wild soul of Islay. The Dun Naomhaig Bay Batch 1 is not a light dram, but a characterful one that’s great fun and tells new stories with every nosing.
conclusion.sascha: With a balanced blend of intense smokiness, fresh citrus notes, and complex spice, this Islay whisky reveals an impressive structure. The medium-length, salty-fruity finish rounds off the bottling with technical precision and confirms Feinkost Reifferscheid’s knack for harmonious single malts.

faq.question: Why does the Dun Naomhaig Bay Batch 1 2022 not carry an age statement?
faq.answer: This whisky is a so-called "No Age Statement" (NAS), meaning no specific age is given. Independent bottlers like Feinkost Reifferscheid often choose this approach to blend different vintages and casks in order to achieve a particular flavor profile.

faq.question: How does the Islay region influence the character of this whisky?
faq.answer: Islay is known for its peaty, smoky whiskies with maritime influences. In the Dun Naomhaig Bay Batch 1 2022, typical notes such as campfire smoke, salty sea breeze, and earthy aromas emerge, reflecting the distinctive style of this region.

faq.question: What significance does the high alcohol content of 56.2% ABV have for enjoying this whisky?
faq.answer: A high alcohol content like 56.2% ABV delivers intense aromas and a full-bodied character. Many enthusiasts recommend adding a little water to better perceive the complex flavors such as citrus notes, heather, and smoke.

item.name: Tormore 12 Years (2011) Spiced vanilla chai cake Cask 105.45 - The Scotch Malt Whisky Society
item.description: The Scotch Malt Whisky Society (SMWS) is known for its unusual and often experimental bottlings. With the Tormore 2011, matured for 12 years and a proud 66.8% ABV, the SMWS has ventured into a powerful yet playful bourbon cask experience. The sonorous name ‘Spiced Vanilla Chai Cake’ raises expectations of a sweet, spicy and deeply layered world of flavours.

image.alt: Tormore 2011 12 Years Spiced vanilla chai cake Cask 105.45 - The Scotch Malt Whisky Society

tasting_notes.nose: A lot is happening here. A mixture of juicy apple and ripe pear meets an unexpected, almost maritime freshness. The whole is accompanied by a perfumed note reminiscent of time-honoured wood polish or even a noble sandalwood scent. This is joined by caramel, a hint of chalk dust and a subtle stony sweetness. If you take your time, you will discover ever new facets - a multi-layered bouquet that balances between sweet and mineral.
tasting_notes.palate: Expecting a certain heaviness on the nose, the whisky is surprisingly light-footed and sweet. An intense sugariness spreads across the tongue, almost as if someone had placed a spoonful of caramelised malt sugar on the taste buds. This is accompanied by light-coloured fruit, a subtle maltiness and an unusual spiciness - a little like a rustic rye whisky, but with more elegance. Interestingly, the sweetness is reminiscent of those dark, brown vodkas that fascinate with their syrupy texture.
tasting_notes.finish: This is where the Tormore shows its edgier side. The finish is rather short, but brings with it a salty breeze - a final nod to the maritime notes from the nosing. Spicy accents, a hint of oak and a gentle peppery tingle finish pleasantly.

conclusion.marcel: This Tormore 2011 is a playful yet sophisticated dram that delights with its sweet spice and maritime freshness – not a whisky for cozy beginner evenings, but for connoisseurs eager to explore something new. The combination of vanilla, chai, and vibrant fruitiness creates a character that lifts the mood and leaves you curious for more.
conclusion.sascha: The Tormore 2011 presents itself as a complexly crafted Speyside whisky with a well-balanced sweetness and mineral nuances. Despite its robust alcohol strength, the dram remains elegant and structured, with a short, spicy finish that provides a crisp conclusion – an intriguing bourbon cask expression with technical finesse.

faq.question: How does the bourbon cask influence the aromas of the Tormore 2011 12 Years "Spiced Vanilla Chai Cake"?
faq.answer: The bourbon cask imparts the whisky with its sweet vanilla and caramel notes, along with a certain spiciness reminiscent of chai spices. At the same time, it supports the fruity aromas of apple and pear and contributes to the subtle maltiness in the flavor.

faq.question: Why does the Tormore 2011 taste surprisingly light and sweet on the palate despite its 66.8% ABV?
faq.answer: Although the alcohol content is high, the maturation in the cask and the complex interplay of aromas create a balanced profile. The sweetness of caramelized malt sugar and bright fruit notes soften the sharpness, making the whisky feel light-footed and pleasantly sweet.

faq.question: What role do the maritime notes play in the flavor and finish of this Speyside whisky?
faq.answer: The maritime notes, reminiscent of salty sea breezes, bring an interesting freshness and minerality to the aroma and finish. They contrast with the sweet and spicy components, giving the whisky a lively, edgy character that adds extra complexity.

item.name: Auchroisk 12 Years (2010/2023) - Sansibar
item.description: The Auchroisk 2010/2023, matured for 12 years and bottled by the independent bottler Sansibar, is a single malt whisky from Speyside that demands attention. With a strong alcohol content of 60.80 % ABV, it brings a strong presence to the glass, but will not suit all tastes.

tasting_notes.nose: The flavour of this whisky initially offers a pleasant sweetness with hints of ripe fruit and vanilla. This light, tantalising note is complemented by creamy milk chocolate, which promises a good start. However, less typical elements then come into play: a slightly grassy hay note and a hint of yoghurt add interesting facets, but are not entirely harmonious. Another point that polarises: a subtle spiciness reminiscent of paprika. Whether this is perceived as positive or irritating is probably in the eye (or rather the nose) of the beholder.
tasting_notes.palate: The first sip immediately reveals the high alcohol content. The whisky is strong, almost sharp, and the flavours initially struggle to develop. Vanilla and a certain fruitiness are noticeable, but they do not really manage to balance out the intensity of the alcohol. A dry impression, accompanied by a hint of chocolate, provides a certain depth. Nevertheless, the overall picture remains rather unbalanced - the individual components seem to stand next to each other instead of harmonising with each other.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish of the Auchroisk is characterised by distinct oak, which is strong and slightly bitter.
