There is a delicate fruity note reminiscent of redcurrants, but it remains in the background and is not very pronounced. Milk chocolate is also present here, albeit rather discreetly.

conclusion.marcel: With the Auchroisk 2010/2023, you experience a rough but honest malt that primarily delivers drinking pleasure through its sweetness and chocolate notes – even though the high alcohol can sometimes be a bit much. Not a masterpiece, but a whisky that shows character and piques curiosity to perhaps discover even more with the next dram.
conclusion.sascha: This Auchroisk impresses with its powerful alcohol structure and interesting aromatic facets, yet suffers from a lack of balance and a somewhat fragmented flavor profile. For lovers of intense, unrefined Speyside whiskies, it offers an exciting, if not entirely harmonious, tasting experience.

faq.question: How does the high alcohol content of 60.8% affect the taste of the Auchroisk 2010/2023?
faq.answer: The 60.8% alcohol content delivers a strong, almost sharp presence on the palate that initially dominates the flavors. As a result, the sweet and fruity notes feel less balanced, and the whisky may benefit from a splash of water to better reveal its aromas.

faq.question: What unusual aromas can be detected in the Auchroisk 2010/2023?
faq.answer: Alongside typical notes like vanilla, ripe fruits, and milk chocolate, the whisky also displays less common aromas such as grassy hay tones, yogurt, and a subtle paprika spice, adding an interesting but not always harmonious facet to the flavor complexity.

faq.question: What makes the finish of the Auchroisk 2010/2023 distinctive?
faq.answer: The finish is characterized by a pronounced oak bitterness, accompanied by restrained blackcurrant fruitiness and a delicate milk chocolate note, which lend depth to the finish even though the fruit elements remain more in the background.

item.name: HeiligenBergFeld 3 Years (2020/2023) PX Cask
item.description: Located in the picturesque Five-Lakes region of the Starnberg district, the HeiligenBergFeld Distillery was established in 2017 and has since specialized in single cask bottlings. The HeiligenBergFeld 2020/2023, bottled at 57.9% ABV, matured for three years in a Pedro-Ximénez sherry cask, offering a glimpse into the burgeoning craftsmanship of this Bavarian distillery.

tasting_notes.nose: The initial impression is characterized by a mild and rounded aroma. Notes of apricot, vanilla, and apple blend harmoniously, complemented by a hint of grassiness and walnut, rounding out the bouquet.
tasting_notes.palate: On the palate, the whisky opens with the freshness of young strawberries, followed by gentle red fruits and aged raisins. A slightly winey character intertwines with caramel and star anise notes, adding complexity.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is lightly woody with intense ginger spiciness and a lingering raspberry brandy essence.

conclusion.marcel: The HeiligenBergFeld 2020/2023 brings a lot of drinking pleasure to the glass with its lively fruitiness and spicy ginger heat, even though its still-young maturation time denies it a bit more depth. For fans of German new-school whiskies, it’s a charming introduction with potential that leaves you wanting more.
conclusion.sascha: Structurally, the HeiligenBergFeld 2020/2023 presents itself clearly and fresh, but the balance between fruit notes and woody spice could become even more harmonious with longer cask maturation. Overall, a solid yet still developing example of modern German single casks.

faq.question: How does the Pedro Ximénez sherry cask influence the flavor of the HeiligenBergFeld 2020/2023?
faq.answer: The Pedro Ximénez sherry cask imparts sweet and fruity notes such as apricot, dried raisins, and a wine-like complexity. The maturation also contributes caramel nuances and a gentle woodiness on the finish.

faq.question: Why is the HeiligenBergFeld 2020/2023 already so complex despite its young age of only three years?
faq.answer: The high cask strength of 57.9% ABV and maturation in the intense Pedro Ximénez sherry cask promote rapid extraction of aromas. This allows for a multi-layered tasting experience despite the short aging period.

faq.question: What distinguishes the HeiligenBergFeld Distillery?
faq.answer: Founded in 2017 in the Fünfseenland near Starnberg, the distillery focuses on single cask bottlings. It emphasizes small batches and experiments with various cask types to create unique character profiles.

item.name: Craigellachie (2013/2024) - Malts of Scotland
item.description: Malts of Scotland is known for its bold and characterful releases, and this one is no exception: a Craigellachie at 61.2% ABV, straight from the cask, unfiltered and untouched. After 11 years in the cask, this Speyside whisky has turned into a true sherry bomb.

tasting_notes.nose: Right from the start, this whisky delivers a whirlwind of aromas. Plums, figs, and dates create an intense sweetness, while morello cherries add a darker fruit note. There are also hints of leather, tobacco, and something reminiscent of a long-heated grain pillow – warm, spicy, and slightly nutty.
tasting_notes.palate: The first sip is a full-on sherry explosion. Sweet, fruity flavors dominate the tip of the tongue before transitioning into old leather sofas, Cognac-like notes, and tobacco leaves. The texture is creamy, almost oily, yet it quickly dries out. Aged oak brings a strong spiciness, while slightly burnt notes add a smoky-bitter accent. Somewhere in between, rust and machine oil play a subordinate role.
tasting_notes.finish: Long and intense. The oak spice builds up further, but the sweetness remains – this time more like heather honey and caramel. The whisky fades out with dry, spicy oak, leaving a lingering warmth.

conclusion.marcel: This Craigellachie 2013/2024 is like a wild rollercoaster ride for the senses – untamed, full-bodied, and packed with plenty of rough edges. If you’re into powerful sherry bombs, you’ll have a blast with every nose and every sip. Elegance is nowhere to be found here, but that’s exactly what gives it its charm!
conclusion.sascha: With its robust structure and intense sherry notes, this Craigellachie presents itself as a technically impressive cask strength release that skillfully balances sweet fruit and austere oak. The complex spice and long finish make it an exciting example of a bold Speyside sherry, even if the edgy dryness might not be to everyone’s taste.

faq.question: Why does the Craigellachie 2013/2024 from Malts of Scotland have such a high alcohol content of 61.2%?
faq.answer: This Craigellachie was bottled at cask strength, meaning it was filled directly from the barrel without dilution. This preserves the whisky’s full intensity and complexity, which is especially noticeable at 61.2% ABV.

faq.question: How does the eleven-year cask maturation influence the flavor of this Craigellachie?
faq.answer: The eleven years in sherry casks impart a pronounced sherry character with sweet aromas like plums, figs, and dates. At the same time, a spicy oakiness develops with notes of leather, tobacco, and a subtle smoky undertone.

faq.question: What does it mean that the whisky was bottled "without filtration," and how does this affect the drinking experience?
faq.answer: Bottling without chill filtration means no flavor compounds were removed before bottling. This keeps the whisky more intense in flavor and retains a fuller texture, resulting in a creamy, almost oily mouthfeel.

item.name: Goa Indian Whisky Ratafia Champenois Cask T-073
item.description: Indian whisky? This is no longer an insider tip. But when a whisky from Goa first matures for four years in bourbon barrels under tropical heat and then rests for another two years in a fine Ratafia Champenois barrel in France, things get exciting!

image.alt: Goa Indian Whisky Ratafia Champenois Cask T-073

tasting_notes.nose: You'll notice it from the very first sniff: This is not about standard notes! A slightly musty red fruit note starts things off - a bit like dried cherries stored in an old wooden box. But then exciting contrasts come into play: fresh leather mixes with brown sugar, joined by a clear orange note. A fragrance that is both exotic and familiar at the same time.
tasting_notes.palate: Velvety and flattering! This whisky is a real charmer - sweet but not sticky, fruity but not overloaded. Vanilla and ripe fruit dance on the tongue, while a hint of leather provides an exciting depth. The sweetness is almost reminiscent of sugar syrup, but thanks to the fine spice, everything remains beautifully balanced.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish of the Goa Indian Whisky has a gentle, almost creamy flavour with a hint of strawberries. Woody nuances ensure a successful finish - soft, but with enough character.

conclusion.marcel: This whisky is a true delight for anyone who enjoys sweet and velvety flavors without slipping into dullness. The unusual combination of tropical maturation and French Ratafia cask aging gives it a charming depth that’s a lot of fun – perfect for relaxed evenings with friends.
conclusion.sascha: A well-balanced whisky that impresses with its harmonious structure, balancing fruit, sweetness, and subtle spice. The maturation in both bourbon and Ratafia Champenois casks creates an intriguing interplay of aromas, though the finish could occasionally benefit from a bit more precision.

faq.question: How does the tropical maturation in Goa influence the whisky’s flavor?
faq.answer: The tropical heat in Goa accelerates maturation in the bourbon casks and intensifies the aromas. This results in a whisky with deeper fruitiness and spice, setting it apart from whiskies matured in cooler climates.

faq.question: What makes the finishing in the Ratafia Champenois cask special?
faq.answer: The two-year finishing period in French Ratafia Champenois casks adds extra complexity to the whisky. The cask imparts sweet, fruity, and lightly spicy notes, giving the whisky an unusual depth and elegance.

faq.question: How does this whisky differ from classic Indian whiskies?
faq.answer: Compared to classic Indian whiskies, this one stands out due to its combination of tropical primary maturation and French cask finishing. This blend delivers a balanced profile with exotic fruit aromas, fine spice, and a velvety texture.

item.name: Lagavulin 11 Years (2024) Offerman 4th Edition
item.description: When it comes to Islay whiskies, there are two types of people: Those who love the intense smoke, and those who wonder if their glass has been matured in a bonfire. The Lagavulin 2024 11 Years Offerman 4th Edition is aimed at the former - those who see the scent of cold smoke and salty sea breeze as an invitation rather than a warning. This fourth collaboration between Nick Offerman and Lagavulin promises a typical Islay malt with a touch of originality.

tasting_notes.nose: Raise your glass and you are immediately transported into a world of cold smoke and gentle sea spray. There is a slight hint of iodine, just as you would expect from a Lagavulin. But there is more: a gentle vanilla sweetness provides balance, accompanied by subtle citrus accents and an almost floral freshness. A hint of menthol-like ice candy flashes up and brings a cool, slightly minty note into play. The flavour is unmistakably Islay, but with a little Offerman twist.
tasting_notes.palate: The smoke remains present on the tongue, but it does not dominate the entire experience. Instead, the whisky opens with a pleasant fruitiness - pear and apple provide a subtle sweetness that elegantly complements the smoky foundation. The texture is soft, almost creamy, with a slightly ashy undertone reminiscent of cooled charcoal. Lagavulin shows a somewhat more accessible side here, without losing its Islay character.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is long, voluminous and expressive. Oaky notes prevail, accompanied by a gentle peppery flavour - white pepper briefly tickles the palate before a hint of icing sugar joins in and rounds off the finish with a mild sweetness. It is a classic Islay finish, but without the brutal force that some younger bottlings bring.

conclusion.marcel: This Lagavulin 2024 Offerman is like a familiar friend around the campfire – smoky, warm, and pleasantly fruity, with the joy of drinking clearly taking center stage. It doesn’t bring any big surprises, but that’s exactly what makes it the perfect companion for cozy evenings when you just want to savor the moment.
conclusion.sascha: The Lagavulin 2024 11 Year Offerman impresses with its balanced interplay of smoke, fruitiness, and subtle cask influences that give it a solid structure. Despite its classic Islay profile, it lacks that certain something that would set it apart from other releases and make it a true character whisky.

faq.question: How does the Lagavulin 2024 11 Year Offerman 4th Edition differ from other Lagavulin releases?
faq.answer: This fourth edition of the collaboration with Nick Offerman showcases a classic Islay character with intense smoke and a salty sea breeze, complemented by a balanced vanilla and citrus note as well as a light menthol freshness. Compared to other Lagavulin releases, it is somewhat more approachable and creamier in taste, without losing the signature smoke.

faq.question: Why is the Lagavulin 2024 Offerman Edition bottled at 46% ABV and not higher like some Islay whiskies?
faq.answer: At 46% alcohol by volume, this Lagavulin strikes a good balance between intensity and drinkability. The slightly lower strength compared to some cask strength expressions allows for a softer texture and lets the complex aromas of fruit, vanilla, and smoke come through harmoniously without being overpowering.

faq.question: Which flavor notes should be particularly highlighted in this Lagavulin?
faq.answer: The Lagavulin 2024 Offerman Edition stands out with a combination of cold smoke, salty sea air, and a delicate iodine note. The aroma profile is rounded out by vanilla sweetness, citrus fruits, floral freshness, and a minty menthol touch. On the palate, fruity hints of pear and apple provide balance, while the long finish is marked by oak and white pepper.

item.name: St. Kilian Grave Digger Erdfassreifung Exhumation – Chapter II – Peated
item.description: Exhumation – Chapter II by St. Kilian is a homage to the mystical side of metal. This four-years German single malt was initially aged in fresh Palatinate oak casks, followed by two more years in small 30-liter casks – including ex-rum, ex-bourbon, and fresh oak casks – underground in the Odenwald. The result is an intense whisky characterized by fruity, spicy, and earthy aromas, along with a hint of dark peat smoke.

image.alt: St Kilian Grave Digger Erdfassreifung Exhumation – Chapter II – Peated

tasting_notes.nose: Surprisingly little peat opens the nose, instead earthy tones, fresh wood, and a certain fruitiness dominate. An intense, almost tangible freshness permeates the aroma, sparking curiosity for the first sip.
tasting_notes.palate: The whisky presents itself oily and spicy on the palate, accompanied by notes of beetroot, vanilla, and caramel. A subtle smoky note resonates, while earthy, root-like elements round off the impression. The dryness in the finish adds extra structure and depth.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is long and voluminous. Earthy, slightly bitter notes of root vegetables come to the forefront, while smoky and steamy hints linger. The balance between sweetness, spiciness, and bitterness is impressive.

conclusion.marcel: Those who embrace St. Kilian’s Exhumation – Chapter II embark on a wild, earthy journey with surprisingly little smoke, a dram that will especially delight lovers of unusual flavors. Its complexity and intense mouthfeel make it a true highlight for anyone who sees whisky as an adventure. Not exactly an entry-level dram, but definitely a whisky with a strong character in the glass.
conclusion.sascha: Exhumation – Chapter II impresses with its clear structure and balanced interplay of fruity, spicy, and earthy notes, complemented by a subtle smoky undertone. The complex cask maturation is reflected in its depth and long-lasting finish, distinguished by dryness and root vegetable nuances. A technically impressive German single malt with solid aging potential.

faq.question: How does the underground maturation in the Odenwald influence the flavor of this whisky?
faq.answer: The underground maturation in small 30-liter casks provides an especially consistent and cool aging environment. This allows earthy and spicy notes to develop more intensely while preserving the fruitiness. The unique surroundings also contribute to the fine integration of smoke and wood aromas.

faq.question: Why is the whisky only subtly smoky in taste despite being labeled as 'Peated'?
faq.answer: Although this St. Kilian whisky is matured with peat smoke, the smoky flavor does not dominate. The combination of fresh oak maturation and the earthy, fruity aromas softens the smoke note, which acts more as a subtle, supportive element rather than a prominent feature.

faq.question: What role do the different cask types play in the flavor development of this whisky?
faq.answer: Maturation in fresh Palatinate oak casks lays the foundation with wood and vanilla notes. Subsequent aging in small ex-rum, ex-bourbon, and fresh oak casks introduces complex aromas of caramel, spice, and fruitiness. The variety of casks ensures a multi-layered balance of sweetness, spice, and earthy tones.

item.name: Port Charlotte 14 Years (2023) - Brühler Whiskyhaus
item.description: The A Dream of Scotland series by Brühler Whiskyhaus stands for independent bottlings with distinctive character. This 14-years Port Charlotte from the Bruichladdich distillery, bottled at a robust cask strength, promises an intense tasting experience rooted in Islay's unique spirit. Is this whisky another highlight of the series?

tasting_notes.nose: The first sniff takes you on a journey: mildly smoky notes of BBQ and ham blend with red fruits, including blackcurrants, adding a fresh and fruity touch. Hints of vanilla and caramel provide a pleasant sweetness, harmoniously rounding off the bouquet.
tasting_notes.palate: On the palate, the 14-years Port Charlotte reveals its full-bodied complexity. The subtle smoke remains present, accompanied by light earthy tones and a hint of maritime breeze, reminiscent of salty waves. The BBQ sauce and ham notes from the nose return, adding memorable depth to the flavor.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is long and layered. Spicy oak notes dominate at first, gradually giving way to a light fruity sweetness, providing a well-rounded conclusion.

conclusion.marcel: This Port Charlotte is like a cheerful evening around the campfire: spicy, smoky, and with a pleasant sweetness that never becomes overpowering. For anyone who enjoys character and a touch of saltiness, this is a genuine taste experience. Simply a whisky that lifts your spirits and leaves you wanting more!
conclusion.sascha: The precise balance between smoke, salty sea notes, and fruity sweetness makes this Port Charlotte an analytically convincing bottling. The complex structure and long-lasting, spicy finish demonstrate a successful maturation at 56.2% ABV. A technically very well-balanced Islay whisky that underscores the quality standards of independent bottlings.

faq.question: How does the Port Charlotte 2023 14 Year differ from other Islay whiskies?
faq.answer: The Port Charlotte 2023 14 Year stands out through its combination of robust cask strength (56.2% ABV) and a balanced interplay of smoky BBQ notes with fruity blackcurrant aromas. The subtle sea breeze and complex spiciness distinguish it from typical Islay whiskies, which are often more heavily smoky or peaty.

faq.question: Why is the cask strength important for the tasting experience of the Port Charlotte 14 Year?
faq.answer: The cask strength of 56.2% delivers a more intense and full-bodied tasting experience since the whisky is undiluted. This keeps the aromas of smoke, fruit, and spice especially vivid and complex, highlighting the depth and multi-layered character of this whisky.

faq.question: How does the 14-year maturation influence the character of this Port Charlotte whisky?
faq.answer: The 14-year maturation imparts a harmonious balance between smoky, fruity, and spicy notes. Time in the cask develops vanilla and caramel aromas as well as spicy oak tones, which shape the complex and long-lasting finish.

item.name: Glenturret Triple Wood (2024)
item.description: Glenturret is one of Scotland’s oldest distilleries, known for its traditionally crafted whisky. With the Triple Wood Release 2024, the distillery relies on a combination of different cask types to create an interesting complexity. Without an age statement but bottled at 44% ABV, this whisky promises a balanced mix of fruit, spice, and oak notes.

tasting_notes.nose: The first impression is pleasant and layered. Malty notes form the foundation, while dark chocolate and fruity hints of apple and pear add variety. Vanilla subtly lingers in the background, providing a creamy touch. The nose is not overly intense but present and inviting.
tasting_notes.palate: The initial sip is distinctly malty before a refreshing citrus note takes over. Cinnamon brings a certain spiciness that blends with toffee and a mix of light and dark fruits. The “wild fruit mix” is particularly intriguing, difficult to pinpoint but adding a dynamic character to the whisky. A spicy oak note provides structure, and the texture has a slight tingle – a pleasant interplay between sweetness and spice.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is marked by solid oak notes, yet remains pleasantly rounded due to soft tannins. A light ginger kick adds a fresh spiciness, while the aftertaste fades slowly with a mix of wood and warm spices.

conclusion.marcel: Here you find a whisky that’s simply fun with its hint of cinnamon and wild fruit mix – not a top-tier delight, but a likeable companion for cozy evenings. If you appreciate character and drinkability, this one’s definitely worth reaching for.
conclusion.sascha: The Glenturret Triple Wood impresses with a well-balanced structure between fruit, sweetness, and spicy oak, even if it doesn’t deliver any big surprises. Its texture and balanced finish make it a solid Highland whisky with an appealing cask combination.

faq.question: What types of casks were used for the Glenturret Triple Wood Release 2024 and how do they influence the flavor?
faq.answer: The Glenturret Triple Wood Release 2024 matured in three different types of casks, each contributing distinct aromas. The combination of oak barrels provides spicy and woody notes, while the use of former sherry or bourbon casks brings out fruity and vanilla nuances. This blend of casks gives the whisky its layered structure with a balance of fruitiness, spice, and wood.

faq.question: Why does the Glenturret Triple Wood Release 2024 have no age statement and how does this affect its quality?
faq.answer: The whisky is offered without an age statement (NAS), meaning no minimum age is indicated on the bottle. This allows the master distillers to blend different vintages and maturation periods to achieve a balanced flavor profile. The quality is not compromised; rather, this approach enables the creation of a complex and harmonious whisky that focuses on character rather than age.

faq.question: How does the Glenturret Triple Wood Release 2024 differ from other Highland whiskies?
faq.answer: Compared to many Highland whiskies, the Glenturret Triple Wood Release 2024 places strong emphasis on the combination of different cask types, giving it a distinctive complexity. Additionally, at 44% ABV, it’s slightly stronger, which intensifies the aromas. Its profile is characterized by a balanced mix of malty base notes, fruity accents, and spicy wood tones, setting it apart from the more peaty or overly sweet Highland whiskies.

item.name: Johnnie Walker 18 Years
item.description: Previously known as "Platinum," the Johnnie Walker 18 Years now carries only its age statement. The content remains unchanged: a premium-positioned blended Scotch whisky designed for broad appeal.

tasting_notes.nose: On the nose, the whisky is accessible and well-balanced. Caramel and vanilla provide a sweet foundation, while a bright fruit basket of apple, pear, and peach adds freshness. A subtle grain note of oats and malt brings depth, complemented by a touch of candied orange peel. A faint wisp of smoke lingers in the background, giving the blend a hint of extra character.
tasting_notes.palate: On the palate, the Johnnie Walker 18 Years initially feels somewhat thin but remains smooth and silky. The flavors are well-balanced: orange marmalade brings fruity sweetness, accompanied by dark fruits and peach. Surprisingly, the malty note is quite present for a blend. The smoke stays subtle, with an earthy, slightly ashy touch that adds a bit of complexity.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is medium in length, marked by pleasant bitter orange notes. A touch of wood emerges without becoming overpowering. The smoke provides a hint of volume but remains in the background.

conclusion.marcel: A smooth, pleasant blend that, with its gentle sweetness and subtle smoke, feels almost like a good friend – uncomplicated and likable, but without much depth. Anyone looking for a relaxed whisky for cozy moments will surely be happy here. For true characterful personalities, however, there are more exciting options.
conclusion.sascha: The Johnnie Walker 18 Year offers a solid balance between sweetness, fruitiness, and a subtle smoky note, yet it remains somewhat one-dimensional and slightly thin on the palate. The structure is clear, with a medium-length finish featuring a delicate bitter orange, but real complexity or depth is lacking. A reliable everyday blend that impresses more with its approachability than with character.

faq.question: How does the Johnnie Walker 18 Year differ from other Johnnie Walker expressions?
faq.answer: The Johnnie Walker 18 Year is a blended Scotch whisky that offers a balanced complexity thanks to a longer maturation period than many other variants. It is characterized by a combination of sweet caramel and vanilla notes, fruity accents, and a subtle smoke, making it more elegant and layered than younger blends.

faq.question: Why does the Johnnie Walker 18 Year taste relatively light and smooth despite its aging?
faq.answer: Although the whisky has matured for 18 years, it has been crafted to remain accessible and soft. The light texture results from a harmonious balance of malty and fruity aromas, while the smoke and bitter orange add a subtle complexity without overpowering the flavor.

faq.question: Is the Johnnie Walker 18 Year suitable for cocktails, or should it be enjoyed neat?
faq.answer: Due to its fine aromas and balanced character, the Johnnie Walker 18 Year is especially well-suited for neat drinking or with a little water to open up its nuances. While it can be used in cocktails, many of its subtle flavor notes are lost in the process, so it is generally appreciated more as a quality sipping whisky.

item.name: Nc'Nean Huntress Orchard Cobbler (2019/2024)
item.description: Nc'Nean makes it exciting again! With the Huntress series, the innovative Highland distillery is exploring bold new paths in the world of whisky. The Huntress 2019/2024 Orchard Cobbler relies on a special fermentation process: Long Maturation Recipe, no mash rest, only one type of yeast - and all in a blend of 66% STR casks, bourbon and a touch of sherry (3%, to be precise - so just enough to feel important).

tasting_notes.nose: The first impression? Vanilla meets mint - as if someone had thrown a bourbon into a herb garden. Then apple and peach come into play, accompanied by a fine acidity that lends freshness to the whole. Red grapes and honey round off the flavour, while a slightly alcoholic note lurks in the background (a little cheeky, but charming). Anyone who likes sweet, fruity yet invigorating whiskies will immediately find their nose stuck in the glass.
tasting_notes.palate: The first sip? Stone fruit at full volume! Peach, apricot and a little nectarine - as if someone had distilled fruit salad in liquid form. This is joined by a load of vanilla, freshly picked herbs and a hint of grapefruit, which provides a tangy freshness. Malty? And how! Anyone familiar with Nc'Nean's Organic Flagship will immediately feel at home here - just in an even rounder, fuller-flavoured version.
tasting_notes.finish: Where other whiskies end with strong oak, the Huntress prefers to remain fresh, fruity and berry-flavoured. The tannins remain in the background while the fruitiness gently fades away. Anyone who has ever tasted Nc'Nean's standard bottling will notice the difference - the Orchard Cobbler is softer and smoother without losing its characteristic freshness.

conclusion.marcel: A lively delight that, with its fruity lightness, really lifts the spirits – perfect for anyone not looking for a heavy hitter, but a whisky with charm and character. Nc’nean shows here just how much fun experimentation can be without ever becoming dull.
conclusion.sascha: The Huntress 2019/2024 impresses with a precise balance of fruit notes and subtle wood spice that skillfully plays around its youthful character. The structured development of aromas and the lasting finish make it an intriguing, expertly crafted reinterpretation in the realm of innovative Highland whiskies.

faq.question: What does the Long Maturation Recipe mean for Nc'Nean Huntress Orchard Cobbler 2019/2024?
faq.answer: The Long Maturation Recipe describes a special fermentation process where no mash rest is used and only a single yeast strain is employed. This leads to a longer and more controlled maturation that supports the whisky’s fruity and fresh character.

faq.question: How does the cask mix influence the taste of the Huntress Orchard Cobbler?
faq.answer: The whisky matures in a blend of 66% STR casks (Straw Wine Casks), bourbon barrels, and a small 3% portion of sherry casks. This combination creates a balanced profile of fruity, vanilla, and lightly spicy notes, with the sherry casks adding only a subtle depth.

faq.question: Why does Nc'Nean Huntress Orchard Cobbler have no age statement (NAS), and how does this affect the whisky?
faq.answer: Nc'Nean deliberately omits an age statement on this whisky to retain maximum flexibility in cask selection and maturation time. The result is a fresh, fruit-forward whisky with a soft, smooth profile that benefits not from long aging but from innovative production methods.

item.name: The Balvenie 14 Years (2009) A Collection of Curious Casks
item.description: The ‘A Collection of Curious Casks’ series is proof of the Balvenie Distillery's innovative strength and craftsmanship. It offers whisky enthusiasts the opportunity to discover unusual and limited bottlings that are off the beaten track. We take a closer look at the 14-years Balvenie from the American Bourbon barrel.

image.alt: The Balvenie 2009 14 Years A Collection of Curious Casks

tasting_notes.nose: The first sniff reveals a surprisingly dirty, maritime character, reminiscent of salty sea air and damp stones. However, clear honey and vanilla tones quickly join in, giving the whisky a sweet base. Light citrus peel and a hint of star anise round off the bouquet and give it an exotic flavour.
tasting_notes.palate: On the tongue, theSingle Malt unfolds as a light yet complex whisky. The sweetness of vanilla dominates at first, followed by a citrusy freshness reminiscent of lime and grapefruit. A chalky minerality pervades the flavour and gives it structure. Notes of light-coloured fallen fruit meadow and fresh grass resonate in the background, lending the whisky a summery lightness.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is long and pleasant, with a distinct oaky spiciness accompanied by earthy and loamy nuances. The maritime, almost dirty character returns and leaves a lasting impression.

conclusion.marcel: With its fruity lightness and maritime notes, the Balvenie 2009 is a true delight for relaxed evenings – a whisky that sparks curiosity and is simply fun to enjoy. It surprises with character and a pleasant sweetness that invites you back for more without overwhelming the palate.
conclusion.sascha: The Balvenie 2009 14 Years presents itself as a well-balanced single malt with complex minerality and a successful harmony between sweet vanilla notes and maritime, earthy accents. Its long, spicy finish underscores the technical finesse of this Speyside bottling.

faq.question: What makes The Balvenie’s 'A Collection of Curious Casks' series special?
faq.answer: The series is distinguished by experimental and limited releases that employ unusual cask types and maturation processes to explore new flavor profiles beyond the classic Balvenie whiskies.

faq.question: How does the American bourbon barrel influence the flavor of this 14-years Balvenie?
faq.answer: Maturation in American bourbon barrels imparts a sweet vanilla note to the whisky, supported by citrus fruits and a subtle spiciness, while the cask structure also promotes mineral and maritime-salty accents.

faq.question: What sensory characteristics set this Balvenie 2009 14 Years apart from other Speyside whiskies?
faq.answer: In addition to the typical Speyside sweetness, this whisky exhibits an unusually maritime, almost dirty-mineral character, combined with exotic spices like star anise and a chalky minerality that give it a distinctive complexity.

item.name: Kilchoman Batch Strength (2024)
item.description: The Kilchoman Batch Strength 2024 is a whisky for real Islay fans. With a full 57.0 % ABV and no age statement, the Kilchoman Distillery focuses on pure intensity here. Those who love peaty smoke, maritime notes and honest cask strength will get their money's worth - but does this whisky also have enough character to stand out from the competition?

tasting_notes.nose: From the very first nosing, the aroma of cold ashes rises to the nose, as if the campfire had just stopped smouldering. There is also a subtle hint of iodine, reminiscent of a fresh sea breeze. The fruity accents of green apple and lemon balm, which blend gently into the smoke, are surprising. A subtle vanilla note rounds off the flavour and gives it a slight, subliminal sweetness.
tasting_notes.palate: The whisky is oily and warming on the tongue. The ash dominance from the nose continues, paired with a dry, chalky note. This is joined by a hint of unripe peach, which adds a subtle freshness, while a fine saltiness is reminiscent of sea spray. The flavours are powerful, direct and uncompromising - typically Islay.
tasting_notes.finish: Kilchoman remains true to itself in the finish: voluminous smoke dominates, accompanied by a light oak note that gives the whisky some depth. A mineral component reminiscent of pebbles resonates and reinforces the earthy character. The intensity lasts a long time without changing significantly.

conclusion.marcel: This Kilchoman Batch Strength 2024 delivers exactly what you expect from a robust Islay: pure smoke, a generous helping of drinking pleasure, and a straightforward, honest statement. While it lacks the exciting depth to become an absolute favorite, it’s definitely a solid companion for fans of rustic peat drams on a campfire evening.
conclusion.sascha: The Kilchoman Batch Strength 2024 impresses with its powerful structure and precise balance between smoky, maritime, and fruity notes, yet its flavor profile remains comparatively linear. If you’re looking for a technically well-balanced Islay whisky with a high proof, this is a solid example, though it leaves some room for more complexity and cask nuances.

faq.question: What does "Batch Strength" mean in Kilchoman Batch Strength 2024?
faq.answer: "Batch Strength" means that the whisky is bottled at cask strength, without dilution to a lower alcohol level. For the Kilchoman Batch Strength 2024, this is a hefty 57.0% ABV, which ensures an intense aroma and a powerful flavor.

faq.question: Why does Kilchoman Batch Strength 2024 have no age statement (No Age Statement)?
faq.answer: Kilchoman deliberately omits an age statement on this whisky to focus on the taste and quality. The maturation can consist of various casks and age ranges, which together create the whisky’s characteristic style and intensity.

faq.question: Which distinctive aromas make Kilchoman Batch Strength 2024 typical for Islay?
faq.answer: Typical for Islay in the Kilchoman Batch Strength 2024 are primarily the peaty-smoky character, the maritime notes with iodine and salty sea breeze, as well as the mineral hints on the finish. These are complemented by fruity and sweet nuances that add complexity and balance to the whisky.

item.name: Compass Box Ultramarine
item.description: Sometimes a whisky stands out not just because of its flavours, but because of the story it tells. Compass Box Ultramarine is just such a whisky - a journey through time and flavour, where old cask maturation meets modern blending techniques. Inspired by ancient flavours, this whisky has a depth that captivates connoisseurs. The basis of this blend was matured once again before the final marriage, with a sherry butt aged for more than 30 years as the centrepiece and a formative proportion of 18.7% Caol Ila.

tasting_notes.nose: A complex aroma unfolds from the very first nosing: a light, ashy peat smoke rises, accompanied by a fresh citrus note reminiscent of lemon and tart grapefruit. But it doesn't stop at fruit and smoke - a hint of leather and herbs combine with hints of cereals and grassy notes that are almost reminiscent of a dense coniferous forest. A flash of sour apple and unripe plum resonate before creamy caramel and fine herbs round off the aroma.
tasting_notes.palate: The complexity continues on the palate. The whisky is oily and intense, with a full, almost creamy mouthfeel. Dried fruit dominates the opening before apple and pear come through. Roasted almonds provide a subtle nuttiness, while menthol provides a cool contrast. And then it's there again - the cold smoke, which is reminiscent of an extinguished ember and perfectly rounds off the symphony of flavours.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is nothing less than spectacular. The blend remains present, with a voluminous depth that is reflected in red fruit flavours, sweet lemon and fine smoke. The wood is perfectly integrated, never over-present, but delicate and elegant.

conclusion.marcel: Ultramarine is like an exciting film for the senses – full of surprises that delight and leave you wanting more. The balance between smoky spice and fruity sweetness invites relaxed enjoyment without ever becoming dull. A blend that scores with character and charm – definitely a highlight for lovers with an adventurous spirit.
conclusion.sascha: Compass Box Ultramarine impresses with its precise structure and balanced complexity, harmoniously combining smoke, sherry, and citrus notes. The perfect integration of the 30-years sherry butts and Caol Ila component creates a profound, multi-layered blend with a long-lasting finish. A technically impressive example of modern blending artistry with clear balance and subtle cask maturation.

faq.question: Why is Compass Box Ultramarine not considered a classic single malt, even though it consists of Scottish whiskies?
faq.answer: Compass Box Ultramarine is a blend that combines various whiskies, including a high proportion of 18.7% Caol Ila and a 30-plus-years sherry butt as its core. It is not labeled as a single malt because it is composed of multiple distillates from different distilleries.

faq.question: How does the finishing in a 30-plus-years sherry butt influence the taste of Ultramarine?
faq.answer: The finishing in the old sherry butt imparts a special depth and complexity to Ultramarine, with notes of dried fruits and sweet lemon shaping the aroma and finish, without losing the smoke or freshness.

faq.question: What role does the Caol Ila component play in this blend and how does it present itself sensorially?
faq.answer: The 18.7% Caol Ila portion brings the characteristic ashy peat smoke to the blend, noticeable both on the nose and palate as cold smoke and extinguished embers, supporting the aromatic balance between smoke, fruit, and herbs.

item.name: Compass Box Metropolis
item.description: The Compass Box Metropolis is intended to tell the story of urbanity. After 12 years pre-blended - married and returned to the cask - the whisky matured for a further 12 to 15 years in sherry casks. The ingredients are mainly from the Aberlour and Miltonduff distilleries, with a small proportion of Bowmore added for the industrial style.

tasting_notes.nose: At first sniff, you feel like you're in a market with the scent of apricots, butterscotch and freshly roasted coffee. A light sea breeze blows past. And then there's that hint of honey that caresses the nose. Coconut? Could also be coconut, perhaps an exotic stall at the market. In any case: a fragrance that arouses curiosity.
tasting_notes.palate: The first sip is like jumping from one skyscraper roof to the next - exciting and sweet! Caramel sweets and creamy fudge coat the tongue, and then comes this light smoky note, reminiscent of a campfire evening in a city park. Copper leaf and a pinch of salt provide the necessary spice, while figs and sultanas give a hint of the Orient. A firework of flavours!
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is long. Dried fruit and a hint of old oak accompany us on the way home, and there is a delicate hint of milk chocolate at the end.

conclusion.marcel: This Compass Box Metropolis brings true city life to the glass – lively, multifaceted, and with a smoky spice that makes you eager for the next sip. It’s not the gentlest dram, but that’s exactly what makes it an exciting companion for those who love whisky with character. An urban delight that takes the palate on a little adventure.
conclusion.sascha: The Metropolis displays a balanced complexity, where the harmonious interplay of sherry cask maturation and subtle smokiness is particularly convincing. The structured balance between sweetness, spice, and dried fruits delivers a long, layered finish, which, through precise cask selection, underlines its urban identity. A well-crafted blend with a clear architectural line.

faq.question: What role do the different distilleries play in the blend of Compass Box Metropolis?
faq.answer: Compass Box Metropolis mainly combines whiskies from Aberlour and Miltonduff, known for their fruity and malty characters. A small portion of Bowmore adds a light smoky note that emphasizes the whisky’s urban, industrial style.

faq.question: How does the finishing in sherry casks influence the taste of Compass Box Metropolis?
faq.answer: The additional maturation of 12 to 15 years in sherry casks imparts the whisky with complex notes of dried fruits like figs and raisins, along with a subtle sweetness and woody depth that shape the long, dry finish.

faq.question: Why is Compass Box Metropolis called a 'pre-blended' whisky and what does that mean for the drinking experience?
faq.answer: 'Pre-blended' means that the individual whiskies are married together before cask maturation. This allows the flavors to develop and mature harmoniously, resulting in a more complex and balanced aroma.

item.name: Cameronbridge 34 Years (1984/2019) - van Wees
item.description: Cameronbridge Distillery, one of Scotland's oldest grain distilleries, is renowned for its role in the history of Scotch whisky. This 34-years bottling from 1984, released by van Wees, demonstrates the transformative effects of decades of maturation in the Lowlands region. Be warned: This whisky challenges the senses and offers an extraordinary experience you won’t forget anytime soon.

tasting_notes.nose: The aroma is a conversation starter. At first, it evokes a gas station – a hint of industrial notes that catch you off guard. Beneath this layer lies a fruity core: overripe cherries, coconut, and a touch of exotic mango, nectarine, and peach. Cashew nuts and a sense of decayed wood add earthy undertones, lending complexity to the bouquet.
tasting_notes.palate: On the palate, this whisky is powerful and multifaceted. The 54.90% ABV makes its presence known without overwhelming. Spicy oak takes the lead, supported by deep dark red fruits in the background. Then it takes an unexpected turn: an extreme dryness reminiscent of chalk dominates the mouthfeel, while an outstandingly oily texture makes the whisky almost viscous. A hint of soapiness may polarize, but it adds an intriguing layer to the complexity.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish surprises with its discrepancy. While the flavors linger on the tongue, the throat remains almost untouched. Subtle notes of honey, hay, and vanilla provide a gentle softness, although the overall impression feels almost ethereal.

conclusion.marcel: A whisky that divides opinion and is great fun – with its unusual blend of industrial notes and exotic fruits, the Cameronbridge 1984 clearly stands out from the crowd. Its distinctive dryness and almost ethereal finish aren’t to everyone’s taste, but they make the character all the more intriguing. For anyone looking for a true whisky adventurer, this dram is a genuine hidden gem.
conclusion.sascha: The Cameronbridge 1984/2019 displays remarkable structural complexity, well supported by its powerful 54.9% ABV. Despite the intense aromas and layered texture, the finish is surprisingly lean, which somewhat limits the balance. Overall, it’s a demanding grain whisky that reveals its strengths primarily through aromatic diversity and its unusual dryness.

faq.question: Why does the Cameronbridge 1984/2019 have such an unusual combination of industrial notes and exotic fruits?
faq.answer: The atypical aroma profile results from the long maturation of 34 years in the Lowlands region and the grain whisky base of the Cameronbridge distillery. The industrial note recalls the original distillery environment, while the exotic fruit notes arise from complex maturation processes and cask interactions.

faq.question: How does the high alcohol content of 54.9% affect the taste and mouthfeel of this whisky?
faq.answer: The high alcohol content makes the Cameronbridge 1984/2019 powerful without overpowering. It enhances spicy oak aromas and provides an oily, almost viscous texture. At the same time, it creates a noticeable dryness on the palate, reminiscent of chalk, which underscores the whisky’s complexity.

faq.question: What does it mean that this whisky was released by van Wees as an independent bottler?
faq.answer: Van Wees is an independent bottler who purchases whiskies from various distilleries and bottles them independently. This allows them to offer rare and exceptional releases like the Cameronbridge 1984/2019 with special maturation and cask selection, which are often not available in the distillery’s regular range.

item.name: Nc'Nean Quiet Rebels Gordon (2009/2023)
item.description: Nc'Nean stands for modern, sustainable whisky production and dares to experiment with the ‘Quiet Rebels’ series. The ‘Gordon’ is a limited, seasonal bottling that consists of a special cask combination: 74 STR casks, 19% sherry casks and 7% French sweet wine casks.

tasting_notes.nose: The complexity of this whisky becomes apparent from the very first nosing. A seductive blend of coconut and vanilla meets fruity notes of plums, apricots and strawberries. These are joined by unripe pears and apples, which bring a slight acidity and lend freshness to the bouquet. Marzipan shimmers through in the background and provides a fine sweetness, while a subtle dryness rounds off the complex impression.
tasting_notes.palate: The first sip confirms the impression from the nose - the ‘Gordon’ is an intense, yet smooth whisky. An oily texture carries the vinous notes, which are characterised by peaches, blackberries and strawberries. Despite its strength, it remains mild and balanced. The fruity sweetness harmonises wonderfully with the spicy undertones of the casks and gives the whisky a pleasant depth. The combination of sherry and sweet wine casks creates an exciting balance between fruitiness and flavour.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is strong and present without being too dominant. The oak provides structure and length, while the fine berry notes continue to be noticeable. The whisky finishes subtly and delicately without losing any of its intensity.

conclusion.marcel: Nc'Nean succeeds with the Gordon in crafting a fruity and spicy whisky that’s a lot of fun and sits wonderfully in the glass thanks to its oily texture. A charming everyday companion for connoisseurs who aren’t afraid of experiments and enjoy exploring new flavor worlds – here, sustainability meets drinking pleasure.
conclusion.sascha: The Gordon showcases a well-balanced cask composition that harmoniously blends fruitiness and spice without letting either dominate. Despite the intense aromas, the structure remains coherent and the finish balanced, making it ideal for fans of complex yet approachable Highland whiskies.

faq.question: What makes the cask combination of the Nc'Nean Quiet Rebels Gordon 2009/2023 special?
faq.answer: The whisky matures in an unusual mix of 74% STR casks, 19% sherry casks, and 7% French sweet wine casks. This combination creates a complex balance of fruitiness, spice, and sweetness that defines the character of the whisky.

faq.question: How does the sustainable production influence the taste of the Nc'Nean Quiet Rebels Gordon?
faq.answer: Nc'Nean relies on modern, sustainable production methods that focus on natural processes and local raw materials. This supports a fresh, clear aromatic profile with emphasized fruitiness and ensures a pure, unadulterated taste.

faq.question: Why does the Nc'Nean Quiet Rebels Gordon 2009/2023 have no age statement on the label?
faq.answer: The whisky is offered as a 'No Age Statement' (NAS), meaning the age is not explicitly stated. This allows Nc'Nean to blend different vintages and cask types to achieve a balanced and complex flavor profile.

item.name: Bowmore 10 Years (2013/2024) - Douglas Laing
item.description: Die Islay-Brennerei Bowmore steht für rauchige Eleganz, oft kombiniert mit maritimen Noten und einer feinen Süße. In unabhängiger Abfüllung von Douglas Laing exklusiv für deinwhisky.de kommt dieser 10-jährige Single Malt mit kräftigen 58,4 % ABV ins Glas – ein Versprechen für Intensität und Tiefe.

tasting_notes.nose: The first impression is unconventional: there is a hint of musty shoebox and sweaty feet - unusual, but not unpleasant. After a few moments, the bouquet opens up: damp moss, a fresh sea breeze and delicate chalk notes are reminiscent of a stormy coast. The vanilla in the background provides a gentle rounding off and combines the rough Islay terrain with a subtle sweetness.
tasting_notes.palate: On the tongue, the Bowmore shows its full strength. A strong ash note dominates at first, before an almost sugary sweetness asserts itself - surprising and fascinating at the same time. Fresh vanilla and pear play with the oily mouthfeel and contribute to the pleasant balance. The smoke always remains present, but without masking the other flavours.
tasting_notes.finish: Long, voluminous and warming. The oak note typical of many Islay whiskies is held back here, instead the impression of light-coloured fruit and sugar remains. Despite its strength, the finish is comparatively fresh and light.

conclusion.marcel: A smoky adventure with plenty of punch and a sweet playfulness – you can practically feel the stormy Islay coast. This isn’t a mainstream whisky, but a characterful dram full of edges and quirks that excites and leaves you wanting more.
conclusion.sascha: Technically well-balanced between powerful smoke and surprising sweetness, the Bowmore 2013/2024 offers a structured complexity with a full-bodied finish. A successful example of an intense yet harmonious Islay whisky with a clearly defined profile.

faq.question: Why does the Bowmore 2013/2024 initially smell unusually like a musty shoebox and sweaty feet?
faq.answer: These unusual notes arise from complex maturation processes and the combination of peat smoke with natural wood and maritime aromas. They are typical for certain Islay whiskies and often dissipate quickly as the bouquet opens up.

faq.question: How does the high alcohol content of 58.4% affect the taste of this Bowmore?
faq.answer: The high alcohol content delivers an intense flavor development and a robust mouthfeel. It amplifies the smoky notes and sweetness but can also add a warming sharpness. It’s best enjoyed with a small splash of water to soften the balance.

faq.question: What role does the independent bottling by Douglas Laing play for this Bowmore Single Malt?
faq.answer: Douglas Laing selects casks and bottling times independently from the distillery, often resulting in unique, characterful expressions. For this Bowmore 2013/2024, that means a particularly intense cask strength and a depth of flavor that can differ from standard releases.

item.name: Glenfarclas (2012/2024) The Trinity Vintage Series
item.description: Ah, Glenfarclas! The distillery from Speyside, Scotland, has once again conjured up a whisky that is as charming as a Scottish sheepdog in a kilt. The Glenfarclas 2012/2024 The Trinity Vintage Series is a no-age-statement marvel with 46% ABV that doesn't adhere to age limits. But let's dive into this liquid gold and find out why this whisky is a real ‘haymaker’.

tasting_notes.nose: Vanilla custard, apple and a pinch of dried apricot join the crumble cake as if they were having a party in a malty orchard. There's also a hint of hay - yes, hay! Not exactly what you'd expect in a whisky, but hey, who needs conventions? This aroma is so inviting that you almost forget to drink the whisky.
tasting_notes.palate: The first sip is like a sugary-sweet kiss from the orchard. Apricot and hay dance on the tongue, while the soft oak snuggles up to your taste buds like a good friend. The texture is creamy and oily, as if the whisky had decided to bathe itself in rape honey. It's sweet, but not overpowering - like a compliment from someone who really means what they say.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is medium to long and leaves you with a smile. The oak makes its presence felt once again, accompanied by a light spiciness and a hint of fruitiness. The honey remains until the end.

conclusion.marcel: This Glenfarclas is like a sunny afternoon on a Scottish meadow – sweet, charming, and with just enough oak character to keep things interesting. A whisky that’s fun and tells stories without trying too hard. For anyone looking for enjoyment with a wink, this one’s a bullseye.
conclusion.sascha: The Glenfarclas 2012/2024 impresses with a well-balanced blend of sweet fruit notes and spicy oak that gives the whisky structure and depth. The complexity remains well-controlled without feeling overloaded, making it a solid representative of Speyside. A technically clean, balanced dram with a pleasant texture and a medium-length finish.

faq.question: What does No-Age-Statement (NAS) mean for the Glenfarclas 2012/2024 The Trinity Vintage Series?
faq.answer: No-Age-Statement (NAS) means that no specific age is indicated on the bottle. Glenfarclas uses casks from the 2012 vintage, bottled in 2024, without explicitly highlighting the age. This allows more flexibility in cask selection and influences the flavor on an individual basis.

faq.question: Why does the Glenfarclas 2012/2024 smell like hay, and is that typical for Speyside whiskies?
faq.answer: The hay aroma in the Glenfarclas 2012/2024 is unusual but not uncommon for certain cask maturations and vintages. It arises from the combination of malt, oak maturation, and climatic conditions. Speyside whiskies often feature fruity and malty notes, but hay is a distinctive nuance that makes this whisky unique.

faq.question: How does the 46% alcohol content affect the tasting experience?
faq.answer: At 46% ABV, this Glenfarclas is slightly above the standard 40-43%. The higher alcohol level delivers more intense aromas and a fuller texture without being harsh. This allows the sweet, fruity, and spicy notes to unfold better while maintaining a creamy and oily mouthfeel.

item.name: Blended Scotch Whisky 40 Years (2024) - Living Souls
item.description: A 40-years blended Scotch whisky? One often expects a deep, heavy maturity, perhaps dominant oak notes and a certain dignity. But the Living Souls Blended Scotch Whisky Release 2024 surprises with a freshness that you wouldn't necessarily expect in this age category.

image.alt: Blended Scotch Whisky Release 2024 40 Years - Living Souls

tasting_notes.nose: The exciting complexity of this whisky is revealed from the very first nosing. Exotic fruits such as papaya, apricot and orange rise to the nose, accompanied by a subtle maritime breeze. Cool smoke is subtly noticeable - not intrusive, but harmoniously embedded. A fine citrus note, perhaps lemon, brings additional freshness. There are also hints of heather and a subtle spiciness. The aroma is not overly intense, but extremely inviting and complex.
tasting_notes.palate: On the palate, the whisky unfolds in all its splendour: oily, voluminous and wonderfully balanced. The tropical notes continue - pineapple and mandarin provide a juicy fruitiness, while a subtle spice of white pepper adds excitement. The smoke remains present but restrained - cold and finely interwoven with a nutty note of roasted almonds. A hint of BBQ sauce adds a slightly sweet and savoury nuance that rounds off the overall impression perfectly.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is medium to long, with a great balance of wood, earth and freshness. The oak is present but not dominant, rather it provides a pleasant structure. An unexpected but extremely exciting effect is the minty-fresh smoky note that is noticeable in the aftertaste. There are also fine pine needles and a slight saltiness, which give the whisky an interesting maritime depth.

conclusion.marcel: A 40-years whisky that surprises not only with its freshness but also delights with lively fruitiness and a hint of smoke – drinking this is simply a pleasure! Living Souls has managed to combine elegance and character without falling into boring oak clichés. For anyone seeking a multi-layered yet never dull dram, this blend is a real winner.
conclusion.sascha: The Living Souls 40 Years impresses with its balanced harmony between fruity freshness, subtle smoke, and a well-integrated wood note that neither dominates nor gets lost. The complex structure, featuring mineral and maritime nuances, gives the blend remarkable depth and makes it a technically accomplished example of a matured blended Scotch. A whisky that captivates both nose and palate with its precision and harmony.

faq.question: How does a 40-years blended Scotch whisky differ from a single malt of the same age?
faq.answer: A 40-years blended Scotch combines various malt and grain whiskies, resulting in a complex, balanced mixture. Unlike single malts, which come from a single distillery, the blend often offers a greater variety of aromas and a more harmonious structure without any single flavor note dominating.

faq.question: What role does the maritime note play in the flavor of this whisky?
faq.answer: The maritime note, recognizable by salty and pine needle-like nuances, gives the whisky a fresh, slightly salty depth. It evokes the proximity to the sea and complements the fruity and smoky aromas, making the flavor more lively and complex.

faq.question: Why is the smoke in the Living Souls 40 Years blended Scotch whisky rather restrained, and how does this affect the tasting experience?
faq.answer: The smoke is subtle and finely embedded so as not to overshadow the whisky’s freshness and fruitiness. This creates a balanced tasting experience where the smoke acts as a harmonious complement, combined with nutty and spicy notes rather than being dominant.

item.name: Islay Blended Malt Scotch Whisky Kildalton 19 Years (2024) - Living Souls
item.description: A whisky from Islay, a blended malt with Ardbeg roots, matured for a full 19 years - you'd expect a massive smoke bomb with a good dose of character. And that's exactly what the Kildalton Release 2024 from Living Souls delivers - but with an unexpected depth and a good pinch of surprise.

image.alt: Islay Blended Malt Scotch Whisky Kildalton Release 2024 19 Years - Living Souls

tasting_notes.nose: As soon as you raise the glass, you know: There's a fire here. Wisps of bacon smoke, barbecue charcoal, liquorice and espresso fill the nose. These are joined by pulled pork, honey and a hint of coniferous wood. An intense, yet harmonious flavour.
tasting_notes.palate: The palate continues just as powerfully: warm embers, earthy tones, honey-bacon and dark cherries. The combination of sweet smoke and red fruit is simply brilliant. This is joined by salty olives, a strong peaty note and a pleasant spiciness that rounds off the flavour. The 42% alcohol is well integrated and ensures a full-bodied, warm mouthfeel.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is long and voluminous, but still pleasantly soft. Sweet oak, liquorice and a hint of tobacco linger as the smoke slowly evaporates. Not a brutal finish, but one with style - smoky, sweet and with just the right amount of spice.

conclusion.marcel: This Kildalton 2024 brings together the smoky Islay power with a surprisingly sweet, almost gentle side – a true pleasure that leaves you wanting more. For fans of character and drinkability, it’s a bullseye, delivering aromas of honey, bacon, and dark fruit that simply make it fun to enjoy.
conclusion.sascha: The Kildalton Release 2024 impresses with its balanced interplay between intense peat smoke and sweet, fruity nuances, all wrapped in a complex, harmonious structure. The 19 years of maturation give it a pleasant depth and a long-lasting, elegant finish that is technically perfectly tuned.

faq.question: What makes the Kildalton Release 2024 from Living Souls stand out compared to other Islay whiskies?
faq.answer: The Kildalton Release 2024 is a 19-years blended malt with Ardbeg roots that, despite its intense smokiness, offers surprising depth and a balanced combination of sweet, salty, and spicy flavors.

faq.question: How does the 19-year maturation influence the taste of this Islay whisky?
faq.answer: The long maturation imparts a complex structure where robust peat and smoke notes harmonize with sweet elements like honey and dark cherries, while the wood contributes to a smooth, lingering finish.

faq.question: Which flavor notes should one particularly pay attention to in the Kildalton 2024?
faq.answer: Particularly notable are the bacon-like smoke, grilled charcoal, licorice, and espresso on the nose, as well as on the palate the combination of honeyed bacon, dark cherries, salty olives, and a pleasant spiciness.

item.name: Ledaig and Tobermory 18 Years (2024) - Living Souls
item.description: Ledaig is the brand from the Tobermory distillery that stands for distinctive smoke, Tobermory for maritime elegance - but what happens when the two come together? The Living Souls Ledaig & Tobermory 2024, matured for 18 years, combines precisely these opposites and creates an exciting variety of flavours. Is this a smoky powerhouse or a finely balanced composition?

image.alt: Ledaig and Tobermory 2024 18 Years - Living Souls

tasting_notes.nose: The first impression: earthy, cold smoke, as if a burnt-out campfire had been left on a damp meadow. This is joined by spicy heather and old forest honey, which gives the fragrance a pleasant depth. There are subtle flashes of vanilla and a hint of ripe apple in the background. Interestingly, there is even a subtle hint of camembert - an unusual but exciting detail.
tasting_notes.palate: The attack is creamy and gentle, almost surprisingly soft. Maritime notes resonate, accompanied by lemongrass and a subtle freshness. The smoke is present, but rather restrained - not the rough peaty club, but a subtle, ashy veil. A slight rubbery note comes through, reminiscent of young Islay whiskies, but it remains in the background. Some tequila-like spice and sea salt round off the experience.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is of medium length and again shows the typical ashy notes of a mature Ledaig. There are also gentle oak tones and a subtle aftertaste of apple and pear.

conclusion.marcel: This Ledaig & Tobermory 2024 is like a peaceful walk by the sea – smoky, but never overpowering. Those expecting wild peat monsters will instead discover a charming, restrained character that delights with its gentle spice and surprising details like Camembert, making it a joy to drink. A whisky that impresses with its elegant balance between power and subtlety.
conclusion.sascha: The Living Souls Ledaig & Tobermory 2024 presents an interesting balance between earthy smoky and maritime notes, yet remains rather subtle and reserved in its structure. The composition feels well balanced but could benefit from more intensity and sharper edges to fully unleash the potential of this 18-years whisky. For lovers of balanced, complex blends, it offers a solid, technically well-crafted alternative.

faq.question: How does the Ledaig & Tobermory 2024 18 Years differ from classic Islay whiskies?
faq.answer: Unlike typical Islay whiskies, the smoke in the Ledaig & Tobermory 2024 18 Years is noticeably more subdued and ashy rather than powerfully peaty. The combination with the maritime and elegant notes from Tobermory creates a balanced interplay between smoke and freshness.

faq.question: Which aromas make the Living Souls Ledaig & Tobermory 2024 particularly complex?
faq.answer: The complexity arises from an unusual blend of earthy smoke, spicy heather, forest honey, and a delicate Camembert note on the nose. On the palate, maritime notes, lemongrass, a subtle rubbery hint, and a tequila-like spice complement the tasting experience.

faq.question: How does the 18-year maturation influence the character of this whisky?
faq.answer: The 18 years of maturation bring a pleasant softness and depth. The smoke becomes less rough and develops an ashy veil, while oak tones, apple, and pear notes make the finish smooth and medium-length.

item.name: Blended Scotch Whisky 15 Years (2024) - Living Souls
item.description: The Blended Scotch Whisky Release 2024 from Living Souls is a fascinating fusion of two outstanding components: 50% Glenrothes and 50% North British. With 15 years of maturation and a pleasant drinking strength of 46% vol., this whisky promises a good balance between complexity and accessibility. But does it deliver what it promises? A closer look at the aroma, flavour and finish reveals more.

image.alt: Blended Scotch Whisky Release 2024 15 Years - Living Souls

tasting_notes.nose: The very first aroma surprises with a fresh, lively note. Honey and a malty, almost cereal-like sweetness dominate, accompanied by a hint of red fruit and a delicate orange marmalade. Berries and red fruits lend the bouquet a fruity liveliness, while a light chocolate note and a hint of rye provide depth. Complex and inviting.
tasting_notes.palate: The whisky is creamy and spicy on the palate. Grapefruit and a delicate dark chocolate harmonise with a subtle date sweetness and a hint of black pepper. Red fruits and brown sugar provide a pleasant sweetness, while turmeric and a dry spiciness round off the flavour. A nice balance between sweet and strong.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is tingling and lively, with a pleasant gingerbread spice and a slightly oaky note. Medium-long and sweet, it nevertheless remains balanced without being overpowering.

conclusion.marcel: A whisky that’s a lot of fun, delighting with its spicy depth and sweet-fruity notes – perfect for enjoyable evenings and fans of complex yet approachable blends. The lively character and subtle sherry touch deliver drinking pleasure with plenty of punch. Add a scoop of hazelnut ice cream, and the evening is saved!
conclusion.sascha: The 15-years blended Scotch from Living Souls strikes a successful balance between sweet fruit aromas and spicy notes, supported by a subtle sherry influence. Its structured aromatics and well-balanced, medium-length finish make it a solid example of complex blends with good drinking strength. Overall, a harmoniously composed whisky that showcases its strengths through a multifaceted flavor profile.

faq.question: What special characteristics arise from the combination of Glenrothes and North British in the Blended Scotch Whisky Release 2024?
faq.answer: The blend of 50% Glenrothes and 50% North British combines the fruity, malty notes of Glenrothes with the spicy, often grainy character of North British. This creates a complex yet balanced flavor that harmoniously unites sweetness and spice.

faq.question: How does the 15-year aging influence the taste and aromas of this whisky?
faq.answer: The 15-year maturation ensures a harmonious development of aromas, with the whisky’s fruity and malty notes well balanced. The aging imparts a smooth creaminess and brings out spicy as well as lightly woody nuances on the finish.

faq.question: What is the best way to enjoy this whisky to fully experience its complex flavor notes?
faq.answer: To optimally perceive the diverse aromas, it is recommended to taste the whisky at room temperature in a tulip-shaped glass. A few drops of water can help open up the aromas and highlight the balance between sweet, spicy, and fruity notes.

item.name: Ruadh Maor (2011/2024) Jack the Ripper - Malts of Scotland
item.description: Anyone expecting a gentle drop from a Highland whisky such as the Ruadh Maor 2011/2024 Jack the Ripper from Malts of Scotland is in for a surprise. With an intensely smoky profile, this independent bottler from the Glenturret distillery brings a real heavyweight to the glass.

image.alt: Ruadh Maor 2011/2024 Jack the Ripper - Malts of Scotland

tasting_notes.nose: The Ruadh Maor reveals its complexity from the very first sniff. Dark fruits such as ripe plums and a hint of blackberries mingle with an intense smoke that is strongly reminiscent of Islay whiskies. The iodised, almost medicinal notes are also striking - an impression of plaster and camomile pervades the aroma. A green apple brings a subtle freshness, while warm, woody notes of sandalwood emphasise the depth.
tasting_notes.palate: The first sip is a powerful prelude: Spicy oak notes meet sweet liquorice and the dark fruits from the nose. The smoke remains present, accompanied by a maritime saltiness that reinforces the Islay feeling. A hint of forest honey provides an unexpected, pleasant sweetness and perfectly balances the spicy intensity.
tasting_notes.finish: In the finish, the whisky remains maritime and spicy, but without being too overpowering. The saltiness gives it a refreshing finish, while the smoke lingers for a short time. The finish is of medium length and leaves a balanced memory of wood, smoke and sea breeze.

conclusion.marcel: A Highland whisky that really lifts the spirits with its smoky power play and sweet sherry note. Ruadh Maor 2011/2024 Jack the Ripper isn’t a cuddly dram, but a characterful one that delights with maritime freshness and robust smoke – perfect for anyone who likes to try something different while still focusing on enjoyment.
conclusion.sascha: With its balanced interplay of intense smokiness, maritime saltiness, and complex fruit notes, the Ruadh Maor 2011/2024 Jack the Ripper impresses as a structured Highland whisky. The harmonious fusion of sweetness, spice, and oak delivers a precise, multi-layered tasting experience with a long-lasting elegant finish.

faq.question: Why does the Ruadh Maor 2011/2024 Jack the Ripper remind one of an Islay whisky despite originating from the Highlands?
faq.answer: Although Ruadh Maor comes from the Glenturret distillery in the Highlands, it features a heavily smoky profile with iodine and maritime notes typical of Islay whiskies. This is due to the special cask maturation and the influence of the independent bottler Malts of Scotland, who deliberately presents the whisky with an intense smoky and sea character.

faq.question: How does the high alcohol content of 55.6% affect the taste and drinking experience of this whisky?
faq.answer: The 55.6% alcohol content delivers a powerful and intense flavor explosion that highlights the spicy oak notes, the sweetness of licorice and honey, as well as the smoky and maritime aromas. It can make the whisky robust and complex, which is why many enthusiasts add a few drops of water to better unfold the flavors.

faq.question: What makes the Ruadh Maor 2011/2024 Jack the Ripper an interesting whisky for experienced connoisseurs?
faq.answer: This whisky stands out due to its unusual combination of intense smokiness, iodine-tinged medicinal hints, and a harmonious balance of sweet and spicy notes. The maritime-salty finish and the layered aroma profile ranging from dark fruits to sandalwood offer seasoned whisky lovers a complex and distinctive tasting experience.

item.name: Macallan 20 Years (2003/2023) Speymalt - Gordon & MacPhail
item.description: When a legend like Macallan meets the expertise of independent bottler Gordon & MacPhail, one thing is certain: the result has the potential to set new standards. The Macallan 2003/2023 Speymalt, a 20-years single malt, is no exception. At a whopping 58.30 % vol., this sherry bomb from Speyside is powerful and complex. But does it live up to its promise?

tasting_notes.nose: This whisky doesn't mince its words from the very first nosing. What emerges from the glass is a true sherry grenade. A heavy, almost dramatic combination of wet carpet, leather and tobacco meets the autumnal freshness of damp leaves. This is joined by the sweet darkness of burnt caramel. It is a fragrance that tells stories - smoky, earthy and deeply rooted in decades of ageing.
tasting_notes.palate: The true magic of this whisky unfolds on the tongue. The first thing you notice is a distinctive dryness reminiscent of a fine sherry. This is accompanied by an amazingly oily texture that takes over the entire mouth. The flavours are intense and clear: fig, rich and dark, is accompanied by earthy leather and bitter cocoa. There is no room for restraint here - this whisky has character and shows it.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is the icing on the cake of this complex drop. It is long, as befits a whisky aged for 20 years, and has a lovely spiciness that finishes astringent and dry. But before it disappears, a slight fruity sweetness flashes through.

conclusion.marcel: This Macallan 2003/2023 is a true taste experience – intense, with a powerful sherry explosion that makes every sip exciting. It demands your attention but rewards you with a complex blend of spice, sweetness, and character that’s simply enjoyable. For connoisseurs seeking a whisky with bite and soul, this is a real bullseye!
conclusion.sascha: With its balanced structure of intense sherry notes, fine oak, and subtle fruity sweetness, the Macallan 2003/2023 showcases an impressive interplay of power and elegance. Despite the high 58.3% ABV, it is well integrated and offers a complex yet harmonious balance that guarantees both depth and a long-lasting finish.

faq.question: What makes the Macallan 2003/2023 20-Year Speymalt from Gordon & MacPhail special compared to the original Macallan release?
faq.answer: The Macallan 2003/2023 was released by Gordon & MacPhail as an independent bottler and features a more intense cask strength at 58.3% ABV compared to many original releases. Additionally, its 20-year maturation in sherry casks imparts a particularly powerful and complex sherry character that is often more subdued in standard bottlings.

faq.question: How does the 20-year maturation in sherry casks influence the flavor of this Speysider?
faq.answer: The extended maturation in sherry casks gives the whisky its intense sherry note with aromas of dried fruits like fig, dark caramel, leather, and cocoa. At the same time, it provides an oily texture and a complex, long-lasting finish with spicy and lightly fruity nuances.

faq.question: What are the drinking recommendations for the Macallan 2003/2023 Speymalt with 58.3% alcohol content?
faq.answer: Due to the high alcohol strength, it is recommended to enjoy the whisky neat to fully appreciate its complex aromas. Those who wish to soften the sharpness can experiment with a few drops of water. This intense single malt also works very well as a digestif after a meal.

item.name: Meikle Tòir 5 Years The Chinquapin One (2023)
item.description: With the Meikle Tòir The Chinquapin One 5 Years, GlenAllachie proves that Speyside whiskies can indeed have a smoky side. However, the true highlight of this release isn't just the peat smoke, but also the cask management: In addition to ex-bourbon barrels, Chinquapin oak is used – an American oak variety known for its spicy, leathery, and subtly sweet notes.

image.alt: Meikle Tòir 5 Years The Chinquapin One Release 2023

tasting_notes.nose: The first impression is complex. Warm charcoal smoke rises, accompanied by sweet, nutty roasted almonds. A deep spiciness unfolds with cinnamon and ginger, while green apple and malty sweetness add freshness. After the first sip, a soft hint of peach emerges.
tasting_notes.palate: The whisky is full-bodied and intense. A smoldering grill fire sets the foundation, over which sweet BBQ notes and spicy cinnamon unfold. Dark caramel and a distinctive teriyaki sauce note enhance the sweet-savory character.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is long and powerfully smoky. Sweet smoke-kissed almonds linger, while cinnamon returns – this time with a touch of chili for an exciting heat.

conclusion.marcel: Here, smoky Speyside becomes an experience – young, yet already full of character and with a spiciness that’s genuinely enjoyable. The Chinquapin oak delivers just the right kick to keep the whisky exciting without overwhelming it. Absolutely recommended for fans of powerful yet playful smokiness!
conclusion.sascha: The harmonious interplay of smoke, sweetness, and spicy Chinquapin oak creates a remarkable balance that feels surprisingly mature for a five-years whisky. The structural clarity and long-lasting finish with subtle heat give this Speyside expression a distinct profile strength. A technically impressive, smoky single malt with a precise cask combination.

faq.question: What makes the Chinquapin oak special in the maturation of this whisky?
faq.answer: Chinquapin oak is an American oak species that imparts spicy, leathery, and slightly sweet aromas to the whisky. It complements the classic ex-bourbon casks and gives the Meikle Tòir The Chinquapin One 5 Years a unique flavor dimension.

faq.question: How does the Meikle Tòir 5 Years differ from typical Speyside whiskies?
faq.answer: Unlike the usually mild and fruity Speyside whiskies, the Meikle Tòir 5 Years surprises with a pronounced smoky note reminiscent of charcoal and campfire. This combination of peat smoke and fresh, spicy aromas makes it exceptional for the region.

faq.question: Which flavor components are especially present in the finish of this whisky?
