faq.answer: The finish is long-lasting and powerfully smoky, accompanied by sweet smoked almonds and a spicy cinnamon note. A hint of chili adds a subtle, exciting heat that rounds off the finish.

item.name: Betz Sauternes Cask (2017/2022) Batch 1
item.description: The Betz Sauternes Cask Batch 1 is a German whisky matured in the Betz Distillery. Its speciality lies in its ageing in Sauternes casks, a sweet French dessert wine that gives the whisky a unique flavour. But does it live up to its promise?

tasting_notes.nose: The first sniff reveals a sweetish fragrance accompanied by light sulphur notes. This is accompanied by fruity flavours of raspberry and a subtle hint of caramel. A hint of mould and sourdough rounds off the aroma profile and gives the whisky a certain complexity.
tasting_notes.palate: A harmonious interplay of honey and marzipan unfolds on the tongue, supported by a light wine note. There is a subtle astringency and a slight bitterness that lends depth to the flavour. A slightly gamy undertone provides an unusual, unpleasant flavour nuance.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is of medium length and leaves an impression of ripe grapes and a slightly sweet note.

conclusion.marcel: The Betz Sauternes Cask Batch 1 is a pleasant whisky with sweet fruitiness and charming marzipan notes – but somehow it lacks the wow factor. Those who enjoy experimental whisky styles and have fun with unusual cask finishes can definitely give this a try, yet genuine depth is hard to find here.
conclusion.sascha: A solid German whisky featuring an interesting Sauternes cask finish that brings sweet and fruity nuances. However, overall the dram feels somewhat underdeveloped and shows slight off-notes that affect its balance and complexity.

faq.question: How does maturation in Sauternes casks influence the flavor of this whisky?
faq.answer: Maturation in Sauternes casks imparts sweet and fruity notes to the whisky, especially honey, marzipan, and ripe grapes. At the same time, complex aromas develop, complemented by subtle sulfur and sourdough hints.

faq.question: Why are unusual flavor notes like mustiness or a slightly off-putting undertone perceptible in the Betz Sauternes Cask 2017/2022?
faq.answer: These atypical notes can arise from the specific cask maturation and microbiological processes during aging. They contribute a complex, though not universally pleasant, flavor nuance that shapes the character of this whisky.

faq.question: How long was the Betz Sauternes Cask 2017/2022 Batch 1 matured, and how does this affect the whisky?
faq.answer: The whisky was matured for five years. This moderate aging period allows the cask notes, particularly those of the Sauternes wine, to stand out clearly without the whisky becoming too woody or overpowered.

item.name: Betz Rye Single Cask (2017/2022) 50
item.description: If you're looking for a German whisky that will win you over with its complexity and finesse, then steer clear of the Betz Rye Single Cask 50. This 4-years whisky from the Betz Distillery is a prime example of how to produce a whisky that simply disappoints. At 48.00% ABV, it promises strength, but what it delivers is a series of bad decisions.

tasting_notes.nose: The first impression is a slap in the face. Instead of a harmonious bouquet, you are met with a spirit-heavy attack that is more reminiscent of cheap booze than a carefully matured whisky. Hidden somewhere in between are notes of raspberry, aniseed and - yes, you read that right - Fisherman's Friend cherry. Who came up with that, please? It doesn't smell like a handcrafted whisky, it smells like a failed experiment in chemistry class.
tasting_notes.palate: The next disaster reveals itself on the tongue. The whisky tastes sweet, but not in a good way. It is a dull, cereal sweetness accompanied by a faint citrus flavour. But instead of coming across as fresh and lively, the flavour remains flat and uninspired. Where is the depth? Where is the complexity? Instead, you get a flavour that is as exciting as a dry bread roll.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is so short that one is almost grateful - if it wasn't so sharp and woody. The oak dominates here in the worst possible way, accompanied by a sharp afterburn that is more reminiscent of cheap grain than a single cask whisky. There is no long, pleasant finish, just an abrupt end that leaves you perplexed.

conclusion.marcel: If you're looking for a whisky that's fun and full of character, you should definitely keep looking rather than choosing the Betz Rye Single Cask 50. Instead of enjoyment, it delivers more of a hangover vibe with a spirit-heavy aroma and a finish that brings no pleasure – better pick another dram!
conclusion.sascha: From a technical standpoint, the Betz Rye Single Cask 50 lacks the necessary balance and complexity to truly impress. The uneven aroma profile and the sharp, woody finish make the whisky feel underdeveloped and argue against recommending it.

faq.question: Why does the Betz Rye Single Cask 2017/2022 taste so strongly of spirits and chemical notes?
faq.answer: The intense spirit smell and unusual notes like cherry Fisherman’s Friend suggest insufficient maturation and possibly unwanted reaction products. This can be caused by a too short aging period of only 4 years and a lack of quality control during production.

faq.question: How does the short maturation period of 4 years affect the taste of this whisky?
faq.answer: With only 4 years of maturation, the whisky lacks the necessary time to absorb complex flavors from the cask and develop harmoniously. The result is a flat, unbalanced taste dominated by oak and a sharp, burning finish.

faq.question: Is the Betz Rye Single Cask 2017/2022 suitable for beginners despite its high alcohol content of 48%?
faq.answer: Due to its strong spirit character, flat aromatic profile, and sharp finish, this whisky is not recommended for beginners. The combination of 48% ABV and lack of flavor balance can be overwhelming and unpleasant.

item.name: Betz PX Single Cask (2017/2021) 52
item.description: When you think of German whisky, you might expect innovation, courage and a fresh approach. Unfortunately, the Betz PX Single Cask 52 delivers exactly the opposite: a boring, flat experience that fails to impress even after four years of maturation.

tasting_notes.nose: The nose does indeed begin interestingly: citrus notes, almonds and a slight raspberry sweetness mingle with dark fruits and sultanas. A hint of rum-grape-nut-chocolate gives hope that something special is waiting here. But then comes the disillusionment - the flavours are superficial and quickly evaporate. The promised sherry bomb? Not a chance. Instead, a sharp, almost aggressive note dominates, clouding the overall impression.
tasting_notes.palate: The first sip confirms our fears: Boring. The flavour is flat and uninspired. There are hints of dark chocolate, a slight sweetness and even a hint of Mon Chéri chocolates, but everything remains pale and underdeveloped. The PX influences are barely noticeable and the whisky does not manage to be full-bodied. Instead, an oaky dryness dominates, leaving only emptiness instead of complexity.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is short and unpleasantly sharp. Pepper and cocoa try to assert themselves, but here too there is a lack of depth and balance. The spiciness lingers persistently in the mouth and leaves an unpleasant aftertaste that is more reminiscent of cheap spirits than a carefully matured single cask.

conclusion.marcel: Unfortunately, the Betz PX Single Cask 52 falls far short of expectations – despite the promising nose, it lacks character and drinking pleasure. It's a shame that four years of maturation and a PX cask yielded nothing more than a flat, somewhat sharp finish. Those who favor German whisky would be better off moving on.
conclusion.sascha: The Betz PX Single Cask 2017/2021 52 displays an unbalanced structure with superficial aromas and a lack of complexity. The balance between sweetness, oak, and sharpness is off, which significantly weakens the overall impression. Technically interesting, but unfortunately disappointing and not very rounded in taste.

faq.question: How does the PX sherry cask influence the flavor of the Betz PX Single Cask 2017/2021 52?
faq.answer: The PX sherry cask would normally impart intense sweet and fruity notes. However, in this whisky, the sherry influences are very weak, resulting in a rather flat and not very complex flavor.

faq.question: Why does the Betz PX Single Cask seem so unbalanced despite four years of maturation?
faq.answer: The relatively short maturation period of four years combined with the choice of cask appears insufficient to develop depth and balance. As a result, sharp and dry notes dominate while the aromas fade quickly.

faq.question: What aromas can be expected on the nose and palate of this whisky?
faq.answer: On the nose, you initially find citrus, almonds, and raspberry sweetness, accompanied by dark fruits and raisins. On the palate, there are subtle hints of dark chocolate, sweetness, and Mon Chéri chocolates, but these remain faint and are overshadowed by an oaky dryness.

item.name: Betz Master Blend Batch 4
item.description: The Betz Master Blend Batch 4 from the Betz Distillery in Germany is a whisky that promises a lot, but ultimately falls somewhat short of expectations. With an alcohol content of 46.00% ABV and no age statement, this blend presents itself as an experiment that offers both fascinating and disappointing moments.

tasting_notes.nose: The nose is initially greeted by an interesting blend of pepper and spicy herbs. Underneath, notes of hazelnut and a hint of caramel shimmer, combining with an unexpected sweetness of candy floss and raspberry. The fragrance is complex and multi-layered, raising hopes for an equally multi-layered flavour.
tasting_notes.palate: A creamy cream caramel initially unfolds on the tongue, accompanied by soft vanilla and orange tones. But then comes the turnaround: notes of leather and cocoa mix with bitter herbs that dominate the flavour. While the first few seconds still seem harmonious, the whisky quickly becomes flat and one-sided. The bitterness overpowers the finer nuances and makes the flavour less enjoyable.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is creamy and of medium length, with an aftertaste of orange and herbs. Unfortunately, the bitterness remains present here too, which detracts from the otherwise pleasant finish. There is a lack of depth and balance to really round off the whisky.

conclusion.marcel: The Betz Master Blend Batch 4 entices with intriguing aromas and a hint of sweetness, but it quickly loses its charm in the glass. Bitterness takes over, making the drinking experience more of a chore than a pleasure – a shame, as it had so much more potential!
conclusion.sascha: Despite an interesting complexity on the nose, the Betz Master Blend Batch 4 lacks structural balance, particularly due to the dominant bitterness on the palate. The harmony between creaminess and bitter notes falls out of sync, which unfortunately weakens the overall impression.

faq.question: Why does the Betz Master Blend Batch 4 have no age statement (No Age Statement)?
faq.answer: The Betz Master Blend Batch 4 deliberately omits an age statement to give the master distiller more freedom in selecting the whiskies used. This allows for a blend of different vintages to achieve a specific flavor profile, even if it may result in a less pronounced depth.

faq.question: Which flavor notes dominate the Betz Master Blend Batch 4 despite its complex nose?
faq.answer: Although the nose is layered with pepper, herbs, hazelnut, and sweet accents, the palate is dominated by bitter herbal tones along with notes of leather and cocoa. This bitterness overshadows the initially creamy and fruity aromas, making the overall taste feel somewhat unbalanced.

faq.question: How does the Betz Master Blend Batch 4 differ from classic German single malt whiskies?
faq.answer: Unlike many German single malts, which often feature a clear age statement and come from pure malt distilleries, the Betz Master Blend Batch 4 is a no-age-statement blend combining various whiskies to create an experimental profile. This results in both interesting and less balanced characteristics that set it apart from traditional single malts.

item.name: Ballechin 19 Years (2004/2023)
item.description: If you are looking for a whisky that will challenge your taste buds and senses in equal measure, you will find it with the Ballechin 2004/2023 19 Years. This single malt from the Edradour Distillery in the Scottish Highlands is a real powerhouse with 55.00% ABV and offers an intense, smoky experience that will delight Islay fans. But beware: this whisky is not for the faint-hearted!

tasting_notes.nose: Ballechin reveals its smoky soul from the very first sniff. Black BBQ and an extremely bacon-y character are dominant, immediately evoking associations with smoked meat. Underneath, notes of tar, lime and even a hint of burnt rubber resonate. It's like entering an old factory - industrial, raw and yet fascinating.
tasting_notes.palate: On the tongue, the Scotch unfolds with a warming, full load of vapour. The flavour is intense and complex: old machine oil, warm rubber tyres and even a hint of linoleum make this whisky a real experience. The smokiness dominates, but it is accompanied by a subtle fruitiness that provides a surprise.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is long and voluminous, with a spicy note that lingers for a long time. The subtle oak is noticeable, but is overlaid by the intense smokiness.

conclusion.marcel: Anyone trying the Ballechin 2004/2023 19 Years should have no fear of intense aromas – this whisky is a smoky rollercoaster ride packed with character and plenty of drinking pleasure. For fans of powerful, bacon-like notes, it’s a real highlight that tells stories and leaves you wanting more. Gentle sippers might be better off moving on.
conclusion.sascha: The Ballechin 2004/2023 19 Years impresses with its complex balance of intense smokiness, robust BBQ notes, and subtle fruitiness that beautifully supports its structure. Despite the high cask strength, the single malt feels well integrated, although the dominant, industrial aromas won’t be to everyone’s taste. A demanding Highland whisky with a distinctive profile.

faq.question: Why does the Ballechin 2004/2023 19 Years remind one of Islay whiskies, even though it comes from the Highlands?
faq.answer: Although the Ballechin 2004/2023 19 Years originates from the Highlands, it is heavily peated and smoky, much like many Islay whiskies. The Edradour Distillery emphasizes intense smoke and peat in Ballechin, which mimics the character of an Islay whisky despite its different geographical origin.

faq.question: How should one best enjoy the Ballechin 2004/2023 19 Years to fully experience its complex aromas?
faq.answer: Due to its high alcohol content of 55%, it is recommended to first taste the whisky neat and then dilute it with a few drops of water. This helps open up the aromas such as smoke, BBQ, tar, and subtle fruitiness, resulting in a more balanced tasting experience.

faq.question: What special qualities make the Ballechin 2004/2023 19 Years a unique whisky for experienced connoisseurs?
faq.answer: This 19-years single malt stands out for its exceptionally intense smokiness and complex aroma profile reminiscent of industrial and meat smoke. With notes of tar, rubber, and linoleum, it offers an unusual sensory experience that particularly appeals to fans of bold and smoky whiskies.

item.name: Glenlivet Nadurra (2015)
item.description: The Glenlivet distillery is one of the best-known names in the Speyside region of Scotland and delivers a strong single malt whisky with the Nadurra 2015, which does not require an age statement. With an impressive 59.8 % vol., this whisky is aimed at connoisseurs who are not afraid of intense flavours and a high alcohol content. But what is really behind the high strength? Is it worth buying?

tasting_notes.nose: On first nosing, the Glenlivet Nadurra unfolds a fascinating spectrum: floral notes mingle with the warm scent of honey and shortbread, reminiscent of a classic afternoon tea. A slight hint of spicy oak and candy floss joins in, with the latter offering almost bourbon-like echoes. A promising start that prepares the senses for a sweet but not overloaded whisky.
tasting_notes.palate: The Nadurra shows its strength from the very first sip. White sugar and coconut notes dominate at first, before a light spiciness and the fruity character of white peach assert themselves. This is accompanied by a creamy cream caramel note and an oily, viscous texture that makes the whisky very present. The white pepper in the finish reinforces the spiciness and provides a warming finish.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish of the Glenlivet Nadurra is surprisingly short to medium in length. Fresh oak notes dominate, accompanied by a hint of honey and minimal light fruity flavours. Although pleasant, it lacks the depth that many connoisseurs might expect from such a strong whisky.

conclusion.marcel: The Glenlivet Nadurra 2015 is a powerful Speyside whisky that really lifts the spirits with its sweet oiliness and intense aroma. Despite its strength, it drinks surprisingly easily and offers drinking pleasure without major hurdles. However, it lacks that certain something that would make it unforgettable – a good dram that just misses a bit of depth to truly crown it.
conclusion.sascha: At 59.8% ABV, the Glenlivet Nadurra 2015 shows an impressive presence, characterized by a clear structure and balanced sweetness. The balance between floral notes, spice, and oiliness is well achieved, yet the shortened finish and lack of complexity somewhat diminish the overall impression. For lovers of robust Speyside whiskies, it remains a solid choice, though not a top-tier representative.

faq.question: Why does the Glenlivet Nadurra 2015 have no age statement (No Age Statement)?
faq.answer: The Glenlivet Nadurra 2015 is bottled without an age statement to allow flexibility in cask selection. This enables the distillery to combine particularly characterful and robust whiskies from different vintages without being tied to a specific age.

faq.question: How does the high alcohol content of 59.8% affect the taste and drinking experience?
faq.answer: The high alcohol content delivers intense aromas and a robust texture. It enhances the spicy and fruity notes but can also bring a certain sharpness. Many recommend adding a little water to open up the aromas and create a more balanced tasting experience.

faq.question: What makes the Glenlivet Nadurra 2015 stand out compared to other Speyside whiskies?
faq.answer: The Nadurra 2015 is distinguished by its natural cask strength and the combination of sweet, fruity, and spicy notes. Unlike many lighter Speyside whiskies, it offers a bold and complex profile with an oily, viscous texture, appealing especially to whisky enthusiasts seeking intense and authentic flavor experiences.

item.name: North British 31 Years (1991) - Hogshead Imports
item.description: The North British 1991, a 31-years single grain whisky from the famous Lowland region of Scotland, is a highlight for lovers of matured whiskies. Bottled by the independent bottler Hogshead Imports at 46.7% vol., this whisky presents an exciting journey through intense aromas and layers of flavour. But does it live up to the promise of its impressive age?

tasting_notes.nose: From the very first nosing, it becomes clear that this whisky has a story to tell. Caramel, as intense as melted sugar on the hob, meets velvety vanilla and sweet honey. A subtle note of soft oak resonates, accompanied by a hint of stone fruit that lends a fruity depth. Surprising and slightly unorthodox is a note reminiscent of thinner or touch-up pencil - an exciting contrast.
tasting_notes.palate: A mixture of fresh wood and damp sawdust unfolds on the palate, providing a rustic touch. The sweetness comes to the fore in the form of cream caramels, giving the whisky a soft and almost creamy texture. Despite the complexity, the flavour is not overloaded, but balanced, with a slightly sweet base note reminiscent of patisserie.
tasting_notes.finish: In the finish, the North British is surprisingly restrained. A slight warmth spreads, but disappears relatively quickly.

conclusion.marcel: This North British 31-years offers an exciting rollercoaster of sensations: sweet, caramel-rich, yet surprisingly light. For connoisseurs seeking whisky with character and a touch of idiosyncrasy, this is a real find – even if the lightness in the finish feels a bit prickly.
conclusion.sascha: The North British 1991 displays an intriguing balance between intense caramel notes and an unexpected lightness on the palate. The structure is solid, although the finish feels somewhat short and reserved, which slightly diminishes the overall complexity.

faq.question: What makes the North British 1991 Single Grain Whisky special compared to Single Malt whiskies?
faq.answer: The North British 1991 is a Single Grain whisky, which, unlike Single Malts, is primarily distilled from grains such as corn or wheat. This often results in a milder, softer texture with more subtle aromas, which in this 31-years whisky is expressed through its creamy caramel and vanilla notes alongside a delicate woodiness.

faq.question: Why does the North British 1991 have an unusual note reminiscent of thinner or lacquer pen?
faq.answer: This slightly unorthodox note likely arises from the long maturation in hogshead casks that may have previously held other spirits or wines. Such cask influences are not uncommon in aged grain whiskies and contribute to the complexity, even if they don’t appeal to every palate.

faq.question: How does the age of 31 years affect the taste and drinkability of this whisky?
faq.answer: The 31-year age allows for intense maturation, during which complex flavors like caramel, vanilla, and wood can develop. At the same time, careful cask management ensures the whisky doesn’t become over-oaked or too heavy despite the long aging, maintaining a balanced, smooth texture with a restrained finish.

item.name: Macallan 20 Years (2004/2024) Speymalt - Gordon & MacPhail
item.description: Independent bottlers like Gordon & MacPhail provide a glimpse into the past, especially when it comes to iconic distilleries like Macallan. With the Speymalt 2004/2024, G&M presents a 20-years malt distilled at the Macallan distillery and matured in a sherry cask (No. 22606605). These casks are renowned for their complexity, and this whisky exemplifies the mastery of two decades of aging.

tasting_notes.nose: The first nosing reveals a fascinating medley of red fruits – sour cherries, dates, and figs lead the way. This is complemented by an elegant woody note reminiscent of driftwood, coupled with a slightly burnt character leaning towards cigar tobacco. This balance of sweet and earthy tones makes the bouquet remarkably layered.
tasting_notes.palate: The first sip is an experience: full-bodied and powerful, a pleasant sweetness greets the palate. The creamy, soft texture reveals notes of leather, cocoa powder, and dried plums. Subtle nutty tones play in the background, while oak takes on a leading role without dominating the profile. Each drop tells the story of a sherry cask transformed by time and care.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is long and multi-layered: a sweet-spicy warmth spreads, accompanied by dry oak and nutty flavors, particularly walnuts. What remains is a harmonious, balanced finish that leaves the impression of a perfectly matured malt.

conclusion.marcel: A whisky that immediately wins your heart with its creamy sweetness and spicy sherry notes – simply a pleasure that relaxes and delights. The 20 years of maturation give it character and depth without feeling overdone. Perfect for connoisseurs looking for a warm, rounded dram full of personality.
conclusion.sascha: Technically impressive, this whisky shows a balanced structure where sweetness, spice, and wood notes harmoniously intertwine. The long maturation in a sherry cask imparts a complex yet clear aromatic profile and a long-lasting, multi-layered finish. A prime example of a well-balanced, matured Speyside whisky.

faq.question: What makes the maturation in a sherry cask special for this Macallan 2004/2024?
faq.answer: Maturation in a sherry cask, specifically cask number 22606605, imparts complex aromas of red fruits, nuts, and spices to the whisky. Sherry casks retain and develop sweet and spicy notes, giving this 20-years Macallan a layered flavor profile and a long-lasting finish.

faq.question: How does the Macallan 2004/2024 differ from other Macallan releases?
faq.answer: This Macallan was released by Gordon & MacPhail as an independent bottler, meaning it does not come directly from the distillery. The combination of 20 years maturation in a special sherry cask and the higher drinking strength of 59.4% ABV results in a more intense and complex tasting experience than many standard releases.

faq.question: How should this whisky be enjoyed to fully experience all its aromas?
faq.answer: The Macallan 2004/2024 reveals its aromas best at room temperature and can be opened up with a few drops of water to highlight the complex notes of red fruits, leather, and nuts. Using an appropriate nosing glass helps capture the layered nose fully, while slow sipping brings out the long, spicy finish to its best effect.

item.name: Aberfeldy 18 Years (2021) French Red Wine Casks
item.description: If there's one thing a whisky from the Highlands promises, it's character - and the Aberfeldy 2021, 18 years in French Red Wine Casks doesn't disappoint. This limited bottling shows how a mature spirit can shine with a touch of French elegance.

image.alt: Aberfeldy 2021 18 Years French Red Wine Casks

tasting_notes.nose: Even the nosing of this whisky is an experience. Notes of dates and figs rise to the nose, carried by a gentle hint of light-coloured grapes and clove. An interesting ‘sandy’ undertone is reminiscent of a walk in an autumn landscape - earthy, slightly rough and inviting at the same time. After the first sip, increasingly fruity flavours unfold, intensifying the impression.
tasting_notes.palate: On the tongue, the Aberfeldy 18 years is initially creamy - almost like a velvety wine. But then the Highlands make their unmistakable mark: oak and a hint of coloured pepper provide a spicy base, accompanied by subtle notes of red fruit. The slightly vinous note from the maturation in French red wine casks adds an elegant dimension without being too dominant.
tasting_notes.finish: And then comes the finish - wow! The interplay of flavours reaches its climax here. A clear strawberry note leads into raspberry ice cream before everything dissolves into a creamy, spicy warmth. The strong spiciness leaves a lasting impression that lingers on the palate for minutes afterwards.

conclusion.marcel: Aberfeldy 18 years impressively demonstrates how a whisky can benefit from spicy sherry notes and fruity complexity. Particularly fascinating: with time in the glass, it gains in fruitiness, which makes every new tasting an experience. Lovers of intense and complex whiskies will be more than satisfied. This whisky is not a simple sipper - it invites you to engage with it and experience the development of its flavours. The Aberfeldy 2021, 18 years French Red Wine Casks harmonises perfectly with duck breast in a red wine reduction, as the fruity notes of the whisky elegantly pick up on the flavours of wine and meat. For dessert, raspberry ice cream with dark chocolate complements the berry and creamy finish.
conclusion.sascha: Aberfeldy 18 years impressively demonstrates how a whisky can benefit from spicy sherry notes and fruity complexity. Particularly fascinating: with time in the glass, it gains in fruitiness, which makes every new tasting an experience. Lovers of intense and complex whiskies will be more than satisfied. This whisky is not a simple sipper - it invites you to engage with it and experience the development of its flavours. The Aberfeldy 2021, 18 years French Red Wine Casks harmonises perfectly with duck breast in a red wine reduction, as the fruity notes of the whisky elegantly pick up on the flavours of wine and meat. For dessert, raspberry ice cream with dark chocolate complements the berry and creamy finish.

item.name: Deanston 11 Years (2012/2023) - Best Dram
item.description: Rum finishes are like the bell bottoms of the whisky world - sometimes totally trendy, sometimes highly controversial. In 2024, they are definitely in the spotlight, and the Deanston 2012/2023 11 Years from Best Dram shows why. This independent bottler has once again managed to deliver an affordable bottle that will delight novices and connoisseurs alike. But beware: rum finishes are a bit like blind dates - they can be sweet and exciting or just plain meh. This one? Clearly a fruit bomb with a wow effect.

tasting_notes.nose: From the very first sniff, Deanston catapults your nose into a tropical fruit basket. Imagine opening a fresh tin of peaches and apricots, sprinkling a few pieces of pineapple on top and topping it all off with honey sweetness and a hint of whipped cream. A gentle touch of creamy caramel and a small herbal greeting à la mate tea round off the experience. Sounds fancy? It is - but without being lifted off.
tasting_notes.palate: This is where it gets really exciting! The Deanston packs quite a punch at 55.7 % vol. but without overwhelming the palate. The oily texture is like silk on the tongue, while lemon balm and heather add fresh accents. The tropical fruits from the nose are also present here - apricot, pineapple and the like make an appearance. At the same time, a good portion of oak provides spice and depth. It is a balance of power and elegance that is simply fun.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is like the final act of a good film: warm, dry and a little pensive. The oak comes to the fore and brings spicy, almost tart notes, while the fruitiness slowly fades away. A hint of tea lingers - a subtle finish that immediately invites you to take the next sip.

conclusion.marcel: This Deanston 11 Years from Best Dram is a true fruit bomb that delights with its rum finish and never becomes boring. The tropical aromas and oily texture turn every sip into a little holiday flirt – perfect for anyone who wants to have fun with whisky without spending a lot of money.
conclusion.sascha: The Deanston 2012/2023 presents itself as a well-balanced whisky with an impressive harmony between the powerful 55.7% ABV and the fine integration of tropical fruit notes and spicy oak. The rum finish gives this cask-strength dram a harmonious, complex character that appeals to both newcomers and connoisseurs alike.

faq.question: How does the rum finish influence the taste of the Deanston 11 Years?
faq.answer: The rum finish imparts a pronounced fruitiness to the Deanston 11 Years, with notes of peach, apricot, and pineapple. It adds a sweet honeyed sweetness and a creamy touch that make the whisky lively and exotic without overshadowing the typical Highland characteristics.

faq.question: Is the Deanston 11 Years suitable for whisky beginners despite its high alcohol content?
faq.answer: At 55.7% ABV, the Deanston 11 Years is quite powerful, but its balanced texture and fruity aromatics can also appeal to beginners who are willing to be a bit adventurous. Adding a few drops of water helps to soften the intensity and better reveal the flavors.

faq.question: What special qualities does the independent bottler Best Dram bring to this whisky?
faq.answer: Best Dram focuses on exciting finishes and affordable bottlings. For the Deanston 11 Years 2012/2023, they deliberately chose a rum finish that enriches the whisky with tropical fruitiness and a complex array of aromas without diluting the character of the distillery.

item.name: Edradour 12 Years (2012/2024) Frühburgunder Barrique Finish
item.description: When you think of Edradour, sherry bombs and heavy, dark notes come to mind. But wait a minute! The Edradour 2012/2024 Early Burgundy Barrique Finish is ready to throw all clichés overboard. No sherry? WTF, Edradour! But don't worry, this Highland malt surprises in the best possible way. The colour alone is stunning: an intense rosé that shimmers almost teasingly in the glass. The early Burgundy cask has done a great job here. You could almost think you have a wine in your glass - but don't worry, the 12-years whisky remains true to its Scottish roots.

tasting_notes.nose: The nose is a real adventure! Strawberries? Check. Red fruits and berries? There too. A vinous bouquet gently reminiscent of a summer evening in Tuscany. But then comes the surprise: a hint of yoghurt creeps in, paired with a delicate hint of grey bread. Yes, grey bread - and no, that's not a typo. Sounds crazy, but smells brilliant.
tasting_notes.palate: With the first sip, you immediately know why this whisky is fun: strawberry yoghurt, sweet and fruity, dominates the start. But before it becomes too sweet, peppery spice and a pleasant dryness bring balance. Dazu gesellen sich Karamell und Honig – wie ein Werther’s Original, aber mit Stil. Es ist, als würde man sich in einer französischen Patisserie befinden, während ein Highland-Cow einem freundlich zunickt.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is of medium length and is characterised by spicy oak with a subtle winey note, reminiscent of a good glass of red wine. Perfect for anyone who likes to enjoy their whiskies with a touch of elegance.

conclusion.marcel: Who would have thought a whisky could be so playful and yet elegant? The Edradour 2012/2024 Frühburgunder surprises with its fruity lightness and charming strawberry yogurt twist – a true delight for the palate that livens up any tasting. Perfect if you're in the mood for a bit of unconventional drinking fun!
conclusion.sascha: A successful interplay of fruity Frühburgunder notes and the typical Highland structure makes this 12-years Edradour an intriguing subject for study. The balance between sweet fruitiness, peppery spice, and the elegant oak finish demonstrates how well a wine-barrel finish can shape a whisky to be both complex and harmonious.

faq.question: How does the Frühburgunder barrel finish influence the taste of the Edradour 2012/2024?
faq.answer: The Frühburgunder barrel finish gives the whisky an intense rosé hue and imprints the aroma with fruity notes of strawberries and red berries. It also adds a sweet-fruity flavor component reminiscent of strawberry yogurt, along with an elegant wine and oak spiciness on the finish.

faq.question: Why does the Edradour 2012/2024 smell like rye bread and yogurt?
faq.answer: The unusual aromas of rye bread and yogurt arise from the combination of maturation in Frühburgunder wine casks and the natural development of flavors in the whisky. Such notes are typical for whiskies finished in wine casks and bring an interesting, slightly tangy and bready complexity to the nose.

faq.question: How does the Edradour 2012/2024 differ from classic Edradour sherry cask releases?
faq.answer: Unlike the typical sherry cask whiskies from Edradour, which often feature heavy, dark, and sweet notes, the 2012/2024 Frühburgunder barrel finish offers a lighter, fruitier, and more elegant profile. Maturation in red wine casks imparts a fresh, rosé character and a balanced harmony of sweetness, spice, and fruity aromas.

item.name: Sild Flören (2018/2023) Single Cask 5 Years
item.description: Sild Flören is distilled in the renowned Lantenhammer distillery in Bavaria and then stored under very special conditions on the North Sea island of Sylt. Rumour has it that part of the maturation process even took place on ships, which gives this whisky a touch of adventure and maritime flair. With its 50.10 % vol. it has plenty of strength without being overpowering.

tasting_notes.nose: As soon as you open the bottle, you are greeted by an intense aroma. Fruity-sweet with a slightly artificial note reminiscent of cherry lollipops, combined with a hint of sultanas and nutty flavours. This is joined by a warm spiciness of slightly burnt wood, which immediately arouses the nose's curiosity.
tasting_notes.palate: The first sip confirms that this whisky is no ordinary representative. The flavour is fruity and berry, with a slightly bitter undertone of tobacco and leather. Walnut notes provide a pleasant depth that is not too heavy, but not too light either. The 50.10 % vol. is noticeable but well integrated - a whisky that shows character without setting the throat on fire.
tasting_notes.finish: In the finish, the Sild Flören shows its spicy, chocolaty side. The fruity notes recede into the background, replaced by fresh tobacco leaves, a pleasant bitterness and a hint of spice.

conclusion.marcel: A whisky that really lifts the spirits with its powerful 50.1% ABV and maritime maturation on Sylt. Fruity, nutty, and with a surprisingly spicy finish, the Sild Flören brings fun to the glass – perfect for those who love character without being overwhelmed.
conclusion.sascha: The Sild Flören Single Cask 5 Years strikes a successful balance between fruity notes and spicy depth, enhanced by its maritime aging. Despite its impressive alcohol strength, the structure remains harmonious and the finish well-balanced, making it an intriguing example of experimental German whiskies.

faq.question: How does maturation on Sylt influence the flavor of the Sild Flören Single Cask?
faq.answer: Maturation on the North Sea island of Sylt imparts a distinctive spiciness and freshness to the whisky through the maritime climate and salty air. These conditions can intensify the aroma and give the whisky a subtle salty, maritime note that sets it apart from other German whiskies.

faq.question: What does 'Single Cask' mean for this whisky and why is it important?
faq.answer: 'Single Cask' means the whisky was bottled from just one single barrel without blending with others. This makes each release unique and allows for a special range of flavors, as the character of the cask comes through more strongly.

faq.question: How should the Sild Flören Single Cask 5 Years be best enjoyed?
faq.answer: Due to its 50.1% alcohol content, it is recommended to drink the whisky neat or with a few drops of water to better unlock the aromas. A room temperature of around 18 to 20 degrees Celsius is ideal to fully appreciate the fruity and spicy notes.

item.name: Compass Box Delos
item.description: The Compass Box Delos is a remarkable blend that captures the essence of 20-years matured grain whisky and selected malts. At 49% alcohol by volume, it showcases the craftsmanship of independent bottler Compass Box. The whisky contains ingredients from the closed Imperial distillery and Glen Elgin - a dream for connoisseurs seeking historical and flavourful depth.

tasting_notes.nose: The Delos impresses with a wonderfully fresh bouquet from the very first sniff. Intense notes of ripe pear, honey and vanilla dominate, accompanied by delicate oak and a slightly resinous undertone. A hint of lemongrass and floral accents, combined with maltiness à la butter biscuits, creates an almost tropical atmosphere: dragon fruit, apricot and pineapple combine to create a waxy, elegant flavour that invites you to savour it.
tasting_notes.palate: The sophistication continues seamlessly on the palate. The Compass Box Delos is ultra-creamy, soft and round, with an impressive complexity. Old oak provides an elegant structure, while the fruity pear and citrus notes retain freshness. Floral nuances and subtle herbal spice add depth, while the cereal notes pay homage to bourbon maturation.
tasting_notes.finish: The medium-length finish leaves a pleasant echo of intense oak. Green tea rounds off the experience and adds a slightly bitter note that emphasises the full-bodied character of Delos.

conclusion.marcel: With the Compass Box Delos, you experience a blend that’s simply a joy – creamy, fruity, and full of character, inviting both enjoyment and admiration. For anyone looking for a whisky with charm and a touch of history, it’s a genuine highlight not to be missed.
conclusion.sascha: The Delos impresses with its well-balanced interplay between creamy texture and complex structure, where the influences of the closed Imperial distillery are subtle yet distinctly present. A very balanced blend that delivers technically with its fine oak spice and multifaceted fruit notes.

faq.question: What makes the Compass Box Delos special compared to other blended whiskies?
faq.answer: The Compass Box Delos combines 20-years grain whisky with selected malt whiskies, including rare stocks from the closed Imperial distillery and Glen Elgin. This blend gives it an exceptional depth and complexity that is rare among many blends.

faq.question: How does the high alcohol strength of 49% affect the taste and aroma of the Delos?
faq.answer: At 49% ABV, the Delos offers an intense aromatic profile and a full-bodied texture without coming across as harsh. The higher strength amplifies the fruity, floral, and woody notes, creating a lively and complex tasting experience.

faq.question: What role does bourbon maturation play in the character of the Compass Box Delos?
faq.answer: Bourbon cask maturation contributes the grainy, vanilla, and honeyed nuances that give the Delos its creaminess and sweetness. At the same time, aging in oak barrels provides the elegant structure and spicy wood notes that define the whisky.

item.name: St. Kilian Single Cask – Irish Style – Unpeated
item.description: With the "Irish Style," Germany's St. Kilian Distillery proves it can craft more than just heavily peated whisky. This single cask release was made from 100% Pilsner malt and distilled with maximum reflux – resembling a triple distillation. The spirit then matured for five years in a 190-liter ex-Jack Daniel’s Tennessee whiskey barrel. Combining technical precision with flavor finesse, this whisky promises a unique experience.

image.alt: St. Kilian Single Cask – Irish Style – Unpeated

tasting_notes.nose: A fresh opening with intense fruity notes. Citrus aromas like apple peel and lemon zest blend with a lightly perfumed floral hint. The alcohol stays discreetly in the background.
tasting_notes.palate: Ultra smooth yet remarkably fresh. A touch of fruity funk runs through, with pineapple blossoms and green apples standing out. The balance between sweetness and zesty freshness is pleasantly maintained.
tasting_notes.finish: Medium-long with fruity accents of apple and lemon zest, accompanied by gentle oak. The aftertaste is clean, leaving a harmonious overall impression.

conclusion.marcel: This St. Kilian Irish Style is a genuine delight, whose lightness and fruity freshness instantly lift the mood. Despite its 57.4% ABV, it feels pleasantly smooth and invites relaxed sipping – almost an elegant surprise coup from Germany.
conclusion.sascha: The bottling impresses with its precise balance between fruitiness and subtle wood notes, with the high alcohol content seamlessly integrated. Technically cleanly distilled and featuring a fine structural balance, this whisky offers a successful interpretation of the Irish style.

faq.question: What does 'Irish Style' mean for this St. Kilian Single Cask Whisky?
faq.answer: 'Irish Style' refers to the production method: the whisky is made from 100% Pilsner malt and distilled with maximum reflux, resembling a triple distillation. This results in an especially soft and fresh character, typical of Irish whiskies.

faq.question: How does the ex-Jack Daniel’s Tennessee Whiskey cask influence the flavor?
faq.answer: The 190-liter ex-Jack Daniel’s cask contributes mild wood aromas and a subtle sweetness that complement the whisky’s fruity and floral notes in a flattering way. The cask maturation imparts a gentle oak structure and a harmonious finish.

faq.question: Why is the 57.4% alcohol content relevant for this whisky?
faq.answer: At 57.4% ABV, this is a cask strength bottling that offers more intense aromas and greater complexity than standard whiskies. Despite the high alcohol level, the whisky remains soft and fresh, thanks to careful distillation and maturation.

item.name: Macallan 12 Years Double Cask
item.description: Macallan is one of the most renowned whisky brands globally, often associated with luxury and prestige. The Macallan 12 Years Double Cask combines American and European oak, both seasoned with sherry. Its elegant presentation emphasizes the brand's premium aspiration.

tasting_notes.nose: The first impression is characterized by tangy red fruits and sweet honey. Light hay notes, vanilla, and mild oak follow. A hint of baking powder, cinnamon, and candle wax rounds off the aromatic profile, adding a subtle spiciness.
tasting_notes.palate: Smooth and creamy, the Macallan 12 Double Cask unfolds on the palate. Honey sweetness dominates at first, followed by grassy tones, plum, and a pinch of white pepper. Cinnamon adds a warming spice that complements the overall impression harmoniously.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is rather short, with light oak spice and gentle dryness. A subtle wood note appears but fades quickly.

conclusion.marcel: If you're looking for a gentle, straightforward whisky that scores with honeyed sweetness and warm sherry notes, the Macallan 12 Year Double Cask will bring you joy. For me, it’s a pleasant companion that doesn’t offer any big surprises but precisely because of that fits nicely in the glass – a solid everyday whisky with character that doesn’t annoy.
conclusion.sascha: The Macallan 12 Year Double Cask shows a solid balance between fruit, sweetness, and light spice, yet remains rather unassuming and not very complex. Its short finish and mild aftertaste create an accessible but somewhat flat overall impression, appealing especially to beginners seeking a harmoniously structured Speyside whisky.

faq.question: What does 'Double Cask' mean in Macallan 12 Year Double Cask?
faq.answer: 'Double Cask' refers to maturation in two different types of casks: American and European oak, both of which were previously seasoned with sherry. This combination gives the whisky a balanced mix of sweet and spicy aromas.

faq.question: How does the Macallan 12 Year Double Cask differ in taste from other Macallan 12 Year expressions?
faq.answer: The Double Cask is characterized by the blend of American and European oak, which imparts a distinctive honeyed sweetness and subtle spice. Compared to purely sherry-cask matured variants, it offers a softer, creamier texture with fruity and vanilla notes.

faq.question: Is the Macallan 12 Year Double Cask suitable for whisky beginners?
faq.answer: Yes, with its moderate 40% ABV and gentle, creamy flavor, this whisky is well suited for newcomers. The combination of sweet honey and fruit notes along with mild spice makes it an enjoyable introduction to the world of single malts.

item.name: Ardmore 15 Years (2007/2023) Mouton bouillie Cask 66.239 - The Scotch Malt Whisky Society
item.description: In the heart of the Scottish Highlands, where the air is infused with peat and heather, the Ardmor Mouton Bouillie was created, a whisky that has not only been matured for 15 years, but also combines the essence of the Ardmore distillery with the eccentricity of an independent bottler such as the Scotch Malt Whisky Society.

image.alt: Ardmore 2007/2023 15 Years Mouton bouillie Cask 66.239 - The Scotch Malt Whisky Society

tasting_notes.nose: From the very first breath, this whisky transports the senses to a freshly heated sauna, where the warm wood fills the room. A hint of ripe pears and freshly cut hay mingle with an invigorating note of green tea. It's like walking across a freshly mown meadow in summer, while the clear mountain air refreshes the spirit.
tasting_notes.palate: The whisky is oily and smooth on the tongue, with a sweet honey note reminiscent of dark forest honey. But then it becomes deeper, almost shadier - the sweet notes give way to a hint of pine cones and a slightly mineral freshness.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is not intrusive, but medium-long and elegant. A slight tingle is reminiscent of the spicy intensity of the Highlands, while the sweet pear note lingers discreetly.

conclusion.marcel: A whisky that immediately wins your heart with its warm wooden sauna character and sweet honey notes. The combination of freshness and depth makes it a charming companion that leaves you wanting more – perfect for relaxed evenings with good food.
conclusion.sascha: The Ardmore 2007 impresses with its balanced interplay between sweet honey notes and mineral freshness, supported by an oily, smooth texture. Its complex structure and elegant, medium-length finish make it a high-quality Highland whisky with technical finesse.

faq.question: What does 'Mouton Bouillie' mean in the name of this Ardmore whisky?
faq.answer: 'Mouton Bouillie' is a French term that translates to 'boiled sheep.' In the whisky world, it refers to a traditional malt treatment method where the malt is steamed with hot water after drying. This process imparts a distinctive, slightly sweet and complex flavor profile to the whisky.

faq.question: How does cask number 66.239 influence the flavor of the Ardmore 2007/2023?
faq.answer: Cask 66.239 is a Mouton Bouillie cask that gives the whisky its characteristic combination of sweet honey notes and mineral freshness. The long maturation period of 15 years in this special cask ensures a balanced harmony between oily smoothness and the spicy intensity typical of the Highlands.

faq.question: Why does this Ardmore whisky have such a high alcohol content of 57.9% ABV?
faq.answer: The high alcohol content of 57.9% ABV indicates that the whisky was bottled without coloring and without dilution, often referred to as cask strength. This preserves the original aromas and allows the drinker to add water as desired to explore the flavor nuances individually.

item.name: Single Malt Miyagikyo
item.description: The Miyagikyo Single Malt hails from the Miyagikyo Distillery in Japan, renowned for its slow distillation process and innovative methods. This whisky is distilled using a combination of traditional pot stills and "giant stills," with a long (bended) line arm that ensures a particularly gentle distillation. With just 5 ppm (parts per million), the Miyagikyo has a subtle, almost invisible smoky presence that complements the flavors without dominating them. The whisky comes in at 45% ABV and does not carry an age statement, speaking to its modern yet well-structured expression.

tasting_notes.nose: The first impression of the Miyagikyo is marked by a pleasant sweetness, with notes of banana and delicate vanilla. The smoky character is subtle but still noticeable, adding depth to the aroma. There's also a slightly earthy note reminiscent of potatoes—a mix of earth and peel, giving the nose a wild edge. Alongside this, malty undertones form a solid base for the aroma.
tasting_notes.palate: On the palate, the Miyagikyo is exceptionally creamy and smooth. The flavors unfold with a juicy, lightly fruity profile, with apple and a fine fruit sweetness standing out. There's a pleasant mix of sweet and lightly bitter tones that combine for a fascinating flavor experience. The smoky influence adds extra body to the whisky, while grain and a touch of brown sugar come through. The finish brings a slight bitterness that isn't overpowering and nicely rounds off the whisky's complexity.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish of the Miyagikyo is medium-length, with a mild but still present smoky character. It follows with a robust oak spice that gives the whisky structure, along with a hint of white pepper, tickling the palate and keeping the finish lively.

conclusion.marcel: A whisky that simply delights with its creamy sweetness and subtle smoke – perfect for relaxed evenings without any fuss. The slight earthiness gives the Miyagikyo a charming character that doesn’t come across as too polished but remains lively. For fans of a gentle yet characterful single malt, this dram is definitely worth a sip.
conclusion.sascha: The Miyagikyo impresses with its balanced blend of sweet fruit notes, gentle smokiness, and a clear, structured harmony. The subtle bitterness on the finish adds a pleasant complexity without disrupting the overall balance. A technically cleanly distilled whisky with a modern approach, it stands out especially for its precise flavor profile and fine oak spice.

faq.question: Why does the Miyagikyo Single Malt have no age statement?
faq.answer: The Miyagikyo Single Malt deliberately forgoes an age statement to emphasize quality and flavor. Rather than focusing on a specific age, the distillery combines various casks to create a balanced and modern whisky with a clear structure.

faq.question: How does the low peat level of 5 ppm affect the taste of Miyagikyo?
faq.answer: With only 5 ppm of peat, Miyagikyo is subtly smoky, giving the whisky a delicate depth without overpowering the fruity and sweet aromas. This light smokiness harmoniously complements the fruity notes of apple and banana as well as the malty and spicy nuances.

faq.question: What is the difference between the traditional pot stills and the 'Giant Stills' used in Miyagikyo’s distillation?
faq.answer: The Miyagikyo distillery uses both traditional pot stills and larger 'Giant Stills' equipped with a long, curved distillation arm (Bended Line Arm). This combination allows for an especially gentle distillation that preserves complex aromas and makes the whisky soft and creamy.

item.name: The Westfalian TW74
item.description: The Westfalian Cask 74 from the Northoff distillery is a remarkable single malt whisky, characterised by its 10-year maturation and an exceptional cask strength of 64.10% vol. Produced in Germany, distilled in 2013 and matured in a Macallan cask until 2024, it offers an impressive variety of flavours that are reflected in every drop. We are extremely excited!

tasting_notes.nose: From the very first whiff, a distinctive cold, almost pavement-like smoke rises to the nose, paving the way for a dominant nuttiness. The flavours of roasted hazelnuts and almonds merge with a fruity magic that is mainly carried by ripe plums. This composition is accompanied by a hint of old leather, nutmeg and the spicy scent of old wood - as if you were sitting in a decades-old study surrounded by books and history.
tasting_notes.palate: The first sip unfolds oily and full-bodied in the mouth, a sensual texture that immediately reaches every corner of the palate. The cold iodine smoke meets a fruity sweetness that is intense and multi-layered. The notes of plums and dates combine with leather and tobacco to offer a deep, almost mystical flavour experience. A slight saltiness emphasises the whole and gives the whisky a maritime note reminiscent of the rugged coast of Scotland - and all this from the heart of Germany. With a few drops of water, the profile becomes even more complex: Morello cherries come to the fore, while the smoke gently recedes and gives way to a wild mix of berries. It's like discovering a new whisky in an old guise.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is of medium length, with the smoke still present and accompanied by fruity notes of plum and cherry. It is a finish that does not end abruptly, but fades gently, like a slow sunset over the hills - warm, calming and full of depth.

conclusion.marcel: The Westfalian TW74 captivates with its smoky depth and fruity accents – a genuine pleasure that leaves you wanting more. This combination of plum jam and nutty warmth delivers pure drinking enjoyment without ever becoming dull. For anyone seeking a whisky with character and heart, this dram is a bullseye.
conclusion.sascha: With a precise balance between intense cask strength and complex aromatics, The Westfalian TW74 impresses across the board. The delicate interplay of smoke, fruit, and spicy notes reveals a sophisticated structure that never overwhelms, even at 64.1% ABV. A technically remarkable single malt from Germany that stands confidently alongside top Scottish distillates.

faq.question: How does the cask strength of 64.1% ABV influence the taste of The Westfalian TW74?
faq.answer: The high cask strength of 64.1% ABV delivers an intense and powerful flavor profile rich in aromas. Adding water can expand the range of flavors and bring out nuances such as Morello cherries and wild berries more distinctly.

faq.question: What special characteristics does maturation in a Macallan cask bring to this German single malt?
faq.answer: Maturation in a former Macallan cask imparts The Westfalian TW74 with a complex combination of fruity notes like plum and date alongside spicy and woody elements. This cask choice provides exceptional depth, blending German distilling craftsmanship with Scottish barrel tradition.

faq.question: Why does The Westfalian TW74, despite its German origin, have maritime notes reminiscent of the Scottish coast?
faq.answer: The maritime note arises from a salty nuance in the flavor that recalls Scotland’s rugged coastline. These aromas may be influenced by the peat smoke used and the cask maturation, demonstrating that German whiskies can develop diverse and unexpected flavor profiles despite their origin.

item.name: St. Kilian Bud Spencer - The Legend - Rauchig - Batch 03
item.description: Bud Spencer embodies unyielding justice and uncompromising boldness—traits that resonate in the smoky edition of "The Legend," a German single malt whisky crafted by St. Kilian Distillery. Aged in Italian Amarone red wine casks and ex-bourbon barrels, this whisky combines the robust character of Scottish malt (80 ppm smoke) with youthful freshness. Does it live up to Bud Spencer's legendary name?

image.alt: St. Kilian Bud Spencer - The Legend - Rauchig - Batch 03

tasting_notes.nose: A cold, smoky aroma immediately takes center stage, reminiscent of smoldering campfire embers. Beneath the smoke lies a layer of green apple and a fleeting hint of exotic fruits. Over time, vanilla and caramel emerge, providing a balancing sweetness.
tasting_notes.palate: On the palate, the whisky delivers a bold spiciness with a kick of white pepper. Its texture is creamy yet dry, complemented by a herbal freshness reminiscent of menthol. The smoke dominates, leaving little room for fruity notes, though a faint hint of banana makes an appearance. The whisky’s youthful edge is noticeable but not overpowering.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is robustly spicy with prominent oak notes. The smoke lingers persistently, accompanied by a subtle exotic fruit residue and a touch of caramel. An intense and smoky conclusion.

conclusion.marcel: This smoky St. Kilian Bud Spencer impresses with its powerful presence and straightforward drinking pleasure – perfect for anyone who likes their whisky bold and direct. The Amarone note stays in the background, which only makes the dram feel more honest and approachable. A whisky that delights with charm, character, and rough edges, doing justice to Bud Spencer’s rugged persona.
conclusion.sascha: The balance between smoke intensity and spicy freshness in this St. Kilian Bud Spencer is solid, though the whisky still lacks a fully integrated Amarone cask influence to refine its structure. The youthful freshness is quite evident, somewhat limiting the complexity, yet the finish remains powerful and long-lasting. For fans of smoky German single malts, Batch 03 offers a technically sound, if still developing, composition.

faq.question: How do the Amarone red wine casks influence the flavor of this whisky?
faq.answer: Maturation in Italian Amarone red wine casks imparts a subtle fruit note and complex depth that complement the smoky aromas. This results in delicate exotic fruit nuances and a slightly sweet undertone in the flavor.

faq.question: What does the 80 ppm smoke level mean for this whisky?
faq.answer: A smoke level of 80 ppm (phenol parts per million) indicates that the whisky has a strong, intense smoky flavor, comparable to heavily peated Scottish malts. This makes it especially appealing to lovers of smoky whiskies.

faq.question: Why does this St. Kilian whisky have no age statement, and how does that affect the flavor?
faq.answer: The whisky is a so-called No-Age-Statement (NAS) release, meaning no minimum age is specified. This allows the distillery to blend casks of varying maturation periods to create a youthful, fresh character with intense aromas, without being restricted to a specific age.

item.name: St. Kilian Sie nannten ihn Spencer – Unpeated
item.description: „Sie nannten ihn Spencer – Unpeated“ from the St. Kilian Distillery is more than just a whisky – it’s a tribute to the unforgettable film legend Bud Spencer. Aged in ex-Marsala Ambra fortified wine casks, this three-years single malt with 59.1% ABV offers an intense, multi-layered experience. Its label in Braille reflects the special attention to detail and highlights the unique character of this whisky.

image.alt: St. Kilian Sie nannten ihn Spencer – Unpeated

tasting_notes.nose: From the first sniff, a bouquet of intense red fruits reminiscent of juicy plum jam unfolds. Enticing notes of dark chocolate and a hint of macadamia nuts follow. A subtle wine aroma adds depth, while a touch of mint provides a refreshing twist. This complex aromatic profile is a true fruit explosion with harmonious elegance.
tasting_notes.palate: On the palate, the whisky reveals a bold, oily, and sweet character. Dominant flavors of dark grapes and red wine merge with a rich herbal spiciness. Dried fruits and bitter chocolate add further depth and balance. The texture remains velvety and pleasantly smooth.
tasting_notes.finish: The medium-long finish is warming and slightly sticky, accompanied by intense oak spice and a hint of lovage. Red fruits linger until the end, giving the whisky a charming freshness.

conclusion.marcel: This St. Kilian impresses with a powerful intensity and a hearty character that turns every sip into an experience – just like Bud Spencer himself. Despite its youthful maturity, the choice of cask provides an exciting depth and a fruity fireworks display that leaves you wanting more. A whisky that simply brings joy with its personality and warmth.
conclusion.sascha: The harmonious balance between the intense wine aromas and the spicy oak maturation reveals an impressive structural profile that is remarkable for a young single malt. The complex array of aromas combines with a velvety texture to create a well-rounded overall impression, which could gain even more complexity with extended aging. Technically solid and full of character, but with room to grow.

faq.question: Why is this whisky unpeated, even though many St. Kilian whiskies are peated?
faq.answer: "They Called Him Spencer – Unpeated" deliberately avoids peat smoke to clearly highlight the fruity notes and the aromas from the Marsala Ambra cask. This way, the character of the whisky takes center stage without the typical smoky notes.

faq.question: How do the Marsala Ambra fortified wine casks influence the taste of this whisky?
faq.answer: The former Marsala Ambra casks impart the whisky with its intense fruitiness and sweet spiciness. They bring notes of red fruits, dark chocolate, and a subtle wine character that add depth and complexity to the whisky.

faq.question: What does the Braille label on the bottle mean?
faq.answer: The Braille label on the bottle is a special design feature that highlights the whisky as a tribute to Bud Spencer. It symbolizes attention and respect for special details and underscores the unique character of the whisky.

item.name: anCnoc 18 Years (2024)
item.description: The Knockdhu Distillery, home of anCnoc whisky, is located in Scotland’s Speyside region. Since its founding in 1894, it has combined traditional craftsmanship with a modern approach. Known for its versatile range of releases, the portfolio includes everything from fresh, citrus-driven malts to more robust, wood-forward whiskies. The anCnoc 18 Years stands among the more mature expressions, promising a complex yet approachable tasting experience.

tasting_notes.nose: From the first nosing, the whisky's 18 years in oak casks are unmistakable. A warm spiciness rises, underscored by sweet almonds and dried orange peel. Light leather and subtle banana chips add an elegant depth. In the background, a touch of honey rounds out the aromatic spectrum.
tasting_notes.palate: On the palate, the anCnoc 18 Years is creamy yet dry. European oak asserts itself with prominent tannins, giving the whisky structure and fullness. Fruity notes of apricot and juicy raisins meet delicate milk chocolate. Dates provide a gentle sweetness, harmoniously blending with the whisky’s woody foundation.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is medium-length and warming, characterized by toasted oak and a pleasant spiciness from white pepper. A hint of raisins lingers on the tongue, accompanied by a touch of baking cocoa. A satisfying finale that invites the next sip.

conclusion.marcel: The anCnoc 18 Years is a true delight for anyone seeking a whisky that is both spicy and gentle on the finish. The fine balance of sweet fruits and spicy oak makes it the perfect companion for relaxed evenings – simply a delicious easy sipper with character and heart.
conclusion.sascha: With its clearly defined balance between the distinctive tannins of European oak and fruity apricot notes, the anCnoc 18 Years offers a well-rounded aromatic profile. The harmonious combination of creaminess, dryness, and a spicy finish makes this Speyside whisky a technically impressive and complex classic.

faq.question: How does the 18-year maturation influence the taste of the anCnoc 18 Years?
faq.answer: The 18-year maturation in European oak casks imparts a complex structure with pronounced tannins that provide fullness. This allows warm spice notes, sweet almonds, dried orange peel, as well as fruity aromas like apricot and raisins to develop, creating a balanced tasting experience.

faq.question: What distinguishes anCnoc whiskies from other Speyside distillates?
faq.answer: anCnoc whiskies come from the Knockdhu distillery and combine traditional craftsmanship with a modern approach. Compared to many Speyside whiskies, they are often more versatile in style, ranging from fresh, citrus-driven malts to more robust, wood-forward expressions like the 18 Years, which stands out through its oak spice and fruity depth.

faq.question: How should one best enjoy the anCnoc 18 Years to fully experience its aromas?
faq.answer: The anCnoc 18 Years reveals its full aroma best when enjoyed neat at room temperature. A slight dilution with a few drops of water can help release the complex notes of leather, honey, and spices more effectively. It also pairs well with mild cheese or dried fruits to complement the fruity and woody nuances.

item.name: Ben Nevis 10 Years (2014/2024) - James Eadie
item.description: The Ben Nevis Distillery has long been a hot tip among connoisseurs when it comes to Highland whiskies with a strong character. With the Ben Nevis 10 years 2014/2024, independent bottler James Eadie presents us with a real sherry bomb - at 58.9% ABV. A whisky that not only impresses with its strength, but also offers subtle nuances. Will it deliver what the key figures promise?

tasting_notes.nose: The first impression? A sultana explosion straight to the face - like opening a bag of Christmas cheer! Caramel and soft oak emphasise this sweet and spicy base, while earthier notes of tobacco and brown sugar tickle the nose. If you sniff closely, you will even detect a hint of peanuts, which gives the fragrance a nutty depth.
tasting_notes.palate: The whisky is viscous, almost syrupy on the tongue - a mouthfeel that triggers feelings of pure luxury. Malty notes dominate at the beginning before a symphony of sultanas, dates and candied apple unfolds. A touch of caramel provides a pleasant sweetness, while the nutty peanut flavour provides a contrast. Anyone who enjoys rich, full-bodied whiskies could go into raptures here.
tasting_notes.finish: Medium length with roasted almonds and cloves. Candied fruit adds a spicy twist, and there is a sudden flash of tropical freshness from mango and papaya.

conclusion.marcel: This Ben Nevis really packs a punch – the sherry explosion and the full cask strength make it a genuine experience for connoisseurs who enjoy intensity and character. A whisky that’s a lot of fun and truly comes alive when paired with dark chocolate or fruitcake. Highly recommended for anyone looking for a robust, lively Highland dram!
conclusion.sascha: The Ben Nevis 10 Year 2014/2024 presents itself as a well-balanced sherry expression with impressive depth and structure. The combination of sweet and spicy notes, carried by the cask strength, delivers a harmonious mouthfeel and a complex finish. A technically convincing Highland whisky with a clear cask signature and a solid finish.

faq.question: How does the sherry cask influence the flavor profile of the Ben Nevis 10 Year 2014/2024?
faq.answer: The sherry cask imparts the whisky’s characteristic sweet and spicy notes with intense aromas of raisins, caramel, and candied fruits. At the same time, it provides nutty and spicy nuances like roasted almonds and cloves, making the flavor complex and multi-layered.

faq.question: Why does the Ben Nevis 10 Year 2014/2024 have such a high alcohol content of 58.9%?
faq.answer: The high alcohol content results from the cask strength bottling, which preserves the whisky in its pure and powerful form. This ensures that the intense aromas and full flavor spectrum remain intact, which is especially important for a sherry-forward whisky like this to deliver an intense tasting experience.

faq.question: What distinguishes the Ben Nevis 10 Year 2014/2024 from other Highland whiskies?
faq.answer: This whisky combines the typical maltiness of the Highlands with an exceptional sherry note and a complex aroma profile of raisins, nuts, and tropical fruits. Its syrupy texture and medium-length, spicy finish make it a particularly rich and characterful representative of its region.

item.name: Teaninich 11 Years Malaga Cask Finish - James Eadie
item.description: For whisky lovers looking for an easily accessible yet exciting bottling, the Teaninich 11 Years Malaga Cask Finish from James Eadie offers a successful overall package. This Highland whisky is a harmonious combination of fruity and creamy notes, which are given an exciting twist by the special finish in Malaga casks.

image.alt: Teaninich 11 Years Malaga Cask Finish - James Eadie

tasting_notes.nose: A potpourri of fruit flavours unfolds from the very first nosing: mango, grapefruit and peach characterise the opening and give the whisky a pleasant tropical freshness. These notes are accompanied by a hint of caramel and a nutty flavour of almond and marzipan. The light hints of milk chocolate are particularly charming, adding a subtle sweetness. An inviting start that is uncomplicated and harmonious.
tasting_notes.palate: On the palate, the Teaninich develops a pleasant freshness, carried by green grapes and red fruits, which give it a lively, fruity flavour. The whisky is wonderfully full-bodied and has a warming presence. The base is reminiscent of fresh cereals - perhaps even a slightly sweet Bircher muesli - with a slightly straw-like and toasted undertone. This combination gives the whisky structure and depth without making it too heavy.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is long and balanced, with an unexpected hint of cheese curd that gives the whisky a creamy and slightly sweet component. Light tannins add a touch of dryness, while wet coffee powder and herbal tea add a spicy finish. A successful finish that is not too intrusive and makes you want to take the next sip.

conclusion.marcel: A whisky that’s simply fun and spreads good cheer with its fruity lightness. The Malaga finish adds a charming twist that neither overwhelms nor bores – perfect for relaxed evenings or as a sweet companion to desserts. Not the most complex Highland, but precisely for that reason so likeable and approachable.
conclusion.sascha: The Teaninich 11 Year Malaga Cask Finish impresses with a balanced harmony between fruity notes and a creamy texture that the Malaga cask expresses with precision. The structure remains clear and well-defined, without being weighed down by excessive complexity – ideal for connoisseurs seeking a harmonious Highland whisky with a solid finish.

faq.question: How does the Malaga Cask Finish influence the taste of the Teaninich 11 Year?
faq.answer: The Malaga cask finish imparts a distinctive fruity sweetness and creamy notes to the whisky, which reveal themselves in hints of milk chocolate, marzipan, and a subtly sweet quark cheesecake on the finish. This secondary maturation adds extra complexity and highlights the fruity aromas.

faq.question: What flavor profiles can one expect from the Teaninich 11 Year Malaga Cask Finish?
faq.answer: The whisky presents tropical fruits such as mango, grapefruit, and peach on the nose, accompanied by caramel and nutty notes. On the palate, green grape, red fruits, and a cereal-like base with straw and roasted undertones dominate. The finish is long, creamy, and spicy, with notes of quark cheesecake, wet coffee, and herbal tea.

faq.question: Who is the Teaninich 11 Year Malaga Cask Finish particularly suited for?
faq.answer: This whisky is ideal for enthusiasts seeking a balanced Highland whisky with fruity and creamy notes. It appeals both to beginners who prefer an uncomplicated flavor and to seasoned lovers who appreciate an exciting finish and complex aromas.

item.name: The English Whisky Infinity Cask - Distillery Exclusive (2024) Batch 3
item.description: St. George’s Distillery in Norfolk demonstrates the potential of English whisky with Infinity Cask Batch 3. This distillery-exclusive, single cask whisky is unique, non age stated, and bottled at 48.7% ABV. Its philosophy of continuously topping up the cask with whisky from different cask types promises a layered and intriguing tasting experience.

image.alt: The English Whisky Infinity Cask - Distillery Exclusive 2024 Batch 3

tasting_notes.nose: Fresh and crisp, reminiscent of a spring meadow. Subtle hints of forest honey and beeswax add a sweet, natural depth without overpowering the profile.
tasting_notes.palate: The initial impression is creamy and mild, with a pleasant sweetness of honey and sugary undertones. Fruity notes of apricot and peach gently come to the fore. A slight warmth develops over time, harmonizing the overall flavor.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is short but delightful. Sweet and fruity notes linger briefly, followed by a light dryness with a touch of white pepper, creating an elegant conclusion.

conclusion.marcel: This Infinity Cask is like a lively stroll full of surprises – sweet, fruity, and with a charming hint of roughness that gives it character. It’s simply fun to sip your way through the flavors without it ever feeling too complicated. For anyone looking for a whisky that stays vibrant yet remains pleasantly approachable, this comes highly recommended.
conclusion.sascha: The Infinity Cask impresses with its balanced interplay of sweetness, fruitiness, and subtle spice notes, even though the somewhat rough texture keeps it from achieving perfect harmony. Its clear structure and moderate finish reveal an interesting cask strategy that still leaves room for greater finesse. For connoisseurs who appreciate complex English single casks, this whisky offers a solid, if not flawless, experience.

faq.question: What does it mean that the Infinity Cask Batch 3 is a "Single Cask" whisky without an age statement?
faq.answer: A "Single Cask" whisky comes from just one individual barrel, making each bottling unique. Since the Infinity Cask Batch 3 has no age statement, the whisky wasn’t classified by a minimum age but was selected based on the distillery team’s taste and philosophy.

faq.question: How does the method of continuously refilling the cask with different types of whisky affect the flavor?
faq.answer: Continuously refilling the cask with various whisky types creates a layered flavor profile that can subtly change with each bottling. This method results in a complex range of aromas, from fruity to spicy notes, keeping the whisky’s character lively and dynamic.

faq.question: What role does the Norfolk region play in the production of this whisky?
faq.answer: Norfolk, home to St. George’s Distillery, offers a temperate climate and a pristine environment that positively influence the whisky’s maturation. The regional water quality and air conditions contribute to the development of the distinctive aromas found in Infinity Cask Batch 3.

item.name: Dailuaine (2012/2022) - Malts of Scotland
item.description: You don't see Dailuaine bottlings that often. So we are all the more delighted to once again have a dram from this distillery in our glass. The single malt matured for around 10 years in a bourbon hogshead was bottled, as always unfiltered and uncoloured, in 2022 by Malts of Scotland as a Corona Edition.

tasting_notes.nose: The Dailuaine reveals its complexity from the very first inhalation. A malty base is accompanied by sultanas soaked in rum and a hint of cream and custard. The fragrance is dense and rich, almost heavy. Another inhale and the flavours of buttery red apple crumble, pear and peach syrup emerge. The olfactory experience is rounded off with notes of honey, pear and butter biscuits. A hint of cream caramel and sultanas gives the whisky a pleasant sweetness, which is complemented by hints of apple sauce, vanilla and coconut shell.
tasting_notes.palate: On the palate, the Single Malt unfolds with an oily, rich texture. The interplay of apple-pear compote and vanilla custard forms the prelude, followed by a subtle herbal note and a hint of wine gum. The lemon zest adds a refreshing citrus note that harmonises perfectly with the buttery apple crumble. White grape, rum and stewed pear contribute to the fruity depth, while hints of varnished wood and ginger add a spicy complexity. This bottling offers a rare balance between fruit, sweetness and spice that we really like.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish of the Speysider is long-lasting and warming. A gentle spiciness, dominated by pepper and ginger, leaves a pleasant aftertaste. Mixed fruits marinated in rum, apple crumble and a subtle woody note round off the experience.

conclusion.marcel: A genuine pleasure that’s fun and full of character – the Dailuaine 2012 from Malts of Scotland impresses with its fruity sweetness and buttery apple notes. For me, it’s a bottling you’re happy to pour yourself another glass of, even if it doesn’t quite reach the absolute highlights.
conclusion.sascha: Technically clean and well balanced, this Speyside presents a complex fruit structure and a successful balance between sweetness and spice. Despite the high strength, the Dailuaine 2012 feels somewhat conventional and could offer more depth and tension.

faq.question: Why is the Dailuaine 2012/2022 from Malts of Scotland special as a Corona Edition?
