This composition fits the definition of a wheat whisky, which is characterized by a softer and often fruitier flavor profile compared to pure malt barley whiskies.

faq.question: What is the recommended drinking temperature for the Finch Distillers Choice Barrique R to best express its aromas?
faq.answer: The Finch Distillers Choice Barrique R reveals its fruity and floral notes best at a drinking temperature between 16 and 18 degrees Celsius. At this temperature, the creamy texture is preserved and the complex aromas of raspberry, vanilla, and oak come fully to the fore.

item.name: Teaninich (2008/2021) - Signatory Vintage
item.description: The Teaninich distillery, not far from Alness in the Highlands, shares its neighbourhood with top-class distilleries such as Dalmore and Glenmorangie - you could almost say it lives in a really posh area. Right next door is Teaninich Castle, which was once renovated by the distillery's founder and now shines as a chic hotel and restaurant.

tasting_notes.nose: Ricola lemon and herb sweets hit your nose as soon as you take a whiff - like spring in a Swiss Alpine meadow. The whole thing is accompanied by honey, lime zest and a fruit basket filled with pears and apples. Fruity, floral and somehow a little tart.
tasting_notes.palate: In the mouth, the whisky first greets us with a grassy and floral flavour - as if you had just walked through a freshly mown meadow. Suddenly the orange appears and gives the whole thing a sunny touch before a herbaceous tartness takes hold. Anyone who thinks this sounds too good is mistaken. Because here it comes: a small but subtle toffee attack - sweet caramel fudge meets light tingling effects. Sounds unusual? It is.
tasting_notes.finish: Unfortunately, the Teaninich finishes a little quicker than one would wish - the finish is rather short to medium-long. However, it leaves behind a final greeting of oak spice, vanilla, a hint of pepper and, as if that wasn't enough, a spicy ginger kick.

conclusion.marcel: Light, fresh, and with a surprisingly sweet-bitter spice, the Teaninich 2008/2021 brings good cheer to the palate – ideal for relaxed summer evenings in the garden. Despite its charming lightness, it sometimes lacks depth, and the short finish is a bit disappointing. Still, it’s a fun whisky that leaves you wanting more without demanding too much seriousness.
conclusion.sascha: The Teaninich 2008/2021 displays an interesting balance between fruity freshness and herbal bitterness, with an overall solid but not complex structure. The short finish length and slight aggressiveness on the palate feel somewhat unbalanced, which affects the harmony. A light, summery Highland whisky with potential, but it leaves room for improvement in terms of persistence and depth.

faq.question: How does the location of the Teaninich distillery influence the character of this whisky?
faq.answer: The Teaninich distillery is situated in the Highlands near well-known distilleries like Dalmore and Glenmorangie. The region is known for fresh, often grassy and floral aromas, which are also reflected in the Teaninich 2008/2021. The proximity to nature and the climate promote this distinctive style.

faq.question: What special qualities does the bottling by Signatory Vintage bring?
faq.answer: Signatory Vintage is an independent bottler that often releases limited single casks or special vintage editions. This means the Teaninich 2008/2021 offers a more individual and often higher-quality selection than the distillery’s standard releases, focusing on unadulterated flavors.

faq.question: Why does the finish of the Teaninich 2008/2021 seem relatively short despite its 46% alcohol content?
faq.answer: Despite a solid drinking strength of 46%, the finish of the Teaninich 2008/2021 is rather short to medium in length, which can be attributed to the influence of cask maturation and distillation methods. However, the oak spice and ginger kick ensure the aftertaste remains intense and distinctive.

item.name: Turntable Spirits Track 6: All My Life
item.description: Track 6: All My Life, named after a 2002 Foo Fighters hit, is the most personal release from independent bottler Turntable Spirits to date. This blend combines whiskies from renowned distilleries like GlenAllachie, Balblair, Benriach, Knockdhu, and North British, reflecting the deep collaboration of the Stevenson brothers with these distilleries. The result is a harmonious, yet multi-layered whisky.

image.alt: Turntable Spirits Track 6: All My Life

tasting_notes.nose: Right from the start, you're greeted by the freshness of an orchard: baked apple and a touch of citrus lend a slightly tart opening that merges with darker fruit notes such as raisins and dates. Cinnamon and a hint of leather add depth, while a subtle bitterness of red fruits and a trace of beeswax round out the aromatic profile. This balance of bright and dark notes hints at the complexity of this blend.
tasting_notes.palate: On the palate, the fruity component continues with apple compote, paired with a prominent oak note. Raisins and dates once again make an appearance, combined with an intriguing mix of fresh and aged wood. These wood elements give the whisky a spiciness, accompanied by cinnamon. There's also a hint of caramel, which blends into a creamy texture on the tongue.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish remains distinctly spicy and oak-driven. Orange adds a fruity highlight, while roasted walnuts round off the experience. The finish is medium-length, leaving a satisfying, nutty aftertaste.

conclusion.marcel: Track 6: All My Life is simply fun – the fruity sweetness and creamy texture invite you to enjoy it, even though the bold oak note can sometimes feel a bit too dominant. For anyone looking for a full-bodied, characterful blend, this is a very successful dram with plenty of personality.
conclusion.sascha: A well-balanced blend with a harmonious fruit-wood symbiosis and complex sherry notes, distinguished by its structured oak character that borders on dominance. Despite the somewhat high price, the multilayered complexity and the balanced finish impress on a technically high level.

faq.question: Which distilleries are included in the blend of Track 6: All My Life?
faq.answer: The blend combines whiskies from the distilleries GlenAllachie, Balblair, Benriach, Knockdhu, and North British.

faq.question: How does the age of Track 6: All My Life influence its taste?
faq.answer: Track 6: All My Life is a no-age-statement (NAS) blend. This means the focus is on the harmonious interplay of aromas from various whiskies rather than the maturation time of individual casks.

faq.question: Which flavor notes dominate the tasting of Track 6: All My Life?
faq.answer: The whisky displays a complex aroma profile with fruity notes such as baked apple, citrus, raisins, and dates, complemented by spicy elements like cinnamon, leather, and an oak-forward woody note.

item.name: Nine Springs 10 Years PX Finish
item.description: A German whisky with a promising reputation that epitomises the passion for special drops. This 10-years whisky with a Pedro Ximénez (PX) sherry finish is 46% vol. So how does this single malt from the Number Nine distillery fare in the world of experienced whisky lovers?

tasting_notes.nose: The first impression? A veritable explosion of fragrance. The first thing that hits you in the face is an intense cinnamon bun - but really hello, cinnamon lovers will get their money's worth here. The scent of vanilla, blackberries and caramel resonates wonderfully, while a note of grilled peach rises in the background. The sherry influence is clear, but not overwhelming - rather a cosy, warm cloak.
tasting_notes.palate: In terms of flavour, the Nine Springs goes one better. We really are talking about a sherry bomb here! The sweet honey notes envelop the tongue, accompanied by a juicy peach flavour that is infused with fruity sherry and a hint of caramel. What is particularly striking is that there is no leather or tobacco, as is sometimes found in whiskies with a strong sherry flavour - that would not have been appropriate here. Instead, an almost playful wine gum sweetness creeps in, making the whisky round and pleasant. It is rich, sweet and yet complex enough to make you curious about the next sip.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish does not disappoint. Spicy, slightly crisp on the tongue and with a pleasant oak note, the Nine Springs finishes in style. It remains present for a long time without imposing itself too much. A hint of dark chocolate mixes in at the end - enough to round off the flavour perfectly, but never stale. The whisky leaves a warm, satisfying feeling.

conclusion.marcel: This Nine Springs 10 Year PX Finish is a genuine delight for anyone who loves sweet, fruity sherry notes while still seeking a well-balanced character. It’s not over the top, but brings a playful wine gum sweetness and a warm finish that offers plenty of drinking pleasure – perfect for cozy evenings with friends.
conclusion.sascha: With its remarkable balance between sweet honey and fruity peach notes, the Nine Springs 10 Year PX Finish demonstrates how German single malt can be structured and harmonious. The subtle sherry influence and the elegant, spicy finish make it a solid example of innovative whisky from Germany, without ever feeling overloaded.

faq.question: How does the Pedro Ximénez (PX) sherry finish influence the taste of the Nine Springs 10 Year?
faq.answer: The PX sherry finish imparts a pronounced sweetness with notes of honey, peach, and a fruity sherry complexity. It provides a warm, velvety texture without the typical leather or tobacco notes found in other sherry-finished whiskies.

faq.question: What makes the Nine Springs 10 Year special compared to other German whiskies?
faq.answer: This single malt stands out for its intense cinnamon aroma and the harmonious balance between sweet fruit notes and spicy wood tones. The combination of 10 years of maturation and the special PX finish delivers a complex yet approachable tasting experience.

faq.question: How long is the finish of the Nine Springs 10 Year, and which flavors remain particularly prominent?
faq.answer: The finish is long-lasting and spicy with a subtle oak note. Particularly noticeable are a slight tingling on the tongue and a hint of dark chocolate that pleasantly rounds off the enjoyment.

item.name: Breizh Blended
item.description: When you think of whisky, complex flavours, rich oak barrel notes and a long, smooth finish often come to mind. Well, the Breizh blended whisky has bypassed all these expectations in a remarkable way.

tasting_notes.nose: From the very first whiff, it is clear that Breizh Blended is not about traditional whisky flavours. Instead, the smell is reminiscent of a visit to a child's paradise: Balloons and jelly babies dominate, followed by a light, almost unobtrusive maltiness. The characteristic distillery odour does come through, but it seems to have lost its way. Barrel notes? Virtually non-existent - perhaps the barrels were used for something else instead.
tasting_notes.palate: The first sip confirms what the nose has already surmised. Breizh Blended tastes as if water has been mixed with a hint of whisky flavour. Hardly any character, but a sweet, almost sugary smoothness that floods the palate. However, this ‘smoothness’ lacks any depth. Instead, a flavour reminiscent of soap imposes itself - as if you had accidentally nibbled on a baked block of gummy bears from the dashboard.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is so short that one wonders whether it exists at all. A barely perceptible oak note quickly disappears to make way for a slightly ethanolic, sugary aftertaste. It is as if the whisky is literally running away from itself.

conclusion.marcel: Anyone seeking enjoyment and character would be better off steering clear of the Breizh Blended – here, an overly sweet, almost childishly naive aroma dominates, lacking depth or soul. This is a whisky that evokes more the taste of sweet gummy bears than genuine pleasure, leaving one disappointed.
conclusion.sascha: From a structural standpoint, the Breizh Blended lacks the necessary balance between cask, malt, and alcohol, resulting in a flat, unbalanced composition. The exaggerated sweetness and barely noticeable finish make this blend come across more like an industrially produced product than a true whisky.

faq.question: Why does the Breizh Blended Whisky have hardly any cask notes in its flavor?
faq.answer: The Breizh Blended Whisky shows almost no cask notes because it was apparently matured in very neutral or heavily used barrels that impart little oak or wood character to the whisky. This leads to a rather sweet and mild profile without the typical nuances of oak.

faq.question: What explains the unusual smell of gummy bears and balloons in this whisky?
faq.answer: The aroma of gummy bears and balloons arises from unusual ester-rich compounds and flavor substances formed during production or maturation. Such sweet and artificially seeming aromas are rare in traditional whiskies and suggest a rather experimental or heavily filtered bottling.

faq.question: How does the lack of an age statement affect the taste of the Breizh Blended?
faq.answer: Since the Breizh Blended has no age statement and is presumably young, it lacks the complexity and depth that longer maturation in wooden casks typically provides. The result is a light, sweet whisky with little character and a very short finish.

item.name: Teeling 21 Years - Rising Reserve No. 3 - Ruby Port Cask
item.description: The Teeling 21 Years – Rising Reserve No. 3 is a Single Malt that stands out due to its long maturation period and exceptional cask selection. It initially aged for 18 years in ex-Bourbon barrels made from American white oak before receiving a three-year finish in Ruby Port casks. It is this finish that sets the whisky apart, adding an unmistakable sweetness and fruitiness to its profile. With an ABV of 46%, this Teeling is a true highlight within the portfolio of the Irish distillery.

image.alt: Teeling 21 Years - Rising Reserve No. 3 - Ruby Port Cask

tasting_notes.nose: On the nose, the Teeling 21 Years immediately reveals its complexity. A sweet jam note reminiscent of a mix of apricots and strawberries rises to greet you. These fruity aromas are accompanied by a hint of vanilla before evolving into the scent of a freshly baked apple crumble. In the background, subtle herbal notes of dark chocolate and cinnamon can be detected, along with a trace of orange marmalade and a whiff of lightly toasted oak. This combination creates a pleasant balance and arouses curiosity for the first sip.
tasting_notes.palate: The palate is smooth and creamy. The sweetness of overripe red grapes, almost reminiscent of cherry bubblegum, comes to the forefront. This is followed by warming Christmas spices like cinnamon and cloves, adding a comforting, almost cozy depth to the whisky. The fruit notes from the nose remain present: apple crumble transforms into juicy apple pie, accompanied by exotic mango. Walnut and caramelized brown sugar with a touch of vanilla complete the flavor profile before slightly bitter notes of chocolate and oak coat the mouth. A faint hint of tobacco adds an additional layer of complexity.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is long and multifaceted. Light citrus notes mingle with a sweet, dry caramel nuance. This is accompanied by a distinct woody note and elegant oak tannins that linger until the end. A light, tingling freshness and a hint of fresh grass round off the experience, making the Teeling 21 Years a harmonious work of art.

conclusion.marcel: With its sweet fruitiness and velvety texture, the Teeling 21 Year – Rising Reserve No. 3 is a genuine pleasure that delights and indulges the senses. Those who enjoy sweet, complex whiskies will find here a successful combination of a port wine finish and ripe fruit notes – simply a charming Irish dram that leaves you wanting more.
conclusion.sascha: The Teeling 21 Year impresses with its balanced interplay between sweet port wine notes and well-integrated wood and spice aromas. Its complex structure, featuring elegant tannins and a fresh, lightly spicy touch, makes it a technically accomplished single malt that, despite its intensity, feels harmonious and thoughtfully crafted.

faq.question: What special characteristics does the Ruby Port Cask Finish bring to the Teeling 21 Year?
faq.answer: The three-year finish in ruby port casks imparts a pronounced sweetness and fruity notes, especially of red grapes and berries, which clearly distinguish the tasting experience from classic bourbon barrel maturations.

faq.question: How does the combination of bourbon barrels and port wine casks influence the character of this whisky?
faq.answer: The 18 years of maturation in bourbon barrels provides vanilla and oak aromas, while the subsequent ruby port finish adds fruity, jammy notes and a velvety sweetness, giving the whisky a complex and well-balanced flavor profile.

faq.question: Is the Teeling 21 Year – Rising Reserve No. 3 suitable for whisky beginners?
faq.answer: With 46% ABV and a complex aromatic profile of fruitiness, spices, and wood notes, this whisky is more suited to experienced connoisseurs who appreciate intense and multi-layered whiskies.

item.name: Evermann Wilhelm Single Malt Whisky
item.description: The Evermann Wilhelm Single Malt Whisky comes from the renowned Bimmerle Distillery in the Black Forest, Germany. Launched in the summer of 2022, the distillery introduced two distinctive whiskies: the blended whisky "Theo" and the single malt "Wilhelm." Wilhelm represents the traditional Black Forest spirit, focusing on regional craftsmanship and a diverse barrel maturation profile.

tasting_notes.nose: The first nosing reveals aromas of red fruits, vanilla, and caramel. Base notes of apple and pear are complemented by a subtle, slightly perfumed oak aroma. The complex aging in seven different cask types—German virgin oak, acacia, American oak, ex-bourbon, sweet wine, chestnut, and Oloroso sherry—provides an unexpectedly deep and harmonious scent profile.
tasting_notes.palate: On the palate, the single malt impresses with its pleasantly oily mouthfeel and a mild sweetness. Woody caramel and hints of red fruits create an initial impact, while a gentle maltiness rounds off the overall experience. A touch of black pepper adds a pleasant spiciness without overwhelming the palate.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is medium-length and shows spicy notes of oak and caramel that gradually fade. The fruity flavors remain subtly in the background, almost reminiscent of bourbon-like traits, but with a more balanced and refined approach.

conclusion.marcel: A whisky that simply delivers enjoyment without any pretensions, offering cozy moments with its gentle sweetness and red fruit aromas – perfect for relaxed evenings or an easy introduction to the Black Forest whisky scene.
conclusion.sascha: The Evermann Wilhelm presents a well-structured balance between fruitiness, wood notes, and spice, without any striking edges or quirks; a solid, balanced single malt that prioritizes harmony over complexity.

faq.question: What makes the cask maturation of the Evermann Wilhelm Single Malt Whisky special?
faq.answer: The Evermann Wilhelm Single Malt Whisky matures in seven different types of casks, including German virgin oak, acacia, American oak, ex-bourbon, sweet wine, chestnut, and Oloroso sherry. This diverse cask maturation creates a complex and harmonious aroma profile with notes of red fruits, vanilla, and caramel.

faq.question: How does the Evermann Wilhelm Single Malt Whisky differ from other German whiskies?
faq.answer: Originating from the Black Forest, Evermann Wilhelm places special emphasis on regional craftsmanship. Its multifaceted cask maturation and the absence of an age statement set it apart from many other German whiskies by offering a unique balance of fruitiness, maltiness, and spice.

faq.question: What flavor notes can be expected from the Evermann Wilhelm Single Malt Whisky?
faq.answer: On the palate, Evermann Wilhelm presents an oily mouthfeel, subtle sweetness, and flavors of woody caramel and red fruits. A delicate maltiness and a hint of black pepper complete the profile, while the finish is medium-length, spicy with oak and caramel notes.

item.name: Evermann Theo Blended Whisky
item.description: The Evermann Theo Blended Whisky hails from the Black Forest region of Germany and is produced by the Bimmerle Private Distillery. Launched in 2022, the Evermann range includes two expressions: Theo, a blended whisky inspired by American styles, and Wilhelm, a more traditional single malt with roots in the Black Forest. The Theo variant is twice distilled in pot stills and matured in a unique combination of four cask types: German oak, acacia, American oak, and ex-bourbon barrels. This diverse cask management imparts a balanced mix of fruitiness, subtle woodiness, and a light spice to the whisky.

tasting_notes.nose: The nose opens with fresh and fruity aromas. Predominant notes include citrus fruits like orange and apricot, complemented by a touch of pear. There’s a delicate sweetness of honey and vanilla, layered with hints of caramel. In the background, there are subtle oak tones and a surprising scent reminiscent of dried hay, adding a unique twist. One particularly intriguing note is a subtle whiff that some might describe as “jeans fabric,” giving the whisky an unconventional character​.
tasting_notes.palate: On the palate, the Evermann Theo presents a light, oily texture that highlights its fruity nature, featuring nectarine, orange, and persimmon. Vanilla and honey also make a reappearance, yet the whisky remains somewhat flat in overall flavor intensity. Despite the use of multiple cask types, the blend’s profile is more on the subdued side, making it an approachable and easy-drinking option​.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is short to medium in length but pleasantly warming. Light peppery and oak notes linger, accompanied by a faint hint of dried wood. Although not particularly complex, it remains an enjoyable conclusion for those seeking a straightforward whisky experience.

conclusion.marcel: Light, fresh, and straightforward – the Evermann Theo does exactly what you expect from an entry-level whisky: fun in a glass without any fuss. For the price, you get a solid blend that, with its fruity character and smooth profile, is genuinely enjoyable, even if it could use a bit more punch.
conclusion.sascha: The Evermann Theo reveals a clean and well-balanced cask combination that harmoniously blends fruity and woody notes. Despite its clear structure, it lacks the depth and intensity to rise above a solid entry-level standard – perfect for beginners, but rather unremarkable for seasoned connoisseurs.

faq.question: What makes the Evermann Theo Blended Whisky from the Black Forest special compared to other blended whiskies?
faq.answer: Evermann Theo stands out due to its maturation in four different types of casks: German oak, acacia wood, American oak, and ex-bourbon barrels. This combination gives it a balanced mix of fruity notes, gentle wood aromas, and subtle spice, making it modern and approachable.

faq.question: Why is the Evermann Theo Blended Whisky distilled twice in pot stills, and how does this affect the flavor?
faq.answer: The double distillation in pot stills gently cleanses the distillate while preserving complex aromas. The result is a whisky with clear fruit and vanilla notes, a lightly oily texture, and a restrained yet harmonious flavor.

faq.question: What flavor notes are typical for Evermann Theo, and how does its finish present itself?
faq.answer: Typical flavors include fresh citrus fruits like orange and apricot, complemented by honey, vanilla, and a light caramel note. On the palate, nectarine, persimmon, and a gentle woody tone dominate. The finish is short to medium in length with delicate pepper and oak notes, along with a subtle hint of dried wood.

item.name: Tomintoul 14 Years
item.description: The Tomintoul 14 years is a classic single malt whisky from the Speyside region of Scotland, which is a particularly exciting option for beginners. With 46.00% ABV and a maturation period of 14 years in oak casks, this whisky promises an interesting blend of classic Speyside notes and a slight smoky flavour.

tasting_notes.nose: The first impression of the Tomintoul 14 Years is dense and complex. The nose is enveloped by a subtle smoke that remains pleasantly subtle and does not play the dominant role that one might expect from more heavily peated whiskies. In the foreground are honey notes that provide a pleasant sweetness, accompanied by a hay and vanilla flavour that is typical of Speyside whiskies. Floral notes give the flavour a slight freshness, while the smoke in the background adds a certain depth. An interesting comparison here could be Clynelish, which also has smoky tones, but is much ‘funkier’. The Tomintoul remains more elegant and accessible.
tasting_notes.palate: On the tongue, the Tomintoul 14 years shows its malty side, with the honey flavour still present and given an additional dimension by the light smoke. The whisky has a heavy, spicy texture, which is in contrast to many fruity, light Speyside whiskies. Interesting notes of apricot brandy and a potato spice mix bring an unexpected spiciness that is rounded off with a hint of mint.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish of the single malt is of medium length and remains predominantly spicy and oaky. A slight sweetness of sugar mixes with a sour note, which gives the whisky an interesting finish. This acidity ensures that the whisky does not remain too heavy on the tongue and has a pleasant finish.

conclusion.marcel: The Tomintoul 14 Years is a pleasant companion for cozy evenings, offering enjoyment with its sweet honey notes and subtle smoky character without overwhelming the palate. For anyone looking for an uncomplicated Speyside whisky, it’s definitely a solid choice – but nothing more. Those seeking a whisky with a truly distinctive personality should look elsewhere.
conclusion.sascha: With its balanced blend of sweet honey and spicy oak notes, the Tomintoul 14 Years delivers a structured and clear aromatic profile that particularly appeals to newcomers. The light smoky nuance adds extra depth, yet the lack of complexity limits the tasting experience for seasoned connoisseurs. Overall, it’s a well-crafted Speyside whisky with solid maturity, but without any real surprises.

faq.question: How does the Tomintoul 14 Years differ from other Speyside whiskies?
faq.answer: The Tomintoul 14 Years combines classic Speyside notes like honey, vanilla, and floral aromas with a subtle smoky hint that gives it added depth. Compared to many fruity and light Speyside whiskies, it comes across as maltier and spicier, which makes it particularly interesting.

faq.question: Which flavor notes are especially noticeable in the Tomintoul 14 Years?
faq.answer: The taste is dominated by honey and malt, complemented by a light smoky nuance. Additionally, notes of apricot brandy, a potato spice mix, and a hint of mint can be detected, giving the whisky an unexpected spiciness.

faq.question: How does the 14-year maturation affect the character of the Tomintoul whisky?
faq.answer: The 14 years spent in oak casks impart a heavy, spicy texture and a medium-length finish with oaky and sweet-sour notes. The aging period ensures a balanced harmony between sweetness, spice, and subtle smokiness.

item.name: Glen Moray 12 Years (2008/2021) old - Rum Matured & Cognac Finish
item.description: The Glen Moray distillery from Speyside has created an interesting cask project with its 2008/2021 - 12 years whisky, which has been matured in rum casks and refined with a cognac finish. This Private Cask Collection, bottled as a single cask with only 498 bottles, is definitely something special.

image.alt: Glen Moray 2008/2021 12 Years old - Rum Matured & Cognac Finish

tasting_notes.nose: This whisky reveals a wonderful aroma from the very first sniff. The fruitiness immediately jumps into the glass: notes of Bircher muesli with grapes, apple and a hint of peach dominate, accompanied by vanilla and a sweet honey flavour that combines elegantly with walnut. The fragrance is malty and slightly floral, while a hint of pear rounds off the fine, balanced sweetness.
tasting_notes.palate: On the palate, the single malt unfolds with an intense, almost surprising creaminess. The pepper notes provide a spicy kick, which is accompanied by ginger and counteracts the sweetness with an exciting sharpness. Apricot and malt come more to the fore, giving the whisky a velvety depth, while the oak spice provides structure and character.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is short to medium in length, but leaves a pleasant, warming trace on the palate. The colourful peppercorns once again set the tone, while nutty tones and honey-like sweetness gently fade away. A malty base note and a good portion of oak spice give the finish depth before the whisky leaves a light, wintry spice note with a hint of speculoos.

conclusion.marcel: A whisky that truly delights with its creamy spice and fruity sweetness – a genuine moment of enjoyment, even if it still lacks the final polish. Glen Moray shows character here without taking itself too seriously, making it perfect for relaxed evenings with friends.
conclusion.sascha: A solid balance of fruitiness, spice, and oak defines this Glen Moray, which gains interesting facets through its rum and cognac finishes. However, the profile still feels somewhat underdeveloped in places, which slightly limits its harmonious rounding.

faq.question: How do rum casks and the cognac finish influence the flavor of this Glen Moray whisky?
faq.answer: Maturation in rum casks imparts sweet, fruity notes like apricot and honey to the whisky, while the cognac finish adds a fine, elegant spiciness and extra complexity, expressed through hints of nuts and spices.

faq.question: What does 'Single Cask' mean for this Glen Moray 2008/2021 whisky?
faq.answer: 'Single Cask' means the whisky was bottled from just one single barrel, resulting in a unique flavor profile. For this whisky, only 498 bottles were produced from that exact cask, making it particularly limited and full of character.

faq.question: How does the high alcohol content of 57.83% affect the drinking experience?
faq.answer: The high alcohol content delivers an intense aroma and robust spiciness that unfolds on the palate. Many enthusiasts recommend adding a few drops of water to better open up the flavors and soften the sharpness.

item.name: Patrisbrunna Peated (2017)
item.description: The Patrisbrunna Peated 2017 is a young, peated whisky from Germany, produced by the Kloster Dalheim Distillery and distributed by the independent bottler Kö13 Paderborn. With only 4 years of maturation in virgin oak casks and an alcohol content of 40%, this whisky offers a profile that should be of particular interest to lovers of straightforward, peaty flavours.

tasting_notes.nose: The Patrisbrunna Peated shows its intense side from the very first nosing. A strong portion of smoke dominates the flavour, accompanied by clear medicinal notes reminiscent of iodine. Behind this, the youth of the whisky comes to the fore, supported by a light oak note that is gently softened by caramel and vanilla. A hint of malt rounds off the overall picture, with the flavours coming across as classic but unsurprising.
tasting_notes.palate: On the palate, the Patrisbrunna Peated unfolds an unusual interplay of buttery soft and extremely dry textures. The flavour is initially reminiscent of vinegar and rubber tyres, which brings a certain originality to the fore. The tongue sensation is almost powdery, which gives the whisky an interesting but also challenging flavour.
tasting_notes.finish: The classic, long peat finish remains present, but without the spicy depth familiar from other smoky whiskies. A slight saltiness brings additional complexity, but overall the finish remains rather restrained and not very spectacular.

conclusion.marcel: A whisky that scores with its smoky straightforwardness but remains a bit too tame. The Patrisbrunna Peated 2017 is like the buddy who’s always there but rarely surprises you – solid, uncomplicated, and with a touch of peat that’s enjoyable but not captivating.
conclusion.sascha: The Patrisbrunna Peated 2017 impresses with a clear structure and a dominant smoky note, but feels somewhat unbalanced in terms of complexity and finish. For fans of straightforward, peaty German whiskies, it’s a reliable, if not very nuanced, option without any extraordinary character.

faq.question: How does the Patrisbrunna Peated 2017 differ from other peated whiskies?
faq.answer: The Patrisbrunna Peated 2017 is distinguished by its short maturation period of only 4 years in virgin oak casks and a moderate alcohol content of 40%. Compared to classic peated whiskies, its smoke profile is clear and intense, yet it offers an unusual palate combining buttery softness with dry textures. The finish is rather restrained and less spicy.

faq.question: Why does the Patrisbrunna Peated 2017 taste unusually dry and almost powdery on the palate?
faq.answer: The unusual mouthfeel, featuring dry and almost powdery textures, results from the combination of its youthful maturation and cask choice. The virgin oak casks impart fresh wood aromas which, together with the smoky and medicinal notes, create a distinctive flavor experience with slight vinegary and rubbery nuances.

faq.question: Who is the Patrisbrunna Peated 2017 particularly suited for?
faq.answer: This whisky is primarily aimed at lovers of straightforward, peaty flavors who prefer a clear smoke profile without too much complexity. Due to its youthful freshness and challenging taste, it is less suitable for beginners and more for experienced connoisseurs looking to explore new German interpretations of smoky whiskies.

item.name: Glenrothes (2013/2024) - Malts of Scotland
item.description: Gingerbread and cinnamon stars are filling the shelves everywhere - the perfect way to get in the mood for the cold season, not only in terms of food but also in terms of whisky. Malts of Scotland has the perfect whisky for this: Glenrothes, bottled exclusively for WHISKY & ART. The artistic label, designed by David Schlierenkämper, shows the Glenrothes distillery and also makes the bottle a small collector's item.

tasting_notes.nose: The first impression on nosing is immediately reminiscent of a winter fruit market. Cinnamon-plum jam dominates the flavour, followed by the sweet depth of cherry jam and the aroma of wild strawberries and blackberries. These are joined by subtle notes of nutty chocolate and a pleasant spiciness reminiscent of tobacco. You immediately feel like you're in a cosy corner of the sofa next to the Christmas tree.
tasting_notes.palate: The Glenrothes unfolds its full power on the tongue. Spicy and warming is the best description here, and again it is the plums and cherries that dominate the flavour. A dark, sweet fruitiness reminiscent of nut chocolate and blackberries is complemented by the earthy flavour of roasted walnuts and tobacco. The flavours of old wood and dried fruit resonate in the background - this combination gives the whisky depth and impressive complexity without being too overpowering.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is of medium length and brings the plum back to life. The spicy finish goes perfectly with the Christmas notes and makes you want to take the next sip. A pleasant tingling sensation remains on the palate, which prolongs the enjoyment a little longer.

conclusion.marcel: This Glenrothes 2013/2024 is a true winter dream, immediately creating moments of comfort with its fruity sweetness and spicy depth. Perfect for sinking into on cold evenings and letting the aromas enchant you – a genuine pleasure that leaves you wanting more.
conclusion.sascha: The Glenrothes 2013/2024 impresses with its balanced harmony between fruity sweetness, spicy tobacco notes, and a subtle woodiness. Its complex structure and medium-length finish make it a technically well-crafted Speyside whisky, suitable both for the winter season and discerning connoisseurs.

faq.question: Why does the Glenrothes 2013/2024 from Malts of Scotland have no age statement?
faq.answer: The Glenrothes 2013/2024 is a single malt without a specific age statement (No Age Statement), meaning the bottler focuses on the whisky’s taste and quality rather than highlighting its age. The exact age may vary, but the emphasis is on the character of the cask and maturation.

faq.question: How does the high alcohol content of 57% affect the taste of this Glenrothes?
faq.answer: At 57% ABV, the Glenrothes 2013/2024 has a robust alcohol strength that intensifies the aromas and provides a warming mouthfeel. Adding a few drops of water opens up additional flavor notes like dried fruit and spices, enhancing the whisky’s complexity.

faq.question: What makes the Malts of Scotland bottling of this Glenrothes special?
faq.answer: Malts of Scotland is an independent bottler who selected the Glenrothes 2013/2024 exclusively for WHISKY & ART. This bottling stands out due to its careful cask selection and limited quantity, paired with an artistically designed label by David Schlierenkämper, making it a collector’s item.

item.name: Teeling Single Pot Still (2024)
item.description: The Teeling Single Pot Still marks an exciting milestone in the history of the Teeling Distillery, which opened its doors in Dublin in 2015. This whiskey is special as it is the first independently produced Single Pot Still whiskey from the distillery. Made from a mix of 50% malted and 50% unmalted barley, it represents Irish whiskey tradition at its finest. Triple-distilled and aged in a combination of fresh white oak, ex-bourbon, and wine casks, it offers a unique array of aromas and flavors. With an ABV of 46% and no age statement, this whiskey delivers a refreshing reinterpretation of the Irish Single Pot Still whiskey.

tasting_notes.nose: The first impression of this Irish whiskey is typically Irish yet rich in complexity. Floral notes dominate the bouquet, complemented by hints of grapes and light citrus like lemon and lime. A grassy, grainy character underscores the whiskey's freshness, while touches of hay lend a warm, earthy quality.
tasting_notes.palate: On the palate, Teeling Single Pot Still is pleasantly fruity with notes of apple and peach. These fruity tones are balanced by a hint of white pepper and cane sugar, which add a slight spiciness. Particularly intriguing are the flavors of oat cookies, offering a cozy, almost nostalgic element to the overall taste.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is medium in length, pleasantly warming, and brings a solid oakiness that gives the whiskey a robust ending. A subtle wine note lingers, providing a rounded finish that invites another sip.

conclusion.marcel: This Teeling Single Pot Still brings a lot of drinking pleasure to the glass with its charming spiciness and cozy oat biscuit aromas – an Irish whiskey that surprises with its rough edge while remaining pleasantly rounded. Perfect for anyone looking for an uncomplicated yet characterful dram.
conclusion.sascha: The Teeling Single Pot Still impresses with a solid balance between floral, fruity, and spicy elements, complemented by a structured oak note in the finish. Despite its youth, it shows a clear signature but remains somewhat restrained overall, leaving room for further maturation.

faq.question: What does Single Pot Still mean in Teeling Single Pot Still (2024)?
faq.answer: Single Pot Still refers to an Irish whiskey made from a mash of both malted and unmalted barley distilled in a single distillery. In the case of the Teeling Single Pot Still (2024), the mash consists of 50% malted and 50% unmalted barley, which gives it its characteristic, full-bodied flavor.

faq.question: How does the cask maturation influence the taste of the Teeling Single Pot Still (2024)?
faq.answer: The whiskey matures in a combination of fresh American oak, ex-bourbon, and wine casks. This variety of casks brings out complex aromas: the American oak provides freshness, the bourbon casks contribute vanilla and caramel notes, and the wine casks add fruity depth along with a subtle wine character on the finish.

faq.question: Why does the Teeling Single Pot Still (2024) have no age statement and how does this affect the flavor?
faq.answer: The Teeling Single Pot Still (2024) is a no age statement (NAS) whiskey, meaning no specific minimum age is declared. This allows Teeling to blend different distillates to achieve a balanced flavor profile that is fresh, fruity, and spicy without being tied to a particular maturation period.

item.name: WillowBurn The Distillery Exclusive Cask (1445)
item.description: WillowBurn is part of the prestigious Hercynian Distilling Co. in the heart of the Harz Mountains, Germany. Since its establishment in 1985, Hammerschmiede (also known as the Elsburn Distillery) has been producing unique spirits. The WillowBurn The Distillery Exclusive Cask 1445 is a special bottling, hand-filled on-site, showcasing the craftsmanship of this distillery. A standout feature of this release is that 50% of the malt is smoked not with peat but with wood, giving the whisky a distinctive character.

tasting_notes.nose: The aroma opens with a subtle hint of smoldering woodfire, gently rising from the glass. Sweet notes of crème brûlée and caramel add richness, while intense sherry notes dominate. Marsala, raisins, and candied dates bring a fruity, complex dimension to the bouquet.
tasting_notes.palate: On the palate, the WillowBurn feels creamy yet dry, almost astringent. The aging in the Octave Sherry cask is evident with strong oak flavors and a rich, red fruit profile. Dried fruits dominate, and the underlying wood smoke adds a delicate smokiness that never overwhelms.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is medium-long with a hint of peppery spice. The intense oak notes persist, complemented by raisins and a light leather aroma. The lingering smoke is reminiscent of a smoldering incense stick, enhancing the whisky's smoky character.

conclusion.marcel: A whisky that immediately delights with its smoky wood fire and sweet sherry notes – here, character meets artisanal passion. The robust alcohol strength isn’t for the faint-hearted, but it delivers plenty of punch and an unforgettable tasting experience. For anyone seeking an exciting German whisky with edges and personality, this dram is just right.
conclusion.sascha: Technically, the WillowBurn demonstrates an impressive balance between intense sherry cask maturation and subtle smoke, with the high alcohol content of 60.6% ABV clearly defining its structure, though making the entry a bit challenging. The complex combination of dried fruits, oak, and spicy pepper is well balanced, although the youthfulness and dry astringency add a certain sharpness to the mouthfeel. Overall, a structured, powerful whisky with a clear profile and a distinctive character.

faq.question: What makes the smoky aroma of WillowBurn The Distillery Exclusive Cask 1445 special?
faq.answer: The smoky flavor comes from malt smoked not over peat, but over wood. This gives the whisky a subtle, woody smoke note that clearly distinguishes it from classic peat smoke.

faq.question: Why does this whisky have no age statement despite its complex taste?
faq.answer: WillowBurn The Distillery Exclusive Cask 1445 is a so-called No-Age-Statement whisky (NAS). The distillery focuses here on the quality and combination of casks, especially maturation in octave sherry casks, to achieve complexity and flavor without specifying a fixed age.

faq.question: What role does the type of cask play in this whisky and how does it influence the flavor?
faq.answer: Maturation in octave sherry casks imparts intense oak notes as well as a rich, red-fruit profile with aromas of dried fruits like raisins and dates. This cask choice creates the complex combination of sweet, fruity, and spicy flavor notes.

item.name: Glenalba 25 Years Madeira Finish
item.description: If you think Lidl is just for the weekly shop, then you haven't tried the Glenalba 25 Year Madeira Finish. This whisky is like a well-aged leather book: full of stories that are yet to be discovered.

tasting_notes.nose: It is clear from the first sniff that this whisky has a lot to offer. The nose is greeted by a bouquet of sweet sultanas and a vinous note reminiscent of a well-aged port wine. A slight leatheriness resonates - as if you had just tracked down an old Chesterfield armchair. Dark berries dance alongside, bringing with them an underlying acidity that balances the whisky wonderfully. A hint of chocolate and coffee grounds mingle, accompanied by a nutty flavour reminiscent of roasted nuts. Almost dusty, with a hint of grain.
tasting_notes.palate: In the mouth, the Glenalba 25 years unfolds in all its splendour. Grape must and juicy cherries dominate the first impression, followed by a seductive combination of grape-nut-chocolate and rum-chocolate. It's like sinking into a cosy leather sofa - soft yet with structure. The beautiful oak adds a pleasant woodiness to the whole.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is of medium length. The subtle oak notes linger on the tongue for a while, while coffee chocolate and a hint of nutmeg round off the flavour profile. This demonstrates the true art of distillation - not too overpowering, but not too restrained either.

conclusion.marcel: The Glenalba 25 Year Madeira Finish is a real mood booster for the living room – sweet, with a touch of leather and dark chocolate that’s just plain fun. Sure, it feels a bit thin in places, but hey, considering the price and availability, that’s almost forgivable. Absolutely recommend it for a relaxed evening with friends!
conclusion.sascha: With a balanced interplay of sweet fruitiness and subtle oak notes, the Glenalba 25 Year Madeira Finish shows a solid structure, though it somewhat lacks depth. The finish is medium-length and harmonious, even if the complexity doesn’t quite keep pace with its age. Overall, a good whisky with potential that scores well on an attractive price-performance ratio.

faq.question: What does "Madeira Finish" mean for the Glenalba 25 Year whisky?
faq.answer: "Madeira Finish" means that after the main maturation in traditional casks, the whisky was aged for a period in casks that previously held Madeira wine. This imparts fruity, wine-like notes and a distinctive complexity to the whisky.

faq.question: How does the 25-year age affect the taste of the Glenalba whisky?
faq.answer: The 25 years of maturation provide pronounced depth and complexity. The whisky develops rich aromas such as leather, dark berries, and nutty nuances, while the wood tones are well integrated thanks to the long cask aging.

faq.question: Is the Glenalba 25 Year Madeira Finish a single malt or a blend?
faq.answer: The Glenalba 25 Year Madeira Finish is a single malt whisky, meaning it comes from a single distillery and is made exclusively from malted barley.

item.name: Epicurean Small Batch Release - Douglas Laing
item.description: The Epicurean Small Batch Release from Douglas Laing's is a classic representative of Lowland whiskies that is suitable for both beginners and experienced connoisseurs. With an alcohol content of 46.20% ABV and no age statement, this whisky is an accessible yet complex companion for any occasion.

image.alt: Epicurean Small Batch Release - Douglas Laing

tasting_notes.nose: On first nosing, the Epicurean unfolds a bouquet that is immediately reminiscent of the fresh, flowery meadows of the Lowlands. Floral notes dominate, accompanied by a hint of dried grass that has not yet fully turned to hay. A distinct lemon note adds freshness, while subtle honey and a hint of dust give the whisky a slight sweetness and depth. This combination makes for a complex yet accessible flavour.
tasting_notes.palate: The Epicurean shows its full strength on the palate. The flavours unfold sparkling and lively, supported by a pleasant maltiness that gives the whisky body. The sour, lemony note, which was already noticeable on the nose, is particularly present here and makes for a refreshing, almost tangy experience. An oily texture helps to ensure that the flavours completely fill the mouth. Light caramel and brown sugar notes round off the flavour profile, resulting in a balanced sweet and sour harmony that makes this whisky special.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish of the Epicurean is short to medium in length and leaves a light but pleasantly bitter aftertaste. The flavours of honey and caramel linger gently on the palate and ensure a harmonious finish that underlines the refreshing character of the whisky.

conclusion.marcel: Douglas Laing’s Epicurean Small Batch scores with its zesty lemon note and pleasant sweetness – a genuine feel-good whisky that enlivens the palate without being overly complicated. It’s just right for relaxed moments of enjoyment in between, even though the finish fades a bit quicker than one might hope.
conclusion.sascha: A well-balanced Lowland whisky that impresses through the interplay of floral and malty notes combined with the distinctive acidity of lemon. Its structured texture and harmonious sweet-and-sour balance make it a solid representative of its class, though the short finish slightly tempers the overall impression.

faq.question: What sets the Epicurean Small Batch Release apart from other Lowland whiskies?
faq.answer: The Epicurean Small Batch Release stands out with its fresh, floral character featuring pronounced lemon and honey notes. At 46.2% ABV, it offers a lively, oily texture and a balanced sweet-and-sour harmony that distinguishes it from lighter or more heavily peated Lowland whiskies.

faq.question: Why does the Epicurean Small Batch Release have no age statement, and how does that affect the flavor?
faq.answer: The Epicurean is a no age statement (NAS) whisky, which allows Douglas Laing, as an independent bottler, to blend casks of varying maturation periods. This approach enables a targeted flavor balance that is both fresh and complex without being constrained by a specific age.

faq.question: How should one best enjoy the Epicurean Small Batch Release to fully experience its aromas?
faq.answer: The Epicurean reveals its floral and lemony notes best at room temperature. A slight addition of water can open up the aromas and enhance the sweet-and-sour balance. It works well both neat and with a mild splash of water for a refreshing tasting experience.

item.name: Whesskey - Hessian Corn Whisky - 10 Years
item.description: The ‘Whesskey - Hessian Corn Whisky - 10 years’ is a real insider tip from Germany, more precisely from the Höhler distillery in Hesse. With a full 64.70 % vol. and without colouring or chill-filtration, this whisky promises not only strength, but also a deep, unadulterated taste experience. Will it live up to the high expectations?

tasting_notes.nose: It is clear from the first sniff: this whisky is in the intense league. The flavours are strong and unmistakable. The aroma is sweet and immediately reminiscent of fresh, buttery popcorn. The corn flavour is dominant, accompanied by a hint of vanilla extract, which gives the whole thing a soft note. Light citrus accents bring a certain freshness into play, which prevents the fragrance from being too heavy. But beware: it is this sticky-sweet note that immediately grabs the senses - there is no room for restraint here. The hint of lime is particularly exciting, providing a surprising freshness and skilfully breaking up the sweet profile.
tasting_notes.palate: You almost feel like you're biting straight into a ripe corn cob. The sweet notes of brown sugar and caramelised popcorn really jump out at you, intense, almost overwhelming. Vanilla underpins the whole thing with a creamy undertone. What makes this whisky special is its oily consistency, which literally lies on the tongue - extremely warming and soothing. Slightly salty and dusty flavours are mixed in, giving the whole an interesting, spicy depth.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish of this corn whisky is medium-long, but spicy and distinctive. The oak, which has been at work for ten years, is very present here. The whisky does not fade away quietly, but leaves a lasting impression with plenty of flavour.

conclusion.marcel: A whisky that immediately impresses with its full corn power and delivers pure drinking pleasure – no frills here, just straightforward enjoyment. Sure, it lacks a bit of complexity, but that's exactly what makes it so honest and approachable. For anyone who likes their whisky robust and intense, Whesskey is a real winner!
conclusion.sascha: Structurally solid, this corn whisky scores particularly with its clear corn dominance and balanced spiciness on the finish. The 10 years of barrel aging show their effect; although it lacks a bit of depth, it remains harmonious and intense. A successful example of a powerful German corn whisky with a distinct profile.

faq.question: How does the high alcohol content of 64.7% ABV affect the taste of Whesskey – Hessian Corn Whisky?
faq.answer: The high alcohol content delivers an intense and concentrated flavor experience that particularly highlights the aromas of corn, vanilla, and caramel. At the same time, it gives the whisky an oily texture and a warming effect that shapes the enjoyment. Many recommend diluting the whisky with a few drops of water to better bring out the aromas.

faq.question: What makes Whesskey – Hessian Corn Whisky special compared to classic Scottish whiskies?
faq.answer: Whesskey stands out primarily because of its corn (maize) base, which gives it a sweeter and fuller-bodied profile. Additionally, it comes from Hesse in Germany, not Scotland, and avoids chill filtration and coloring, resulting in an unadulterated aroma. The ten-year maturation in oak barrels imparts a distinctive spiciness that sets it apart from the typical peaty character of Scottish whiskies.

faq.question: How should Whesskey – Hessian Corn Whisky be best served to optimally experience its aromas?
faq.answer: Due to its high alcohol content, it is recommended to try the whisky neat first and then refine it with a few drops of still water. This helps open up the aromas of corn, vanilla, and citrus fruits. It is also excellent for slow enjoyment at room temperature in a tulip-shaped glass, which concentrates the intense scents and enhances the tasting experience.

item.name: Stauning Smoke Distillery Edition Calvados Cask
item.description: Visiting a distillery usually means taking at least one exclusive bottling from the distillery with you or - if possible - a hand-filled bottle, i.e. a self-filled bottle from a specially designated single cask. The Stauning distillery offers several Distillery Exclusives. We took a look at the ‘Barley’ and the ‘Smoke’.

image.alt: Stauning Smoke Distillery Edition Calvados Cask

tasting_notes.nose: From the first whiff, it is clear that this whisky has a powerful personality. Sweet campfire smoke dominates the flavour profile and creates an intense, warm atmosphere. Behind this are notes of green, sour apple, which are intensified by the post-maturing in Calvados casks. There is a slight hint of grain, paired with a subtle note of vanilla. The youth clearly shines through here from time to time.
tasting_notes.palate: The smoky character continues seamlessly in the flavour. The first impression is characterised by dense smoke that envelops the tongue. However, the high abv of 62.2% quickly makes itself felt and provides a certain sharpness, which is due to the young age of the whisky. After this powerful start, the sour green apples come back into play, accompanied by a surprisingly salty undertone and a hint of citrus fruits that bring freshness and vibrancy to the flavour experience. The Calvados influence is clearly noticeable here.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is surprisingly short for a peated whisky. While the smoke remains dominant, the citrus and apple flavours fade quickly without any noticeable oak notes.

conclusion.marcel: Stauning Smoke Distillery Edition Calvados Cask is a smoky adventure with fruity apple notes that will especially delight fans of robust firewood character. Despite its youthful freshness and intense smokiness, I personally find it lacks the depth to make it a long-term favorite – but as a spontaneous treat, it’s definitely an exciting experience.
conclusion.sascha: The Stauning Smoke Calvados Cask clearly showcases the dominance of smoke, contrasted by the fresh, tart apple notes from the Calvados cask. The balance between smoke and fruitiness is intriguing, yet the complexity remains limited, and the short finish diminishes the structural persistence of this young, high-proof whisky.

faq.question: How does the finishing in Calvados casks influence the flavor of this Stauning Smoke whisky?
faq.answer: The finishing in Calvados casks imparts distinct notes of green, tart apple and adds a fruity freshness to the aroma profile. This cask maturation complements the smoky character with subtle citrus and apple freshness, making the tasting experience more lively.

faq.question: Why is the 62.2% alcohol content relevant to the taste of this whisky?
faq.answer: The high alcohol content of 62.2% delivers an intense sharpness and warmth on the palate, which is especially noticeable in young whiskies like this three-years. It amplifies the smoky notes and gives the whisky a powerful presence that shapes the overall flavor experience.

faq.question: What makes the Stauning Smoke Distillery Edition Calvados Cask special compared to other smoky whiskies?
faq.answer: Besides the typical campfire smoke, this whisky stands out due to its youthful character and the unusual combination with Calvados cask maturation. The result is a smoky whisky with a surprisingly fresh, fruity note and a relatively short but intense finish without dominant oak tones.

item.name: Stauning Barley Distillery Edition Makers Mark Bourbon Cask
item.description: Visiting a distillery usually means taking at least one exclusive bottling from the distillery with you or - if possible - a hand-filled bottle, i.e. a self-filled bottle from a specially designated single cask. The Stauning distillery offers several Distillery Exclusives. We took a look at the ‘Barley’ and the ‘Smoke’.

image.alt: Stauning Barley Distillery Edition Makers Mark Bourbon Cask

tasting_notes.nose: The Stauning Barley is very fresh and youthful from the very first nosing. The dominant notes of light-coloured fruit, especially apple, are immediately apparent. This fruity bouquet is accompanied by subtle hints of vanilla, caramel and a touch of oak - typical characteristics of ageing in bourbon barrels. With a little imagination, you could also sense a hint of malt, but this tends to remain in the background. Overall, the flavour is somewhat one-dimensional and not very complex.
tasting_notes.palate: The impression of youthfulness is further emphasised on the palate. The apple dominates here too, accompanied by a clear tingling sensation on the tongue. The fruity flavours are more reminiscent of a fruit brandy such as apricot or quince than a classic malt whisky. A hint of vanilla emerges, but quickly disappears again. Unfortunately, the flavour lacks depth.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish of the Stauning Barley surprises with a subtle sweetness reminiscent of cream caramel and refined sugar. The slightly dry note of oak is also evident here, which at least gives the whisky some character in the aftertaste. Nevertheless, the overall impression remains rather flat and unexciting.

conclusion.marcel: The Stauning Barley Distillery Edition brings fresh, youthful apple notes to the glass, but overall it feels like a whisky still in its infancy. The delicate oak hint in the finish is charming, yet it can’t save the overall flat and rather uninspiring tasting experience. Those looking for a light, uncomplicated dram might find some enjoyment here, but depth or complexity should not be expected.
conclusion.sascha: From a structural standpoint, the Stauning Barley shows a clear fruit-forward profile dominated by apple and vanilla notes, but it loses impact due to a lack of complexity and an unbalanced profile. The maturation in Makers Mark bourbon casks delivers recognizable bourbon-typical nuances, yet the missing depth and the flat finish make the whisky feel overall underdeveloped. For connoisseurs who value layered aromatics, this bottling remains too superficial.

faq.question: What makes the Stauning Barley Distillery Edition Makers Mark Bourbon Cask special in terms of flavor?
faq.answer: The Stauning Barley presents fresh, youthful notes of light fruit such as apple, accompanied by vanilla, caramel, and a touch of oak from the bourbon cask maturation. The flavor is dominated by fruity notes reminiscent of fruit brandy, with a slightly sweet and dry, oak-driven finish.

faq.question: How does the bourbon cask maturation influence the character of this Stauning whisky?
faq.answer: The bourbon casks impart vanilla and caramel notes as well as a subtle oak presence in the finish. This cask aging provides a certain sweetness and gentle wood tones that underline the fruity character of the young whisky.

faq.question: Who is the Stauning Barley Distillery Edition Makers Mark Bourbon Cask suitable for despite its young age of 4 years?
faq.answer: This whisky is ideal for beginners or lovers of light, fruity, and less complex whiskies. The fresh apple and fruit brandy notes, along with the mild finish, make it a good choice for those seeking an uncomplicated, youthful whisky.

item.name: Jura 10 Years
item.description: The Isle of Jura, a remote island on the west coast of Scotland, is known for being the only distillery to trade under the same name. The Jura 10 Years is a whisky that reflects both the wild nature of the island and the artisan tradition.

tasting_notes.nose: At the first nosing, the Jura 10 years shows its fruity side. Notes of muesli and oatmeal meet the unusual aroma of fermented strawberries. There is a slight hint of chocolate, which gives the whisky a certain depth without being overpowering. The flavours are appealing, but already appear somewhat diffuse and less coherent here.
tasting_notes.palate: On the palate, the Jura 10 Years unfolds a mixture of apricot, dark chocolate and toasted oak. Liquorice and cinnamon provide a spicy component, while raspberry notes add a fruity freshness. However, despite these diverse flavours, the taste is unsettled - it lacks the harmony you would expect from a whisky of this age. The flavours fight against each other rather than complementing each other, resulting in an overall unbalanced experience.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is short and slightly spicy, with a hint of oak. This reveals another shortcoming: the flavours that unfold in the mouth disappear just as quickly without leaving a lasting impression. Here, too, the restlessness in the overall profile is evident, which further clouds the enjoyment.

conclusion.marcel: The Jura 10 Year offers a few nice fruity and chocolatey moments, but overall it feels somewhat scattered and fleeting – a whisky that is approachable but doesn’t truly excite. It’s fine for a relaxed drink now and then, but if you’re looking for character and depth, you’d be better off looking elsewhere.
conclusion.sascha: The lack of balance between the diverse aromas and the short, unsatisfying finish make the Jura 10 Year an underdeveloped example of its class. Despite some interesting notes, it lacks harmony and structural complexity, which significantly diminishes the overall enjoyment.

faq.question: How does the Jura 10 Year differ from other whiskies from Scotland?
faq.answer: The Jura 10 Year reflects the remote nature of the Isle of Jura, combining fruity notes with a spicy complexity. Compared to other Scottish whiskies, however, it feels less harmonious, as the flavors often come across as unsettled and not fully balanced.

faq.question: Which flavor notes should I pay special attention to in the Jura 10 Year?
faq.answer: The Jura 10 Year is dominated by fruity aromas like apricot and raspberry, complemented by dark chocolate, roasted oak, and spicy hints of licorice and cinnamon. Despite this variety, there is a lack of clear harmony, making the overall taste seem unbalanced.

faq.question: How long does the finish of the Jura 10 Year last and how is it characterized?
faq.answer: The finish of the Jura 10 Year is comparatively short and lightly spicy with a subtle oak tone. The flavors fade quickly, leaving the impression of a less lasting and distinctive finish.

item.name: Teeling Whiskey Wonders of Wood Series 3 Swedish Oak
item.description: The Irish Teeling Distillery is releasing the third part of its Wonders of Wood series in Germany today. After Chinkapin Oak (Part 1) and Portuguese Oak (Part 2), the next cask from Northern Europe is now selected: Virgin Swedish Oak. The recipe is supposedly simple: the triple-distilled single pot still Irish whiskey, made from 50% malted and 50% unmalted barley, matures for an unknown number of years exclusively in barrels of a rare Swedish white oak. The whiskey is bottled non-chill-filtered at an alcohol strength of 50%. We are very curious to see what influence the wood has had on the distillate. Speaking of wood: Teeling works together with the Tree Council of Ireland and actively cares for the wood stock of native trees.

image.alt: Teeling Whiskey Wonders of Wood Series 3 Swedish Oak

tasting_notes.nose: It is clear from the very first nosing that this whiskey has depth. The flavours develop slowly, starting off light and floral, with a hint of citrus fruit that seems almost playful. But then the heavier notes come into play. A fascinating spiciness, paired with toasted oak, runs through the entire flavour. The vanilla remains subtle, but it is there, lending the whole a soft, sweet note. After a little time in the glass, there is even a floral hint of elderflower - a surprising twist.
tasting_notes.palate: In the mouth, the woody flavour asserts itself with full force. The first sip is intense, almost rough, with a clear woody character reminiscent of a well-seasoned oak barrel. But the Teeling whiskey does not remain one-dimensional. Over time, exciting nuances develop in the glass: mint, dark chocolate and a bright cherry note emerge. The longer you keep the whisky on your tongue, the nuttier and stronger it becomes. The mouthfeel is almost oily, the texture rich and heavy. There are sweet notes of molasses and hints of walnut oil, which are rounded off by a light tannin note.
tasting_notes.finish: It lingers on the palate for a long time, dry and with a light pepper spice that pleasantly rounds off the flavour. A pinch of nutmeg and the omnipresent toasted oak wood accompany the final moments, while the vanilla note gently fades away.

conclusion.marcel: A whisky that’s great fun with its robust woodiness and playful citrus flair. The 50% ABV is wonderfully integrated, making every sip an experience and inviting you gladly on this spicy journey. For anyone who enjoys a whisky with character, featuring sweet and nutty depth, this Teeling is a well-rounded choice.
conclusion.sascha: The balance between the intense wood character and the delicate aromas of mint, cherry, and chocolate is remarkably well achieved here. The powerful structure at 50% ABV is neatly rounded off by a long, dry finish with spicy nuances, even though the wood dominance occasionally feels a bit too pronounced. A technically flawless Irish Pot Still that impresses with its complexity and texture.

faq.question: How does the Swedish white oak influence the taste of this whiskey compared to other types of wood?
faq.answer: Swedish white oak imparts an intense wood note with roasted oak and fine tannin elements to the whiskey. Compared to chinkapin or Portuguese oak, it brings a stronger, almost rougher wood spice along with subtle floral nuances like elderflower.

faq.question: Why is the Teeling Whiskey Wonders of Wood Series 3 bottled without chill filtration, and what effect does this have on the tasting experience?
faq.answer: Non-chill filtration preserves more natural oils and aromas in the whiskey, resulting in a fuller mouthfeel and more intense flavor. This ensures that the complex notes of wood, spices, and fruit remain better intact, providing a richer tasting experience.

faq.question: What significance does the collaboration with the Tree Council of Ireland have for the production of this whiskey?
faq.answer: Teeling collaborates with the Tree Council of Ireland to promote the preservation and sustainable management of native tree species. This partnership supports responsible use of wood resources, which are also crucial for the whiskey’s barrel maturation.

item.name: Dailuaine (2012/2022) #308764 - Signatory Vintage
item.description: The Dailuaine 2012/2022 #308764 from Signatory Vintage, bottled exclusively for Kirsch Import, was bottled at 59.20% and matured for 9 years. The Speyside distillery Dailuaine has positively surprised us in recent years. This time too?

tasting_notes.nose: The first flavours are reminiscent of malt sweets and cream caramel, followed by sweet blossom honey. Exotic fruit notes such as plantain and papaya add an extra dimension, which is rounded off by a light vanilla sugar note. Despite this tantalising sweetness, the whisky is already slightly demanding and unpleasant on the nose.
tasting_notes.palate: On the palate, the Dailuaine develops a sugary sweetness accompanied by an oily texture. The flavours wet the mouth and continue to develop intensively. However, the distinct ethanol sharpness makes the flavour somewhat rough. Fresh wood and creamy caramel again characterise the taste, while an unripe mango provides a fresh but unripe contrast.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is surprisingly short. It remains juicy and is dominated by fresh oak, which is accompanied by a pleasant vanilla note. However, the brilliance that one might expect from a single cask in this category is also absent here.

conclusion.marcel: This Dailuaine 2012/2022 #308764 is a wild daredevil in the glass, scoring with sweet fruit notes and a hefty dose of ethanol – though it’s a bit too rough around the edges for my taste. Those who enjoy an exciting, if not entirely polished whisky story might still have fun with it, but I’d personally reach for something a bit smoother.
conclusion.sascha: The Dailuaine 2012/2022 comes across as structurally unbalanced, with a dominant ethanol sharpness that overshadows the otherwise present sweet fruit notes and malty depth. For fans of powerful yet somewhat undeveloped Speyside whiskies, it offers an interesting, albeit not completely harmonious, experience.

faq.question: Why does the Dailuaine 2012/2022 taste somewhat sharp despite its 9 years of maturation?
faq.answer: The high alcohol content of 59.2% ABV results in a pronounced ethanol sharpness that makes the flavor seem rougher. Additionally, the relatively short maturation period of 9 years can lead to less balanced aromas and a certain rawness in this whisky.

faq.question: What role does the Dailuaine distillery play in the Speyside region and how is that reflected in the whisky?
faq.answer: Dailuaine is an established distillery in Speyside, often known for its malty and fruity whiskies. This whisky displays typical Speyside notes such as malted candies, honey, and vanilla, combined with exotic fruit nuances, reflecting the regional style.

faq.question: What distinguishes an independent bottling like this one from Signatory Vintage from official distillery releases?
faq.answer: Independent bottlers like Signatory Vintage select individual casks and often bottle them at cask strength without further processing. This results in unique and often more intense flavor profiles that can differ significantly from official distillery releases.

item.name: Beauchamp Whisky Pur Malt
item.description: Of course we also buy whisky on holiday. On our last holiday in France, a small corner wine shop recommended the Beauchamp Whisky Pur Malt. The whisky comes from the Bercloux Distillery and shows that France has more to offer than just wine and cognac. But how does this French exotic perform in the glass?

tasting_notes.nose: The dominant flavour here is an intense orange note that really explodes. The sweetness of caramel and brown sugar harmonises perfectly with a hint of honey. But it's not just the sweetness that stands out - a slight bitterness and an underlying malty sweetness give the fragrance depth. Also surprising is a subtle pear note, reminiscent of the distillery's characteristics, as well as a hint of white wine, which blends delicately into the overall flavour.
tasting_notes.palate: The first taste impression confirms the promise of the nose: sweetness is the central element. Sweets, sugar and once again the omnipresent orange dominate the flavour. It is interesting how a light grape note, reminiscent of white wine, blends into the sweet flavours. You could almost believe that the whisky is unnaturally sweet - that's how intense this flavour experience is.
tasting_notes.finish: The whisky remains true to its line in the finish. The orange peel is even more prominent here and is accompanied by a slight bitterness. The pear note also reappears and is complemented by a subtle oak note. Overall, the finish is harmonious, but here too it is clear that the whisky could still do with some ageing.

conclusion.marcel: A whisky that’s great fun with its intense orange sweetness and playful character – almost like a sweet little holiday in a bottle. Sure, it might be a bit too sweet for everyday drinking, but anyone looking for something different should definitely give the Beauchamp Pur Malt a chance.
conclusion.sascha: The Beauchamp Whisky Pur Malt displays a remarkable balance between intense sweetness and subtle bitterness, which, despite its youth, feels well-structured. An interesting French malt that, with its clear aromatic profile and harmonious finish, certainly shows potential for further maturation.

faq.question: What makes the Beauchamp Whisky Pur Malt special compared to Scottish whiskies?
faq.answer: The Beauchamp Whisky Pur Malt comes from France, specifically the Bercloux Distillery, and is distinguished by an intense orange note alongside pronounced sweetness featuring caramel, honey, and brown sugar. Unlike many Scottish whiskies, it presents a unique combination of fruity white wine and pear notes, as well as a subtle bitterness, making it an exceptional whisky exotic.

faq.question: How does the age of 0 years affect the taste of the Beauchamp Whisky Pur Malt?
faq.answer: Since the Beauchamp Whisky Pur Malt carries no age statement, it is presumably a young whisky. This is reflected in the taste through its intense fruitiness and sweetness, but also by a certain immaturity, which manifests in a somewhat unbalanced finish with stronger bitterness and a slight lack of complexity.

faq.question: Which aromas stand out in the Beauchamp Whisky Pur Malt and how do they interact?
faq.answer: The Beauchamp Whisky Pur Malt is dominated by intense orange notes, accompanied by sweet aromas such as caramel, brown sugar, and honey. These are complemented by subtle pear and light grape or white wine notes that add freshness. The slight bitterness and a delicate oak touch in the finish round out the flavor profile and provide depth.

item.name: Miltonduff (2009/2011) - Singatory Vintage
item.description: The Miltonduff Signatory Vintage 2009/2021 is a representative from the Speyside region in Scotland. Bottled by the independent bottler Signatory Vintage, this whisky matured for 11 years and has an alcohol content of 46% ABV. The Speyside region is known for its accessible and often fruity whiskies, which makes this drop particularly interesting for beginners. But can this whisky really convince?

tasting_notes.nose: From the very first nosing, you are greeted by a fresh and light aroma. Notes of green apple and mint dominate, accompanied by a gentle vanilla and a hint of maltiness. The subtle spiciness of curry is particularly exciting and provides an unexpected depth. Overall, the nose is reminiscent of a fresh spring day with the scent of hay and grass in the air.
tasting_notes.palate: The first sip continues the impression of the nose, but with a surprising twist. The flavour is very light, almost watery. The green fruits are present, but there is a slight bitterness. Interestingly, there is a note reminiscent of a wet concrete wall. A hint of chalk and cobbles provides a slightly mineral component. Overall, the flavour is rather restrained and not very exciting.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is short and spicy, with a slight peppery tingle on the tongue. The whisky does not linger here for long.

conclusion.marcel: Fresh and light like a springtime stroll, this Miltonduff offers some casual enjoyment—even if it doesn’t exactly set the taste buds dancing. For fans of straightforward Speyside whiskies who aren’t looking for big surprises, it’s a pleasant companion, but nothing more.
conclusion.sascha: The Miltonduff 2009/2021 presents an intriguing combination of fruity and spicy aromas, yet the palate remains thin and lacking in complexity. While there is a balance between freshness and minerality, the fleeting presence and slight bitterness prevent it from leaving a lasting impression.

faq.question: What makes the Miltonduff 2009/2011 from Signatory Vintage special in terms of flavor?
faq.answer: The Miltonduff 2009/2011 is characterized by a fresh and light aroma profile featuring green apple and mint notes. An unusual spiciness of curry and a subtle mineral touch reminiscent of damp concrete walls and chalk give it an unexpected depth.

faq.question: How does the Miltonduff 2009/2011 differ from typical Speyside whiskies?
faq.answer: While many Speyside whiskies are fruity and full-bodied, this Miltonduff is rather light and restrained, with an almost watery texture and a short, spicy peppery finish. The mineral notes are also quite unusual for the region.

faq.question: Who is the Miltonduff 2009/2011 from Signatory Vintage best suited for?
faq.answer: This whisky is interesting for connoisseurs seeking a more subtle and unusual Speyside expression. Due to its light structure and complex yet restrained aromas, it’s also suitable for beginners looking to explore new flavor profiles.

item.name: Kavalan SOLIST Fino Sherry Cask
item.description: In the world of whisky, there are a few names that immediately stand for quality and innovation. Kavalan, a Taiwanese distillery, has firmly established itself among the best whisky producers in the world in recent years. The Kavalan SOLIST Fino Sherry Cask in particular has attracted the attention of whisky connoisseurs worldwide with its exceptional maturity and impressive depth. In this review, we take a closer look at this fascinating single malt.

tasting_notes.nose: The very first nosing reveals a true explosion of flavours. The Kavalan SOLIST Fino Sherry Cask seduces the nose with intense fruity flavours.
