Mango and apple dominate at first, followed by a hint of nutty flavours reminiscent of freshly cracked walnuts. Notes of caramel and sugar syrup unfold in the background, accompanied by brown sugar reminiscent of fresh Black Forest cake. Leather and sultanas join in and give the fragrance a warm, earthy depth, while coffee, peach and sandalwood round off the scent.
tasting_notes.palate: In the mouth, the Kavalan SOLIST Fino Sherry Cask presents itself as a thick syrup whose viscosity literally melts on the tongue. The intense fruitiness of plums remains present for a long time and is accompanied by strong sherry notes, which give this whisky its depth and strength of character. The taste experience is rounded off by the combination of toffee and milk chocolate - a harmonious blend of nut and caramel that emphasises the sweetness. A surprising note of blueberry jam brings additional freshness, while a hint of boiled egg adds an interesting, slightly savoury component.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish of the Taiwanese is exceptionally long and intense. Roasted flavours dominate at first and slowly turn into the sweetness of a dessert wine. The sherry remains the undisputed star here and accompanies the finish to the end. A slight bitterness and the tart notes of black tea provide an elegant, dry finish.

conclusion.marcel: This Kavalan SOLIST Fino Sherry Cask is a genuine taste experience that immediately captivates with its intense fruity sweetness and velvety texture. For anyone who loves sherry whiskies and wants to try something exotic from Taiwan, it’s an absolute bullseye – complex, profound, and with a finish that lingers long after the last sip.
conclusion.sascha: With its balanced structure between intense fruitiness, robust sherry notes, and a clearly defined cask strength, the Kavalan SOLIST Fino Sherry Cask impresses through complexity and depth. The harmonious balance between sweet and tart elements makes it a technically sophisticated single malt that sets standards in the Taiwanese whisky scene.

faq.question: What makes the Kavalan SOLIST Fino Sherry Cask special compared to other sherry-cask whiskies?
faq.answer: The Kavalan SOLIST Fino Sherry Cask stands out due to its maturation in fino sherry casks, which impart a unique combination of intense fruitiness, nutty notes, and a complex sweetness. Its thick, viscous texture and the long, sherry-dominated finish clearly distinguish it from classic sherry-cask whiskies.

faq.question: Why does the Kavalan SOLIST Fino Sherry Cask have no age statement (No Age Statement)?
faq.answer: Kavalan deliberately omits an age statement on this single malt to focus on the quality and character of the whisky. The maturation in specially selected fino sherry casks and the high cask strength (57.8% ABV) deliver a complex tasting experience that convinces regardless of age.

faq.question: How should one best enjoy the Kavalan SOLIST Fino Sherry Cask to fully appreciate its aromas?
faq.answer: To fully experience the diverse aromas, it’s recommended to drink the Kavalan SOLIST Fino Sherry Cask neat and at room temperature. A wide nosing glass helps to unfold the intense fruit, nut, and sherry notes. If needed, a few drops of water can be added to further open up the aromas.

item.name: Big Peat Small Batch Release - Douglas Laing
item.description: You might think the glass is empty - that's how transparent the Big Peat Small Batch Release from Douglas Laing's is. But don't be fooled! But waiting in this glass is an exciting Islay experience.

image.alt: Big Peat Small Batch Release - Douglas Laing

tasting_notes.nose: The first impression? Smoke. And then more smoke. It's as if a strong sea breeze is blowing directly from a smoking fisherman's hut. But beneath this intense blanket of smoke lurk subtle notes of iodine and lemon, which give the Big Peat an exciting depth. A hint of vanilla and salt mingles underneath, followed by an unusual note reminiscent of parsnip puree - unusual, but fascinating.
tasting_notes.palate: This whisky is like a sip of pure Islay. Oily and full-bodied, it lies on the tongue and unfolds a phenolic note that is strongly reminiscent of plasters and an idea of a rubber band. This may sound off-putting at first glance, but don't worry - it harmonises perfectly. Buttery flavours, combined with a hint of grass and vanilla ice cream, give the Big Peat a pleasant sweetness. This is joined by an unexpectedly gentle note of sweet leather, which adds an extra dimension to the whisky.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is long, lingering and as smoky as you would expect from a real Islay whisky. Sea salt and a fresh breeze, as it blows on a rocky cliff, make the farewell to this whisky an intense experience. A hint of burnt toast remains as a final memory.

conclusion.marcel: Big Peat Small Batch is like a smoky adventure by the sea – wild, powerful, yet surprisingly approachable. This whisky delivers genuine Islay character in the glass without any rough edges to get in the way. A clear recommendation for fans of maritime dram moments!
conclusion.sascha: This Big Peat impresses with its balanced interplay between intense phenolic notes and a subtle sweetness that elegantly complements the maritime profile. Its clear structure and long-lasting finish make it a solid representative of typical Islay whisky blends, even if its complexity has its limits.

faq.question: Why is the Big Peat Small Batch Release so clear, despite being an intense Islay whisky?
faq.answer: The Big Peat Small Batch Release is neither colored nor chill-filtered, which contributes to its exceptional clarity. Despite this transparency, it delivers an intense smoky flavor experience typical of Islay whiskies.

faq.question: What unique characteristics make the taste of the Big Peat Small Batch Release stand out?
faq.answer: Beyond its dominant smoky flavor, the whisky features subtle notes of iodine, lemon, vanilla, and even parsnip purée. These unusual aromas, combined with a phenolic touch and a gentle leather note, give it a complex and multi-layered flavor profile.

faq.question: How does the independent bottler Douglas Laing influence the character of this whisky?
faq.answer: Douglas Laing carefully selects and blends various Islay whiskies for the Big Peat Small Batch Release to create a distinctive smoky blend. The bottler’s expertise ensures that, despite the absence of an age statement, the whisky offers a balanced and intense tasting experience.

item.name: Balcones Brimstone
item.description: Balcones Brimstone is an exceptional whisky that is smoked using a special method known as the "secret process". Unlike conventional methods, the already distilled product is smoked with sun-dried Texas Schurwald oak rather than the grain. This 100% blue corn whisky is aged for less than 2 years.

tasting_notes.nose: The nose immediately develops the characteristic Balcones pepper, which is combined with the aroma of grilled corn. Notes of burnt wood and sawdust follow, adding a smoky, woody depth. A hint of vanilla and brown sugar brings a subtle sweetness into play, while the smell of BBQ ribs and steak dry rub mingle to create a savoury, spicy profile.
tasting_notes.palate: Am Gaumen präsentiert sich der Balcones Brimstone als intensiv und sehr ölig, mit einer kräftigen Pfeffernote. Der kalte, frische Rauch bietet eine komplexe Rauchnuance, die sich mit getrockneter Petersilie und Chili zu einer faszinierenden Geschmackskombination vereint. Intensiv!
tasting_notes.finish: The finish of the Balcones Brimstone is long and leaves a lasting grilled sausage note, accompanied by pepper and heather. A hint of black tea rounds off the finish.

conclusion.marcel: If you're looking for a whisky with character and a good dose of Texas smoke, the Balcones Brimstone is an excellent choice. It's like a wild harbor cruise: powerful, surprising, and above all, highly entertaining in the glass – a genuine flavor experience that’s a lot of fun.
conclusion.sascha: The Balcones Brimstone impresses with its clear structure and the intense interplay of pepper and smoky notes, which stand out distinctly thanks to the unusual smoking method. Despite its young age, it offers a balanced harmony between spice, sweetness, and a long-lasting, complex finish.

faq.question: How does Balcones Brimstone differ from other smoky whiskies?
faq.answer: Unlike the usual practice of smoking the grain, Balcones Brimstone is smoked after distillation using sun-dried Texas scrub oak wood. This special method imparts a unique smoky depth with aromas of BBQ, pepper, and charred wood.

faq.question: Why is Balcones Brimstone so intense in flavor despite its young age?
faq.answer: Although Balcones Brimstone is under 2 years old, its high alcohol content of 53% and the special smoking process create a very oily texture and a complex flavor profile with strong pepper and smoky notes.

faq.question: What role does 100% Blue Corn play in the production of Balcones Brimstone?
faq.answer: Balcones Brimstone is made exclusively from 100% Blue Corn, giving it a distinctive corn flavor. Combined with the smoke treatment, this results in an intense, spicy whisky that clearly stands apart from traditional barley whiskies.

item.name: Speyburn 16 Years
item.description: When you hear ‘Travel Exclusive’, you immediately think of something special, something that you can only get hold of with difficulty when travelling, don't you? Well, the Speyburn 16 years fulfils this claim... but not in a positive sense.

tasting_notes.nose: From the very first sniff, you get the feeling that it could be something after all. The aromas of ripe peaches and oranges almost jump out at you, accompanied by a pleasant sweetness reminiscent of honey and vanilla. A hint of almond and white chocolate lend the whole thing an almost seductive note. But beware: the fragrance promises more than the whisky can deliver.
tasting_notes.palate: The promising aroma is followed by disillusionment. In the mouth, the Speyburn 16 years is unfortunately more watery than hoped for, which is rather disappointing for a 16 years whisky. Yes, there are spices such as pepper and oak, but they are more intrusive than balanced. The sweetness of honey and white chocolate comes through, but struggles in vain against the excess of old wood and the surprisingly flat structure. A hint of pear and apple tries to turn the tide, but it remains a desperate attempt.
tasting_notes.finish: But the finish saves? Unfortunately not. Medium-long and quickly forgotten, one thing above all remains in the memory: oak spice, a little pepper, a hint of vanilla and, at the very end, a subtle hazelnut note. But even here the experience is rather flat and not very complex.

conclusion.marcel: If you’re looking for a travel bargain with character, you might want to think twice before choosing the Speyburn 16 Years – unfortunately, the wood dominates here, and the promised journey of enjoyment falls flat. Despite its 16 years of maturation, it noticeably lacks depth and excitement, leaving you wondering if this whisky would have been better off staying on the shelf.
conclusion.sascha: The Speyburn 16 Years delivers the typical Speyside aromas but suffers from an unbalanced woodiness and a flat overall structure. The lack of complexity and the watery palate prevent a harmonious balance, which is why this Travel Exclusive comes across as rather average and not particularly memorable.

faq.question: What does the term "Travel Exclusive" mean for the Speyburn 16 Years?
faq.answer: "Travel Exclusive" indicates that the whisky was originally intended to be available only in duty-free shops or travel retail locations. However, in the case of the Speyburn 16 Years, this claim should be viewed critically, as availability can now vary and the designation does not necessarily guarantee a special quality.

faq.question: How does the Speyburn 16 Years differ in taste from other 16-years Speyside whiskies?
faq.answer: The Speyburn 16 Years shows a promising nose with fruity and sweet notes but disappoints on the palate with a watery texture and unbalanced spice. Compared to other 16-years Speyside whiskies, it often lacks the depth and complexity one would normally expect.

faq.question: Which aromas are particularly notable in the Speyburn 16 Years?
faq.answer: On the nose, ripe peaches, oranges, honey, vanilla, as well as almond and white chocolate dominate. On the palate, pepper, oak, pear, and apple come forward, while the finish is characterized by oak spice, pepper, vanilla, and a subtle hint of hazelnut.

item.name: Benromach 10 Years
item.description: In recent years, the small distillery in the north-west of Scotland's Speyside has gained more and more popularity. The Benromach 10 offers a good and affordable entry.Benromach, now part of the independent bottler Gordon & MacPhail, is committed to traditional craftsmanship. They believe that making whisky by hand guarantees real character. Benromach only uses first-fill casks for their whiskies: for the 10-years, 80% are bourbon barrels and 20% Spanish sherry casks. The pleasantly simple and uncluttered packaging and bottle design runs through all age groups.

tasting_notes.nose: After the nose has become accustomed to the smoke, we begin to perceive sweet aromas like sherry. After a while in the glass, light vanilla and caramel and a hint of toffee become noticeable.
tasting_notes.palate: At first, we have the feeling of an overripe, mouth-filling cherry in the mouth. Afterwards, the sweetness changes from a kind of white chocolate to a harsher dark chocolate. The smoke is almost no longer present, instead it subliminally accompanies the slightly leathery and dry feeling on the tongue.
tasting_notes.finish: Also in the medium-long finish the smoke is rather subliminal and flavours like honey, vanilla and toffee are dominant.

conclusion.marcel: Here, Benromach 10 Years shows that good whisky doesn’t have to be old or expensive to be enjoyable. The sweet cherries and subtle smoke create a character that leaves you wanting more – a true everyday hero with charm and soul.
conclusion.sascha: The Benromach 10 presents a balanced aroma profile from bourbon and sherry cask maturation, with the smoke remaining discreetly in the background. Its clear structure and medium-length finish make it a technically solid Speyside whisky with good balance.

faq.question: How do the first-fill casks influence the flavor of Benromach 10 Years?
faq.answer: Benromach 10 Years matures 80% in first-fill bourbon barrels and 20% in Spanish sherry casks. The bourbon barrels contribute notes of vanilla and caramel, while the sherry casks enhance sweet and fruity aromas such as cherry and toffee.

faq.question: How pronounced is the smoky character in Benromach 10 Years?
faq.answer: The smoke in Benromach 10 Years is subtle and develops more as an undercurrent, especially on the palate and finish. Initially, it is noticeable on the nose but is then replaced in the mouth by sweeter and fruitier flavors.

faq.question: What makes the production at Benromach traditional and artisanal?
faq.answer: Benromach emphasizes traditional craftsmanship, including manual steps during production. This approach aims to give the whisky a distinctive and authentic character that sets it apart from industrial mass-produced spirits.

item.name: Balcones Bourbon Ratafia Champenois Cask T-053
item.description: The Balcones Bourbon Ratafia Champenois Cask T-053 is a whiskey that impresses with an exciting cask finish. Produced in the United States, it combines the robust tradition of American bourbon with an exquisite finish in a Ratafia Champenois cask. This bourbon begins its life with three years of maturation in new oak barrels, followed by 52 months of refinement in the special Ratafia Champenois barrel. This results in an abv of 55.2% and a total ageing period of six years.

image.alt: Balcones Bourbon Ratafia Champenois Cask T-053

tasting_notes.nose: The depth and complexity of this drink is revealed from the very first nosing. A sweetish cloud envelops the nose, followed by a fine orange note that subtly shines through. The pepper typical of Balcones is unmistakably present and gives the bouquet a spicy base. This is joined by flavours of rum and baking spices, reminiscent of a warm kitchen. Earthy notes of moss and wet cardboard and a hint of dusty oak shelves complete the olfactory experience. The scent of rye bread is particularly interesting, emphasising the spiciness of the bourbon and at the same time highlighting its origins.
tasting_notes.palate: The Balcones Bourbon Ratafia Champenois shows its best side on the palate. The bourbon character immediately comes to the fore, carried by the typical flavours of corn, rye and caramel. This classic sweetness is complemented by an indefinable note reminiscent of cranberries and BBQ sauce - an unusual but intriguing combination. The slightly smoky undertone and omnipresent pepper add an extra layer of complexity to the flavour. The influences of the Ratafia Champenois cask are clearly noticeable and give the bourbon a soft, almost velvety texture that harmoniously balances the intense flavours. The high rye content (36%) provides a dry, spicy note.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish of the Bourbon is of medium length and leaves a lasting impression. A soft, subtle oak note accompanies the finish, followed by a good portion of rye spice that gently caresses the palate. The kick that comes through at the end is just right and rounds off the flavour experience without being overpowering.

conclusion.marcel: With its intriguing cask maturation, the Balcones Bourbon Ratafia Champenois brings a uniquely playful note to the glass that leaves you wanting more. The sweet fruit aromas meet spicy pepper – a bourbon full of character that never bores but makes drinking fun.
conclusion.sascha: The combination of traditional bourbon and finishing in Ratafia Champenois casks results in a well-balanced profile with complex aromas and a fine structure. Despite the unusual choice of cask, the whisky remains clearly focused, offering a harmonious maturity with a distinctive rye spiciness.

faq.question: What makes the Ratafia Champenois cask finish on the Balcones Bourbon so special?
faq.answer: The Ratafia Champenois cask finish imparts a unique flavor dimension to the Balcones Bourbon. After three years of aging in new oak barrels, the bourbon spends an additional 52 months in casks that previously held Ratafia Champenois. This secondary maturation introduces fruity, almost wine-like notes and a velvety texture to the bourbon, harmoniously complementing its spiciness and sweetness.

faq.question: How does the high rye content influence the taste of this bourbon?
faq.answer: With a rye content of 36%, the Balcones Bourbon exhibits pronounced spiciness expressed through peppery and dry notes. This rye spice adds a dry, slightly sharp component that balances the sweetness from corn and caramel, giving the bourbon greater complexity and structure overall.

faq.question: What aromas can one expect from the Balcones Bourbon Ratafia Champenois in the glass?
faq.answer: In the glass, a complex bouquet unfolds featuring sweet orange notes, characteristic pepper, hints of rum and baking spices, as well as earthy elements like moss and damp cardboard. On the palate, corn, rye, caramel, and unusual nuances of lingonberries and BBQ sauce come together, supported by a subtle smoky undertone and a soft, velvety texture.

item.name: Beverbach Wheat Malt Einbecker German Beer Cask Finish
item.description: The Hardenberg distillery in southern Lower Saxony produces German whisky, which they distribute under the name Beverbach. In a regional collaboration with the Einbecker brewery, today's whisky was further matured after aging in ex-bourbon barrels in former Bock beer casks. Distilled from 100% local wheat malt, they follow a field-to-glass approach here. We're tasting what this locally sourced dram, limited to 1500 bottles and aged for 5 years, has to offer.

image.alt: Beverbach Wheat Malt Einbecker German Beer Cask Finish

tasting_notes.nose: The aroma is exotic. We detect classic notes of green apples and pears, but they are surrounded by a pineapple gummy bear (the white ones) aroma. The wheat malt is lightly floral, and the hops, coming from the beer barrels, are distinctly present. Lighter hints of buttered toast and a touch of vanilla hover above it all.
tasting_notes.palate: On the palate, Beverbach Einbecker is mild, soft, and creamy. The still very light fruity notes are accompanied by a similarly light and fresh herbal quality. The creamy mouthfeel is nicely complemented by Campino candies. On the palate, as in the nose, a slightly bitter hop note emerges.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is short, fresh, and sparkling. Here, the whisky lacks a bit of depth. The young age is unfortunately still noticeable.

conclusion.marcel: Beverbach Wheat Malt is a charming, light treat that’s enjoyable with its sweet-fruity notes and hoppy twist, even though it still lacks depth. As a young craft whisky from the region, it’s a nice introduction, but it won’t captivate every whisky enthusiast.
conclusion.sascha: The Beverbach Wheat Malt shows a solid balance between fruitiness and subtle hop bitterness, yet it remains somewhat flat in structure and complexity. Its young age of five years is noticeable in the short, fresh finish, highlighting the potential for more maturity and finesse.

faq.question: How does the finish in Einbecker Bockbier barrels influence the flavor of Beverbach Wheat Malt?
faq.answer: The finish in former Einbecker Bockbier barrels imparts delicate hop aromas and a slight bitterness, which manifest as fresh herbal and hop notes on the palate. This beer barrel maturation complements the fruity notes and provides a sparkling, fresh finish.

faq.question: What does the 'field-to-glass' approach mean in the production of this whisky?
faq.answer: The 'field-to-glass' approach means the whisky is made from 100% locally grown wheat malt that is processed directly on site. This ensures a high level of regionality and transparency in production, which influences the quality and character of the whisky.

faq.question: Why does Beverbach Wheat Malt still taste relatively young despite its aromas, and how is this noticeable?
faq.answer: At five years old, the whisky is relatively young, which is reflected in a rather short, slightly flat finish. It lacks complexity and depth, typical for young whiskies, as they have had less time to develop rich flavors and a long-lasting structure.

item.name: Old Forester 86 Proof
item.description: The distillery, built in 1935, is the largest distillery in Louisville. However, unlike many other bourbon distilleries, only two bourbons are still produced here today. The first is the Old Forester, which inherited its name from the Forester distillery that used to be not far away. The second and far more famous bourbon is Early Times, whose name is also borne by the distillery. Old Forester is the only bourbon that was continuously distilled by the founding family before, during and after Prohibition.

tasting_notes.nose: The Old Forester 86 Proof immediately impresses with its dominant banana flavour. The thought "What the hell: banana!!!" comes to mind, as this note is so unusual and yet so tantalising. A hint of marzipan potato continues to unfold, followed by caramel, which envelops the nose with a sweet warmth. The oak emerges discreetly and complements the sweet flavours perfectly. A splash of vanilla ice cream rounds off the bouquet and you can literally sense a banana split with amaretto, which adds a seductive depth and complexity.
tasting_notes.palate: The sweet profile continues on the palate, starting with a clear caramel note. This is accompanied by nutty and marzipan-like flavours that give the bourbon a rich texture. The flavour is a little flat, but this is compensated by the distinctive note of rye bread, especially pumpernickel. A hint of ginger provides a subtle spiciness and enlivens the palate with a fresh flavour.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish of the Old Forester 86 Proof is long and satisfying. The oak remains present and forms the basis of the aftertaste, while a spicy-sweet component ensures lasting pleasure. The balance between the sweet and spicy elements makes the finish particularly pleasant and invites further enjoyment.

conclusion.marcel: Old Forester 86 Proof impresses with its surprising banana note and sweet, almost playful character – a bourbon that's genuinely fun and, with its spicy finish, leaves you wanting more. For anyone seeking uncomplicated drinking pleasure with a touch of mischief, it’s a loyal companion.
conclusion.sascha: The Old Forester 86 Proof displays a solid balance between sweet caramel and spicy rye notes, yet remains somewhat one-dimensional in flavor. Its clear structure and long, balanced finish make it a reliable bourbon, especially providing newcomers with a straightforward point of reference.

faq.question: Why does the Old Forester 86 Proof have such a pronounced banana aroma on the nose?
faq.answer: The distinctive banana scent arises from the specific yeast strains and fermentation process used in crafting this bourbon. These fruity esters are typical of certain bourbon styles and give Old Forester 86 Proof its unusual yet pleasant aromatic profile.

faq.question: How does Old Forester 86 Proof differ from other bourbons in terms of its production history?
faq.answer: Old Forester is unique because it is the only bourbon continuously distilled by the founding family before, during, and after Prohibition. This uninterrupted production history is reflected in its traditional style and time-tested recipe.

faq.question: What does the '86 Proof' designation mean for this bourbon, and how does it relate to the alcohol content in percent?
faq.answer: '86 Proof' is an American measure of alcohol content and corresponds to 43% alcohol by volume. The proof number is twice the alcohol percentage, so Old Forester 86 Proof has exactly 43% ABV.

item.name: Maclean’s Nose
item.description: The independent bottler Adelphi has been a staple of high-quality whiskies for many decades. To meet the high demand, they opened the Ardnamurchan Distillery in 2014, returning to their roots as distillers. Recently, a Blended Malt has found its way into Adelphi's portfolio with Maclean's Nose. With a malt content of 70%, and bottled following Adelphi's classic philosophy of no added coloring or chill filtration, "the Nose" aims to impress.

tasting_notes.nose: The nose (pun intended) immediately surprises with its complexity. We find an extremely warm but also "dirty" smoke. This is accompanied by citrus and orange zest, as well as freshly grated applesauce. Over time, the malt opens up to reveal herbal notes with licorice and freshly cut grass. The aroma is rounded off by a touch of sea salt and a pleasant hint of almond.
tasting_notes.palate: On the palate, the sherry cask influence becomes quite evident. We detect both fresher notes of vanilla ice cream with chocolate shavings and the smoke of a recently extinguished campfire. In addition, there's burnt caramel, a pronounced chocolatey richness, and a hint of plum compote.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is long and carried by smoke. We continue to taste the pleasant sweetness of plums and raisins, complemented by white chocolate. The aftertaste lingers with hints of licorice and nutmeg that remain on the palate for quite some time.

conclusion.marcel: Maclean's Nose proves that a no-age-statement blended malt can be genuinely enjoyable – warm, smoky, and packed with plenty of character. If you appreciate layered aromas and drinkability, this is a real bargain. A whisky that’s sure to spark conversation at your next gathering.
conclusion.sascha: With Maclean's Nose, Adelphi delivers a well-balanced blended malt that impresses with its structured harmony between sherry cask notes, smoke, and delicate fruit tones. The complex aroma profile at 46% ABV without chill filtration demonstrates technical finesse, making it a fine example of modern whisky craftsmanship. A cleverly composed whisky that offers stability and depth despite the absence of an age statement.

faq.question: What makes the Maclean’s Nose blend stand out compared to other blended malts?
faq.answer: Maclean’s Nose contains a high malt content of 70% and is bottled according to Adelphi’s philosophy without added coloring or chill filtration. This preserves a natural complexity with a balanced interplay of smoky, fruity, and spicy notes.

faq.question: What influence does the sherry cask maturation have on the taste of Maclean’s Nose?
faq.answer: The sherry cask maturation imparts sweet and fruity aromas such as vanilla ice cream, caramel, chocolate, and plum jam. At the same time, it supports the smoky notes and contributes to a complex, long-lasting finish.

faq.question: Why does Adelphi avoid chill filtration and coloring in Maclean’s Nose?
faq.answer: Adelphi focuses on natural bottlings to present the whisky as authentically as possible. Avoiding chill filtration preserves the flavor diversity and texture, while no coloring is added to maintain the authenticity of the whisky’s color.

item.name: St. Kilian The Early Days – Ketterer Wash X St. Kilian – Unpeated
item.description: Bit by bit, as the St. Kilian distillery in Rüdenau continues its presence, we're seeing older releases emerge. Today, we sampled an 8-years Ketterer x St. Kilian from the X-Series, named after the customs numbers of the barrels containing foreign wash. The wash for this single malt was sourced from the Ketterer family brewery, the former domain of Master Distiller Mario Rudolf. Unusually for St. Kilian, this whisky isn't a marriage of fully matured spirits. Instead, it has been re-racked, spending portions of its maturation in ex-bourbon and ex-Sauternes barrique casks.

image.alt: St. Kilian The Early Days – Ketterer Wash X St. Kilian – Unpeated

tasting_notes.nose: The nose presents itself delicately and harmoniously, with subtle sweetness of light grapes and green apples. This unfolds into lightly vinous notes of strawberries, cream toffee, and subtle vanilla, complemented by a hint of spicy oak.
tasting_notes.palate: On the palate, this St. Kilian is full-bodied and creamy-smooth, offering pleasantly balanced, yet somewhat exotic fruitiness of pineapple and coconut, accompanied by hints of forest berries. Here too, the whisky evolves into a spicy direction with oak and very fine tannins.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is finely tuned and very rounded, medium in length and not overly spicy, with the fruit notes receding further into the background. What lingers is dark caramel, slight nuttiness, and a wonderful dryness with a hint of sherry.

conclusion.marcel: This St. Kilian proves that even an 8-years single malt with an unusual wash can be a truly well-rounded experience – creamy, fruity, and with a delightful dose of toffee that simply melts on the palate. For connoisseurs who love bold combinations, this is a genuine recommendation from Rüdenau that’s fun to drink yet never loses its subtly playful elegance.
conclusion.sascha: The complex balance between ex-bourbon and Sauternes cask maturation reveals itself here in a structured, well-balanced profile with clear fruity sweetness and delicate tannin notes. Despite its 58.3% ABV, the whisky remains harmonious, offering a precise interplay of fruit, spice, and subtle dryness – a technically solid example of St. Kilian’s innovative spirit.

faq.question: What makes the St. Kilian The Early Days – Ketterer Wash X St. Kilian – Unpeated special in its production process?
faq.answer: This whisky is special because the wash comes from the family brewery Ketterer, the former workplace of Master Distiller Mario Rudolf. Additionally, the whisky was not bottled as a pure full maturation but underwent a transfer between ex-bourbon and ex-Sauternes barrique casks, which imparts a complex flavor structure.

faq.question: How do the different cask types influence the taste of this St. Kilian whisky?
faq.answer: The combination of maturation in ex-bourbon and ex-Sauternes barrique casks creates a balanced harmony between sweet, fruity notes like pineapple and coconut and spicy oak and sherry hints. This gives the whisky a complex depth with a fine dryness on the finish.

faq.question: Why is the St. Kilian The Early Days – Ketterer Wash X St. Kilian – Unpeated already very complex in taste despite being only 8 years old?
faq.answer: The complexity arises from the combination of the special Ketterer wash and maturation in different cask types. The transfer between bourbon and Sauternes casks brings out a variety of aromas that give the impression of greater maturity than the age alone would suggest.

item.name: Compass Box Celestial
item.description: The Compass Box Celestial is a fascinating blend that combines the best characteristics of Caol Ila, Ardbeg, Cameronbridge and Port Dundas. This masterfully composed whisky brings together the characteristic aromas and flavours of these renowned distilleries and offers an unparalleled enjoyment experience. In this review, we delve into the complex and multi-layered characteristics of this unique blend.

tasting_notes.nose: The nose of Compass Box Celestial is dominated by a campfire smoke that immediately brings to mind a warm, cosy fire. A medicinal undertone gives the fragrance an interesting depth, which is complemented by caramel and maritime notes. A hint of baked apple and lime brings a sweet and citrus-fresh component into play. The nutty and tropical flavours are almost reminiscent of rum, while a subtle note of ginger pleasantly invigorates the nose.
tasting_notes.palate: On the palate, the blend develops a warm and campfire-like smokiness that immediately brings to mind a meaty BBQ. The multi-layered smokiness is impressive and is complemented by an intense sweetness and an oily (almost sticky) texture. The whisky is full-bodied and develops a remarkable complexity that makes every sip an experience.
tasting_notes.finish: The very long finish also leaves a lasting impression. The great oak is really impressive and is complemented by recurring ginger and citrus notes. The finish is voluminously smoky, with a hint of light tar and dried red fruit. A subtle bacon flavour rounds off the finish and provides a unique and long-lasting aftertaste.

conclusion.marcel: If the Compass Box Celestial doesn’t make you swoon, you probably don’t have fire in your heart – this whisky is a true taste experience full of smoke, sweetness, and character. It impressively demonstrates just how exciting blends can be when passion and craftsmanship go hand in hand. A dramatic delight that brings good cheer on any occasion.
conclusion.sascha: The Compass Box Celestial impresses with its remarkable balance between intense smokiness, complex sweetness, and a multi-layered texture. The precise composition of flavors and the long-lasting finish make it an outstanding example of masterful blending with technical finesse. An analytically impressive whisky that perfectly unites structure and character.

faq.question: Which distilleries are included in the Compass Box Celestial blend?
faq.answer: The Compass Box Celestial combines whiskies from the distilleries Caol Ila, Ardbeg, Cameronbridge, and Port Dundas, offering a complex mix of different styles.

faq.question: How does the high alcohol content of 50% affect the taste of the Compass Box Celestial?
faq.answer: At 50% ABV, the Compass Box Celestial delivers an intense flavor development that emphasizes smokiness, sweetness, and spice without being too harsh, especially when diluted with a little water.

faq.question: Why does the Compass Box Celestial have no age statement (NAS)?
faq.answer: The Compass Box Celestial is a No Age Statement (NAS) blend, meaning it consists of whiskies of various ages to achieve a balanced and complex flavor profile without being tied to a specific age limit.

item.name: Teeling Riesling Wine Cask Grand Cru Edition (2021)
item.description: The Teeling Riesling Cask in the Grand Cru Edition 2021 is the third edition of the collaboration between the Irish distillery and the German Reichsrat von Buhl winery. After maturing in ex-bourbon casks, the Irish whiskey has been refined for 18 months in selected Grand Cru Riesling casks.

image.alt: Teeling Riesling Wine Cask Grand Cru Edition 2021

tasting_notes.nose: The nose of the Teeling Riesling Wine Cask Grand Cru is a fruit firework. Bright fruits and grapes are immediately present and give the flavour a fresh, sweet note. The flattering notes of a little honey and orange/lime add a delicate sweetness and citrus freshness. Grain notes are subtle, while a slight sour note adds an extra dimension to the bouquet.
tasting_notes.palate: On the palate, the Irishman is reminiscent of white rum, which adds an unusual but fascinating component. The whisky appears youthful and shows an extreme acidity that is strongly reminiscent of lime and pineapple. Salted caramel and vanilla provide a balanced sweetness that softens the acidic character somewhat and gives the flavour additional depth.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is short, but notable for the presence of oak and a slight furry note, which gives the finish a certain charm. Although the finish is rather short, it leaves a lasting impression and rounds off the flavour experience.

conclusion.marcel: This young Teeling Riesling Wine Cask 2021 brings plenty of fun to the glass with its lively acidity and unusual white rum touch. It’s not a classic whisky, but rather a refreshing, slightly cheeky dram for those eager to try something new – perfect for sociable evenings without any claim to perfection.
conclusion.sascha: The Teeling Riesling Wine Cask Grand Cru Edition 2021 presents an intriguing balance between pronounced acidity and sweet vanilla notes, yet it remains somewhat underdeveloped in structure and finish. For fans of experimental cask maturation, it offers interesting aromas, but it doesn’t quite achieve the harmony needed to become a true long-term favourite.

faq.question: How does maturation in Grand Cru Riesling casks influence the flavor of the Teeling Riesling Wine Cask Grand Cru Edition 2021?
faq.answer: Maturation in selected Grand Cru Riesling casks imparts fruity aromas of white grapes, citrus notes like orange and lime, as well as a subtle acidity reminiscent of lime and pineapple. This cask aging complements the bourbon barrel notes with a fresh, sweet-and-sour complexity.

faq.question: Why is the Teeling Riesling Wine Cask Grand Cru Edition 2021, despite lacking an age statement, not a typical young whisky?
faq.answer: Although there is no age statement, the combination of bourbon barrel maturation and an additional 18 months in Grand Cru Riesling casks creates a complex aroma profile. This gives the whisky a matured fruitiness and depth that you don’t always find in young whiskies.

faq.question: Which flavor notes make the Teeling Riesling Wine Cask Grand Cru Edition 2021 particularly unusual compared to other Irish whiskeys?
faq.answer: The whisky displays an unusual acidity and fruitiness reminiscent of white rum, combined with intense citrus notes and a pronounced lime and pineapple acidity. This is accompanied by sweet accents of salted caramel and vanilla, plus a short, oak-driven finish with a slight velvety touch.

item.name: Fettercairn 4th Release 16 Years (2023)
item.description: This Highland single malt from the Fettercairn distillery matured in various cask types. These include former bourbon casks and first fill port casks. Fettercairn regularly releases new batches of the core bottling. Here we have the 4th batch / 2023.

tasting_notes.nose: The Fettercairn 16 years unfolds a true malt bomb on the nose that immediately catches the eye. The intense cereal notes are dominant and are accompanied by a clear, fresh hay flavour - without the horses that nobody likes, but with the pleasant freshness of a well-kept stable. A sweet hint of apple sauce and honey brings a pleasant fruitiness, while pineapple and custard powder provide additional exotic and creamy notes.
tasting_notes.palate: On the palate, the Highland whisky has a seductive fig note and a mix of exotic fruits that provide an intense sweetness. The toasted oak brings a complex, woody depth and elegantly complements the flavour experience. The balance between fruitiness and oak is remarkable and gives the whisky a balanced yet exciting flavour.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish of the Fettercairn 16 Years is long and round, leaving a lasting impression. The bitter oak provides an interesting twist, while ginger, banana, cocoa sultana and walnut offer a rich and complex finish. This finish is both warm and layered, rounding off the experience in an impressive way.

conclusion.marcel: The Fettercairn 16 Years is a true delight for whisky lovers – malty, fruity, and with a pleasant touch of warmth on the finish. Anyone looking for a whisky that’s both fun and full of character will be well served here. For me, it’s a Highland that easily deserves a spot in the home bar.
conclusion.sascha: A well-balanced single malt with a convincing structure that sits perfectly between toasted oak and fruity notes. The combination of first-fill port casks and bourbon barrels provides an elegant depth, even if the complexity doesn’t quite reach the top tier. Solid maturation and harmonious balance make it a highly recommendable Highland whisky.

faq.question: What types of casks were used for the Fettercairn 4th Release 16 Years 2023?
faq.answer: The whisky matured in ex-bourbon barrels and first-fill port casks, giving it a combination of sweet vanilla and fruity port wine notes.

faq.question: How does the 4th batch of Fettercairn 16 Years differ from previous releases?
faq.answer: The 2023 4th batch is characterized by a particularly intense malt note and a balanced interplay between fruity and woody aromas, enhanced by maturation in port casks.

faq.question: Which flavor notes are especially noticeable in the Fettercairn 16 Years?
faq.answer: The whisky offers aromas of malt, hay, applesauce, honey, pineapple, and vanilla on the palate, complemented by notes of figs, toasted oak, and a long finish featuring ginger, banana, cocoa raisins, and walnut.

item.name: Glenfarclas 15 Years
item.description: Glenfarclas 15 years is a whisky that embodies the spirit of independence. As a traditional single malt from the Speyside region, which has been run by the Grant family for generations, it stands for quality and authenticity. A true classic.

tasting_notes.nose: On the nose, the Glenfarclas 15 years shows a slightly dry note, which is complemented by a tart freshness. Green apples are prominent, accompanied by a sweet honey note and a hint of cream that smooths the flavour. Cinnamon spice and almonds add another dimension, while an exotic kumquat note gives the bouquet an interesting depth.
tasting_notes.palate: On the tongue, the single malt is mildly fruity at the top and provides a tingling sensation that invigorates the taste buds. The whisky is full-bodied and develops a spicy intensity that is complemented by the flavour of roasted honey dates and toasted oak. Notes of old wood give it a deep, earthy complexity that rounds off the overall picture.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is warming and dry, leaving a pleasant aftertaste of walnut and spicy flavours. A hint of coloured pepper and coffee beans provides additional depth and makes the finish particularly long and complex.

conclusion.marcel: A whisky that’s a lot of fun and scores with its warm spiciness and fruity notes – the Glenfarclas 15 Year is a charming classic that impresses without any fuss. Perfect for cozy evenings when you just want to enjoy without overthinking.
conclusion.sascha: The Glenfarclas 15 Year captivates with its balanced structure and the harmonious integration of sherry aromas with spicy and fruity nuances. Thanks to its complex yet well-balanced composition, it’s an excellent choice for connoisseurs who appreciate depth and precision.

faq.question: How does the Glenfarclas 15 Year differ from other Speyside whiskies?
faq.answer: The Glenfarclas 15 Year stands out through its combination of fruity notes like green apples and kumquat, along with a spicy depth featuring cinnamon, almonds, and charred oak. Its full-bodied texture and long, warming finish with walnut and pepper notes set it apart from many other Speyside whiskies.

faq.question: Why is the 46% alcohol strength important for the Glenfarclas 15 Year?
faq.answer: At 46% ABV, the Glenfarclas 15 Year offers a more intense range of aromas and greater complexity than many standard 40% whiskies. The higher alcohol content preserves the spicy and fruity notes and provides a fuller mouthfeel without coming across as harsh.

faq.question: What type of cask maturation is used for the Glenfarclas 15 Year and how does it influence the flavor?
faq.answer: The Glenfarclas 15 Year matures predominantly in Oloroso sherry casks, which impart its characteristic sweet honeyed note, nutty aromas, and spicy depth. This cask maturation creates a balanced combination of fruitiness, spice, and a pleasant, slightly dry oak structure.

item.name: Haran 12 Years Sherry Cask Finish
item.description: The Haran 12 Years Sherry Cask Finish raises the exciting question: Spanish single malt - what is it? With this question in mind, we can look forward to the flavour experiences of this whisky. The combination of Spanish origin and sherry cask finish promises an interesting and possibly surprising tasting experience.

tasting_notes.nose: At first sniff, the Haran 12 Years reveals a primarily earthy note that spreads pleasantly throughout the glass. This profile is complemented by a light caramel note that adds a subtle sweetness. A hint of hay and grapefruit peel is added, giving the flavour a fresh, citrusy note. The light cobbled note adds an unconventional but interesting dimension, while a delicate malt tone provides a pleasant depth.
tasting_notes.palate: On the palate, the Spaniard presents itself with an acidic note accompanied by a clear peat character. Citrus fruits and cereals provide a fresh yet warming flavour experience. The creamy texture harmoniously complements the flavours, while herbs and a slight saltiness provide additional complexity.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is of medium length and is characterised by aromas of yellow peach and light oak tones. A gentle nuttiness rounds off the finish and leaves a pleasant, slightly sweet aftertaste.

conclusion.marcel: The Haran 12 Years Sherry Cask Finish proves that Spanish single malt can indeed have character – it’s lively, with an intriguing acidity and a pleasant creaminess. A whisky that’s fun to drink and piques curiosity with its blend of earthy and fruity notes. Not mainstream, but precisely for that reason, a rewarding moment of enjoyment.
conclusion.sascha: With well-balanced aromas of peat, citrus, and sherry influence, the Haran 12 Years impresses through its complex structure and medium-length, nuanced finish. The unusual acidity is somewhat dominant but integrates well into the harmonious overall profile. A technically solid example of Spanish single malt with potential, though it could still benefit from a bit of refinement.

faq.question: What makes the Haran 12 Years Sherry Cask Finish a Spanish single malt whisky?
faq.answer: The Haran 12 Years Sherry Cask Finish is produced at the Spanish Acha distillery and uses only malted barley, which classifies it as a single malt. The sherry cask finish also imparts typical Spanish sherry aromas, distinguishing it from classic Scottish or Irish whiskies.

faq.question: How does the sherry cask finish influence the taste of this whisky?
faq.answer: The sherry cask finish brings a subtle sweetness and fruity notes like yellow peach to the aroma and finish of the Haran 12 Years. At the same time, it supports the earthy and caramel base notes, creating a balanced and complex flavor profile.

faq.question: Which flavor notes stand out particularly in the Haran 12 Years Sherry Cask Finish?
faq.answer: Characteristic are the earthy and caramel notes, complemented by fresh citrus hints such as grapefruit peel and hay on the nose. On the palate, sour components, peat, herbs, and a light saltiness come through. The medium-length finish reveals flavors of yellow peach, oak, and a gentle nuttiness.

item.name: Sicilian Fruitcake - The Cask Wizard
item.description: The Sicilian Fruitcake from The Cask Wizard is a fascinating whisky matured in the rare White Marsala Cask. The cask was pre-aged for 15 long years with the Sicilian liqueur wine Marsala.

tasting_notes.nose: The nose of the Sicilian Fruitcake is a real treat. Strawberries dominate the flavour, followed by a refreshing hint of peach iced tea. A subtle almond note adds a nutty dimension, while wine/grape contributes a fruity undertone. The burnt caramel and dates lend the bouquet a sweet, slightly smoky depth that invites you to linger.
tasting_notes.palate: On the palate, the whisky is oily and full-bodied. A clear sherry note dominates the flavour, complemented by overripe mango and blood orange, which offer a tropical sweetness and a slight acidity. Currants and refined sugar provide additional sweetness, while the ripe flat peach and freshly tanned leather give the whisky a complex and earthy depth.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is medium to long and leaves a warming sensation. Oak spice emerges, accompanied by red fruits and caramel, giving the finish a pleasant sweetness and complexity.

conclusion.marcel: The Sicilian Fruitcake is a true flavor experience, delighting with its fruity notes and creamy mouthfeel. Despite the intriguing White Marsala cask maturation, it ultimately lacks that certain something to truly surprise – but it’s definitely a great companion for enjoyment.
conclusion.sascha: Technically clean and well-balanced, the Sicilian Fruitcake delivers a complex array of aromas shaped by the White Marsala pre-aging. Nevertheless, the cask influence remains rather subtle, making the whisky solid but not exceptional.

faq.question: How does the White Marsala cask influence the flavor of the Sicilian Fruitcake whisky?
faq.answer: The White Marsala cask in which the whisky was matured imparts pronounced fruit notes such as strawberries and peach iced tea, along with a sweet depth from burnt caramel and dates. The 15-year pre-aging with Marsala fortified wine creates a complex combination of fruitiness and subtle spice.

faq.question: Why does the Sicilian Fruitcake have such a high alcohol content of 59.1%?
faq.answer: The Sicilian Fruitcake is bottled at cask strength of 59.1% to fully showcase the intense aromas of the White Marsala cask and the whisky’s complex flavor profile. This high alcohol content preserves the depth and character of the whisky without dilution.

faq.question: What distinguishes the Sicilian Fruitcake from classic sherry whiskies?
faq.answer: Unlike classic sherry whiskies, the Sicilian Fruitcake matures in a rare White Marsala cask, which brings a different kind of sweetness and fruitiness. The combination of tropical fruits like mango and blood orange with nutty and leathery notes makes it particularly complex and distinctive.

item.name: The Targe (1994)
item.description: LIDL's The Targe 1994 is an interesting grain whisky that takes the limelight thanks to its maturation and attractive competitive price. This whisky offers a wealth of aromas and flavours that make it a remarkable offering in the LIDL whisky range.

tasting_notes.nose: The single grain whisky from the Scottish Highlands opens the nose with a bready aroma reminiscent of freshly baked bread. Vanilla and tobacco add a sweet and slightly smoky depth, while a hint of soap offers a subtle but interesting note. The scent of coconut crumble dough brings a pleasant, sweet complexity, complemented by light, bright fruit notes that add freshness to the bouquet.
tasting_notes.palate: On the palate, the LIDL whisky has a flavour of hay and honey, which lends a natural sweetness and depth. Vanilla comes through clearly and gives the whisky a rich sweetness, while fresh wood and a well-defined oak provide a solid structure. The note of coconut completes the flavour profile and brings an exotic sweetness into play.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is of medium length and offers a pleasant oak flavour. A hint of stevia provides additional sweetness, which blends harmoniously into the warming finish.

conclusion.marcel: Lighthearted and charming, The Targe 1994 shows that grain whisky doesn’t have to be boring – with its sweet vanilla and exotic coconut notes, it’s simply enjoyable. At this bargain price, it’s a genuine pleasure that leaves you wanting more, even if it just slightly misses out on the deepest complexity.
conclusion.sascha: The Targe 1994 impresses with a solid balance between sweet vanilla, oak, and a subtle fruitiness that gives this grain whisky structure. The maturation brings a pleasant complexity, although there is still room for greater precision in the fine-tuning of its aromatic profile.

faq.question: What distinguishes a single grain whisky like The Targe 1994 from a single malt?
faq.answer: A single grain whisky is primarily made from grains such as corn, wheat, or oats, whereas single malt is made exclusively from malted barley. The Targe 1994 is a Highland single grain that stands out through its maturation and diverse aromas, without the typical malt character of a single malt.

faq.question: How does the 23-year aging influence the taste of The Targe 1994?
faq.answer: The lengthy 23-year maturation gives The Targe 1994 a complex structure with pronounced oak and woody notes. Over time, sweet aromas like vanilla, honey, and coconut develop, while the medium-length oak spice in the finish rounds out the flavor.

faq.question: Why is the 44% alcohol content relevant for this whisky?
faq.answer: At 44% ABV, The Targe 1994 sits slightly above the standard 40%, which helps to present the aromas more intensely and balanced. This alcohol level supports both the sweetness and the spice, delivering a full-bodied taste without coming across as too harsh.

item.name: Three Ships 12 Years
item.description: We speak for ourselves and probably for many others: We didn't realise that whisky comes from South Africa. And we were even less aware that this single malt whisky is smoky and yes - small spoiler - it's really not bad!

tasting_notes.nose: The Three Ships 12 Years greets the nose with a vanilla-sweet note that immediately conveys a pleasant warmth. This is followed by a malty, cereal flavour reminiscent of freshly baked bread. A hint of sponge cake lends the flavour a subtle but seductive sweetness. Interestingly, iodised nuances also emerge, which, together with medicinal smoke, are reminiscent of a gentle, peaty landscape. A subtle note of suede rounds off the complex bouquet and gives the whisky an elegant, earthy depth.
tasting_notes.palate: On the palate, the Three Ships 12 Years unfolds a fascinating palette of flavours. The first impression is of a pine forest, fresh and green, with hints of forest floor and decayed needle leaves adding an earthy, natural note. These woody flavours are enlivened by a zesty citrus freshness that adds a vibrant and refreshing dimension to the whisky.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is long and salivating, a sign of its high quality and complexity. A peppery, spicy note lingers on the tongue and ensures a warm, pleasant finish that leaves you wanting more.

conclusion.marcel: The Three Ships 12 Year old surprises with a charming blend of vanilla, malt, and a hint of peat smoke – a genuine taste experience from South Africa that delights with its lively freshness and peppery-spicy finish. Anyone looking for a whisky with character and drinkability should definitely grab this one!
conclusion.sascha: A well-structured whisky with a balanced harmony between sweet vanilla notes, earthy forest aromas, and a subtle smoky element. The Three Ships 12 Year old reveals clear aromatic depth and a long-lasting, spicy finish that underscores its maturity and quality – technically well-crafted, with a distinctive profile outside the classic whisky regions.

faq.question: How does the Three Ships 12 Year old differ from traditional single malts from Scotland?
faq.answer: The Three Ships 12 Year old hails from South Africa and is characterized by a unique combination of peaty-smoky and iodine notes reminiscent of a gentle, peaty landscape. Additionally, it brings fresh, forest-like aromas and a zesty citrus brightness, setting it apart from many Scottish single malts, which often focus more heavily on smoke and peat.

faq.question: Which flavor notes should I pay special attention to when tasting the Three Ships 12 Year old?
faq.answer: On tasting, vanilla-sweet and malty-cereal notes stand out first, followed by aromas like ladyfinger biscuits, iodine nuances, and a touch of medicinal smoke. On the palate, fresh, foresty aromas dominate with hints of forest floor and citrus brightness. The finish is long-lasting with peppery-spicy notes.

faq.question: How should the Three Ships 12 Year old ideally be served to best unfold its aromas?
faq.answer: The Three Ships 12 Year old reveals its complexity best at room temperature in a tulip-shaped whisky glass. A few drops of water can help open up the aromas and better perceive the fresh, foresty, and smoky nuances. It is suitable both neat and with a light addition of water.

item.name: GlenAllachie 9 Years Oloroso Sherry Cask Finish - The Wood Collection
item.description: In the limited Wood Collection, GlenAllachie showcases the effect that different types of sherry can have on whisky maturation. They transferred their Single Malt, aged for 6.5 years in ex-bourbon casks, into casks previously filled with different sherries: Fino, Amontillado, and Oloroso. After a total maturation period of 9 years, all three variants were bottled at 48%, without coloring or chill filtration. On invitation from Kirsch Import and Billy Walker himself, we had the pleasure of comparing these three bottlings side by side, focusing on how cask management and selection influence the aroma.

image.alt: GlenAllachie 9 Years Oloroso Sherry Cask Finish - The Wood Collection

tasting_notes.nose: Even in the Oloroso maturation, the typical GlenAllachie character shines through: heather honey, vanilla, and an impressive malty sweetness. On top of that, there are intense, rich, and quite typical sherry aromas. We detect plums, overripe cherries, and candied oranges. Additionally, there is a pleasant spiciness of cinnamon, nutmeg, perhaps a hint of cloves, and a certain herbaceousness. The finish then becomes slightly peppery, with a touch of bitterness and a hint of leather.
tasting_notes.palate: On the palate, the malt delivers what the nose promises. Full-bodied, oily, and voluminous, this whisky fills the mouth. The sweetness is complex, comprising malt, red fruits like cherries, and a hint of strawberries. After a short while, the spicy notes become more pronounced. Coffee grounds, dark chocolate, and again the light aroma of old leather are accompanied by the taste of black pepper. There is also a pronounced oak presence, which, however, does not carry the initially suspected bitterness.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is very long and warming. Intense oak is accompanied by the lingering flavor of dark chocolate. The spiciness recedes somewhat, giving way to the aroma of dried plums.

conclusion.marcel: This GlenAllachie Oloroso is like a cozy chat by the fireplace: full-bodied, warm, and with a seductive sweetness of red fruits and chocolate that’s simply a joy. Anyone who loves sherry-matured whisky will find here an intense yet never overwhelming character – a true evening of enjoyment bottled up!
conclusion.sascha: The 9-years GlenAllachie finished in Oloroso impresses with its complex balance between heavy sherry intensity and malty sweetness, while the spicy oak provides solid structural support to the malt. Despite the opulent aromas, the whisky remains harmonious and well-balanced, even though the freshness of the ex-bourbon casks recedes noticeably.

faq.question: How does the Oloroso Sherry Cask Finish influence the flavor of the GlenAllachie 9-years whisky?
faq.answer: The Oloroso Sherry Cask Finish imparts intense sherry aromas to the GlenAllachie 9-years, such as plums, overripe cherries, and candied oranges. Additionally, it brings spicy notes of cinnamon, nutmeg, and cloves, along with a subtle leather and pepper finish.

faq.question: Why was the GlenAllachie 9-years whisky bottled without chill filtration and coloring?
faq.answer: Bottling without chill filtration and without added coloring preserves the whisky’s natural aromas and original color. This allows for a more authentic tasting experience and showcases the quality of the cask management.

faq.question: How important is cask management in the maturation of the GlenAllachie 9-years Oloroso Sherry Cask Finish?
faq.answer: Cask management is crucial, as the whisky initially matures for 6.5 years in ex-bourbon barrels before being transferred to Oloroso sherry casks. This combination significantly influences the whisky’s aroma profile, complexity, and character.

item.name: GlenAllachie 9 Years Amontillado Sherry Cask Finish - The Wood Collection
item.description: In the limited Wood Collection, GlenAllachie showcases the effect that different types of sherry can have on whisky maturation. They transferred their Single Malt, aged for 6.5 years in ex-bourbon casks, into casks previously filled with different sherries: Fino, Amontillado, and Oloroso. After a total maturation period of 9 years, all three variants were bottled at 48%, without coloring or chill filtration. On invitation from Kirsch Import and Billy Walker himself, we had the pleasure of comparing these three bottlings side by side, focusing on how cask management and selection influence the aroma.

image.alt: GlenAllachie 9 Years Amontillado Sherry Cask Finish - The Wood Collection

tasting_notes.nose: On first sniff, the GlenAllachie 9 Years reveals an intriguing combination of woody notes and a subtle alcohol presence. The nose is accompanied by a vibrant fruitiness, with lemon zest and honey being the dominant flavours. Nectarines and pecan nuts complete the profile and provide a pleasant sweetness, which is rounded off by slight hints of red grapes and caramel. Nuances of pear and spicy notes are also recognisable, harmoniously interacting with milk chocolate and nutty undertones.
tasting_notes.palate: The flavour of the Amontillado Sherry Cask Finish reveals a powerful fruity explosion. Distinctly spicy notes, particularly of ginger, come to the fore and give the whisky a pleasant warmth. The creamy texture of milk chocolate and the buttery flavours of a butter biscuit round off the Scotch and ensure a good balance between sweetness and spice.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish of this whisky is of medium length. The flavours are well balanced, with very light leather notes adding depth without overloading the overall impression. The fruitiness remains subtle and blends harmoniously into the overall picture.

conclusion.marcel: With its full-bodied character and the fruity-spicy explosion in the glass, this GlenAllachie 9 Year Amontillado Sherry Cask Finish invites you to enjoy it in a relaxed way – it shows that maturation can sometimes mean more depth and less freshness without becoming boring.
conclusion.sascha: A well-balanced Speyside single malt with complex aromas and a harmonious structure; the combination of the Amontillado finish and 48% ABV creates an interesting balance between sweetness, spice, and a velvety texture, although it occasionally drifts into a somewhat muddled overall impression.

faq.question: How does the Amontillado Sherry Cask Finish influence the taste of this GlenAllachie 9 Year whisky?
faq.answer: The Amontillado Sherry Cask Finish imparts a robust fruity note with spicy accents like ginger. It provides a creamy texture with flavors of milk chocolate and butter biscuit, creating a balanced interplay between sweetness and spice.

faq.question: Why does GlenAllachie forgo chill filtration and coloring in this whisky?
faq.answer: Foregoing chill filtration and artificial coloring preserves the whisky’s natural aromas and original color. This keeps the flavor profile unadulterated and authentic, which is especially important for cask finishes like the Amontillado Sherry.

faq.question: What makes maturation in different sherry casks within the Wood Collection special?
faq.answer: The Wood Collection demonstrates how various types of sherry casks – Fino, Amontillado, and Oloroso – uniquely shape the whisky’s character. This variety of casks allows for discovering subtle differences in aroma, flavor, and texture through targeted finishing.

item.name: GlenAllachie 9 Years Fino Sherry Cask Finish - The Wood Collection
item.description: In the limited Wood Collection, GlenAllachie showcases the effect that different types of sherry can have on whisky maturation. They transferred their Single Malt, aged for 6.5 years in ex-bourbon casks, into casks previously filled with different sherries: Fino, Amontillado, and Oloroso. After a total maturation period of 9 years, all three variants were bottled at 48%, without coloring or chill filtration. On invitation from Kirsch Import and Billy Walker himself, we had the pleasure of comparing these three bottlings side by side, focusing on how cask management and selection influence the aroma.

image.alt: GlenAllachie 9 Years Fino Sherry Cask Finish - The Wood Collection

conclusion.marcel: This GlenAllachie 9 Year Fino Sherry Cask Finish brings a real summer hit to the glass with its lively freshness and sweet aromas – creamy, dry, and with a touch of humor in the flavor. If you’re looking for an uncomplicated yet characterful whisky that gladly invites you back for a second sip, this is absolutely the one.
conclusion.sascha: The GlenAllachie 9 Year Fino Sherry Cask Finish impresses with its balanced interplay of dryness, maltiness, and delicate fruit notes, precisely reflecting the cask management with Fino sherry. Despite its pleasant creaminess, the whisky remains structurally clear and well-proportioned, though it doesn’t surprise with great depth.

faq.question: How does the Fino Sherry Cask Finish influence the taste of the GlenAllachie 9 Year?
faq.answer: The Fino Sherry Cask Finish imparts a dry, fresh note to the GlenAllachie 9 Year, with aromas of baked apple, caramel, citrus fruits, and subtle oak tones. It creates a harmonious balance between creaminess and dryness, as well as a complex flavor profile.

faq.question: Why was the GlenAllachie 9 Year matured first in ex-bourbon and then in sherry casks?
faq.answer: The two-step maturation allows the whisky to first absorb vanilla and honey notes from the ex-bourbon casks before developing additional freshness, dryness, and fruity, nutty aromas in the Fino sherry cask. This cask management intensifies the whisky’s complexity and depth.

faq.question: What are the special features of this whisky regarding filtration and coloring?
faq.answer: The GlenAllachie 9 Year Fino Sherry Cask Finish is bottled at 48% ABV, without added coloring and without chill filtration. This preserves the natural aromas and full flavor intensity.

item.name: Agitator Chestnut Cask
item.description: The Agitator distillery from Sweden describes itself as a modern whisky producer. Their stated goal is to break with traditions and use new and unconventional methods to produce high-quality single malt. This includes vacuum distillation, which is supposed to be gentler due to the lower boiling point. Today, we get to try one of their core-range releases: full maturation in a chestnut cask.

tasting_notes.nose: The nose is pleasantly fruity from the start. We find dried forest berries paired with a light sugary caramel note. There’s also a subtle acidity, some grape, and a slight charred wood note.
tasting_notes.palate: On the palate, the malt is creamy soft, remarkably mild, and round. The fruit notes give way to a light vanilla. Now we find distinctly more bitter notes. There’s a bit of leather and a somewhat herbal aroma. The wood notes take on a clear burnt tone, creating a smoky experience (not peaty - this is a very unusual type of “smoke”).
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is rather short but extremely mild. Only a slightly tingling but pleasant spiciness remains.

conclusion.marcel: Agitator Chestnut Cask brings a breath of fresh air to the whisky world and proves that chestnut casks can offer exciting flavor experiences. Its creamy smoothness and unusual smoky note make for an engaging pleasure – you’ll want to take another sip again and again. A clear recommendation for anyone who values character and drinking enjoyment!
conclusion.sascha: The Agitator Chestnut Cask strikes a successful balance between fruity and smoky notes, with the chestnut cask maturation adding an interesting structural depth. Despite its mild, rounded profile, there seems to be untapped potential here for more complex cask variations. A promising single malt that well represents innovative maturation methods in the Swedish whisky scene.

faq.question: What makes the vacuum distillation in Agitator Chestnut Cask whisky special?
faq.answer: Vacuum distillation lowers the boiling point of the liquid, allowing for a gentler distillation process. This preserves more delicate aromas and prevents overheating, resulting in a milder and more complex single malt.

faq.question: How does maturation in chestnut casks influence the flavor of Agitator Chestnut Cask?
faq.answer: Maturation in chestnut casks imparts a unique combination of fruity wild berry aromas and a lightly charred wood note. On the palate, creamy vanilla tones emerge alongside more robust leather and herbal notes, as well as an unusual smoky aroma without peatiness.

faq.question: Why does Agitator Chestnut Cask have no age statement on the bottle?
faq.answer: Agitator Chestnut Cask is a no age statement (NAS) whisky because the distillery focuses on taste and quality of maturation rather than the length of aging. This approach allows greater flexibility in selecting casks and crafting the maturation profile.

item.name: MyBar South German Malt Whisky Tawny Port Finish
item.description: Malts from three German distilleries (Swabian Alp, Black Forest, Lake Constance) were married together. Some of these were previously matured in former Islay casks and the oldest of the three components was allowed to mature for over 12 years in bourbon casks. The malt was then finished in ex-Tawny port casks.

image.alt: MyBar South German Malt Whisky Tawny Port Finish

tasting_notes.nose: The first impression on the nose is mild and fruity, dominated by juicy cherries and an abundance of red fruit. A sweetish note resonates, which is perfectly balanced by a subtle spiciness. The aroma promises a multi-layered flavour experience and immediately arouses curiosity for the first sip.
tasting_notes.palate: A surprising versatility unfolds in the mouth. The texture is oily and pleasantly full-bodied. Right at the start, an unexpected smokiness surprises, which blends harmoniously with the lingering cherry sweetness. This combination makes for an extremely clean and clearly structured flavour experience that impresses with its depth and complexity.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is long-lasting and beautifully spicy. Notes of roasted coffee emerge discreetly, accompanied by a fine sweetness that gently caresses the palate. The spicy finish remains pleasantly present and rounds off the South German Malt Whisky.

conclusion.marcel: This MyBar South German Malt Whisky Tawny Port Finish is simply a joy – the smoky bursts collide with fruity cherries like a little fireworks display in the glass. For a whisky from German soil, it impresses with character and drinkability, all at a price that really makes it a must-buy. A genuine experience for anyone looking for something different!
conclusion.sascha: With its balanced interplay of smoke, fruit, and spicy complexity, the MyBar South German Malt Whisky Tawny Port Finish captivates through a clear structure and a long-lasting finish. The combination of Islay cask maturation and Tawny Port finishing creates a harmonious balance that remains very approachable despite the 51% ABV. Solid craftsmanship, though the depth in the finish could still be improved.

faq.question: Why is the MyBar South German Malt Whisky finished in Tawny Port casks?
faq.answer: The finish in former Tawny Port wine casks imparts additional fruity and sweet notes that enhance the cherry and red fruit aromas, giving the whisky a special complexity as well as a long-lasting, spicy finish.

faq.question: How does maturation in former Islay and Bourbon casks influence the whisky's flavor?
faq.answer: The Islay casks contribute a subtle smoky note to the aroma, while the portions matured for over 12 years in Bourbon casks provide a full-bodied texture along with vanilla and caramel notes. This combination results in a balanced and multi-layered tasting experience.

faq.question: What makes the marriage of malts from three different German distilleries special?
faq.answer: The blend of malts from the Swabian Alb, the Black Forest, and Lake Constance combines distinct regional characteristics and distilling traditions. This creates a unique whisky with a harmonious balance of fruitiness, spice, and smoke that represents the diversity of German malt whiskies.

item.name: St. Kilian Tag des Deutschen Whiskys (2023)
item.description: It's that time of year again. On the last Saturday in June, we celebrate the Day of the German Whisky. The St. Kilian Distillery is hosting its Whisky Festival for the second time. With great anticipation, we take this opportunity to closely examine the special edition from last year's festival. This time, it's the popular Hungarian oak from St. Kilian. The relatively small 95-liter casks impart a very rich color to this single malt, despite an aging period of just under five years.

image.alt: St. Kilian Tag des Deutschen Whiskys (2023)

tasting_notes.nose: The nose opens robustly, as expected. We find a mélange of intense dark fruits, dominated by prunes and overripe cherries. Light spicy oak notes accompany these aromas. After some time in the glass, subtler notes of vanilla, caramel, and a hint of clove emerge.
tasting_notes.palate: The taste is richly sweet and extremely fruity. A wild mix of pureed summer berries goes hand in hand with raisins and cherry jam once again. The mouthfeel is oily, very pleasant, and the cask strength integrates the flavors wonderfully. On the palate, the oak becomes more pronounced. The woodiness comes to the forefront and is accompanied by nutmeg and cinnamon.
tasting_notes.finish: The malt's finish is medium-long. The journey continues consistently here. Now, oak tones and spices dominate. The sweetness of the dried fruits is still noticeable but takes a back seat.

conclusion.marcel: This St. Kilian impressively demonstrates just how much fun Hungarian oak can bring in a relatively short maturation period – fruity, sweet, and with a pinch of spice that invites you to take another sip. A whisky bursting with character and perfectly suited to the German Whisky Festival, even though it’s unfortunately already sold out.
conclusion.sascha: With its balanced harmony of intense fruitiness, spicy oak, and a rich, oily mouthfeel, this St. Kilian convinces despite the short aging time. The cask strength and interplay of vanilla, nutmeg, and cinnamon create a structured complexity that clearly places this German single malt on par with established standards.

faq.question: How does the 95-liter Hungarian oak cask influence the flavor of this St. Kilian whisky?
faq.answer: The relatively small 95-liter Hungarian oak casks impart an intense wood character and a deep color despite the comparatively short maturation of just under five years. This brings forward spicy oak notes, nutmeg, and cinnamon more prominently, complementing the fruity character.

faq.question: Why is the alcohol content of 53.3% at the 'Day of German Whisky' higher than that of many other single malts?
faq.answer: At 53.3% ABV, this whisky is bottled at cask strength, meaning it has not been diluted with water. This preserves the full intensity of the aromas and provides an oily mouthfeel that particularly enhances the expression of fruitiness and spice.

faq.question: What makes St. Kilian’s 'Day of German Whisky' release special compared to other German whiskies?
faq.answer: This special release was created specifically for the whisky festival celebrating the Day of German Whisky and matured in small Hungarian oak casks, which is unusual for German whiskies. The combination of intense fruitiness, spicy oak, and cask strength distinctly sets it apart in terms of flavor.

item.name: Glentauchers 11 Years (2012/2024) - Signatory Vintage
item.description: The Glentauchers distillery in Speyside is one of the lesser-known but not exactly small distilleries. This is primarily because there is no visitor center, and most of the production is used in blended whiskies. Besides its own core range bottling, there are occasionally independently bottled Glentauchers malts. Today, we sampled the 11-years from the 100 Proof series by Signatory Vintage. The latter is very popular with us, and we were looking forward to this dram.

tasting_notes.nose: The nose is immediately malty and caramel-sweet. We find vanilla and fresh shortbread before the scent unfolds into fruity notes. Yellow raisins, apricot, mango, and a hint of dark forest berries. Surrounded by perfumed floral notes, we think of forest honey and get a very lively impression of the malt.
tasting_notes.palate: On the palate, this Glentauchers hits with full force. Oily, almost syrupy, we get a load of fruity intensity. Cherries and grainy sweetness are accompanied by toffee. Here too, there is a quick transformation, and the spicy-nutty aromas dominate. We find cloves, a good dose of oak spice, and a small cup of café au lait.
tasting_notes.finish: he finish is long-lasting, warming, and full-bodied. We have a combination of baking cocoa and oak wood, accompanied by ripe mango and some herbal notes, where we most associate with licorice.

conclusion.marcel: A lively Speyside dram that delights with its fruity sweetness and full body – perfect for anyone who enjoys intense tasting experiences. The combination of cherries, Toffifee, and a hint of café au lait makes it a real mood booster. Signatory Vintage once again proves that quality doesn’t have to come with a high price tag.
conclusion.sascha: This 11-years Glentauchers displays a clear, structured balance between fruity sweetness and spicy oak notes, well integrated at 57.1% ABV. The complex aroma profile, ranging from yellow raisins to baking cocoa, is harmonized by a syrupy texture, yet remains precise and well defined throughout. A technically accomplished example of a well-balanced sherry cask maturation from Speyside.

faq.question: Why is Glentauchers so little known despite large production volumes?
