faq.answer: Ardbeg An Oa is the first expansion of the standard range in a long time and stands out through a complex combination of smoke, fruitiness, and spicy notes, achieved by maturation in various types of casks.

faq.question: Which types of casks were used to mature Ardbeg An Oa and how do they influence the flavor?
faq.answer: Ardbeg An Oa was matured in a mix of ex-bourbon, sherry, and virgin oak casks, resulting in a balanced harmony of sweet, fruity, and smoky aromas.

item.name: Ardmore Port Wood Finish 12 Years
item.description: Older Whiskys from the Ardmore distillery are rarely found. Among the younger standards is the Ardmore Legacy, as well as the Ardmore Port Wood Finish 12 years, reviewed here.A special characteristic of the distillery from the Scottish Highlands is that, unlike its neighbours, it uses peated barley and thus a fruity-smoky note can be found in the spirits.

tasting_notes.nose: An old solid wood cupboard in a room with a recently extinguished fireplace is the first image we have in our mind after having the aroma of the scotch in our nose for the first time. Slightly maritime-mineral aromas of a fruit cake follow. Every now and then we find red berries.
tasting_notes.palate: The Ardmore is dry and very sweet in the mouth. Red berries, fig and pine needles follow. A light peat smoke lies over everything.
tasting_notes.finish: Soft, relatively short and slightly smoky, but with flavours of oak and potting soil, the Port Wood Finish concludes.

conclusion.marcel: The Ardmore Port Wood Finish 12 Years is a pleasant companion for cozy evenings, scoring points with its sweet fruitiness and subtle smoky character. However, for fans of intense port wine notes, it remains more of a gentle attempt without that big wow factor.
conclusion.sascha: From a structural perspective, the Ardmore Port Wood Finish shows a solid balance between sweet fruit aromas and light smokiness, yet it falls short in the complexity and intensity of the port wine influences. Overall, a well-crafted Highland whisky, but one that doesn’t set a new benchmark.

faq.question: What makes the Ardmore Port Wood Finish 12 Years special compared to other Highland whiskies?
faq.answer: Ardmore uses peated barley, which is rather uncommon in the Highlands. This gives the Ardmore Port Wood Finish a unique combination of fruity notes and a subtle peat smoke that sets it apart from many other whiskies in the region.

faq.question: How does the Port Wood Finish influence the taste of the Ardmore 12 Years?
faq.answer: The finishing in port wine casks imparts sweet fruity notes like red berries and figs, complementing the smoky character of the peated barley and creating a complex, harmonious depth of flavor.

faq.question: Which aromas are particularly noticeable in the Ardmore Port Wood Finish 12 Years?
faq.answer: The nose is dominated by fruity notes such as red berries and fruitcake, complemented by a maritime minerality and light peat smoke. On the palate, pine needles and sweet elements join in, while the finish is soft, fading out with bitter oak wood and floral earth aromas.

item.name: Lochlea Our Barley
item.description: In August 2018, almost exactly four years ago, the independent family-owned distillery started operations. After the first limited bottling at the beginning of 2022, and now the first standard bottling follows. We were provided with a sample by the distillery before the release and are now happy to add our two cents.The farm distillery works under the motto dare to be honest. It puts into practice what many whisky lovers want: Honesty and transparency. From the local cultivation of the barley to the bottling - the whole production process is traceable. Master blender John Campbell, previously distillery manager of Laphroaig for 25 years, seized the opportunity to create a Single Malt Whisky in a new distillery, which is to set new standards in terms of quality. By the way: The packaging is chic and classy, as is the bottle.

tasting_notes.nose: Is this a honey wine? We have a massively sweet, fruity sweetness. Ripe pears, syrup and cream caramel. Very mild, spiciness is almost not noticeable at all. Maybe a little in the background.
tasting_notes.palate: Sweet and spicy and with a good portion of grain and honey, the Lochlea Our Barley makes its way into the mouth. Burnt toast and an alcoholic spiciness follow. There's a little vanilla and some cinnamon in the background. A quite interesting breakfast.
tasting_notes.finish: The spiciness settles on the palate. Dry and medium in length, a young, yet well-balanced single malt says goodbye with a little sweetness reminiscent of sunflower honey.

conclusion.marcel: Lochlea Our Barley surprises with its sweet honey note and its fruity-mild character, which offers great drinking pleasure despite its youth. You can taste the passion and courage of John Campbell, who here makes a charming entry into the whisky world – definitely a successful first release with potential for more.
conclusion.sascha: The Lochlea Our Barley presents itself as structurally balanced, with the youthful freshness and the local origin of the raw materials standing out positively. Despite the lack of maturity, the single malt shows a solid balance between sweet and spicy components, with complexity likely to grow as it ages.

faq.question: What makes the production process of Lochlea Our Barley particularly transparent?
faq.answer: Lochlea Our Barley follows the motto “dare to be honest” and documents the entire production process from the local barley cultivation to bottling. This allows whisky enthusiasts to trace every step.

faq.question: How does John Campbell’s background influence the character of this whisky?
faq.answer: John Campbell, formerly Distillery Manager at Laphroaig for 25 years, brings his experience to the new distillery. His goal is to create a single malt with Lochlea Our Barley that sets high quality standards while developing its own style.

faq.question: Which flavor notes dominate Lochlea Our Barley and how is the finish?
faq.answer: The whisky shows a pronounced sweet and fruity nose with notes of pears, syrup, and creamy caramel. On the palate, sweet-spicy aromas of grain, honey, burnt toast, as well as vanilla and cinnamon come together. The finish is dry, medium-length, and reminiscent of sunflower honey.

item.name: St. Kilian Bud Spencer - The Legend - Rauchig - Batch 02
item.description: The German distillery St. Klilian has chosen Bud Spencer and Terrence Hill as characters for its marketing. Both peated and non-peated Whiskys as well as liqueurs appear as part of the series.The label, adorned with Bud Spencer's world-famous fist, promises a quick-witted and humorous experience. Those who have seen the movies of Bud Spencer & Terrence Hill know what we are talking about. Nevertheless, we are actually reviewing a Single Malt Whisky from Germany.

image.alt: St. Kilian Bud Spencer - The Legend - Rauchig - Batch 02

tasting_notes.nose: The scenery: A campfire on a fresh, lush and dewy meadow actually describes the nose quite well. A hint of citrus aromas and occasionally a bit of vanilla can still be found in the background.
tasting_notes.palate: The punch: Slightly peppery-spicy, but without a salty maritime quality, Bud Spencer opens with the first punch. The second fist follows sweetly with red berries, caramel sweetness, and typical red wine flavours.
tasting_notes.finish: The end: The knockout punch is dry, spicy and yet fruity at the same time, landing the opponents next to the campfire again.

conclusion.marcel: A smoky single malt that remains pleasantly rounded and easy-drinking despite its NAS status – the combination of smoke and red wine cask simply makes for a fun experience. Does the connection to Bud Spencer really stand out? More of a nice gimmick, but it doesn’t detract from the whisky itself. For fans looking for uncomplicated drinking enjoyment with a wink, this is a well-rounded choice.
conclusion.sascha: Structurally, the St. Kilian Bud Spencer Batch 02 shows a solid balance between smoky notes and the fruity complexity of the red wine cask, even though the nose overall feels somewhat one-dimensional. The finish impresses with dry spice and a fruity aftertaste, but falls a bit short in depth. Overall, a well-balanced German single malt that comes across surprisingly harmonious for a NAS.

faq.question: What makes the St. Kilian Bud Spencer - The Legend - Smoky - Batch 02 special in terms of taste?
faq.answer: The whisky combines smoky notes with a complex aroma profile of campfire, citrus fruits, and vanilla on the nose. On the palate, it delivers a peppery-spicy character followed by sweet red berries, caramel, and red wine flavors, before finishing dry, spicy, and fruity.

faq.question: Why does this whisky have no age statement?
faq.answer: The St. Kilian Bud Spencer - The Legend - Smoky - Batch 02 is offered without an age statement, which is common for many modern single malts. Instead of focusing on a specific age, the distillery emphasizes the quality and flavor profile of the casks and components used.

faq.question: How does the Bud Spencer whisky differ from other German single malts?
faq.answer: Besides its distinctive smoky note, this whisky stands out through its connection to the cult films of Bud Spencer & Terence Hill. In terms of flavor, it combines German craftsmanship with an unusual mix of campfire aromas, fruity and spicy nuances, making it unique in the German whisky landscape.

item.name: Secret Speyside 13 Years - 82 Chapters to Newcastle
item.description: Last month, the madmen from 82 Chapters to Newcastle released a new bottling. This time a single cask from a secret distillery in Scotland's Speyside. It's our first bottle by this independent bottler from Germany. We are beyond excited.A total of 259 bottles are the result of the first fill bourbon cask with which something naughty must have happened. Because otherwise, how would they have gotten these flavours into the Whisky? Bottled at cask strength, the Single Malt Whisky with the unicorn can also be enjoyed slightly diluted with water.

image.alt: Secret Speyside 13 Years - 82 Chapters to Newcastle

tasting_notes.nose: Vanilla and lemon balm start off. Quickly followed by feta cheese with Mediterranean herbs (WTF?!) and almonds. Wild forest honey and light white wine notes top off this incredibly pleasant and deliciously scented Speysider. Water: The sweetish aromas become more pronounced. More vanilla, creamy, slightly grassy.
tasting_notes.palate: Now that is a strong character. The mouth fills with caramel syrup, butter biscuits and ladyfingers. An interesting turn to fir wood and coniferous forest follows. There are red berries and dark dried fruits in the background. Somewhere on the forest floor lies a tiny orange hidden and apparently green tea has also been spilled (WTF again?!). Water: More pleasant to drink. The flavours spread out and are more recognisable.
tasting_notes.finish: In the finish, strong red dark fruits and light hay notes remain in between. Dry - and long. Water: Not delicate, as one would expect, but still very powerful, intense, and layered.

conclusion.marcel: Cask strength is very beneficial to the Whisky. The 13-years is incredibly powerful, very well balanced and has some really interesting flavours that we would not have expected from a Speysider matured in a bourbon cask. It's perfect for the time of year when the spiders start crawling out of the corners and you sit on the garden bench snuggled up in a woollen blanket.
conclusion.sascha: Cask strength is very beneficial to the Whisky. The 13-years is incredibly powerful, very well balanced and has some really interesting flavours that we would not have expected from a Speysider matured in a bourbon cask. It's perfect for the time of year when the spiders start crawling out of the corners and you sit on the garden bench snuggled up in a woollen blanket.

item.name: Starward Left-Field
item.description: The Single Malt Whisky Left-Field by Starward from Australia matures exclusively in former Australian red wine casks made of French oak. We are pretty happy to say that we found our Whisky of the Summer 2022.The Left-Field belongs to the core-range of Starward and is thus always available. You often have to put down quite a sum of money for Single Malt Whiskys from countries other than Scotland. This bottling is affordable even for the smaller purse. Starward themself praise the Whisky as an easily accessible all-rounder.

tasting_notes.nose: A strong and very intense smell of honey with orange pieces, apricot jam and vanilla literally shoots up our noses. Sweet grapes and juicy apple follow shortly after. A finch of nutmeg and mild pepper are perceptible. It feels like we are sitting in the summer in the south with a sea view in front of a fruit plate.
tasting_notes.palate: A very wine-like (I wonder. why...) taste presents itself. Apricot and tropical fruits dance with vanilla, malt and oak spice. One could almost say: Wine vs. Bourbon. Only the solvent-like bourbon aromas are missing. On the whole the whisky presents mild, sweet, and slightly oily. A few more %ABV might have made this flavour-profile of the NAS Whisky a little more interesting.
tasting_notes.finish: Smooth, nutty, peppery an slightly woody, the wine - eh Whisky - disappears after some time. The tropical fruits linger on.

conclusion.marcel: A true summer buddy with a fruity bouquet that’s fun and sure to delight whisky newcomers. If you’re looking for a light, sweet dram that’s not too complicated, here you’ll find a charming companion for garden parties – more charm than depth, but just right for relaxed evenings.
conclusion.sascha: The Starward Left-Field impresses with its balanced interplay of fruity notes and subtle oak spice, yet it somewhat falls short of its full potential. At 40% ABV, a slightly stronger presence would be desirable to more clearly define the balance between sweetness and spice.

faq.question: Why does Starward Left-Field mature exclusively in Australian red wine barrels made from French oak?
faq.answer: Maturing in former Australian red wine barrels made from French oak gives the Left-Field its distinctive fruitiness and wine-like character, setting it apart from classic single malts. These barrels impart aromas of red fruits and vanilla to the profile.

faq.question: How does Starward Left-Field differ from traditional Scottish single malts?
faq.answer: Starward Left-Field is Australian and matures in red wine barrels, which gives it a fruitier and more wine-like flavor profile. Additionally, at 40% ABV, it is somewhat lighter and more approachable compared to many Scottish single malts, which are often stronger and peatier.

faq.question: Is Starward Left-Field suitable for whisky beginners?
faq.answer: Yes, the Left-Field is well suited for beginners due to its mild, sweet, and fruity aromas. Its light oiliness and moderate 40% alcohol content make it an accessible whisky that impresses even without extended aging.

item.name: Mackmyra Intelligens AI:02
item.description: Mackmyra Intelligens AI:02, the successor to Mackmyra Intelligens AI:01, promises an improved recipe and an adjustment of the casks in which this Swedish single malt Whisky matures. To refine the flavour depth, small ex-Oloroso Sherry casks and a little peat smoke was added.We still like the marketing very much. On the website, there are release notes for the Whisky, which is actually more usual for software. For example, the change in colour compared to the predecessor is indicated in hex code. A touch nerdy, but very fitting. The design of the label has also been updated slightly.

tasting_notes.nose: We smell vanilla, honey, pear, apple, caramel. Very summery-sweet. Deeper Lemon balm sweets and a slight hint of spices and white pepper. We were not able to perceive the background smoketail mentioned in the release notes.
tasting_notes.palate: The Swedish Single Malt Whisky opens with vanilla caramel as well as notes of citrus fruits and a strong orange aroma. Woven into a light smoky shell, the initially sweet start turns into unexpectedly dry and bitter oak notes. The mouthfeel remains very soft, almost creamy.
tasting_notes.finish: The subtle smoke is found in the finish paired with slightly bitter oak notes.

conclusion.marcel: Mackmyra Intelligens AI:02 delivers noticeably more drinking pleasure than its predecessor – the smoky cloak adds character, while unfortunately the sweet notes are abruptly replaced by dry oak tones. Still, this Swedish single malt remains a likeable companion for relaxed moments thanks to its creamy mouthfeel. I'm curious to see how the story continues with the AI:03!
conclusion.sascha: The AI:02 version of Mackmyra impresses with an improved balance between sweet vanilla and citrus notes and a subtle smoky structure, although the sudden shift to bitter oak tones slightly disrupts the harmony. The inclusion of small ex-Oloroso casks adds depth, yet the finish feels somewhat unbalanced as a result. A technically interesting experiment that still leaves room for improvement.

faq.question: What does the addition "AI:02" in the name Mackmyra Intelligens AI:02 mean?
faq.answer: "AI:02" denotes the second version of Mackmyra's Intelligens series, where data analysis and artificial intelligence were used to optimize the recipe and cask selection in order to enhance flavor depth and complexity.

faq.question: How does the use of small ex-Oloroso casks influence the taste of Mackmyra Intelligens AI:02?
faq.answer: The small ex-Oloroso casks impart additional aromas of dried fruits and nuttiness, as well as a more intense oak character, which together with the subtle peat smoke contribute to greater depth and complexity in the flavor.

faq.question: Why is there no age statement on Mackmyra Intelligens AI:02?
faq.answer: Mackmyra Intelligens AI:02 is a so-called No-Age-Statement (NAS) whisky, meaning the exact age of the whiskies used is not disclosed. This allows the producer to blend different vintages to achieve a consistent flavor profile.

item.name: Redbreast 15 Years
item.description: The Redbreast series consists of so-called single pot still whiskey. This means that both malted and unmalted barley are used in the mash. Unique in Ireland, this process gives the whiskey its rich character.Fun Fact: If you sort the Redbreast bottles by age in ascending order, starting with the 12-years, the Readbreast looks at you first. On the label of the 15-years, the Readbreast takes off and is finally in flight on the 21-years.

tasting_notes.nose: At first, you might think it's a bourbon. We have the typical smell of solvent in our nose. A short time later, the full force of the aromas is released. Starting with caramel toffee and honey, we travel on through an orchard of apple and pear. Sweet and sour, we perceive red wine and sherry notes for the crowning finish.
tasting_notes.palate: Like the nose, the taste feels like a journey, where there is always something new to discover. Toffee and vanilla give the start. Further on, we discover a light, very pleasant bitterness, as well as dark fruits and a little oak at the very end. A velvety and caramel layer hovers over everything. It is remarkable that the flavours are very delicate, i.e. not penetrating and cleanly coordinated.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is dry and spicy with baking cocoa and toasted oak.

conclusion.marcel: The Redbreast 15 is an emotional journey through sweet and spicy aromas that leaves you wanting more – a true characterful dram with depth, telling new stories with every glass. Perfect for relaxing and enjoying life with a smile in your glass.
conclusion.sascha: A well-structured single pot still whiskey that impresses with its balanced harmony of fruity, sweet, and spicy notes. The delicate layering of aromas and the dry, spicy finish make it a sophisticated choice for connoisseurs who appreciate complexity and harmony.

faq.question: What does Single Pot Still Whiskey mean for Redbreast 15 Years?
faq.answer: Single Pot Still Whiskey is an Irish specialty where both malted and unmalted barley are used in the mash. This process gives Redbreast 15 Years its characteristically rich and complex flavor.

faq.question: How does the Redbreast 15 Years differ in taste from younger expressions?
faq.answer: The 15-years Redbreast displays a deeper range of flavors with notes of caramel, toffee, honey, and a hint of red wine and sherry, whereas younger versions tend to be fresher and less complex. The aging imparts a velvety texture and a balanced bitterness in the finish.

faq.question: Why does the Redbreast 15 Years initially smell like solvent?
faq.answer: The initial solvent-like aroma is typical for single pot still whiskeys and arises from the complex composition of the distillates. After a short time, this scent gives way to intense aromas like caramel, honey, and fruit that define the whiskey’s character.

item.name: Johnnie Walker Black Label 12 Years Sherry Finish
item.description: The Johnnie Walker Black Label 12 Years Sherry Finish gives the classic whisky a - as the name suggests - sherry finish, i.e. a post-maturing in Jerez sherry casks. This is to put the proverbial icing on the cake of the iconic whisky.With a slightly adapted design with distinct accents in pink, Johnnie Walker has remained true to the familiar style, as with all bottlings.

image.alt: Johnnie Walker Black Label 12 Years Sherry Finish

tasting_notes.nose: The profile is logically similar to that of the Johnnie Walker Black Label 12 Years. In addition, we have dominant notes of plum and red berries. In the background, a little thin smokiness, as well as the familiar honey-caramel flavours.
tasting_notes.palate: Soft and easy to drink with delicate smoke, the Johnnie Walker nevertheless comes across as thin and flat. The fruity sweetness at the front gradually changes to a marzipan-amaretto note. A little fig, a little vanilla. Nothing more.
tasting_notes.finish: Quite short and with light oak notes, the blend says goodbye to the palate.

conclusion.marcel: A light, fruity blend that’s pleasant to sip thanks to its sherry finish, but unfortunately leaves no lasting impression. For the price, you can find whiskies with more character elsewhere – yet it works well as an uncomplicated companion in cocktails.
conclusion.sascha: The Johnnie Walker Black Label 12 Years Sherry Finish shows a solid balance between fruity notes and gentle smokiness, but overall feels a bit thin and lacking in complexity. The short finish and moderate sherry influence provide decent structure, yet fall short of what you’d expect from a 12-years blend.

faq.question: How does the sherry finish influence the taste of the Johnnie Walker Black Label 12 Year?
faq.answer: The sherry finish adds extra aromas of plum and red berries, along with a fruity sweetness that develops into marzipan and amaretto notes. This gives the whisky a softer and more complex flavor profile compared to the standard bottling.

faq.question: Why is the finish of the Johnnie Walker Black Label 12 Year Sherry Finish comparatively short?
faq.answer: The finish is rather short and characterized by light oak notes. This is because the blend, despite the sherry finish, is kept soft and approachable without an intense or long-lasting smoky character.

faq.question: What role do the Jerez sherry casks play in the maturation process of this whisky?
faq.answer: The additional maturation in Jerez sherry casks imparts the characteristic fruity and sweet aromas like plum, red berries, and marzipan. These casks add extra complexity and a distinctive flavor dimension compared to the classic 12-years expression.

item.name: Ardbeg Ardcore Committee Release
item.description: Inspired by the punk past from the 1970s, Ardbeg releases Ardcore as this year's committee release. By the way, it is said to taste "like biting a spiky ball".The releases are very polarising. Very interesting for collectors and sold out just as quickly, those who want to taste the latest Committee release often don't get their money's worth.

tasting_notes.nose: At first, the smell reminds us more of a Laphroaig than an Ardbeg. We identify phenolic smoke that fades into a steam note. It reminds us of neatly burnt toast. Fighting through the smoke, an Ardbeg-typical vanilla-citrus note emerges. This is accompanied by roasted malt and ham.
tasting_notes.palate: The typical Ardbeg anise with a certain seawater saltiness hovers over a not-so-intense campfire ember flavour. Gradually, vanilla and chocolate fight their way to the fore. We would have expected more chocolateiness from the dark roasted barley malt. Roasted aromas are present, but rather subtle. Overall, not much, we have to say, unfortunately.
tasting_notes.finish: The spicy-dry finish is medium long, but that's not what we've come to expect from Ardbeg. Not very impressive.

conclusion.marcel: An Ardbeg that, with its punk attitude, stays rather tame – solid, but without the expected kick. Certainly interesting for collectors, but for drinkers seeking fire and drama, it’s more of a gentle breeze.
conclusion.sascha: A well-balanced Islay whisky with a clear structure, yet it lacks the complexity and intensity one expects from an Ardbeg. The price-performance ratio and flavor variety could be better.

faq.question: How does the Ardbeg Ardcore Committee Release differ in taste from other Ardbeg bottlings?
faq.answer: Compared to typical Ardbeg whiskies, the Ardcore shows a stronger smoky note reminiscent of Laphroaig, with phenolic smoke and burnt toast. The vanilla-citrus notes are present, but the campfire aroma and roasted flavors come across as overall more subtle and less intense than in other Ardbeg releases.

faq.question: Why is the Ardbeg Ardcore Committee Release particularly interesting for collectors?
faq.answer: The Ardcore is part of Ardbeg’s limited Committee Releases, which appear annually and often sell out quickly. Its strong polarization and connection to Islay’s punk past make it a sought-after collector’s item that isn’t always easy to find.

faq.question: What aromas can be discovered in the Ardbeg Ardcore besides the smoke?
faq.answer: Alongside the characteristic phenolic smoke and burnt toast, vanilla and citrus notes come forward, accompanied by roasted malt and a salty sea breeze. On the palate, subtle hints of chocolate and anise complement the tasting experience.

item.name: Ardbeg Corryvreckan
item.description: As a permanently available Single Malt Whisky, it was considered the most intense Scotch of the Ardbeg distillery for a long time. With a quasi-barrel strength of 57.1 ABV, a pure and a slightly water-stretched tasting is compulsory.Ardbeg Corryvreckan is named after the Corryvreckan whirlpool. This legendary whirlpool forms in the strait between the Jura and the island of Scarba - so at first glance it has nothing directly to do with the island of Islay, the home of the Ardbeg distillery.

tasting_notes.nose: Very powerful, we have a fir tree-like, spicy savoury scent in our nose at the first sniff. Discreet and not very intrusive, the typical Ardbeg smoke is in the background. After a while in the glass, the perceived aromas change to butter heated in the microwave, a dark, berry fruitiness and some forest honey. With a little water: the sweetish aromas take over. Clearly more vanilla and honey, but also more charcoal and smoked ham. Some sauerkraut in the background.
tasting_notes.palate: A fruity oil-grease-butter-flavour with sea salt and black pepper lies broadly on the tongue. Butter caramel, ash and campfire float above. At the top are citrus aromas, maybe some bergamot. With a little water: Clearly oilier and with more citrus, but still very sweet, we taste red gummy bears, blueberries and other dark fruits. In the background, clear roasted aromas and a little nut.
tasting_notes.finish: Deep and powerful, the exceedingly intense Ardbeg remains salty on the palate with a slight peppery spiciness. A certain tarry note resonates. With a little water: More smoke and ash.

conclusion.marcel: For lovers of intense flavors, the Ardbeg Corryvreckan is a true adventure, delighting with its sweet sherry notes and the characteristic Ardbeg smoke. It is powerful, smoky, and at the same time surprisingly complex – a dram that’s fun and memorable.
conclusion.sascha: The Ardbeg Corryvreckan impresses with a complex balance of intense sherry aromas, distinctive smoke, and a precise structure on the palate. The combination of fruitiness, spices, and salty seasoning is technically impressive, yet its sheer intensity means it’s not accessible to everyone.

faq.question: Why is the Ardbeg Corryvreckan perceived as particularly intense?
faq.answer: With an alcohol content of 57.1% ABV, the Ardbeg Corryvreckan has a very high strength, which makes its aromas especially powerful and concentrated. Additionally, the combination of smoke, spices, fruitiness, and salty notes creates a complex tasting experience.

faq.question: How does the taste of Corryvreckan change when diluted with water?
faq.answer: Adding a little water brings out sweeter aromas like vanilla, honey, and fruit notes such as blueberries more prominently. The texture becomes oilier, and the smoky notes along with charcoal and ash become more intense and noticeable.

faq.question: Why is the Ardbeg Corryvreckan named after a sea whirlpool, even though the distillery is located on Islay?
faq.answer: The name Corryvreckan refers to the legendary whirlpool of the same name between the islands of Jura and Scarba, near Islay. It symbolizes the whisky’s powerful and wild nature, even though the whirlpool itself is not directly on Islay.

item.name: Ardbeg Wee Beastie 5 Years
item.description: After the No Age Statement (NAS) Whiskys (legitimately) earned some criticism in recent years, the Islay distillery Ardbeg dared to take the bold step of releasing a young single malt with an age statement and including it in the standard bottlings.The Ardbeg Wee Beastie is intended as a young, wild and untamed bottling to expand the standard range. After a maturation period of 5 years, it is to be expected that some flavours will not be quite round yet. Incidentally, the Wee replaces the Ardbeg Corryvreckan as Ardbeg's most peated Scotch.

tasting_notes.nose: We smell vinegary acidity, black pepper and strong sweet tobacco smoke. The combination somehow reminds us of the rubber of bicycle tyres that have just ridden through barbecue sauce. A little salty liquorice and vanilla bean shine through.
tasting_notes.palate: Darkly burnt smoked bacon with a little sugar first hit the tongue. In the middle are tar, leather and intense peat smoke. As in the nose, saltiness and a nuance of vanilla also resonate here.
tasting_notes.finish: A dry, peaty something lingers forever in the aftertaste.

conclusion.marcel: The Wee Beastie is no gentle introduction to the world of Ardbeg – it comes across wild, raw, and with a sharp distillery character that won’t immediately win everyone over. Those who enjoy intense, unrefined Islay power and are happy to engage in a smoky dance with tarry notes will definitely have a blast here. However, it’s definitely not suited for cozy evenings or whisky beginners.
conclusion.sascha: At just 5 years old, the Ardbeg Wee Beastie presents an unusual, still unfinished structure dominated by peat and smoke notes. The absence of the usual Oloroso sherry cask finish results in a less harmonious overall profile, deliberately smokier and thus somewhat unbalanced. For lovers of complex, powerful Islay whiskies, it still offers interesting facets but demands a mindful tasting.

faq.question: How does the Ardbeg Wee Beastie 5 Years differ from older Ardbeg releases?
faq.answer: The Wee Beastie is significantly younger at 5 years compared to many other Ardbeg whiskies. As a result, it shows a fresher, somewhat more unruly aromatic profile with intense smokiness and a pronounced peaty note that often appears softer and rounder in older releases.

faq.question: Why did Ardbeg introduce the Wee Beastie with an age statement, despite many NAS whiskies being criticized?
faq.answer: Ardbeg wanted to bring a young single malt with an age statement to market to expand their core range with a particularly smoky and lively expression. The 5-year age statement signals transparency and sets it apart from the mostly NAS whiskies offered without an age declaration.

faq.question: Which flavor notes make the Ardbeg Wee Beastie particularly distinctive?
faq.answer: Typical for the Wee Beastie are aromas of vinegar-like acidity, black pepper, sweet tobacco smoke, and salty licorice. On the palate, darkly burnt smoked bacon, tar, leather, and intense peat smoke dominate, accompanied by a subtle vanilla note and a long-lasting dry, peaty bitterness on the finish.

item.name: Annandale (2017/2020) ManOSword Founders Selection Cask 348
item.description: The casks in which the New Make was later to mature took an exciting route. If Annandale's single malt whisky tastes anywhere near as complex as the Shaved Toasting and Recharring (STR) cask-burning process was, then we have a unique single cask bottling here.Originally, the barrels were filled with French Burgundy for up to seven years. The staves of the barrels were planed and toasted over fire with oak chips. This process is supposed to caramelise sugar and vanillin - exposed by the planing process. Finally, the casks were burnt out again and transported from Spain to Scotland. During the transport, the barrels were filled with Oloroso sherry. This ensured that the barrels remained hydrated.

tasting_notes.nose: With a neat cold, medicinal and iodine-like smoke, the single cask whisky ManOSword by Annandale greets us in the nose. Shortly after the olfactory receptors get used to the smoke, coconut and almond aromas follow, layered with bark mulch and a fresh potting soil. This reminds us a little of the coconut-soil potting tablets you can get in any DIY store.
tasting_notes.palate: The more than 60% vol. highlight the flavours very clearly and manage to create a full and at the same time dusty mouthfeel. A true peat explosion. We clearly perceive some pineapple next to the peat-smoke. A little later, vanilla and peach follow with a slight saltiness and subliminal but not unpleasant spiciness.
tasting_notes.finish: What remains is strong peat-smoke, a peppery spiciness and a slight tingle.

conclusion.marcel: This Annandale ManOSword is a true character, delivering intense smokiness and surprising fruity notes that make for a thoroughly enjoyable experience on the palate – perfect for a relaxed evening with friends and engaging conversations about the cask and its story.
conclusion.sascha: The complex cask treatment and high alcohol content give the Annandale 2017/2020 ManOSword an impressive balance of smoke, sweetness, and spice, maintaining a structured and nuanced profile – a technically sophisticated single cask Lowland whisky with a clearly defined character.

faq.question: What is special about the maturation process of the Annandale 2017/2020 ManOSword?
faq.answer: The casks were originally seasoned with French Burgundy, then planed, toasted with oak chips, and charred (STR process). During transport to Scotland, they were hydrated with Oloroso sherry, which promotes a complex aromatic profile in the whisky.

faq.question: How does the high alcohol content of 60.6% affect the taste?
faq.answer: The high alcohol content intensifies the aromas and provides a full, dusty mouthfeel. It enhances the peat notes, fruity sweetness, and peppery spiciness in the finish.

faq.question: Why is there no age statement on this Annandale single malt?
faq.answer: The whisky is a new make spirit matured directly in the special STR cask without traditional aging. The complexity arises from the cask process rather than extended maturation.

item.name: Bunnahabhain Stiuireadair
item.description: Bunnahabhain Stiuireadair was created as a tribute to the sea and sailors, because without the sea and without a ship it is almost impossible to get from the island to the mainland. So we expect something very maritime.As the first NAS (No Age Statement) Whisky from Bunnahabhain, the Stiuireadair was chosen as the entry point. Matured in first- and second-fill sherry casks, we would not be surprised if there was a strong resemblance to the Bunnahabhain 12 Years.

tasting_notes.nose: Sweet, really sweet, really really sweet. We think of prunes and buttered speculoos. Behind the dried fruit hides an interesting variety of aromas. Once the nose has become accustomed to the dark dried fruit, vanilla and caramel notes resonate and later the salty-maritime character also appears.
tasting_notes.palate: "There greets the sea: as if you gargled a good sip of seawater." Sweet on the lips, salty on the palate we again have dried fruit and strong nutty aromas. Compared to the nose, the ratio is exactly reversed. The salty-maritime clearly predominates and the sherry flavors remain in the background. There is also a hint of nutty chocolate and dried apricot.
tasting_notes.finish: The long finish is very dry. A slight fruitiness remains and is again accompanied by a salty feeling.

conclusion.marcel: This Bunnahabhain Stiuireadair is a genuine little adventure for the senses – sweet, salty, with a good dose of character that’s a real joy. For anyone looking for a maritime, youthful whisky with a charming sherry note, this is a bullseye at a fair price. A dram that leaves you wanting more and truly captures the soul of the island.
conclusion.sascha: The Stiuireadair impresses with its well-balanced interplay between sweet sherry notes and a clear, salty maritime profile, offering surprisingly good structure for a NAS whisky. The maturation in first- and second-fill sherry casks is evident in its complex aroma, even if it lacks some of the depth found in older vintages. Overall, a technically solid composition with excellent value for money.

faq.question: What does NAS mean in Bunnahabhain Stiuireadair and how does it affect the whisky?
faq.answer: NAS stands for 'No Age Statement', meaning the whisky does not specify an age. In the case of Stiuireadair, this allows greater flexibility in cask selection without being tied to a specific age. As a result, the flavor can vary, but it remains characterful and complex thanks to maturation in first- and second-fill sherry casks.

faq.question: How does the maritime influence manifest in the taste of Bunnahabhain Stiuireadair?
faq.answer: The maritime character is especially noticeable on the palate, where salty notes dominate and evoke seawater. This salty element blends with sweet dried fruits and nutty aromas, clearly underscoring the maritime influence from the Islay region.

faq.question: What role do the sherry casks play in the maturation of Bunnahabhain Stiuireadair?
faq.answer: Maturation in first- and second-fill sherry casks imparts the whisky with sweet notes of dried fruits like plums, as well as vanilla and caramel aromas. At the same time, the sherry influence remains present in the flavor without overpowering the salty, maritime character.

item.name: Lagavulin 16 Years
item.description: The single malt whisky Lagavulin 16 Years is the best-selling bottling of the Islay distillery. Every now and then, there are even supply bottlenecks, which is rather unusual for a classic malt. Is the Scotch really that good?We can no longer count how many bottles it is now. It may even be the 6th or 7th. We often hear that Lagavulin 16 has lost some of its quality. We absolutely do not see it that way. We have had at least consistent quality over the last 12 or 13 years. But perhaps the competition has become stronger, so that the Islay malt no longer stands out so much?

tasting_notes.nose: On the nose, Lagavulin 16 always reminds us of an old leather sofa in a dark corner with an oak table on which berries are standing. We smell strong peat smoke, iodine and earth. A berry note coats various sweet smoky aromas with a certain saltiness at the edge.
tasting_notes.palate: Peat smoke spreads softly and very intensively. The following sweetness, which covers everything, is gradually replaced by a maritime saltiness. Later, a light oak note and, with a little imagination, a kind of crème brûlée become perceptible. The composition reminds us a little of slightly burnt wholemeal spelt toast.
tasting_notes.finish: Full and with lots of salt and peat smoke, the Lagavulin 16 years says goodbye. It lingers for a long time.

conclusion.marcel: Lagavulin 16 Year is, for me, a true classic of enjoyment – complex, intense, yet wonderfully balanced. This smoky Islay gem brings joy to the glass every time without overwhelming. The price-to-quality ratio is absolutely spot on, so you can buy with confidence!
conclusion.sascha: The Lagavulin 16 Year impresses with an outstanding balance between intense smokiness and complex aromas that unfold in a structured and harmonious way. Despite its depth, it never feels overloaded, making it an archetypal Islay malt with excellent maturity. From a technical standpoint, it sets the benchmark for smoky single malts.

faq.question: Why is Lagavulin 16 Year considered typical for Islay whiskies?
faq.answer: Lagavulin 16 Year combines characteristic Islay traits such as intense peat smoke, maritime saltiness, and a hint of iodine. This combination makes it a prime example of the rugged, maritime whiskies from this region.

faq.question: How does the taste of Lagavulin 16 Year change as you drink it?
faq.answer: Lagavulin 16 Year starts soft with strong peat smoke, followed by a sweet note that gradually gives way to a salty sea breeze. The finish is dominated by salt and smoke, which linger for a long time.

faq.question: Has the quality of Lagavulin 16 Year changed in recent years?
faq.answer: Contrary to some opinions, the quality of Lagavulin 16 Year has remained consistent according to long-term observation. It may seem less dominant because competition from other whiskies from Islay and around the world has become stronger.

item.name: Mackmyra Intelligens AI:01
item.description: Mackmyra Intelligens AI:01 is a single malt whisky from Sweden whose recipe was created with the help of artificial intelligence. Since both of us (Marcel & Sascha) come from a computer science background, we are more than excited to see what awaits us here.Combine customer preferences with sales data and existing recipes, throw a bit of statistics into the pot and then run the learning model on a Microsoft cloud and you magically get a whisky of the highest order. At least that's the idea.

tasting_notes.nose: Orange and apricot come to us with a slightly tangy scent. Surrounded by citrus fruits, we have light oak barrel notes, a hint of vanilla and, with a little imagination, a nuance of aniseed and ginger. There is also some indefinable herbal stuff.
tasting_notes.palate: In contrast to the nose, we have a surprising amount of oak here. Especially the bitterness of the tannins is very present. Nevertheless, the composition is soft and sweet enough (pear, caramel) so that it remains interesting. We think we perceive a little beech smoke, but we are not quite sure. It could also be a toasted oak barrel. By the way, the herbal stuff is there again, which we still don't know what it is supposed to be.
tasting_notes.finish: The herbal stuff remains. Otherwise slightly dry with citrus and malt. A little bitter and sharp at the same time. Slightly sweet again towards the end.

conclusion.marcel: Mackmyra Intelligens AI:01 shows that AI whisky has yet to hit the mark – the character comes across as a bit sharp and the herbal chaos doesn’t quite ignite. Still, it’s fun to try such experiments, even if the drinking experience is more solid than spectacular.
conclusion.sascha: The balance between sweetness and austere oak is intriguing, but the dominant bitterness of the tannins and the indefinable herbal profile feel underdeveloped. Mackmyra Intelligens AI:01 remains an exciting AI project, though it lacks the elegance and harmonious structure needed for top-tier status.

faq.question: How does the use of artificial intelligence influence the flavor profile of Mackmyra Intelligens AI:01?
faq.answer: Artificial intelligence combines customer preferences, sales data, and existing recipes to develop a balanced and novel flavor profile. This results in complex aromas that blend classic notes like citrus, vanilla, and oak with unusual herbal and spice nuances.

faq.question: Why is there no age statement on Mackmyra Intelligens AI:01?
faq.answer: Mackmyra Intelligens AI:01 is a so-called 'No Age Statement' (NAS) whisky. This means the whiskies used can have varying maturation periods, and no single age is specified. The goal is to achieve a consistent and innovative flavor profile through blending.

faq.question: What role does the oak cask play in the taste of this whisky?
faq.answer: The oak cask significantly contributes to the bitterness and tannin structure present on the palate. Alongside classic oak notes, hints of toasted wood or possibly beech smoke can be detected, making the whisky complex and multi-layered.

item.name: Kavalan Distillery Select No.1
item.description: In 2008, just under 15 years ago, the first bottlings of the Taiwanese distillery appeared and only four years later conquered the world market within a very short time. Rightly so?The Whiskys of the Kavalan distillery mature in a subtropical climate. Unlike European or Scottish Whiskys, an intense flavor can be achieved even in much shorter storage time. In principle, Kavalan Whiskys are delivered without age indication. Presumably, this is usually a maximum of 5 years, rather less.

tasting_notes.nose: We smell a fresh, soft and fruity Whisky. Aromas of sweet honey, apple, vanilla and cherry are clearly dominant. Ripe and soft mango is also present and we'd have to lie if we didn't say the stuff clearly reminds us of oatmeal pops. Overall pleasant and not intrusive.
tasting_notes.palate: What the smell promises, the taste keeps. Notes of caramel and honey are accompanied by a slight bitterness at the edge. The fruitiness of the nose is not as intense again here. Sweet spices and grains can be tasted in the background and perhaps a very marginal note of cinnamon. Ripe banana rounds out the picture.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is medium long. A little dry on the palate, also bitter in the marginal notes. Instead of caramel, we have more brown sugar and a slightly alcoholic undertone.

conclusion.marcel: Kavalan Distillery Select No.1 delivers a whole lot of sweet fruit on the palate, almost like a drinkable oat pop – charming, but a bit too scattered for my taste. For fans of young, exotic Taiwanese whiskies, it’s definitely worth a try if you want to experience something different.
conclusion.sascha: The structure of the Kavalan Distillery Select No.1 still feels unbalanced, with a dominant sweetness and little harmony between fruit and bitterness. Despite the interesting variety of aromas, it lacks depth and clear balance, which somewhat limits the enjoyment.

faq.question: Why do Kavalan whiskies mature faster than Scottish whiskies?
faq.answer: Kavalan whiskies mature in a subtropical climate with high humidity and temperatures significantly higher than in Scotland. These conditions accelerate maturation in the cask, allowing flavors to be extracted more quickly and enabling the whisky to develop complex taste profiles in a shorter time.

faq.question: How does Taiwan’s subtropical climate influence the flavor of Kavalan Distillery Select No.1?
faq.answer: The subtropical climate leads to more intense interaction with the cask, resulting in fruity and sweet aromas like honey, apple, and mango. At the same time, the faster maturation also produces subtle bitter and spicy notes, giving the whisky a balanced complexity.

faq.question: Why does Kavalan Distillery Select No.1 not display an age statement on the bottle?
faq.answer: Kavalan usually omits an age statement because maturation in Taiwan happens faster due to the climate, and age alone does not reflect the whisky’s quality or style. These whiskies are typically younger than five years but impress with intense aroma and flavor despite the short aging period.

item.name: Knockando Richly Matured 15 Years - (2005)
item.description: Little black hill is roughly the translation of Knockando from the Gaelic. The distillery is located near Tamdhu and Cardhu. However, the geographical proximity has nothing to do with the taste profiles. These are in fact very different.Knockando actually focuses on the production of blended malts. However, there are a few bottlings of their own, such as the 12-years Classic Malt and the 15-years Richly Matured. We have the older of the two in our glass.

tasting_notes.nose: Forest floor with a blob of mashed potatoes. With far too much nutmeg and a musty muskiness. Somehow there is also a bit of sherry and oak in there. There are also slight roasted aromas and something fruity hidden somewhere. A very strange combination.
tasting_notes.palate: Spicy and oaky, the Knockando 15 year turns into a fruity but malty sweetness. The fruitiness reminds us a little of baked apple pie. This single malt is not suitable for beginners. A very complex taste experience that gains in sweetness from sip to sip.
tasting_notes.finish: The "Richly Matured" Whisky accompanies us for a long time, sweetly spicy and warming.

conclusion.marcel: Knockando Richly Matured 15 Years is definitely not a gentle, bittersweet caress, but rather a whisky with character that doesn’t reveal itself immediately to everyone. The unusual combination of forest floor and sweet-spicy notes creates an exciting drinking experience that should be enjoyed with a sense of humor and patience.
conclusion.sascha: The Knockando 15 Years displays a complex aroma profile with spicy-oaky and fruity elements that are structurally well balanced, yet the overall impression feels somewhat discordant. For lovers of layered Speyside profiles, it offers an interesting, albeit challenging, tasting experience.

faq.question: What makes the taste of Knockando Richly Matured 15 Years special?
faq.answer: The whisky is characterized by an unusual combination of forest floor, nutmeg, sherry notes, and light roasted aromas. On the palate, spicy oak tones develop, transitioning into a malty fruit sweetness with hints of baked apple pie.

faq.question: Why is Knockando 15 Years not suitable for whisky beginners?
faq.answer: The complex and multi-layered flavor profile, featuring unusual notes like mashed potatoes, moss, and intense spices, requires some experience to fully appreciate the aromas. For beginners, this combination can come across as too idiosyncratic or difficult to access.

faq.question: How does Knockando differ from neighboring distilleries like Tamdhu or Cardhu?
faq.answer: Although Knockando is geographically close to Tamdhu and Cardhu, the flavor profiles differ significantly. Knockando focuses on producing malts for blended whiskies, with its own bottlings often showcasing a distinctive and complex character with unusual notes.

item.name: Glenmorangie 10 Years
item.description: Glenmorangie 10 Years is one of the best-selling single malts in Scotland and is now available in a wide variety of editions, such as the giraffe edition. This is supposed to stand for the particularly high stills of the distillery located in the Highlands.The malt was matured in first and refill bourbon casks, which Glenmorangie fills no more than twice. The soft character of the single malt whisky is supported by the 8-metre-high stills.

tasting_notes.nose: Vanilla, fruity, fresh. Very summery and soft, we smell light fruits, a little nut. A very clear vanilla note hovers over everything. A very clear aroma profile that is pursued here. "You know what this reminds me of? Jam." / "No, it's clearly jelly to me!" So much for that.
tasting_notes.palate: The taste is also quite clearly defined. We think of almonds, apricot, cotton candy. Sweetish malty and very soft, mild flavors make us think of summer.
tasting_notes.finish: Medium length says goodbye to a slight oakiness in combination with white wine.

conclusion.marcel: A whisky that’s simply fun with its sweet vanilla and fruity character – perfect for relaxed summer evenings. Not too complicated, but precisely because of that, very approachable and pleasant to drink. For just under 35 euros, you get a solid malt that fits perfectly into any collection.
conclusion.sascha: The Glenmorangie 10 Year impresses with a clearly structured aroma profile and a balanced interplay between sweet bourbon notes and subtle oakiness. Its gentle texture and medium-length finish reveal an elegant maturation, making it a reliable Highland standard. For beginners and enthusiasts who appreciate clarity and finesse, it’s an affordable recommendation.

faq.question: How do the tall stills influence the taste of Glenmorangie 10 Year?
faq.answer: The approximately 8-meter-high stills ensure an exceptionally gentle distillation, as only the lighter vapors condense. This process gives Glenmorangie 10 Year its soft, mild character with clear vanilla and fruit notes.

faq.question: Why does Glenmorangie mainly use first-fill and refill bourbon casks for maturation?
faq.answer: Glenmorangie uses first-fill and refill bourbon casks, which are filled a maximum of twice, to give the whisky a balanced combination of fruity, vanilla aromas and light oakiness without overpowering the flavor.

faq.question: What flavor notes can be expected from Glenmorangie 10 Year?
faq.answer: Typical aromas include vanilla, light fruits like apricot, almonds, cotton candy, and a subtle nuttiness. The finish is medium-length with a fine blend of gentle oakiness and hints of white wine.

item.name: Dingle Single Malt Irish Whiskey (2021)
item.description: "Dingle. Hand-made character. Unique Whiskey." - This is the slogan under which the still small distillery has been producing its whiskey by hand since 2012. Distillery young, whiskey young. Can that work?Dingle Single Malt Irish Whiskey from 2021 is mainly a 6-7 years spirit matured in first-fill bourbon casks and PX sherry casks and first bottling. Dingle is one of the few Irish distilleries that exclusively produce their own spirit.

tasting_notes.nose: We have a heavy-smelling, deep honey-coloured single malt in the glass, which begins with an aniseed smell and transitions into a sweet citrus note. After the heavy notes fade away, softer notes take over, such as peach, ripe pear and creamy caramel.
tasting_notes.palate: Very strong, the palate is startled by a strongly acidic note. Again, softer flavours like sugar syrup, baked apple with vanilla sauce and candy floss take over after the initial tightening. Bitterness can be tasted briefly in between. We think of baking cocoa here.
tasting_notes.finish: The finish is rather short, partly spicy-minty and reminds us strongly of forest honey and spruce forest.

conclusion.marcel: A whisky that invites you to dream with its sweet and soft aromas – perfect for relaxed late summer evenings. You can sense the youthful notes of the distillery, but that’s exactly what makes the Dingle so charming and lively. The more you enjoy it, the more it reveals its character – a genuine pleasure to drink for those who don’t take it too seriously.
conclusion.sascha: The balance between heavy, sour, and sweet notes is quite successful here, even though the brief spiciness and bitterness somewhat fragment the finish. The structure feels young and vibrant, which is typical for a first release matured in first-fill bourbon and PX sherry casks. For connoisseurs looking for a craftily solid composition with potential for development, this Irish single malt is an interesting choice.

faq.question: How does the Dingle Single Malt Irish Whiskey 2021 differ from older Irish whiskeys?
faq.answer: The Dingle Single Malt 2021 is a comparatively young whiskey, primarily aged 6-7 years in first-fill bourbon and PX sherry casks. Unlike many traditional Irish whiskeys, Dingle focuses on artisanal production using exclusively their own distillate without blending in any foreign whiskeys.

faq.question: What aromas can be expected from the Dingle Single Malt Irish Whiskey 2021?
faq.answer: On the nose, anise and sweet citrus notes dominate, followed by peach, ripe pear, and creamy caramel. On the palate, sour notes appear first, then flavors of sugar syrup, baked apple with vanilla sauce, and cotton candy, accompanied by a brief bitter hint of baking cocoa. The finish is spicy and minty with touches of forest honey and spruce forest.

faq.question: Why is the Dingle Single Malt Irish Whiskey 2021 considered to be aged 6-7 years despite lacking an official age statement?
faq.answer: Although there is no official age statement on the bottle, the whiskey is mainly based on distillates aged about 6-7 years in first-fill bourbon and PX sherry casks. This information comes from distillery sources and allows for a realistic assessment of the maturation period.

item.name: Octomore 10.3 6 Years (2013/2019)
item.description: Statistically, Octomore is all about the degree of peatiness. Despite its young age, however, we have an incredibly complex whisky in the glass.Why 10.3? There is a simple system behind the numbering. The 10 stands for the series, the 3 for a specific characteristic. In this case, it is the maturation in exclusively ex-American oak barrels. In the 10.3 we have a peat content of 114PPM (phenol parts per million).

tasting_notes.nose: "For such a smoky one, it smells bright and fresh. Not like an Ardbeg ashtray." was Sascha's first comment.Instead of the dark smoke, we have spring-like notes of lemon balm. We also smell freshly roasted coffee and boiled custard. Peat smoke is also present, of course.
tasting_notes.palate: What kind of sugar is that? We think of a very sugary cake with a massive layer of fondant. Somehow also peat smoke, but damn sweet. A variety of flavours were hiding underneath. On the one hand we have a salty marimality, on the other hand a slightly lemony-limey sourness. Very, very exciting.
tasting_notes.finish: Really warming and "ultra long" we have smoked ham, rubber, spices, and iodine-like notes in the finish. For half an hour!

conclusion.marcel: Who would have thought that a six-years whisky could be so multi-layered and enjoyable? Octomore 10.3 surprises with its sweet peat note and ultra-long finish – a genuine tasting experience that’s a lot of fun, even if the price is quite steep.
conclusion.sascha: The Octomore 10.3 impresses with a remarkable balance between intense peatiness and complex sweetness, showing astonishing depth despite its youth. The structured variety of aromas and the long finish make it a technically impressive smoky whisky, whose price reflects its exclusivity.

faq.question: What does the high peat level of 114 PPM in the Octomore 10.3 mean?
faq.answer: The peat level of 114 PPM (Phenol Parts per Million) indicates how heavily the barley was peated before distillation. The result is a very smoky whisky, yet Octomore 10.3 offers a surprisingly fresh and multi-dimensional aroma profile that goes beyond pure smoke despite the high value.

faq.question: How does maturation in ex-American oak casks influence the flavor of the Octomore 10.3?
faq.answer: Maturing exclusively in ex-American oak casks imparts sweet vanilla and caramel notes as well as a certain fruitiness to the Octomore 10.3. This cask choice adds complexity and complements the intense peat smoke with sweet and spicy flavors.

faq.question: Why does such a young whisky like the Octomore 10.3 taste so complex?
faq.answer: Although the Octomore 10.3 is only six years old, the combination of heavily peated barley, cask selection, and the high alcohol strength of 61.3% ABV creates an intense and multi-layered aroma profile. The balance of sweet, salty, smoky, and fresh notes makes it particularly exciting.

item.name: Glenfiddich 12 Years
item.description: The Glenfiddich 12 year is perhaps the best known classic and Scotch for beginners. However, there are also alternatives in this price range.A golden stag proudly presents its antlers on the bottle. In dark green and gold, the bottle and outer packaging look very noble. A successful and harmonious design.

tasting_notes.nose: Sweetly, the Scotch reminds us of bright fruits, such as ripe pears and apples. A hint of honey is perceptible in the background. Like the bottle - an elegantly balanced composition.
tasting_notes.palate: Not too complex and rather straightforward, we taste mild and balanced fresh fruits. A light honey aroma also crystallises.
tasting_notes.finish: Very briefly, a little oak and a slight bitterness remain.

conclusion.marcel: A mild, straightforward pleasure that scores with fruity notes and a hint of honey – perfect for cozy evenings without any fuss. Those looking for a relaxed introduction to the world of single malts won’t be disappointed here, even if the Glenfiddich 12 Year doesn’t hold any surprises.
conclusion.sascha: The Glenfiddich 12 Year impresses with a clear, balanced structure featuring subtle fruity sweetness and gentle honey tones, yet it remains somewhat shallow and short on the finish. It offers a solid, well-balanced foundation for beginners, but connoisseurs will likely crave more complexity and depth.

faq.question: How does the Glenfiddich 12 Year differ from other 12-years single malts?
faq.answer: The Glenfiddich 12 Year is characterized by its mild, fruity notes with aromas of ripe pears, apples, and a touch of honey. Compared to other 12-years single malts, it is less complex and more straightforward, making it especially accessible for newcomers.

faq.question: What kind of maturation influences the taste of the Glenfiddich 12 Year?
faq.answer: The Glenfiddich 12 Year matures primarily in American white oak barrels that previously held bourbon. This cask aging imparts the sweet fruity aromas and the gentle wood notes that are noticeable in both flavor and finish.

faq.question: Is the Glenfiddich 12 Year suitable for cocktails, or should it be enjoyed neat?
faq.answer: The Glenfiddich 12 Year is suitable both for drinking neat and for classic whisky cocktails. Thanks to its mild and fruity flavors, it remains distinctive in mixed drinks without losing character.

item.name: Johnnie Walker Black Label 12 Years
item.description: Blended from over 40 different Scotch whiskies and grains, Johnnie Walker is the most widely used whisky for cocktails in bars. We take a look at whether it also delivers what it promises when neat.First bottled in 1909, the Black Label has been with us for over 100 years and has become the world's most famous Scotch blend. Over the years, the whisky, against which everyone had to measure themselves at the time, has won countless awards.

tasting_notes.nose: Sweet and tart. Bright grapes, lemony aromas and grapefruit are accompanied by vanilla. A light smoky note resonates.
tasting_notes.palate: It tastes younger than it is. A certain alcoholic sharpness transitions into strong oaky, cereal aromas. Fresh rye bread comes to mind. The light smoke from the nose is also recognisable here. Later, we also notice the smelled aromas of vanilla and lemon. However, the oaky taste clearly predominates.
tasting_notes.finish: In the finish we have bitter notes paired with light fruit and a not excessive amount of dried wood. There is also a hint of smoke.

conclusion.marcel: For just under 20 euros, the Johnnie Walker Black Label 12 Year offers a solid companion, especially impressive as a cocktail base. While it lacks the wow factor when sipped neat, its charming smoke and sweet-fruity notes make it approachable and easygoing.
conclusion.sascha: The Johnnie Walker Black Label presents a structurally balanced profile but falls short of expectations in complexity. The dominant oak tones and slight alcoholic sharpness make it feel more like a mixer than a whisky to savor neat.

faq.question: What makes the Johnnie Walker Black Label 12 Year special compared to other blended Scotch whiskies?
faq.answer: The Johnnie Walker Black Label 12 Year combines over 40 different malt and grain whiskies, giving it a complex flavor structure. Its balanced blend of sweet, fruity, and smoky notes, along with a pronounced oak character, sets it apart from many other blends.

faq.question: How does the 12-year age influence the taste of the Johnnie Walker Black Label?
faq.answer: The 12 years of maturation ensure a harmonious integration of flavors: the whisky displays youthful freshness with lemony and fruity notes alongside matured oak tones and a subtle smoky hint. The age contributes to a balanced profile that’s not too heavy.

faq.question: Is the Johnnie Walker Black Label 12 Year recommended to drink neat or mainly for mixing?
faq.answer: Although the Johnnie Walker Black Label is often used as a cocktail base, its complexity and balanced flavors also make it well suited for drinking neat. Its multi-layered character unfolds especially well when sipped slowly without additives.

item.name: Paul John Oloroso Select Cask
item.description: Indian Whisky - Amrut probably comes to mind most. But the distillery John Paul should also be on your mind. Although they have only been distilling there since 1992, which is rather young compared to most Scottish distilleries, the Whiskys are still worth a look.The Select Cask Whisky series is composed of selected casks, in this case Oloroso Sherry casks. The bronze colour (tinted) in the glass is also intended to reflect this maturation.

tasting_notes.nose: We are overwhelmed by very strong and intense sherry aromas. The initial sweet impression quickly fades and a tart note of dark chocolate spreads. A little caramel and blood orange resonate in the background.
tasting_notes.palate: Straightforward, the probably relatively young malt comes along with a biting alcohol suit. Softer and sweeter aromas are released shortly afterwards. We think of chocolate (no whole milk!) and red berries. A little oak appears now and then.
tasting_notes.finish: The sherry bomb stays for a very, very long time. The chocolate stays, caramel also. A little coffee is added.

conclusion.marcel: This Paul John Oloroso Select Cask is a true experience for anyone who loves bold sherry explosions – not overly sweet, but packed with character and a long finish that warms the senses. There’s hardly room for another dram afterward, so intense is the finish. If you’re in the mood for a daring Indian whisky with edges and character, this one serves you perfectly.
conclusion.sascha: The Paul John Oloroso Select Cask impresses with its balanced structure and the successful harmony between intense sherry notes and a subtle oak presence. The 48% ABV gives it a clear bite without overpowering the enjoyment, and the long-lasting finish reveals a harmonious complexity. Overall, a very well-crafted, powerful whisky with precise cask maturation.

faq.question: What makes the Paul John Oloroso Select Cask stand out compared to other Indian whiskies?
faq.answer: The Paul John Oloroso Select Cask distinguishes itself through maturation in carefully selected Oloroso sherry casks, which impart intense sherry aromas and a complex depth of flavor featuring notes of dark chocolate and caramel. Unlike many other Indian whiskies, the focus here is strongly on cask selection and the resulting sherry influence.

faq.question: How does maturation in Oloroso sherry casks influence the flavor of this whisky?
faq.answer: The Oloroso sherry casks impart robust, intense aromas of sherry, dark chocolate, and caramel to the whisky. This cask maturation provides a long-lasting, slightly dry sweetness and complex nuances such as blood orange and coffee on the finish, making the whisky particularly layered and aromatic.

faq.question: Why does the Paul John Oloroso Select Cask have no age statement on the bottle?
faq.answer: The Paul John Oloroso Select Cask is a so-called No-Age-Statement (NAS) whisky, meaning the exact age is not disclosed. The distillery focuses here on the quality and character of the whisky, determined by cask selection and blending, rather than relying solely on age as a measure of quality.

item.name: Nikka Whisky From The Barrel
item.description: Since the beginning of 2021, Nikka Whisky From The Barrel can no longer officially call itself Japanese whisky. No reason not to taste the blend, we think.At the beginning of 2021, the Japanese Spirits and Liqueurs Association announced the standards for the labelling of Japanese whisky.This clearly defined what is and what is not Japanese Whisky. It is an open secret that many Nikka Whiskys contain a little Ben Nevis from Scotland - including Nikka Whisky From The Barrel. Nikka promises to continue producing wonderful whiskies and is making every effort to comply with the new standards, and at that time also updated its website accordingly at short notice. We think more transparency makes sense - and even knowing that we don't have real Japanese whisky in our glass, we can still enjoy it.

tasting_notes.nose: At first, we are struck by the very intense aromas of vanilla and pear. Interestingly, the fruitiness predominates here and we only perceive subliminal spices, such as nutmeg. We feel a bit like we are in a Japanese orchard.
tasting_notes.palate: On the palate, oaky tannins clearly predominate, even if the fruitiness remains. Nuts and dried fruit linger in the background.
tasting_notes.finish: Finally, the vanilla notes fight their way to the fore in the medium-long finish. Nevertheless, oaky notes resonate. Despite the 51.4% ABV, the bottling is mild and very enjoyable even without dilution with water.

conclusion.marcel: A genuine delight for anyone who appreciates fruity whiskies with plenty of character – despite lacking the official Japanese origin, this glass is pure drinking pleasure. This blend demonstrates that Nikka convinces with passion and craftsmanship, even if it is no longer officially classified as Japanese whisky.
conclusion.sascha: Technically, a well-balanced blend with clear structure and robust oaky tannins, which impresses with its fruitiness and medium-length vanilla finish. The 51.4% ABV is well integrated, yet it lacks some of the depth one would expect from premium Japanese whiskies.

faq.question: Why has Nikka Whisky From The Barrel not been allowed to be labeled as Japanese whisky since 2021?
faq.answer: Since early 2021, the Japan Spirits & Liqueurs Makers Association has established clear standards for the designation "Japanese whisky." Because Nikka Whisky From The Barrel contains some Scottish whisky (Ben Nevis), it no longer meets these criteria and therefore cannot officially be labeled as Japanese whisky.

faq.question: How does the high alcohol content of 51.4% affect the tasting experience of Nikka Whisky From The Barrel?
faq.answer: Despite the high alcohol content of 51.4%, the whisky comes across as surprisingly mild and balanced. The intense vanilla and fruit notes remain prominent, while the oaky tannins provide a complex structure. Many enthusiasts even prefer to enjoy it neat, without adding water.

faq.question: Which flavor notes make Nikka Whisky From The Barrel particularly distinctive?
faq.answer: The whisky is characterized by intense fruitiness with aromas of vanilla and pear, accompanied by subtle spices like nutmeg. On the palate, oaky tannins, nuts, and dried fruits dominate, while the finish is marked by vanilla notes and a delicate oak spiciness.

item.name: Kinahan's Irish Whiskey KASC Project Batch 03
item.description: Whisky production as an experiment - that's something you have to dare to do. The KASC Project uses handmade hybrid casks. Let's take a look at what that means.Think of a hybrid cask as a newly assembled cask made from the staves of various other casks. Five casks of different origins are used: Portuguese oak, American oak, French oak, Hungarian oak and chestnut. This allows for unique flavours to emerge. At Kinahans, they write about it: So - if you are not ready to leave your comfort zone, the hybrid barrel whiskey releases are simply not for you.

image.alt: Kinahan's Irish Whiskey KASC Project Batch 03

tasting_notes.nose: Fruity! We have strong aromas of caramelised fruits, such as pear and apricot on the nose. Some fig and wood notes can be perceived subliminally.
tasting_notes.palate: It tastes like it smells. Besides the apricot, we also have the taste of ripe mango and a little more in the foreground an intense oak aroma. In the background we taste fine nuts. Pecans.
tasting_notes.finish: In the finish, the fruitiness disappears and is replaced by a sharp peppery spiciness. A little oak resonates.

conclusion.marcel: A bold experiment that’s a lot of fun and leaves you wanting more from the KASC Project. The fruity character, with its peppery spice, delivers genuine drinking pleasure—perfect for relaxed summer evenings. Definitely an exciting release for fans of new taste experiences.
conclusion.sascha: The KASC Project Batch 03 impresses with an unusual cask composition that gives the whisky a complex structure. Despite the experimental cask blend, the whisky feels well balanced, with clearly defined oak spice and a pronounced fruit note. For lovers of innovative Irish whiskeys, this release offers a solid foundation for what’s to come.

faq.question: What is a Hybrid Cask and how does it influence the flavor of Kinahan's KASC Project Batch 03?
faq.answer: A Hybrid Cask is a barrel constructed from staves of different types of wood. In Kinahan's KASC Project Batch 03, staves from Portuguese, American, French, and Hungarian oak, as well as chestnut, are combined. This unusual cask construction results in complex and unique aromas ranging from fruity notes to intense oak and spicy pepper tones.

faq.question: Why does Kinahan's KASC Project Batch 03 have no age statement (No Age Statement)?
faq.answer: Kinahan's KASC Project Batch 03 deliberately forgoes an age statement because the focus is on the experimental maturation process in the hybrid casks. The emphasis is on the combination of different wood types and their influence on the flavor, rather than the whisky’s age.

faq.question: How should Kinahan's KASC Project Batch 03 be best tasted to fully appreciate all its aromas?
faq.answer: To fully experience the complex aromas of Kinahan's KASC Project Batch 03, it is recommended to taste the whisky at room temperature in a tulip-shaped glass. This allows the fruity notes of pear, apricot, and mango, as well as the subtle wood and nut aromas, to develop optimally. A small splash of water can help highlight the intense oak notes and the peppery spice on the finish.

item.name: Starward Octave Barrels The Octavius 3 Years (2018/2021)
item.description: Specialising in red wine barrels, Starward takes advantage of the Australian climate and experiments with various bottlings in Melbourne, Australia's second largest city.Australia is really not known as a whisky country. "Four seasons in a day" say the inhabitants about their own climate. In the morning, you never know whether you need a sunshade or an umbrella. But this means that the whisky works much faster with the cask than it would if the temperatures were constant. At Starward, they think in terms of projects. There is not "the one" standard bottling, as is the case with other distilleries. Basically, they bottle with a lower alcohol content than usual, which should lead to a sweeter character. In addition, the climate is drier. This in turn means that the alcohol content of the whisky increases instead of decreasing.

image.alt: Starward Octave Barrels The Octavius 2018 / 2021 3 Years

tasting_notes.nose: We immediately feel like we are in a car paint shop. After the first impression has faded, bourbon-like aromas of ripe peach take over, which are clearly replaced by berry-like red wine at the end. An interesting journey.
tasting_notes.palate: Toasted toastbread with maple syrup goes hand in hand with overly toasted croutons. In the background, we perceive oak and a slight alcoholic spiciness.
tasting_notes.finish: If we were in Finland in the snow at -20 degrees, the long, very alcoholic, spicy finish would probably do us good and warm us up. Otherwise, unfortunately, there's not much.

conclusion.marcel: For a three-years Australian whisky, the Octavius surprisingly delivers a lot of character and drinking pleasure – even if the alcoholic sharpness sometimes takes center stage. The experiment with small casks and the Down Under climate produces exciting aromas that leave you wanting more, but an additional two years of maturation would definitely do it good.
conclusion.sascha: The Octavius shows interesting approaches in balance and complexity, but suffers from an overly dominant alcoholic sharpness that limits the tasting experience. The use of small 100-liter casks and the Australian climate accelerate maturation, yet a longer cask time would be necessary to harmonize the structure and fully realize its potential.

faq.question: How does the Australian climate influence the maturation of the Starward Octave Barrels The Octavius?
faq.answer: The variable climate in Melbourne, with rapid temperature fluctuations, causes the whisky to interact with the cask more quickly than under constant temperatures. This results in more intense flavor development, while the alcohol content tends to rise due to the dry climate rather than decrease.

faq.question: What role do red wine casks play in this whisky?
faq.answer: Starward uses red wine casks to impart fruity and berry notes to the whisky. These casks contribute to the characteristic aromas of ripe peach and red wine that define the flavor profile of the Octavius.

faq.question: Why does the Starward Octave Barrels The Octavius have a comparatively low alcohol content of 48%?
faq.answer: Starward bottles this whisky at a lower-than-usual alcohol level to create a sweeter and more approachable character. However, the subsequent maturation in the dry Australian climate causes the alcohol content to increase through evaporation.

item.name: Highland Park 15 Years Viking Heart
item.description: The 15 Years Viking Heart is supposed to fill the gap of the standard bottlings between the 12 Years and the 18 Years Highland Park and thus expand the range of the distillery from the Orkney Islands. Unfortunately, we are not entirely convinced.The topic of Vikings as a marketing tool is certainly debatable, especially since the Vikings certainly did not produce whisky in their 500 years on Orkney. Nevertheless, the legends and symbols of the Nordic people always lend themselves to an exciting all-round marketing package.The ceramic bottle is of course an eye-catcher on the shelf and feels very good. The well-known lion and snake pattern as well as a heart are embossed on the bottle. The reason for the choice of the bottle is commitment to the environment, as well as the storage of Whiksy in stone vessels, as was the rule 200 years ago - back to the roots combined with the idea of sustainability, which shapes our future. We like that.
